raw_text: ABERDEEN AND TEMAIR, 1ST MARQUESS OF (creat. 1915); 7TH EARL, creat. 1682; VISCT. FROMARTINE, BARON HADDO, METCHIC, TAVES, and KELLIE, 1882; Bart., 1642 (Scot.); VISCT. GORDON OF ABERDEEN, 1814 (U.K.), under which title he sits in the House of Lords; K.T. (1906); P.C. (1886); G.C.M.G. (1896); G.C.V.O., JOHN CAMPBELL HAMILTON-GORDON, 3RD son of the 5th Earl.—B. 1847; succeeded his brother, 1870; lord-lieut. of Aberdeenshire, 1880; high commr. to gen. assm., Church of Scotland, 1881-6; lord-lieut. of Ireland, Feb. to Aug., 1886; gov-gen. of Canada, 1893 to 1898; representative of Canada on Pacific Cable Board, 1899 to 1900; lord-Lieut. of Ireland, 1906-1915.
ABEYEWARDENE, HENRY WILKIN.—B. 1874; apptd. to cls. V. Ceylon civ. serv., Sept., 1923; extra fl. asst., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1923; extra office asst., Colombo kach., Feb., 1929.

ABRAHAM, HENRY CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Taunton's Schl., Southampton and Southampton Univ. Coll.; survr. probn., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; survr., grade II, topo. br., F.M.S., Mar., 1912; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), Sept., 1915; lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), July, 1917; asst. supdt., topo. br. Jan., 1919; rel. comm., R.G.A. (S.R.), Mar., 1920; ag. supdt., topo. br. July, 1927; ag. supdt., surveys, Penang, Feb., 1928; Empire conf., survey offs., Southampton and London, July, 1928; sen. asst. supdt., topo. br., Jan., 1929.

ABRAHAM, JOHN CONRAD, M.A. (Oxon.), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Keswick Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; asst. res., Nyassaland, 1911; sp. serv., carrier transport, N.F.F., 1914-18; ment. in despatches, three times; re., 1920; admtn. offr., 1st g ade, 1923; ag. asst. ch. sec., Nov., 1926 and May to Dec., 1928; examr., Chiyao and Chinyanja languages.

ABRAHAMS, SIDNEY SOLOMON.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A., L.L.B.; called to the bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanzibar, June, 1915; crown counsel, Kenya, Nov., 1915, but did not proceed; seconded for serv. with Mesopotamia civil admtn., Jan., 1920; advoc.-gen. and registr. of companies, Baghdad, Apr., 1920; pres. ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, Jan., 1921; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, 30th June, 1922; atty.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1925; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1928; editor, English Reports, annotated, 1914-15; compiled (jointly), Zanzibar law rept., 1868-1918.

ABRAMS, ARTHUR BARROW.—Fellow of Cent. Assoc. of Accnts. (Incor.) England; asst. head storekeeper, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 9th Oct., 1905; head storekeeper, 26th May, 1906; offl. mem. town coun., Sept.-Oct., 1906; asst. ch. clk. and accnt., 12th May, 1907; ch. clk. and accnt., 18th Oct. 1908.

ABRAMSON, MAJOR ALBERT, C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E.—B. 1876; war serv. with E.E.F.; O.B.E., 1918; twice ment. in desps.; mily. gov., Hebron, 1918; chmn., land comsn., and dir., land dept., 1920; oh. Br. rep., Transjordan, 1921; dist. gov., southern dist., 1922; dist. comsnr., northern dist., 1925.

ACHESON, A. B.—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, Rifle Brigade and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-19; served in France, Belgium and Germany; ment. in desps., Croix-de-Guerre; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to comte., on the development of scientific training and research 19th Nov., 1920; sec. to comte., on trade and taxation for Br. W. Africa, Oct. to Dec., 1921; priv. sec. to permanent under-secr., 3rd Oct., 1923; ag. prin., Jan., 1926.
ACLAND, FREDERICK A.—B. 1861; journalist, England, U.S.A., and Canada; apptd. sec. dept. of lab., Canada, 1907; deputy min. of lab., 1908; King's printer, 15th June, 1921.
ADAMS, HON. ALEXANDER SAMUEL.—B. 1861; ed., pub. schls. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; barrister and solr., 1883; crown solr., 1920; judge, N.Z. sup. obl., 1921.

ADAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, D.Sc. (N.Z.), A.I.A. (Lond.), F.R.A.S.—B. 1870; ed. Christchurch Boys' High Schl., Wellington Coll., Otago Boys' High Schl., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; ent. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1900; oh. trigonometrical computer and dir., tidal survey some years; govt. astronomer and seismologist since 1911; Martin Kellogg fellow in astronomy, Univ. of California, 1915, and assoc. in astronomy, Yale Univ., 1916.
ADAMS, C. H.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. comsnr., Mar., 1917.

ADAMS, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1924), F.C.I.S. (Eng.) F.A.A. (Australia).—B. 1884; ed. Royal Orphanage, Wolverhampton; company sec., Sydney, N.S.W., 1908-1914; office of agt. gen. for N.S.W., London, 1915; liquidator of enemy firms, E.A. Prot., 1915; custodian of enemy property, occupied territory of German E. Africa, 1916 and Tanganyika territory, 1919; compr. of cust., and custodian of enemy property, Tanganyika, 18th Jan., 912; chmn., Tanganyika trade and infrn. local advisory committee.

ADAMS, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1875; ed. at Cranleigh Schl. and City of London Coll.; served articles, Quan. Survrs., London, 1891-1894; passed prelim. exam. Surv. Inst., 1895; Fellow of the Registd. Corp. Acctnts. (F.C.R.A.), 1915; tech. asst., accts. br., H.M. Office of Works, London, 1894-1895; acctnt. clk., civ.-engnr.-in-chf's. dept., Admiralty, in charge, corresp. br., ag. on various occasions as ch. survr., 1895-1900; transf. to Simon's Bay Dkdy. Extension, Cape Colony, as senr. acctnt. elk., in charge accts. and corresp., S.C.E.'s Office, 1900-1905; served as agt. in Simon's Pay Dkdy. Vol. Rifles (Boer War), 1901; recd. comen. in Cape Gar. Artillery, Sept., 1902; prom. captain, Mar., 1905; ch. acctnt., Engnr., India, 1906-1910; ast. sec., Engnr., England, 1910-1912; acctnt. and oshr., Gold Coast Harbrs., Accra and Secondee, 1912-1918; rly. acctnt., in charge rly. and pub. wks. agt., Anglo-French occupnt., Togoland, 1918-1919; ast. reo. gen., Gambia, 22nd May, 1919; senr. ast. reo. gen., 1st Jan., 1920; contrlr., food and fuel, July to Sept., 1920; J.P. and comm., ct. of requests, 14th Sept., 1920; ag. reo. gen., P.M.G., currency off., and chmn., navigation and pilotage bd., July to Oct., 1920, and July, 1921 to Jan., 1922; mem., exec. and leg. couns., July to Oct., 1920 and Sept., 1921, to Jan., 1922; asst. acctnt., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 9th Dec., 1922.
ADAMS, FREDERICK BRADFORD, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1883; ed. Dover Coll. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1908; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1914; res., 1924; staff grade, 1930.

ADAMS, G. H., A.S.A.A.—B. 1891; C.A. office, Nov., 1911; temp. acctnt., Nyasaland, July, 1915; cashier, 1915; asst. comsnr., income tax, 1921; senr. ast. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas. in 1921, 1925 and 1927; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1930.

ADAMS, THEODORE SAMUEL.—B. 1885; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; ag. supervisor of dist., Taiping, 1910-11; asst. dist. offr., Klang, 1911; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 1912; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1914; supdt., lands, Kelantan, 1918; ag. 1st ast. sec., 1919; ag. 1st ast. sec. to govt., 1920; oh. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, July, 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Sept., 1923; ag. regiar. titles, Perak, and chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Selama, Dec., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; dist. offr., Larut and regiar. titles, Perak, Nov., 1925; ag.-dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1927; offr., cls. Ia, chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1928; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1928; ag. advr., lands, Kedah, Apr., 1929.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed. Corstorphine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots Soc., 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Bd., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning inspr., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; examr. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; rep. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Conferees of Greater London, 1913-1914; prep. offl. rept. on housing and town planning investigts. in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first town planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; organised cheap cottage exhibns., Letchworth, 1906, and Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Govt. since 1914; hon. mem. Instn.of Mun. and Cnty. Engrs. (England); mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvements; pres. Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914, and mem. of coun., 1914-1919; mem. bd. of govs., American City Planning Inst., 1915-1918; chmn., Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.

ADAMS, WILFRID GEORGE, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. Shrewsbury and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda, Dec., 1910; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1919; dep. prov. comsnr., Oct., 1928; prov. comsnr., 2nd grade, Sept., 1929; 1st grade, Oct., 1930.
ADAMSON, ALFRED EDWARD.—B. 1871; ed. Godolphin schl.; survr., Kenya, 1911; ditto, Zanzibar, 1922.

ADAMSON, SIR JOHN ERNEST, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1867; ed. in London; Litt.D., M.A., London Univ.; principal, Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educ., Transvaal, 1905; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educ., Transvaal Prov.; vice-cham., Univ. of S. Africa; master, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Oct., 1925.

ADDERLEY, CHARLES J. H., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.C.E.I.—B. 1884; Chinese govt. rly., 1908-16; war serv., R.G.A., France, 1916-19; road survr., Nigeria, 1920; exec. engr., 1923; senr. exec. engr., 1926; ag. asst. dir., in 1927-30; D.P.W. in 1928-29.

ADDIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1901; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (hons.), 1923; asst. dist. comsrr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. asst. sec., clk. of couns., edr., offl. gazette, Mar., 1929 to June, 1930.

ADDISON, D'ARGY WENTWORTH, C.M.G. (1928), M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. Tasmanian pub. serv., 1st Dec., 1887; elk. of exec. coun., 1903; sec. to premier since 1902; ch. electoral offr., 1914; mag., 1918; admstr. of charitable funds, 1914-19; sec. for neglected children's dept., 1914-19; state organiser, visit of H.R.H. Prince of Wales, 1920 and the Duke and Duchess of York, 1927; under sec. for Tasmania, 1928; agt.-gen. in England for Tasmania, 1930.
ADELAIDE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ARTHUR NUTTER THOMAS, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1869; rector of Guisborough, Yorks., 1901-1906; bishop of Adelaide since 1906.

ADERS, WALTER MANSFIELD.—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; Marburg Univ., Prussia, Ph.D. (science honours) 1902; engaged on research work, Marine Biological Assoc., Plymouth, 1903; asst. to pathologist, Egyptian govt. hosp., Cairo, and curator of pathological museum, 1905; res. offr. of rinderpest serum station, Abassia, Cairo, 1906; priv. asst. and scientific sec. to dir. of quarantine bd., Alexandria, 1907; detailed to El Tor for research work re cause of dysentery amongst pilgrims; research work with Prof. Viguier Algiers (marine biology); research work, Welcome Hosp., Khartoum, 1909; London Sch. of Trop. Med., 1910; research work, Zanzibar, since 1911; recd. thanks of S. of S. for foreign affairs and the Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 2nd cls., for services rendered, 1912; asst. postal censor, 1914-1916; ch. censor, 1916-1918; lent to mil. authorities for work in Dar-es-Salaam and Kilwa, 1917; economic biologist, 1st June, 1917; at request of gov. of Tanganyika Territory proceeded to Dar-es-Salaam to advise on anti-malarial work, 1921; author of numerous papers and reports on scientific subjects.

AGAR, ARTHUR KIRWAN.—B. 1877; ed. at Brighton Coll., called to the bar, Grays Inn, May, 1919; temp. lieut. R.A.S.C., Oct., 1915; ag. capt. R.A.S.C., Aug., 1916; staff capt., W.O., Feb.-Dec., 1919; dist. mag., dep. judge, ct. of summary juris., coroner, regir. of sup. ct., escheator gen., provost marshal, regir. gen., regir. of titles and legal adviser to gov't. of Montserrat, 1st Oct., 1920; dist. comsrr., Belize, Br. Hond., Nov., 1923; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1923 to Apr., 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Oct., 1925.

AGASSIZ, EDWARD LEWIS.—B. 1879; office asst., Hong Kong, May, 1920; ag. crown solr. in addn., Aug., 1920; sec. to D.P.W., Jan., 1925; ag. off. recr., Nov., 1925; off. recr. and regir., trade marks and letters patent, Jan., 1927.

AHARNE, CHRISTOPHER DOMINIC.—B. 1886; ed. Christian Schls., Cork, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, scholar 1907; senr. moderator, B.A., 1909; cadet, S. Stirling, 1910; India, studying Tamil, 1911; asst. supt., Indian immigrants, Selangor, 1912; 1st asst. contrlr. of labr., Penang, 1913; dep. contrlr. of labr., Klang, 1914; dep. regir., sup. ct., Penang, 1916; dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1916; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, S'pore, 1917; asst. regir., impts. and exports (war dept.), 1918; offr., cl. iv., Jan., 1919; asst. adviser to gov't. of Johore, Batu Pahat, 1919-21; 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1923; dep. contr. lab., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1923; ag. dep. contr. lab., Penang, Dec., 1923 and July, 1925; ag. contr. lab., Malaya, in addn., Feb., 1926; ag. emigrn. commr. for Malaya, Madras, May, 1926; ag. dep. contr., lab., Penang, July, 1926; ag. contr. lab., Malaya in addn., Feb., 1926; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, Nov., 1926; dep. treas., S.S., 1929.

AINSILK, JAMES ROBERT, B.Sc. (Oxon.), Diploma of Forestry (Oxford), F.L.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. at Graeme Walker's (Aberdeen); Aberdeen Univ. and Oxford; probationary asst. cons., forests, Imp. Indian Forest Serv. (Oxford), Aug., 1907; asst. cons., forests, Ceylon, Nov., 1910; on active serv., 1914-17 (Egypt and France); attd. to W.O., timber supplies dept. (XI Forest Divnl. staff), Aug., 1917; dep. cons., forests, Ceylon, May, 1919; senr. cons., forests, Nigeria, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., forests, Nigeria, Nov., 1928; author of "The Physiography of Southern Nigeria and its effect on the Forest Flora of the Country" and "Timber Resources of Nigeria."

AITKEN, JOHN, B.A., LL.B. (1906).—B.1885; ed. Gigglewick Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1909; asst. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1913; station mag., Nigeria, 1915; crown counsel, Gold Coast, 3rd Nov., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Dec., 1920 to Apr., 1921, July to Sept. and Oct. to Nov., 1921 and June to July, 1922; ag. puisne judge, Sept. to Oct., 1921, and from July to Sept., 1922; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1926; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1929.
AITKEN, JOHN BANKS.—B. 1903; ed. Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1926; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1926.

AKBAR, MAAS THAJOON.—K.C., B.A., LL.B. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1880; ag. crown coun., Ceylon, Aug., 1907; crown coun., June, 1909; ag. 2nd addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, and as solr.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Oct., 1924; ag. puisne just., Nov. to Dec., 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1927; puisne judge, sup. ct. Apr., 1929.

ALABASTER, CHALONER GRENVILLE, O.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1922).—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1904; ag. atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1911, 1912, 1928, 1930; unoffl. mem., leg. coun., 1919, 1924, 1925; edr. of "Laws of Hong Kong 1844-1912."

ALBUQUERQUE, JOHN PEDROSO D', M.A., (Cantab.), F.I.C., F.C.S. (Lond.).—B. 1865; ed. City of London Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; schl. nat. science tripes, part I, 1st cls., 1887, nat. science tripes, part II, 1st cls. (chem.), 1889; govt. prof. chem. and agrl. science, Barbados, 1890; consulting chem., Impl. dept. of agr. for W. Indies, and chem. in charge, sugar cane experiments, 1900; ag. comsrr., Impl., dept. of agr. for W. Indies on several occasions between 1900-1909; ag. dir., agr., Barbados, 1st Apr., 1925; dir., science and agr., Barbados, 1st Sept., 1925.
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island comsrr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 4th grade comsrr., 1912; 3rd grade, Jan. 1918; 2nd grade, 1926.

ALBURY, Wilton Grant.—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-appd., 1909; head-master, boys' central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1915; ag. S. and C. mag., June to July and from July, 1925.

ALCOCK, William D. E.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward's schl. and Birmingham Univ.; B.A., 1913 (1st class, two distinctions); cadet, Fiji, and Western Pacific civ. serv., 1915-20; asst. polit. offr., 2nd grade, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. offr., 1927; seconded as lab. offr., 1929.

ALEXANDER, Cyril Wilson, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1879; ed. Shrewsbury and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1906; junr. asst. sec., 1908; ag. pol. mag., 1908; commr. lands, Lagos, 1908; 2nd cls. rep., 1919; ag. sec., N. Provs., 1924; ag. prin. asst. sec., N. Provs., 1925; staff grade, 1925; ag. lieut.-govr., N. Provs., 1927; lieut.-govr., S. Provs., 1929.
ALEXANDER, John Edward—Asst. land ranger, E.A.P., Oct., 1912; lands offr., Nyasaland, 21st July, 1922.

ALLAN, Kenneth Bruce, M.B., B.S. (Durham) Certif., London Schol. of Trop. Med. and Coll. of Med., Durham Univ.—Ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, May, 1911; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., 1916-17; transfd. as capt. on gen. list and attd. 1st K.A.R., Nyasaland Field Force, July, 1917 to Jan., 1919; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gambia, Mar., 1923; ag. senr. med. offr. on various occasions, 1923-26; senr. med. offr., Sept., 1926; J.P.; offr. mem., exec. and leg. couns.

ALLARD, Jules.—B. 1859; ed. Nicolet Coll., Quebec; lawyer; elec. to legis. assem., Quebec, 1897, 1900, 1904; apptd. to legis. coun. and min. of pub. wks., Quebec, 1905; min. of agric., 1907; min. of lands and forests, 1909-1917; re-elec. to legis. assem. at bye-election 1910 and g.e., 1912; re-called to legis. coun., 1916; prothonotary of the superior ct., Montreal, 1919.

ALLIDER, Wm. Howell.—B. 1873; 5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., O., 1904; ch. clk., do., 1908; ch. clk., audit, offr., 1913; acted on several occasions as clk. of the exec. coun. and comtee., as acctnt. in P.O., and as auditor-gen.; sec. to Barbados quarantine comm., 1903, to West Indian quarantine conf., 1904, and to confere. on trade relations between West Indies and Canada, 1910; sec., Barbados civ. serv. comm., 1920.

ALLEN, Frederick Harold, M.I.Mech.E., A.M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1890; engnr. asst., Port of London Authority, 1910-19; dist. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1919; dist. engnr., Ilorin, Nigeria, 1923; exec. engnr., 1924; native adminstr. engnr., 1925; mech. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1926.
ALLEN, H.—Tempy. asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Apl., 1919; asst. treas., Uganda, Aug., 1920.

ALLEN, H. T.—B. 1879; appld., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1896; conf. clk., Dec., 1907; also clk. to concessions and finance comtees., Sept., 1910 to June, 1916; promoted, under order-in-council of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th June, 1916; asst. sec. to trade relations comtee., Aug., 1917; sec., Dependencies of Falkland Islands comtee., Jan., 1918; sec., raw materials board, Dec., 1918; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Lord Milner), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

ALLEN, Hon. Sir James, G.C.M.G. (1926), K.C.B. (1917), M.A., Camb.—B. 1856; entd. New Zealand Parlim., 1887; lieut.-col. in command Otago Div., New Zealand Garrison Art., 1902; min. of defence, 1912; min. of finance, min. of stamp duties and min. of educn., 1912-15; min. of finance and min. of external affairs, 1919-20; high commr. in England, 1920-26; M.L.C., 1927.
ALLEN, Leonard Gilbert.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 1917; appld., after exam., asst. clk., Zvrd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

ALLEN, Lucien Arthur.—B. 1888; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schol. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Dec., 1913; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Feb., 1915; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1915 to Sept., 1918; passed cadet, June, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, June, 1920; Brit. res., Brunei, Mar., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Jan., 1924; ag. ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Mar., 1924; dep. pub. pros., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Dec., 1924; ag. asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1925 and Sept., 1926; ag. offl. assignee and pub. trustee, F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Sept., 1926; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Feb., 1928; ag. Br. adviser, Perlis, May, 1928; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929.

ALLEN, Percy Tothill.—B. 1878; ed. at Highgate Schol., Christ's Coll., Camb., (hons. class. tripes, 1899); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang Sept., 1906; ag. asst. dist. offr., Matang, June, 1906; ag. dist. offr. Christmas Is., Nov., 1907 and Nov., 1909; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese and supt. of pris-ons, Penang, Feb., 1909; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Apr., 1909; ag. mag., Seremban, Apr., 1912; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese and munic. commnr., Penang, Oct., 1913; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan. and May, 1917; ag. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1917; ag. insp. of prisons, S.S. in add., Jan., 1918; ag. rev. aud., Sel., N. S. and Pahang, April, 1919; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor and Negri Sembilan, June, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Sept., 1919; ag. state treas., Selangor and asst. treas., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. sec. for Chinese affairs, S. S. and temp. offl. mem., leg. coun., S. S., May, 1920; ag. regisr., titles, Perak and aspt., convict estab., Taiping, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, in add., May, 1923; ag. chmn., many, bd., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1923 and July, 1924; asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Nov., 1924; sec., res., Perak, Mar., 1925; regisr., companies and offl. assignee, S.S. in addn., 1925; attld., Chinese sect., July, 1926; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., F.M.S., 1926; sec., ditto, Feb., 1927.

ALLEN, Col. Stephen Shepherd, C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), with bar (1918).—Volunteer serv., Cambridge Univ., 1901-04, and in N.Z., 1911-15; left N.Z. with 6th reinforcement N.Z. Exped. Force, with rank of major, 1915; lieut.-col., Jan., 1917; in comd. 2nd Batt., Auckland Regt., Dec., 1916 to June, 1919; commdr., Auckland Infantry Brigade (T.F.), Feb., 1920 to May, 1925; col., Feb., 1920; in comd., Hauraki Regt., Sept., 1920 to April, 1922; res. of offr., Apr., 1928; admstr., W. Samoa, May, 1928; four times ment. in desps.; hon. A.D.C. to gov.-genl. of N.Z., Jan., 1925 to May, 1928.

ALLEYNE, James Douglas, M.D. (Tuft's Univ., U.S.A.)—Ag. pol. med. offr., dist. A., Barbados and visiting physician, leper asylum, 1917; ast. port health offr., 1919; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, 1919; port health offr., 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIES, E. J.—B. 1891; apptd. ast. clk., Scottish Educ. Dept., 1910; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd divn. of the civ. serv. to National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.
ALLISON, ALFRED ERNEST.—B. 1869; gov't. Christchurch Boy's High Schl.; ent. N.Z. gov't. serv., 1846; comsnr., gov't. life insurance dept., 1926.

ALLISON, WALTER EDWARD, A.M.Inst.T.—Mech. engr., Br. Honduras rly., Aug., 1913; supt. and traffic man., 1914-18; seconded to German E. Africa (Tanganyika Territory), as ast. traffic man., Jan., 1919; traffic supt., Apr., 1928.

ALLISON, WILLIAM FRANCIS DUDLEY, A.M.I.Mech.E., M.I.Loco.E.M.Inst.T.—B. 1882; ed. Glasgow High Schol. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; N.B.R. Co. and L. and S.W.R., 1906; inspgr. engr., crown agts., 1908; ast. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1911; seconded as loco. supt., mil. rlys., Togoland, 1915; ast. loco. supt., Uganda rly., 1917; dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ch. mech. engr., July, 1930.
ALLSOP, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1885; German E. Africa campaign, 1914-16; ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; 1st grade admstive offr., 1st Aug., 1922.

ALUWIIHARE, RICHARD.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Oct., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Jaffna kach., Oct., 1920; office ast. to dir. of agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1923; ditto, Panadure, Nov., 1924; 2nd landing survr., cust., 1926; comsnr., requests, Kandy, Nov., 1928; ast. attlit. offr., Oct., 1929.
ALVAPPILLAI, KOVINIDAPILLAI.—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1929; attd., Matara kach., Sept., 1929.

AMBLED, LLOYD EDWARD.—B. 1894; clk., postal dept., Fiji, 1st June, 1909; 4th cls. clk., lands dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R.C., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res. (comsnr.), Oct., 1917; Baluchistan, 1917-19; 3rd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; attd., lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd cls. clk. and sec., mining bil., Oct., 1919-Oct., 1920; ag. sub-inpr. constab. (during Indian strike), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; ag. ch. clk., Sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., on various occasions, 1926-29.

AMERASINGHE, SAMSON FELIX, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1926; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1926; office ast. govt. agt., E. Prov., Dec., 1927; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1928; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 1928; office ast. to govt. agt., Uva, June, 1930.

AMERY, Lieut.-Col., L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late Fellow of All Souls, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar of Imp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1899-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Imp. War Coun. at Versailles, and as ast. sec. to the War Cabinet and Imp. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner's absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919 to Mar., 1920; First Lord of the Admy., Oct., 1922; S. of S. for the Cols., 7th Nov., 1924; also S. of S. for Dominion affrs., 9th July, 1925 to June, 1929; capt. 14th 1st B'hm Batt., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 1917; M.P. (U) for S. Birmingham, May, 1911; ditto Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited "The Times History of the South African War"; author of "Problem of the Army," 1903; "Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade," 1906; "The Great Question" (with J. M. Robertson, M.P.), 1909; "Union and Strength," 1912.

AMYOT, Lt.-Col. JOHN A., C.M.G., M.D., D.A., D.M.S.—Sanitation, second Canadian divn. area, 1914; O.C., san. sec., 1st Canadian divn., France, ch. adviser, san., with Canadian army in France, 1916; twice ment. in desps.: dep. min., dept. of health, Canada, 1919.
ANDERSON, E. L. B.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; pvt. sec. to ag. gov', 1925.

ANDERSON, GEORGE BARTLET.—B. 1880; ed. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; clk., acct-genl.'s dept., G.P.O., London, 1896; clk., war office, Jan., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., G.P.O., Johannesburg, Dec., 1902; transf'd to exec. and legis. councils dept.; 2nd cls. clk., June, 1903; 1st cls. clk., July, 1905; acct.'s clk. and cashier, Transvaal agt-general's office, London, Oct., 1907; ast. acctnt., Apl., 1908; clk., P.W.D., Nyasaland, June, 1908; chief clk., secretariat, Apl., 1914; ag. ast. chief sec., Aug., 1916; on re-organisation of secretariat apptd. 2nd ast. sec., Aug., 1919; ag. 1st ast. sec., Aug., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1920; admin. offr., 1st grade, July, 1922; ag. 1st ast. sec. Mar. to Nov., 1922; ag. prov. comsnr. on two occasions, 1927-29; prov. comsnr., July, 1929.

ANDERSON, MAXWELL HENDRY.—C.B.E. (mil.) (1919); O.B.E. (1918); K.C., Aug., 1919; capt., R.N. (ret.); ent. Royal Navy, 1893; ret. at own request, 1912; recd. thanks of Admy. for series of magnetic observations carried out in the Pacific Ocean; inst., R.N. Coll., Portsmouth, 1908-10; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1909; younger brother of Trinity House, 1910; rejoined Navy on outbreak of war; promoted capt., Apl., 1919; govt. mem., central (unemployed) body for London, 1913 to 1919; atty-gen., Gibraltar, May, 1919; ag. chief just. and ool. sec. on several occasions; ch. just. Fiji and judl. comsnr., W. Pacific, 1929; ag. high comsnr., W. Pacific, June to Aug., 1930.

ANDERSON, ROBERT MOWBRAY.—Edn. govt. printing dept., St. Vincent, 6th Feb., 1886; chief govt. printer, July, 1894; steward, col. hosp., Aug., 1901; mem. of comte. to inquire into admtn. of poor relief, Aug., 1904; clk. to regier., sup. ct. and regier. of B., M. and D. for dist. I., Nov., 1904; temp'y. ast. landing waiter for spec. excise duties, Nov., 1904; edited first "Illustrated Handbook of St. Vincent," 1907.
ANDERSON, R. T. H.—Ast.-supt. of police, E.A.P., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, 1920.

ANDERSON, THOMAS EDWARD.—B. 1877; excise offr., B. Honduras, Dec., 1902; 2nd grade customs and excise offr., July, 1905; ag. 1st grade customs and excise offr., from April, 1904; confirmed March, 1906, rlyw. acctnt., 1st Aug., 1909; on completion of rlyw. construction reverted to substantive appt., 7th Sept., 1910; ag. chief clk., etc., treas. and customs dept., 20th May to 7th Sept., 1911.
ANDERSON, THOMAS JOHN, M.A., B.Sc. (Hons. Edin.) F.Z.S., F.S.E.—Entomologist, agric. dept., Kenya, May, 1908.
ANDERSON, W. A.—Prof. ast. surveys, Pietermaritzburg, Jan., 1919; ditto, Pretoria, Mar., 1929; insp. survr., Cape Town, Feb., 1930; survr.-gen., Bloemfontein, June, 1930.

ANDREW, ROBERT CHARLES.—Ed. Liverpool Inst., Harper-Adams Agric. Coll., Newport, and Liverpool Univ.; B.Sc.(agric.), London; National Diploma in agric.; mem. (by exam.) of Survr.'s Instit., lecturer under the East Sussex county council, 1905 to 1910; foreign experience, 1911; ast. supt. of agric., N. Nigeria, 4th April, 1912.
ANDREWES, LANGLLOT RUGGLES.—Ed. Fort Street, Sydney; war serv., 1916-18; admitted solr., sup. ct., London, 1924; ast. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1928; treas. solr. in addn., Mar., 1930.

ANDREWS, NORMAN FREDERICK STEWART.—B. 1902; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and Oxford; school, Brasenose Coll., 1919; Bridgeman Prize, 1923; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1924, B.A.; M.A., 1928; cadet, admininstr., Uganda, 1925; ast. dist. offr., 1927; temp. serving in C.O., 1927-28; sec., cotton enquiry comm., 1929.

ANGLIN, HON. FRANCIS ALEXANDER, P.C. (1925).—B. 1885; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Univ. of Ottawa; called to the bar, 1888; K.C. 1902; apptd. sen. puisne judge, exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1904; puisne judge, supreme ct. of Canada, 1909; ch. just., sup. ct., Canada, 1924; dep. of gov-gen.

ANGWIN, HON. WILLIAM CHARLES.—M.L.A., Western Australia (North-East Fremantle), 1904-27; min. for lands, immigr. and industries, 1924-27; mem., East Fremantle munie. coun., 1897-1928; agt.-gen. in England for W. Australia, Mar., 1927.

ANNECKE, D. H. SIEGFRIED.—B. 1895; ed. Grey Univ. Coll. Bloemfontein; B.A. (lit. and phil.), 1914; Univ. C.T., M.A. (physiology), 1918; Guy's Hosp., Lond., 1919-23, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1921; all house apps., Guy's, 1921; M.B., B.S., Univ. Durham, 1924; D.P.H. and B. Hy., Univ. Durham, 1926; research schol. and ast. dept. protozoology, Lond. Schol. Hyg. Trop. Med., 1925-28; D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), 1927; joint educ. grant, govt. S.A. and Health Section L. of N. for study of anti-malarial methods in Italy, 1927; senr. govt. pathologist, Union health dept., Durban, 1928.

ANNETTS, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1888; Ed. Magdalen Coll. schol., Oxford, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1910; ast. master, preparatory schol., Repton, 1910-1911; served in Oxfordshire educ. comtee's. office, and inspr. of evening schols., Oxfordshire county council, 1911-1912; apptd. junr. supt., educ. dept., Northern Nigeria, Sept., 1912; senr. supt., Jan., 1919; dep. dir., Jan., 1920; ag. dir., 1920 and various dates to 1928-29.

ANSDELL, GEORGE ARTHUR REES WETTENHALL.—B. 1894; ed. Liscard High Schol. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; war serv., Egypt, 1916; Turkey in Asia, 1919; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1921; passed lower Swahili, 1922; admin. offr., 2nd gde., 1924; passed law, 1925; ag. dist. offr., 1925; seconded as lab. offr., 1928.

ANTHONY, PHILIP ARNOLD, C.M.G. (1918), M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Schol.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Rly., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engr., F.M.S. Rly., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust. Apr., 1918; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ret., Dec., 1924; reported on Palestine rly., 1925; ag. ch. engr. (design), C.A., Nov., 1926 to Dec., 1926.

ANTHONY, WILLIAM THOMAS.—B. 1874; clk., mag's office, Kokstad, S. Africa, 1894; clk. cust., Port Elizabeth, 1894; eng. off., 1899; 1st cls. clk., Cape Town, 1903; prin. clk., Port Elizabeth, 1915; ch. clk., Durban, 1926; head office, Pretoria, 1926; collr., cust. and shipping mast., Port Elizabeth, 1930; Cape Town, 1930.

ANTROBUS, MAURICE E., B.A.—B. 1895; ed. Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; math. exhibr., Trinity Coll., Dec., 1913; 2nd lieut., 6th King's Royal Rifle Corps, Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Jan., 1917; served in Flanders, Salonica and France, 1915-19; 2nd cls. math. tripes, pt. I., 1919; 2nd cls. hist. tripes, pt. II., 1920; B.A., 1920; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Aug., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., 7th Feb., 1921; ast. priv. sec. to S. of S., 2nd Nov., 1925; seconded as pvt. sec. to govt. Ceylon, July, 1927; prin., D.C., 23rd July, 1930.

ANTROBUS, SIR REGINALD L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1898).—B. 1863; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical mods., 1874; 2nd clas in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; apptd., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., May 3rd, 1877; ast. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 5th Dec., 1880, to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, 16th Dec., 1882, to 24th June, 1886; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1886, to 6th Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; administd. the govt. of St. Helena, 5th Nov., 1889, to 8th June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, 1st Feb., 1892 and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., 17th Aug., 1892; sen. clk., Jan., 1894; princ. clk., 1896; ast. under-secr. of state, Oct., 1896; crown agent for the colo., 1909; retired, 1918.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. ast. col. sec., May to Sept., 1915; travelling commr., Newam, Oct., 1915, to May, 1916, and from Apr., 1917; ast. dist. commr., Kwahu, 1919; dep. prov. commr., 1929.

ARCHER, SIR GEOFFREY FRANCIS, K.O.M.G. (1920), C.M.G. (1913).—Ast. collr., East Africa Prot., Dee., 1902; attached secretariat, June, 1902 to Mar., 1903; dist. commr., Apr., 1907; spec. serv., Northern Frontier, East Africa, 1909; offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; awarded Cuthbert Peak Grant by the R.G.S., 1918, for surveys in E. Africa connecting Major Gwynn's Abyssinian triangulation with the triangulation of E. Africa; transf. to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; admstr. govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from June to Oct., 1913; dep. commr., Mar., 1913; commr. and commr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., May, 1914; assumed govt., May, 1914; present at operations against the Dervishes at Shimber Berria, Feb., 1915 (African General Service Medal and clasp); member of British Mission on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st class); gov., Somaliland Prot., Nov., 1919; directed operations against the Mullah, 1920, resulting in complete destruction of Dervish power in Somaliland; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Uganda Prot., Sept., 1922; gov., Sudan, 1924-26.

ARCHER, NORMAN E.—B. 1892; ed. R.N. Colln., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman in Mediterranean and Home Fleets, 1909-12; sub-lieut. (six 1st cl. certifs.), Sept., 1912; lieut., June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified, specialist course, ast torpedo lieut., 1915; served with Russian Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1915-17 (Order of St. Stanislas, 3rd clas.); passed exam. as Russian interpreter, 1918; intell. divn. admyn., H.M.S. Lowestoft in Adriatic, minelaying squadron, Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1917-20; apptd. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., min. of lab., Sept., 1920; transf. to C.O., Dec., 1921; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for Dom. affrs., Sept., 1927; seconded as sec. to Br. economic mission to Australia and visited Australia, N.Z. and Canada, 1928-29; pvt. sec. to parl. under sec. for Dom. affrs., June, 1929; ag. prin., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929.
ARCHIBALD, CHARLES GEORGE.—B. 1869; entd. Trinidad civ. ser., Jan., 1892; cashier, Trinidad govt. rly., 1908; acctnt., 1919.

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., B.S.A.—B. 1883; recd. pmrv. educn. at Yarmouth pub. schl. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., 1905; also grd. from the Nova Scotia hort. schl. in the same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. coll. in 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Scien. Agric. from the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. and expmtist. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1908; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll. in 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Agric. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal husbandman at the Cent. Expmtl. Farm, Ottawa; ag. dir. of Expmtl. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., June, 1919.
ARDRON, HAROLD GRAHAM.—B. 1892; mily. serv., 1916-21; educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1922; transf. polit. serv. as asst. dist. commr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1930.

ARMITAGE, CAPT. SIR CECIL HAMILTON, K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1911), D.S.O. (1901).—Aast. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1894; served in Ashanti expdt., 1895-6 (star); Northern Territories (Neutral Zone), 1897 (medal and clasp); inspr., 1898; priv. sec. to gov. of G. Coast and olk. of leg. and exec. couns., 1899-1900; Ashanti campaign, 1900 (ag. res. during siege of Coomassie, D.S.O., medal and clasp); trav. commr., 1901; commr. of S. Province of Ashanti, July, 1901; comdt. of Ashanti Mines volunteers with rank of major, Nov., 1905; ag. chief commr. of Ashanti, Oct., 1901, to end of year, Apr. to Oct., 1906, Dec., 1907, June 1908, and from June to Dec., 1909; chief commr. of Northern Territories, 28th Jan., 1910; gov., Gambi, 20th Nov., 1920; assumed govt., 3rd Jan., 1921.
ARMSTRONG, GEORGE JAMES.—B. 1901; elk., Basutoland, grade II., 1920; grade I., 1927; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1929.

ARMSTRONG, ROBERT.—B. 1879; ed. Friends' Schl., Great Ayton, Yorks and Durham Univ.; diploma in agric., Armstrong Coll., Newcastle-upon-Tyne, in Univ. of Durham; lecturer, schl. of agric., Egyptian govt. 1906-1910; asst. dir., agric., Zanzibar, Feb., 1913; ag. dir., agric., Oct., 1915 to Aug., 1916; ag. ch. supply offr., Sept. to Nov., 1918 and Mar., 1919; ag. adminstr.-gen., Nov., 1919 to Jan., 1920; ag. dir., agr., Mar., 1919 to Jan., 1920, and Nov., 1923 to May, 1924; ag. dir. of agr. and ch. supply offr., Feb. to Nov., 1922; ag. dir., agr. and offi. mem., leg. coun., Jan. to July, 1926.

ARNDT, CARL EVAN.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. Anuradhapura kach., Oct., 1920; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1921; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Aug., 1922; extra office asst., Kandy kachcheri, Nov., 1923; offr. asst., Puttalam kach., Jan., 1925; dep. rubber contr., Apr., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Sept., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1929; asst. stimt. offr., May, 1930.

ARNETT, EDWARD JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), B.A. (1898), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1876; ed. St. Paul's schl. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; C.A. office, 1902; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 1903; 3rd cl. res., 1906; 2nd cl. res., 1909; comnr. native rev., 1914; 1st cl. res., 1914; ag. lieut.-gov., N. Prova., Nigeria, 1920, 1921 and 1923; rev., Cameroons Prov., 1925; ag. lieut.-gov., S. Prova., 1929.

ARNOLD, 1ST BARON (creat. 1924), Rt. Hon. SYDNEY ARNOLD.—B. 1878; was partly priv. sec. to the pres. of the bd. of educn. and to the finan. sec. to the treasy., 1914; lib. M.P. for Holmfirth and later for Penistone, 1912-21; partly under sec. for the cols., Jan., 1924 to 7th Nov., 1924.

ARTHUR, JAMES STARTIN WILLS.—B. 1881; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1904); cadet, S. Sttlmtn., Jan., 1905; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Sept., 1907; ag. asst. treas., Singapore, June, 1909; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1910; attached to col. audit office, Feb., Mar., 1911; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, July, 1911; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Aug., 1911; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1912; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Mar., 1915; offr. cls. IV., May, 1916; seconded as asst. adviser, Kedah, May, 1916; ag. temp. as adviser in addn., Feb., Mar., 1917; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1919; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, May, 1922; dist. judge, Penang, Oct., 1922; dep. pres., mun. commr., Penang, May, 1923; dist. judge, Malacca, Jan., 1924; dep. pub. pros., Penang, Jan. 1926; asst. advr., Muar, July, 1927; ag. dist. judge, Penang, July, 1927; asst. adviser, Johore, 1930.

ARUMUGUM, JOSEPH NALLIAH, B.Sc.(Lond.).—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Dec., 1921; itg. pol. mag., W. Prova., May, 1924; pol. mag., Chilaw, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Tangalle, Jan., 1929.

ASHBURNER, R.—B. 1877; served vol. coy., King's Own Royal Lancaster Regt., 1900-01; elk., law dept., 1901; reor., Klerksdorp, 1904; ditto, Krugersdorp, 1910; prin. elk., Joh'burg, 1913; ditto, Pretoria, 1917; survr., inland rev., Pietermaritzburg, 1924; reor., rev., Pretoria, 1928; ch. rev. offr., inland rev. dept., Pretoria, 1930.

ASHTON, DANIEL ASPINALL.—B. 1869; ed. at Manchester gram. schl. and Owens Coll.; M.B., B.Ch., Victoria Univ.; civ. surg., S. African war, 1901-1902 (medal and three clasps); col. med. offr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1903; served in Cross River expdn., 1904 (medal and clasp); med. offr. at Abe and Obubura.
ASHTON, Hugh.—B. 1878; served in S. African War; sub-instr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1906; instr., 1914; served in European War with native labour contingent; asst. commr., Basutoland, 1923.

ASHWORTH, Henry Trewdale, M.M.—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., G.P.O., 1902; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt (Canal Defences) and France; passed supplementary olks exam., Jan., 1921; services loaned to C.O., 1st May, 1922; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to the C.O., 1st Sept., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1927.
ASKWELL, G. G.—B. 1895; ed. St. Bees Sch., Cumberland; on mily. serv., 1914-19; "1914" Star, ment. in desps.; dep. colliery man., Nigeria, 1924.

ASPINAL, Sir Algernon Edward, Kt. Bach., (1928), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; sec., West India Comte., since 1908 and of the Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr. since 1921; hon. treas. of W. Indian Club; mem. of comte., on Col. blue-books and blue-book repts., 1917; mem., W. Indian shipping comte., 1919; hon. sec., Trop. Agr. Coll. comte., 1920; hon. sec., W. Indian Contingent comte., 1915-21; mem., W. Indian currency comte., 1923.

ASSER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Joseph John, K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O. (1917), C.B. (1915), and many foreign decorations.—B. 1867; served with Nile expdn., 1897-9; in command of Kordofan expdn., Sudan, 1910; A.G., Egyptian Army, 1917-16; served in European War, 1914-19; base commdt., 1914-16; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen. offr. commdg. Br. troops, France and Flanders, 1919-20; gov. of Bermuda, 4th May, 1922; assumed govt., 9th Aug., 1922; ret., 1927.

ASTON, Arthur Vincent.—B. 1896; ed. King's Sch., Chester, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (open maths, scholar); R.F.A., 1915-18, M.C., 1917; apptd. cadet, M.C.S., 1919; ag. asst. to senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, June, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, Sept., 1920; ag. harbmr., Malacca, Mar., 1922; ag. collr., income tax, Malacca, Apr., 1922; ag. 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Dec., 1924.

ATHILL, Samuel Lauchland, Junr.—B. 1885; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry commr., Montserrat, 1911; ag. regir., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, 6th Oct., 1917; mag., dist. B., Antigua, 1st July, 1924; city commr., St. Johns, Antigua, Nov., 1925.

ATHLONE, First Earl of (created 1917), Alexander Cambridge, K.G. (1928), G.C.B. (1911), G.C.V.O. (1904), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabelo campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-col., 1919; G.S.O., lat grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig.-gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen., and commdr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923; Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem.
ATKINS, Francis.—B. 1874; Imp. post office, 1893-1923; post & tels. divnl. engr., Nigeria, 1923; asst. engr.-in-ch., 1928.

ATKINSON, Vivian Buchanan.—B. 1886; N. Staffs. Rly. Co., 1907; A.M.I.C.E., 1916; asst. engr., Uganda Rly., Jan., 1912; dist. engr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1920; asst. ch. engr., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harbrs., 1930.
ATTERBURY, H. W.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 14th Oct., 1921.
ATTERBURY, William Albert.—Brit. post office, 1910-19; warserv., 1916-19; sub-tel. engr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; tel. engr., 1926.

AUBER, Egerton Lancelot.—B. 1889; ed. Wesleyan High Sch., Freetown, Sierra Leone; supermny. clk., cust. (indoor), Gambia, Feb., 1908; 3rd clk., treasy., Nov., 1908; 2nd clk., Nov., 1911; re-graded 4th grade clk., 1915; 3rd grade clk., July, 1916; 2nd grade clk., Dec., 1918; ch. clk., treasy., Oct., 1920; seconded for duty as marine acctnt., Feb., 1927 to July, 1929.

AUCHINLECK, Gilbert Graham.—B.Sc. (McGill), F.C.S., B. 1884; ed. West Indies and McGill Univ., Canada; 1st cls. hons. in chemistry and botany, McGill Univ.; govt. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1902-04; McGill Univ., 1904-08; science and 2nd mast., St. Kitts-Nevis gram. schl., 1908-09; supt. of agric., Grenada, 1909-14; ast. dir. of agric. and chemist, Mauritius, 1914; ag. dir. of agric., and regir., C.C. societies, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, Jan. May, 1917; reported on Is. of Rodrigues to the Mauritius govt., 1919; Mauritius del. to Imp. Entom. Conf., in London, 1920; divnl. agric. offr., Ceylon, 1920; seconded to Seychelles to report on the possibilities of agric. development, 1921-22; dep. dir., agric., G. Coast, Aug., 1926; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to rept. on palm oil industry, Apr. 1926 to Jan., 1927; del. to W. African agric. confoe., Nigeria, Mar., 1927; author of papers on land slttmt. and various trop. agric. subjects; dir. agric., Dec., 1928.

AUCKLAND, Bishop of, Right Rev. Alfred Walter Averill, D.D.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. Th. sch.), 1887; M.A., 1891; Ely Coll., 1888; deacon, 1888; priest, 1889, Lon.; formerly curate of St. George's, Hanover Square, 1888-91; Holy Trinity, Dalston, Middlesex, 1891-94; vicar of St. Michael's, Christchurch, N. Zealand, 1894-1909; chaplain of ho.-p., Christchurch, 1895-1909; hon. can. of Christchurch Cathedral, 1902-1909; archdeacon of Akaroa, 1909; bishop of Waipu, 1910-1913; bishop of Auckland, 1913; primate and archbishop of N.Z., 1926.

AUDEN, Francis Thomas.—B. 1859; ed. Shrewsbury; M.D., Edinburgh Univ., 1903; M.B., 1891; civil surg., S. African war, 1900-1901 (medal and clasp); dist. commr. and govt. med. offr., Turks and Caicos Islands, Dec., 1914; med. offr., Jamaica, 1917; Kenya, 1919; Bahamas, 1924; Cayman Is., 1925; St. Vincent, 1926; Seychelles, 1927.
AUDETTE, Hon. Louis Arthur, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1856; apptd. regir. of exchequer et al. of Canada, 1887; asst. judge, exchequer et al., 1912; puisne judge, 1919.
AUSTIN, T. A., L.R.C.P. and S.I.M.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. and H. (Liverpool).—B. 1895; ed. Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Dublin; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), May, 1924.

AVELING, Harold Garrow, M.A.(Cantab.), L.C.P.—B. 1885; ed. Christ's Coll., Blackheath and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; els. I, grade I, admstive serv., 1927.

AVICE, TRISTAN, O.B.E. (1929).—Ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; matric, Lond. Univ., 1883; ent. civ. serv. Mauritius, 1893; 6th cls. clk., 1894; 5th ditto, Apr., 1899; 4th ditto, May, 1899; 2nd ditto, 1903; ch. clk., 1906; asst. regis.-gen., 1912; regis.-gen., rec. of registr. dues, regis. of companies, 1920; nom. mem., coun. of govt., May, 1926.

AYLESWORTH, HON. SIR ALLEN BRISTOL, K.C.M.G. (1911), K.C., B.A. (1874), M.A. (1875).—B. 1854; educ. at Newburgh high school and Toronto univ.; Prince of Wales prizeman; bencher of law soc. of Upper Canada; called to bar, Q.C. (Ontario); one of H.M.'s commsrs. for settlement of Alaska boundary, 1903; elected to the H. of C. for North York, 1904; postm.-gen. of Canada, 1905; min. of just., 1906; British ag. internat. fisheries arbitn. at the Hague, 1910; resigned portfolio and retired, 1911; mem., privy coun. of Canada; called to the Senate, 1923.

AYTON, A. L.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. July, 1899; clk. in charge of acct., Western Pacific high comm., Dec., 1913; ag. sec., W.P.H.C., 29th Mar., 1915; returned to C.O. as staff clk. in acct. dept., 29th Jan., 1920; ag. asst. prin., 16th Aug., 1920; sec. to "Discovery" comittee, 1925; prin., 1st June, 1929.

BABER, EDWARD ALFRED.—B. 1888; copyist, survey dept., B. Honduras, 1905; apprentice, ditto, 1906; passed exam. in theory of land surveying; 2nd cls. clk., Aug., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1911; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Nov., 1913; clk., roads and rivers bd., 1914; ag. clk. and clk. of coun., 1914-15; ag. inspr. of schls. and clk. to bd. of educa., Nov., 1915; senr. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Feb., 1917; ag. ch. clk., etc., for various periods, 1917-21; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1921; ag. priv. sec. to Sir Eyre Hutson, May-Aug., 1922; ag. clk., Apr., 1921; asst. col. sec., June, 1923.

BACKWELL, H. F.—B. 1884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and sen. scholarships), and at King's Coll., Camb., exhib.; asst. mast., Portsmouth gram. schl., 1907-8; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 19th Dec., 1908; asst. dist. offr., 1st Jan., 1914; dist. offr., 2nd cls., 29th Aug., 1917; res., 1925.

RADDELEY, SIR FRANK MORRISH, K.B.E. (1920), KT. R.A.C. (1928), C.M.G. (1925).—B.A., Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, 21st wrangler, Math. tripos, 1896; cadet F.M.S., 1897; passed final exam. in cantonment, Dec., 1900; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, May, 1901; ditto, Serendah, Nov., 1901; recd. thanks of govt. in connection with Ulu Selangor riots, Feb., 1902; revenue auditor, Pahang, Jan., 1904; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu Jan., 1905; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of chinese, Singapore, Aug., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Xmas Is., Oct., 1906; ag. offl. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1907; apptd. a currency commr., May, 1907; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1908; supt. govt. monopolies dept., S. Stiltns., Sept., 1909; P.M.G., S. Stiltns., Feb., 1916; food controller, Oct., 1920; under-secr., S. Stiltns., July, 1921; ag. o.l. sec., S. Stiltns., Apr.-Sept., 1922, May-Oct., 1923 and from Sept.-Nov., 1924; ch. sec., Nigeria, Nov., 1924; O.A.G., Nigeria, in 1925 and 1927; gov't. den. for various periods in 1925, 1926 and 1927; ret., 1930.

RAGENAL, CHARLES JAMES, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. St. Mark's Schl., Windsor, Camborne Schol. of Mines; served Impl. Yeomanry, Boer War, 1899-1901; Union of S. Africa dept. of mines and industries, 1901; served German S.W. Africa campaign, 1914-15; ment. in desps.; German E. Africa campaign, 1915-18; ment. in desps.; dist. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1917; senr. commsr., 1920; prov. commsr., 1926.

BAGSHAWE, ARTHUR WILLIAM GARRARD.—C.M.G. (1915), B.A., M.R., B.C., D.P.H., Camb., M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P., Lond.; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Caius Coll., Camb., served in resident hosp. appts., 1896-1899; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Oct., 1900; served as med. offr. in Lango expedit., 1901; served with Anglo-German bndry. commsr., 1902-4; employed in sleeping sickness extended investigations in Uganda, Apr., 1906, to May, 1907; director of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908.
BAILEY, JOHN F.—Dir. of botanic gardens, Adelaide, S. Australia, 1917; formerly dir. of Brisbane gardens.
BAIWARD, A. N.—Asst. dist. commsr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; junr. sec., native affrs. dept., 1926; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1927.

BAIN, NORMAN KERR, B.A. Cantab.—Cadet, S. Stiltns., Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1907; ag. asst. to res., Malacca, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., 31st Mar., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 29th Mar., 1914; ag. asst. to res. and harbmr., Malacca, Mar., 1918; ag. agent, food controller in addition, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Port Dickson, Negri-Sembilan, Jan., 1921; ag. 2nd mag., Kinta, July, 1921; mag., Ipoh, Aug., 1921; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Aug., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Apr., 1923; pvt. mag., Penang, Sept., 1924; ag. dist. judge, Penang and Singapore, Sept., 1924; offr. 2nd mag., Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. regis., sup. ct., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1925; offr. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1925; ag. contr. rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., ag. valr-gen., F.M.S. and town planning admtr., Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1928.

BAIVES, DENNIS LYNCH, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1881; ed. Wellington and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; asst. collr., Uganda prot., May, 1904; transf. to German E. Africa, 1st July, 1916; senr. commsr., 1st Apr., 1920; prov. commsr., junr. grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1927; prov. commsr., senr. grade, 1929.

BAKER, ALAN CUSTANCE.—B.A., Oxon.; cadet, S. Stiltns., Dec., 1908; attached to land office, Malacca, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. offr., A'or Gajah, 1912; ag. dep. regis., sup. ct., Singapore, Apr. and Sept., 1912; ag. dist. offr., B. Pulau, 1913; joined offr. cadet batt., July, 1916; demob., June, 1919; officiating asst. asp., monopolies, Penang, June, 1919; officiating asst. offl. assignee and dep. regis., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1919; ag. 2nd mag., Aug., 1919; capt., Penang volunteers, Nov., 1919; supernum. offr., cls. III, Oct., 1920; ag. asst. offl. assignee, Penang, Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1921; collr., land rev. and asst. treas., Malacca, Mar., 1923; commsr., trade and cust., Johore, June, 1927; ag. asst. adviser, Muar, July, 1927; commsr., trade and cust., Johore, continues to act as above, Dec. 1927.
BAKER, CHARLES MATTHEW, M.B.E.—B. 1878; ed. Felsted Schl., Essex; supt. of educa., Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

BAKER, ERNEST LAIDLAW.—B. 1880; ed. Australia; clk., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1901; shorthand writer, legis. coun., 1906; sec., Albert Park Bd. (jointly appt.), 1908; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. to legis. coun., 1909; clk., exec. coun. (joint appnt.), 1910; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., 1913; dist. comsnr., Colo East, 1914; ditto, Ra., 1917; ditto, Savu Savu, 1918; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; dist. comsnr., Lautoka, 1920; ditto, Macuata, 1923; ditto, Rewa, 1928.

BAKER, FRANCIS HORACE.—B. 1887; ed. Stratford-on-Avon Sch.; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; practised in England until 1913 and in Hong Kong, Jan., 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; comsnr., R. Artillery, 1915; staff capt., 1919; mily. mag., Nablus dist., Apr., 1919; mily. mag., Jerusalem dist., May, 1919; pres., Jerusalem dist. ct., Aug., 1919; also pres., Esersheba dist. ct.

BAKER, HERBERT ARTHUR, D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Univ. Coll. and London Dist. Training Coll., Univ. of London, 1906-8; B.Sc. hona.; B. of E. teacher's certif., 1918; M.Sc., London, 1913; D.Sc., London, 1920; dipl. of membership, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology (D.I.C.), 1920; R.F.C., France, 1916-16; warrant offr., R.N., 1916-19; govt. geologist, Falkland Is., 1920-22; headmaster, Grenada boys secondary sch., 1924.

BAKKER, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Sch.; junr. draughtsman, off. supdt. civ. eng., H.M. dykd., Rosyth, 1914-15; R.F.C., France, Mesopotamia, Persia, 1916-20; studied Scale Hayne Agr. Coll., 1920-4; agrl. offr., Zanzibar, Nov., 1925; del., Imp. Agr. Res. Conf., 1927.

BAKER, ROBERT, M. Inst. C.E., M.I. Struct. E., F.P.W.I.—B. 1874; F.M.S. Rlys., 1899; Athens water supply, 1906; Kowloon-Canton rly., Hong Kong, 1906; port engrnr., 1925; man. and ch. engrnr., Kowloon-Canton rly., 1927.

BAKER, MAJOR RUPERT HARRY WALPOLE, R. of O.—B. 1885; ed. Malvern Coll.; 2nd lieut. The Buffs, 1904; seconded as asst. comsnr., Gold Coast pol., 1914; served with the W.A.F.F., Togoland, 1914; The Buffs, B.E.F., France; major and 2nd in commd., 10th B.W.I., Regt., B.K.R., Italy, 1917; A.P.M., Italy, 1917-19; retnd. to Gold Coast, 1919; local commd., Cyprus mily. pol., Mar., 1927; ag. A.D.C. and pvt. sec. to gov., June-July, 1927; ag. comsnr., Sept., Oct., 1928 and March to Apr., 1929; hon. admnl. A.D.C. to gov., Nov., 1929; A.D.C. to ag. gov., Dec., 1929 to Jan., 1930.
BALDWIN, JOSEPH MABON, M.A., D.Sc.—B. 1878; ag. govt. astronomer, Victoria, 1915; govt. astronomer, 1st July, 1919.
BALDWIN, THOMAS HAYWARD, B.A.—B. 1896; ed., Stamford Sch. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; sup't., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 19th Oct., 1921.

BALDWIN, WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1882; 2nd div. clk., War Office, July, 1901; army audit off., Cape Town, 1904; army cashier, army acct., dept., O.F.S. and Cape Col., 1908; army cashier, Pretoria (attached to army pay dept.), for S. Africa, 1911; asst. auditor, col. audit dept., S. Nigeria, 2nd Aug., 1911; auditor, Seychelles, 12th Nov., 1912; ag. auditor, Nyasaland, Sept., 1914; attached for one month to audit off., Mauritius, 1915; resumed post of auditor, Seychelles, Mar., 1915; transf'd. to Brit. E. Africa for military service in connection with acct., Apr., 1916.

BALE, EDGAR GEORGE.—B. 1891; ed. King Edward's Sch.; imp. cust. and excise serv., 1912-21; asst. to comsnr. of cust., Kenya and Uganda, Mar., 1921; dep. comsnr., cust., April, 1923; ag. comsnr., cust., for various periods, 1924-29.
BALFE, R. S.—B. 1892; cler.-asst., Basutoland, 1912; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; war serv., 1915-19; inspr., 1927; dep. asst. comsnr., 1930.

BALFOUR, DOUGLAS HASTINGS.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 16th Jan., 1911; attld. to Jaffna Kach., Feb., 1911; ag. pol. mag., P. Pedro, May, 1911; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1911; attld. to Trincomalee Kach., May, 1912; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1913; asst. comsnr. excise, June, 1917; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1923; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Apr., 1925; dist. judge, Negombo, June, 1925; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, July, 1927; ass. judge, Anuradhapura, Oct., 1929.
BALFOUR, STEPHEN FRANCIS.—B., 1905; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1930.
BALMER, A. B.—Asst. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1918.
BALMER, F. E.—Dep. chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ch. acctnt., G.P.O., Kenya, 1st Oct., 1919.

RAMFORD, LIEUT.-COLONEL H.W.M., O.B.E. (1918); M.C. (1916).—B. 1882; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1900-1906; S. African War, 1900-1902; Transvaal Mounted Rifles, Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Natal Police, 1907-1912; dep. sheriff, N. Zululand, 1913-1914; adjkt., Natal Light Horse, S.A. Rebellion and G.S.W., Africa, 1914-1915; adjt. 2nd S. African Infantry, Senussi Campaign, W.F.F. Egypt (wounded Hazalin Jan. 23rd, 1916); to France with S. African bde., 9th Scottish Div. (wounded Delville Wood, July 15th, 1916); bde.-major, 90th infy. bde., France, 1917; comdg. 2nd S. African infv., 1917-1918 (very severely wounded Beaurevoir, Oct. 8th, 1918); service in Fr nce includes Somme, Arras, Messines, Ypres, defense of Hazebrouk, Le Cateau; three times mentioned in despatches: O.B.E., M.C., Croix de Guerre avec palme; com.-nr. of police and inspr. of prisons, Malta, 29th July, 1919; commdt., constab., N. Territories, G. Coast, 28th July, 1922; inspr.-gen., pol. G. Coast, 27th Aug., 1924.
BANGROFT, EDWARD NATHANIEL, M.C., M.Am.Soc.'E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; lieut., R.E., 1916; France, 1917-18; survey engrnr., Nigerian rlys., July, 1927; survr., gen., Jamaica, May, 1929.

BANCROFT, OSWALD LAWRENCE.—B. 1888; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1901; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; admitted bar, Barbados, 1910; barister-at-law and solr., sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1911-24; pol. mag., dist. A., Barbados, 1925; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; crown. coun., N. Rhodesia, 1928.

BANNERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD WOOLHOUSE, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1884; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; admitted to Gold Coast bar as solr. and advoc., Jan., 1914; practised before sup. ct., G. Coast up to 31st July, 1919; pol. mag., sup. ct. 1st Aug., 1919; senr. pol. mag. since 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1928-1930.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1824), RIGHT REV. ALFRED PARKHAN BERKELEY.—Foundation scholar, Codrington Coll., Durham; B.A., 1883; M.A., 1908; D.D., 1917; consecrated Lord Bishop of Barbados, 1917; vicar of All Saint's, Antigua, 1886-88; vicar of the Holy Innocent's, Barbados, 1890-1900; rector of St. Philip, 1901-06; dean and rector of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1906-17.
BARGERY, REV. GEORGE PERCY.—B. 1876; nr. supt., educ. dept., N. Nigeria, 26th Aug., 1913.
BARKER, H. P., D.S.C.—B. 1883; master mariner, 1900; asst. marine supt., mercantile marine; ch. instr., Dublin Sch. of Navig. and Seamanship, 1918; port off., Tanganyika, Oct., 1919.

BARLAS, WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. Pitlochry High Sch. and Thomson's Acad., Dundee; travelling teacher, Falkland Is., Feb., 1908; ag. govt. schoolmr., June, 1913; asst. teacher, govt. schl., July, 1914; mag., &c., S. Shetlands, Nov., 1914; junr. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1915; mag., &c., New Is., Mar., 1916; mag., &c., S. Georgia, Apl., 1918; dep. mag., S. Georgia, Jan., 1920; ag. mag. S. Georgia, on various occasions, 1921-27; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of coun., May, 1922-Feb., 1923; ag. col. postmr. and local audr., June, 1926-Feb., 1927; mag., S. Georgia, Sept., 1928.

BARLEY, JACK CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; 2nd cls. hon.; cadet, W. Pacific civ. ser., Dec., 1911; attd., res. comsnr.'s off., Apl., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, Nov., 1912; ag. treas. and collr. cust., Apl., 1913; passed cadet, Apl., 1914; dep. comsnr., July, 1914; dist. offr., Gizo, 1915; ag. res. comsnr., Apl., 1917; dep. comsnr., Fiji, Mar., 1924; ag. res. comsnr., Jan., 1928; judl. comsnr. (temp. and provisional), Feb., 1928; 1st dist. offr., Apl., 1928; ag. res. comsnr., Mar., 1929.

BARLOW, CAPT. JOHN WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1895; capt. S.R.O. three yrs.; cadetship I.O.M. constab., 1911-14; asst. provost-marshal, Constantinople, 1919-20; 1914-15 Star, British War and Victory meda.; served France, India, Egypt, Macedonia, Turkey and S. Russia; asst. staff instr., pol., Gold Coast, Oct., 1921; title changed to asst. comsnr., pol., Oct., 1924; comsnr., pol., Oct., 1927.

BARNARD, SIR FRANK STILLMAN, K.C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1856; entd. ser. of B. Columbia Express Co in 1879; subsequently became gen. man. and pres. of the company; promoter, and dir. for many years of the B. Columbia Electric Rly.; identified with many other important interests; mem. of H. of C., Canada, 1879-1896; alderman of Victoria, B.C., 1886 and 1887; mayor, 1895; ret. from active business life in 1906; lieut.-govr. of B. Columbia, 1914-1919.

BARNES, ARTHUR CHAPMAN, B.Sc. (Hons.), F.I.C., A.M.I.Ch.E.—B. 1891; ed. Deacon's Schol., Peterborough, Munic. Coll. of Tech. and Victoria Univ., Manchester (schol.); computer, survey dept., E.A.P., Oct., 1914; asst. analyst, Apr., 1916; chem. offr., Kenya, 1919; ag. dir. chem. research, May to Aug., 1920; agrl. chem., Nigeria, Apr., 1923; asst. dir., agr., Zanzibar, June, 1927; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Sept., 1928; prov. offl. mem., leg. coun.; dir., agr., Fiji, July, 1929; offl. mem., leg. coun.; chmn., coconut comte.; chmn., livestock record asso.; dir. agr., 1930.

BARNETT, ERNEST ARTHUR.—B. 1884; clk. and pay and qrtmr., 1900; sub-impr., Fiji constab., 1906; attached to R.I.C. for training, 1908; impr., Fiji constab., 1911; higher grade, 1914; asst. adjutant to local forces, 1914; capt., Fiji Def. Forces, 1914; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, June to Dec., 1916; J.P. for the col., 1916; press censor, 1917; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, 1917 and Sept., 1925; chief inspr., 1918; ag. inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1918; dist. comsnr., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr., and prov. comsnr., Ba., May, 1921-June, 1922; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., Jan., 1922; prov. mem., leg. coun., Nov., 1925.

BARNHILL, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A. Hons. (Cantab.).—B. 1903; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1926; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1930.

BARRATT-LENNARD, SIR FIENNES CECIL ARTHUR, KT. BACH. (1926)—Served in Anglo-Boer War, but invalided through wounds recd. in action, 1899-1900; judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, and ex officio of sup. ct., Nigeria, Sept., 1913; served with local milly. forces and as judge, ct. of appeal for eastern Africa and the high ct. of Uganda, Feb., 1915 to Nov., 1916; judge, sup. cts., S. Stlitms. and F.M.S., Mar., 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Johore, June, 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Kedah, Dec., 1921; ch. just. and keeper of records, Jamaica, Nov., 1925.

BARRON, JACOB MAURICE.—B. 1891; ed.; High Schl., Dublin, 1890-1910; sen. modr., nat. sci., 1914, T.C.D.; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; labr. dept., 1915-17; passed in Tamil, 1917; dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, 1917; labr. dept., 1917; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1917; supervisor of cust., Port Swettenham, 1917-20; labr. dept., Apl., 1921; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Oct., 1922; dep. contr., rubber expts., Jan., 1924; town planning admstr. and valuer-gen., Kuala Lumpur Jan., 1926 and in Mar., 1927 (in addn.); ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1926; ag. mag., ditto, May, 1927; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Feb., 1928; ag. valr.-gen., F.M.S. and town planning admstr., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1928; ag. contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., June, 1928; ag. dep. contr. lab., F.M.S., Aug., 1928.

BARRON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS.—B. 1887; ed. Aberdeen Univ., M.A., 1909, and Bellbird Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, July, 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Doc., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kroh, Perak, Apr., 1913; passed cadet, Doc., 1913; offr., cls. V., Doc., 1914; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Selangor, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1920; dist. offr., Tampin, Nov., 1920; dist. offr., Temerloh, Pahang, Jan., 1921; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Nov., 1922; offr., cls. III. Nov., 1923; asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, Jan., 1925, offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Dec., 1928; collr., land rev., Seremban and regnr., titles, Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1929; state valuer and town-planning admstr. in addn.

BARROW, JOHN.—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge (B.A., 1925, M.A., 1929) abroad and Univ. Coll., Lond.; cadet, Hong Kong, Jan., 1927; 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., Mar., 1929; 4th asst. col. sec., June, 1929; pvtc. sec. to gov., Nov., 1929 and to O.A.G., Feb., 1930; 2nd asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., June, 1930.
BARRY, CHARLES EDWARD, K.C.—B. 1878; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; admitted Inner Temple, 1905; admitted advoc., Pretoria, 1906; judge, sup. ct., 1926.

BARRY, EDWARD.—B. 1896; ed. Mount St. Mary's; served in Army, Apr., 1915-Oct., 1927; expdy. forces, European War, Aug., 1915-Nov., 1918; 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1922 to Oct., 1927; commr., 3rd gde., Somaliland, Mar., 1928.

BARRY, HON. JEREMIAH HAYES, K.C.—B. 1858; ed. at public schls. of Saint Mary's and Fredericton; mom. of Senate of Univ. of New Brunswick; admitted to the bar, 1882; K.C., 1898; judge, sup. ct., New Brunswick, 1909; King's Bench division, 1913; ch. just., King's Bench divn., 1924.

BARRY, RICHARD JOHN.—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk., C.S.O., Cape, 1895; C.S. Law, 1899; clk., mag. dept., 1901; ast. mag., various places; relief staff, 1904; ch. clk., master's office, Cape (insolv. brch.), 1906; Natal, 1912; Transvaal, 1912; master, sup. ct., Natal, 1915; ditto, Cape, 1923.

BARTH, SIR JACOB WILLIAM, KT. BACH.—(1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1895; M.A., 1898; barrister-at-law, Mid. Tem., 1900; registr. and prin. registr. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1902; Crown advoc., Oct., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comnr., Oct., 1904; judge, high ct., E. Africa Prot., April, 1906; atty.-gen., E.A.P., Feb., 1914; ag. chf. sec., Aug., 1918; ch. just., Kenya, Aug., 1920.

BARTLETT, HUMPHREY EDWARD GIBSON.—B. 1880; ed. Winchester and Trin. Coll., Oxford, 2nd cls. class. mods., 2nd cls. final honour, schl. of jurisprudence; B.A., 1903; M.A., 1906; solr., 2nd cls. final honours, 1906; ast. dist. comnr., G. Coast, 1909; dist. comnr., 1913; ag. prov. comnr., Dec., 1915 to June, 1916; and from June, 1919; ag. comnr., Western Prov., Jan. to Feb., 1920; dep. prov. comnr., 1921; ag. comnr., C. Prov., June to Dec., 1921, and from Sept., 1922 to Jan., 1923; ag. comnr., W. Prov., Feb., 1923; prov. comnr., May, 1923; ag. comnr., lands, Dec., 1929; comnr., lands, Jan., 1930; offl. mem., leg. coun., July, 1930.

BARTLEY, THOMAS DOUGLAS MURRAY.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1925; war serv., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1920; adminstr. offr. (cadet) Tanganyika Territory, May 1920; ast. sec., May, 1922; ag. senr. ast. sec., Sep. to Nov., 1923; ast. admstr. gen. and ast. registr. of companies, June, 1924 and Nov., 1925 to Jan., 1926; res. mag., Oct., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., ag. registr.-gen., births, deaths and marriages, ag. registr., companies, ag. liquidator German savings bank, ag. registr., trade marks, Jan. to Aug., 1926.

BARTLEY, WM., M.B.E. (1919).—B.A., Dublin; cadet, S. Stiltns., Dec., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, Nov., 1909; ag. astt. to atty.-gen., S. Stiltns., Apr., 1910; passed in Malay, Jan., 1910; passed in law, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., 1st Jan., 1912; ag. astt. dist. judge and 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1912; ag. inspr. of prisons, Jan., 1912; passed in Javanese, Feb., 1912; dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1912; ag. astt. supt., Netherlands Indian immigrts., F.M.S. and S. Stiltns., June, 1912; ag. astt. contr. 'ab., F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. astt. to atty.-gen., Singapore, Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1915; astt. regar., impts. and expts., S. Settns.; sec., food control comtee., June, 1917; mem., shipping comtee., Apr., 1918; sec., food contrlr.'s comtee., Dec., 1918; agent, food controller, Singapore, June, 1919; services recog. by sec. of state, Aug., 1919; sec., profiteering comnr., 1920; ag. collr.-gen., income tax, Sept., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1920; sec. to advising comtee. on allowances, in addition, Dec., 1920; seconded for serv. under Kelantan Govt. as astt. adviser, Apr., 1922; ag. Br. adviser Kelantan in addn., 1922 and 1923; ag. 1st astt. sec. (B), S.S., and clk. of couns., Jan., 1924; ag. under sec., S.S. Mar., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Apr., 1926; sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1926; ag. comnr., lands, S.S., Sept., 1926; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1927; under-sec. to govt., S.S., 1930.

BARTOLO, PROF. SIR AUGUSTO, KT. BACH.—(1929), LL.D., B.LIT., F.R.H.S.—B. 1883; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; B.Lit., 1904; called to bar, 1907; edr. for many years of "Daily Malta Chronicle"; prof., hist. of legislation and examr. in law and lit., Malta Univ.; chmn., Malta branch, Empire Parly. Assoc.; Malta Univ. del. at congress of Univs. of Empire in Gt. Britain, 1921; el. for 2nd divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for educn. and migrn., Aug., 1927; author of several work on Maltese hist. and Imp. affrs.
BARTON, A.E.V.—Imp. cust. serv., 1912; cust. astt., Kenya, Sept., 1919; collr. of cust., Apr., 1923; comptr., cust., B. Guiana; collr., gen., Jamaica, 1927; collr., cust., Trinidad, June, 1929.

BARTON, C. J. T.—B. 1891; ed. Denstone and Downing Coll., Cambridge; history and law tripeses; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1920; astt. dist. comnr., E.A.P., Aux., 1914; ag. dist. comnr., 1917, 1919, 1921; astt. for native affrs., 1925; ag. senr. astt. sec., 1924-26; ag. astt. col. sec., 1927; ag. prin. astt. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., in 1928 and 1929; author various papers on Kenya tribes and local history.

BARTON, CECIL MOLYNEUX, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and Trinity Coll., Dublin (hons., classics, law); barrister-at-law, King's Inns, Dublin, 1906; ast. dist. comnr., E.A.P., 1913; res. mag., 1914-21; ag. judge, sup. et., Apr., 1921 to Oct., 1922; legal adviser, Gambia, Dec., 1922; comnr. to enquire into double inoculation of cattle, 1918; chmn. of comnr. to enquire into inoculation of cattle for rinderpest by vety. dept., 1919; ag. judge, sup. et., on various occasions, 1923-28; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; circuit judge, Gold Coast, 1929.

BARTON, MAJOR P. F., V.D.—Ast. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; astt. col. sec.'s office, Oct., Nov., 1907 and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. astt. treas., Nov., 1908; ag. chf. astt. treas., Apr., July, 1914 and June 1916; chf. astt. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offr., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920 to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in desps., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offr., Sierra Leone, 6th Aug., 1922.

BARWELL, HON. SIR HERBERT NEWMAN, K.C.M.G. (1922), LL.B. (Adelaide Univ.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide; mem., H. of A., S. Australia, Stanley dist., 1915-25; astt.-gen., 1917 and 1918-20; premier and astt.-gen., 1920-24; fedl. senator, 1925; astt. gen. for S. Australia, 1928.

BASSETT, RALPH HENRY.—B. 1896; on mily.-serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; astt. to Ratnapura Kach., Jan., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, May, 1922; astt. sttlnt. offr., July, 1926; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1930.
BATEMAN, G. S., L.D.S., R.S.O., Eng.—Serv. with Forces in E. Africa 1915 and 1916; dental surg., Uganda, May, 1912.

BATEMAN, WALTER SLADE, O.B.E. (1920).—Served in convict and prisons branch, Cape Colony, 1894-1901; sec. bd. of management, Somerset house, Cape Town, 1897-1901; clk., prisons dept., Transvaal, Jan., 1901; chief clk., July, 1901; ag. inspr. of prisons, June, 1903; astt. dir. of prisons, July, 1905; ag. dir., Nov., 1906; mem. of tender bd. at various times during 1902-1906; ag. gov. of central prison and local gaol, Pretoria, June, 1907; gov. of Johannesburg gaol... and Diepkloof prison, Dec., 1907; ag. ast. dir. of prisons, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1911; dir. and under-sec. for justice, 1918; chmn., Union tender bd., Sept., 1928; contr., supplies, Nov., 1929.

BATES, ISAAC GRAINGER.—B. 1886; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1909; equity bar, 1909-13; ch. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is., Prot., with jurisdiction as a judl. commr. for the Western Pacific, Nov., 1913; ag. res. commr. in admn. during 1919; seconded for serv. as ch. pol. mag., Fiji, May, 1920 to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, high ct., on various occasions 1926-27; ag. solr.-gen., July, 1927.

BATHO, CHARLES PHILIP ARTHUR, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Victoria Coll., Jersey; control and audit office, Cape Colony, 1900; sec., tender bd., 1901; transfd. Transvaal prisons dept., July, 1902; prin. clk., July, 1910; prin. clk., Union prisons dept., Nov., 1912; ch. clk., Nov., 1917; ast. dir. prisons, Apr., 1926; is also commr. of oaths for Union.

BATHURST, WALTER DUNDAS.—B. 1859; ed. Lancing Coll.; agt. of Congo Free State, 1883-6; apptd. to col. audit branch exchequer and audit dep., Jan., 1889; local audr., Gibraltar, under C. and A. G., Jan., 1891; ch. examr., E. and A. dept., col. audit branch, Dec., 1906.

BATTERBEE, SIR HARRY F., K.C.M.G. (1931), K.C.V.O. (1927), C.M.G. (1918), C.V.O. (1919). B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Sch., Faversham, and at Oxford; classical scholar, Hertford Coll., 1899-1904; 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 1st cls. math. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1905; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., May, 1905; ag. 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; 1st cls. clk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. African lands comm., 1912; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Inlington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st May, 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919; priv. 1st Apr. 1920; ag. ast. sec., 1st Mar., 1923; polit. sec. to Adml. commdg. sp. serv. squadron during Empire cruise, 1923-24; ast. sec., 12th Dec., 1924; ast. under sec. of state for dom. affrs. 25th Jan., 1930.

BATTERSHILL, WILLIAM DENIS.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914-19; cadet Ceylon, Apr., 1920; addl. to Colombo cust., May, 1920; astd. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1921; office astd., Badulla kach., Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1922; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1923; addnl. ast. col. sec., Jan., 1923; 4th ast. col. sec., Mar., 1925; 3rd ast. col. sec., May, 1927; 2nd ast. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun. May, 1928; ast. col. sec., Jamaica, 1928; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1929.

BATTISCOMBE, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1890; ed. King's Sch., Canterbury, and Haileybury Coll.; schol., Lincoln Coll., Oxford; B.A., (hons.) 1914; ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1914; served with R. Berks. Regt. and Grenadier Guards during European War; appt. in col. serv. cancelled on acct. of wounds, 1919; re-ent. col. serv. as headmst., Machakos indus. schl. and inspr. of schls., Ukamba prov., E. Africa, 1921; tutor to Prince Seyid Abdulla, son of the sultan of Zanzibar, 1922; sec. to sultan, 1923; admstve. offr., Zanzibar, 1923; Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 4th cls., 1928; astd. as pvtc. sec. to suite of Sultan on state visit to England, 1929; clk., couns., June, 1930; edr., off. gaz., July, 1930.

BAVERSTOCK, WILLIAM CHARLES BEST, B. of O., K.R.R.C.—Ed. Wimborne Grammar Sch., and Milton Abbas Grammar Sch., Blandford; overseer, Pacifco Phosphate Co., (Br. Phosphate Com.), 1905; served, Great War, Queen's Own Dorset Yeomanry, Dorset Regt. and K.R.R.C., Aug., 1914 - July, 1919; min. of munitions, 1919; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Jan., 1920; dep. commr., W. P. high comm., 1920.

BAVIN, HON. THOMAS RAINSFORD, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Auckland, N.Z. and at Sydney Univ.; called to bar, 1897; prof. of law, Univ. of Tasmania, 1900; sec. to prime min., C. of A., 1901-4; M.I.A., N.S.W., from 1917; premier and col. treasurer, N.S.W., 1927.

BAXTER, WILLIAM.—Natal govt. serv., Jan., 1894; ch. clk., atty-gen., Natal, July, 1908; 2nd civ. mag., Durban, Apr., 1914; mag., Ingwavuma, Dec., 1916; crim. mag., Durban, Mar., 1920; senr. professional ast. atty.-gen., Natal Prov., Aug., 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Natal, on several occasions; solr.-gen., Eastern dists. Cape Prov., Jan., 1927.

BAXTER, JOHN BABINGTON MACAULAY, K.C., D.C.L.—Elected to New Brunswick Legislature, 1911; atty.-gen., 1914; leader of provincial opposition, 1920; min. of cust. in Dom. govt., 1921; prime min., New Brunswick, 1925.
