(R.E. and R.A.F.), 8th Oct., 1915 to 27th Feb., 1919; tempy. employment clk., Miny. of Labour, 1st Oct., 1919 to 20th June, 1920; permanent ditto, 21st June, 1920 to 30th Sept., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Oct., 1922.

HARRIS, HENRY GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. Brentwood Grammar Schl.; L.N.W. Rly.; minor appts., Gold Coast and Nigeria, 1901-1906 and 1909-1913; asst. loco. supt., Gold Coast, 1913; dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika, 1920.

HARRIS, HENRY GLADSTONE BOLTON.—B. 1886; ed. Exeter Schl. and Cambridge Schls.; mines; inspr. mines, F.M.S., 1910; ag. ast. warden, mines, Perak, 1914; ag. warden mines, Selangor, 1920; ag. warden mines, Perak, 1923; warden mines, Negri Sembilan, 1927; warden, mines, Selangor, 1929.

HARRIS, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1884; entd. serv. of Imperial Post Office, 1st Jan., 1900; transfd. to dept. of posts and telegraphs, Cape of Good Hope, 13th Nov., 1902; resig., Dec., 1907, and entd. S. Rhodesia telegraph serv., 1st Jan., 1908; seconded for serv. with African Trans-Continental Telegraph Co., 12th Apr., 1908; resig., 31st Jan., 1910; provincial postmaster, S. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; ast. supt. of posts and telegraphs, Fed. Malay States, 14th June, 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., Indian army reg. of offrs., unattached, Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., 31st Punjab, July, 1918; lieut., Mar., 1919; released from army serv., Oct., 1919; resumed duty, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1920; relinquished commn., I.A.R.O., Sept., 1920; contr., posts and tels., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; astd. off. of sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1927.
HARRIS, PHILIP CAPE, F.R.I.B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Parkfield Schl., Liverpool, Liverpool Univ. and the Royal Acad. Schls. of Art; served with H.M. forces 1914-19; architect, Zanzibar, Jan., 1922.

HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Mary's Schl., Basingstoke; clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland Prot., 18th June, 1913; editor, govt. gaz., since 1914; clk., 1st grade, Oct., 1920; ag. ch. clk., secretariat, Nov. 1920 to June, 1921; ast. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. senr. ast. treas., 1930.

HARRIS, HON. ROBERT EDWARD, D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. High School, Annapolis Royal; pri. tuition; D.C.L., King's Coll. Univ., Windsor, N.S.; LL.D., Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, N.S.; called to the N.S. bar, 1882; K.C., 1889; practised at Yarmouth, 1882-92; Halifax, 1892-1915; justice, sup. ct., N.S., 1915; ch. just.,

HARRIS, WILLIAM VICTOR, B.Sc. (Agr.), A.I.C.T.A., F.E.S.—B.1904; ed. Secondary Schol., Stockton-on-Tees; B.Sc., Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1925; Cambridge, 1925-26; assoc., Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1927; asst. entomologist, dept. of agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

HARRISON, ALBERT SEDDON, M.Sc. Manchester, 1908.—B. 1887; on staff of Manchester Univ., and asst. mast., Fielden demonstr. schol., 1908-10; senr. mast., Halesowen commn. schol., 1910-12; on staff Manchester Univ. educ. dept., and headmtr., Fielden demonstr. schol., 1912-14; inspr. of schls., Ceylon, Apr., 1914; on active serv., 1918-19; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Ceylon, May, 1919, to Apr., 1920; ag. dir. of educn., Apr. to Oct., 1920; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Oct., 1920; prin., training coll., Jan., 1921.

HARRISON, ARTHUR ERNEST.—B. 1884; London Schl. of Law, 1911-12; clk., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1913; ag. regiar., high ct., sheriff, offl. recor., regiar., deeds, etc., July, 1915; custodian, enemy property, 1917; regiar., etc., 1918; contr., clearing office, peace treaty, 1920; regiar. companies and patents, 1921; admstr.-gen., June, 1925.

HARRISON, ERNEST, M.S.A. (Ivwa), B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A. (Hons.).—Lecturer in agr., schol. of agr., Groooffontein, Cape, 1904; prin., schol. of agr., Cedara, Natal, 1913; dep. dir., agr., Kenya, 1921; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Nov., 1922 and Mar.-Oct., 1924; dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

HARRISON, CAPT. HORACE DUNFORD.—B. 1884; war serv., with D.L.I., 1914-19; France, 1915-18; twice ment. in despe.; headmtr.-t., govt. primary schls., Gold Coast, 5th June, 1920; mast., training coll., 1st Jan., 1921; inspr., schls., 1s. Apr., 1926.

HARRISON-JONES, CHARLES.—B. 1884; ed. Reading and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1907; pol. mag., Puttalam, Ja., 1909; asst. govt., Colombo and Negombo dists., July, 1910; ag. astt. govt. agt., Hambantota, Apr., 1912; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1912; ag. astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1913; on military duty, Jan., 1915; dist. judge Tangalla, Dec., 1919; ag. astt. govt. agt., Matale, June, 1920; ditto, N'Eliya, Nov., 1924; ag. govt. agt., Eastern prov., May, 1927; govt. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1929.

HART, H. W.—B. 1897; on mily. service in France and Flanders, Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1919; service in corps of mily. accnts., 1919-21; temp. clk., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July 1926.

HART, RUPERT LANGELOT LEON, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Reading Schol. and R. Veterinary Coll., London; govt. vety. surg., N. Rhodesia, 1910-14; vety. offr., Uganda, 1914; served with E. African Forces in Great War, 1914-17 and on Western Front, 1917-19; senr. vety. offr., Uganda, 1920; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, Aug., 1927.

HART, WILLIAM HUME, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.).—B. 1886; ed. Sedberg, Yorks and Dub. Univ. (Trin. Coll.); med. offr., grade III, F.M.S., May, 1912; grade II, Nov., 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Dec., 1915; temp. 2nd lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1916; capt., Feb., 1917; discharged, Sept., 1919; ag. medl. supdt., cent. mental hosp., Tanjong Rambutan, June, 1920; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, May, 1922; ditto, Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1923; ditto, Perak, Nov., 1925; ditto, Pahang, Dec., 1925; ditto, Selangor, Apr., 1926; senr. med. offr., Pahang, but continues ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, Aug., 1926; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, Aug., 1927; senr. med. offr., Perak, Nov., 1927.

HART-DAVIS, CHARLES HENRY, C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1874; ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, B.A.; served in 13th L.Y. in South Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); priv. sec. to Sir W. Haynes Smith, Cyprus, 1901; asst. insp. of schools, Cyprus, 1901 to 1906; passed exams. in Turkish and Greek, 1903; insp. of schools, Gold Coast, 1906; dis. comnr., 1906 to 1910; sec. to high comnr. for the W. Pacifica, 1910; on mily. serv., 1915-19; ch. astt. sec., Cyprus, Apr., 1919; ag. ch. inspr. of schls., Aug. to Oct., 1919; ag. ch. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. registr.-gen., Dec. to May, 1920; ag. comnr., July to Oct., 1920; ag. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1920-21 and 1923; comnr., July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Jan., 1924; ag. col. soc., Mar. to May, 1929 and June, 1930.

HARTER, JEAN DENIS EDGARD.—R. 1879; ed. R. Coll. of Mauritius; astt. mast., R. Coll., 1901; astt. mast., Victoria Schl., Seychelles, Aug., 1902; astt. mast., King's Coll., 1910; headmast., Victoria Schl., July, 1920; ag. dir., educn. and inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1922; inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1924; sec. to bd. of educn.; local sec., Cambridge local exam.; ag. prte. sec., clk. to gov. and clk. to couns., from 16th Dec., 1924.
HARTNELL, G. F. W.—Chief mechanical engr., Uganda rlyw., June, 1918, and from Apr.-June, 1922; comnr., July, 1922; ag.regiar.-gen., July-Sept., 1922.
HARTWELL, CHARLES HERBERT, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1926; attd., Kandy kach., Jan., 1927; attd., secretariat, Oct., 1927; office astt., N. Eliya kach., May, 19 8.

HARVEY, EDRIC, B.Sc. (Agr.), A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1902; ed. Woodford Coll., Essex, Transval Univ. Coll. Pretoria and Colonial Office course, Oxford, 1925; Imp. Coll. Trop. Ag., 1926; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1927.

HARVEY, DAVID HAROLD.—B. 1874; entd. Natal civ serv., Apr., 1898; transf'd, to Swaziland adminstr. as clk., Mar., 1903; sub-inspr., Swaziland police, Apr., 1907; dep. astt. comnr., Apr., 1911; astt. comnr., 1920.
HARVEY, HON. JOHN MUSGRAVE.—B. 1865; judge of the sup. ct., New S. Wales, 1913.
HARVEY, REGINALD JAMES.—B. 1901; ed. Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920-23; 4th cls., hona., mod. hist.; St. George Schl., Jerusalem, 1924-25; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

HASKINS, WILFRED JOSEPH, A.M.I.C.E.—Ast. supt. of wks., S. Stiltns, Mar., 1906; dist. engr., F.M.S. rly., May, 1912; senr. dist. engr., grade B, F.M.S. rly., July, 1919; ag. engr. for ways and wks., Nov., 1920 to Oct., 1921; ag. ch. res. engr. for constrn., Mar., 1923; senr. dist. engr., Johore dist., Nov., 1924; ag. engr., ways and wks., Aug., 1926; engr. for constrn., F.M.S. rly., Mar., 1927.

HASZARD, HON. FRANCIS LONGWORTH.—B. 1849; ed. at Prince of Wales's Coll., Prince Edward Is.; called to the bar, 1872; K.C., 1894: elected to legis., P.E.I., 1904 and 1908; mem. exec. coun., 1905; prem. and atty.-gen., P.E.I., 1908; judge, supreme ct., and master of the rolls, P.E.I., 1911.

HATCHER, C. M.—B. 1873; apptd., after compet. exam., olk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11th Sept., 1893; transf'd to C.O., 18th Mar., 1896; minor staff offr., Feb., 1902; staff olk., first grade, July, 1913; dept. ast., 26th Feb., 1923.

HAVENGA, HON. NICOLAES CHRISTIAAN.—Atty-at-law; mem., prov. coun. for Fauresmith, O.F.S., and mem., exec. comte., for O.F.S., 1910-15; mem., Union H. of A. for Fauresmith from 1915; min. of finance, 30th June, 1924.

HAWKEN, H. E. A.—B. 1879; ed. pub. schls., Ottawa Colleg. Inst.; ent. civ. serv. of Canada as jun. clk., dept. marine and fisheries, 1901; 2nd els. clk., 1908; 1st cls. clk., 1910; ch. clk., 1917; ast. dep. min., 1st Apr., 1920.

HAWKINS, BRIAN CHARLES KEITH.—B. 1900; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.), honos., mod. hist., 1923; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; 2nd ast. collr., treas., 1927; ast. ast., impts. and expts., 1927.

HAWKINS, GERALD.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ.; pte., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., Feb., 1916; B.E.F., Mar., 1915; ment. in desp., Dec., 1917; wounded, and discharged with rank of capt., May 1920; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ast. to land office, Penang, Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1921; ast. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, Dec., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV, May, 1923; ast. ast., sec., res., Selangor, Dec., 1925; sec., res., Pahang, Apr., 1928.
HAY, CLIFFORD H., C.M.G., C.B.E. (1928), M.V.O.—United sec., and perm. head, premier's dept., New S. Wales since Mar., 1916.

HAY, TIMOTHY CECIL.—B. 1891; ed. The Schl., Bishop Stortford and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge (exhib.). B.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; ast. ast. contrlr. of lab., Penang, Mar., 1915; ast. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Mar., 1916; passed cadet, July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; dep. contrlr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; ast. ast. adviser, Endau, Johore, May, 1921; ast. ast. adviser, Batu Pahat, Mar., 1922; prot. of Chinese and contrlr. of lab., Johore, Oct., 1922; ast. ast. adviser, Batu Pahat, Sept., 1923; dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; ast. adviser, Kemaman, Trengganu, June, 1926 to Feb., 1929; ast. valuer gen., F.M.S. and town planning admtr., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1929; ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1930.

HAYCRAFT, SIR THOS. WAGSTAFFE, KT. BACH. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. cham. of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDEN, JAMES J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; clasped in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (duel gold medallist); admitted solr., sup. ct. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; called to Irish bar, 1926; regis., high ct., Uganda, 21st Jan., 1921; also adnl. dist. mag., 1st cls. adminstr.-gen., rev. authority, regis. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house; mag., Uganda, Mar., 1927.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, HUGH.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford; on war serv., Artists Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1924; capt., B. G. Militia (Art.), 1922-25; ag. 2nd cls. clk., July, 1928; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1928.

HAYES, ROBERT EDWARD, C.M.G. (1929), I.S.O. (1926).—B. 1869; ed. New Zealand Univ.; entd. N.Z. postal dept., 1888; acct., tourists and industries and commerce dept., 1903; regis., friendly societies, 1906; also supt., national provident fund, 1910; ast. sec. and acct. to treas., 1922; sec. treas., paym.-gen. and regis. of inscribed stock, 1926; again supt., national prov. fund, 1928; finan. adviser to N.Z. govt., 1930.

HAYES-SADLER, CECIL, WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon)—B. 1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxon; served in France with R. Engnr., (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; ast. dist. commr., Kenya Col., 1920; ag. senr. ast. sec., 1926.

HAYNES, ALWYN SIDNEY.—B. 1878; ed. at Haileybury Coll.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. ser., Nov., 1901; attached to dist. office, Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; ag. finan. ast., Krian, Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1903; passed final exam. in law, Oct., 1902; mag., Perak, 1902; passed final exam. in Tamil, Aug., 1903; ag. finan. ast., Krian, Sept., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Nov., 1905; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1906; ag. inspr., schls., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1906; 1st cls. mag., Negri Sembilan, 1906; ag. ast. ast., Indian immigrts., Penang, Nov., 1906; ast. ast., ditto, Nov., 1908; examr., Tamil, Perak and Penang; ast. ast., Indian immigrts., F.M.S. and S. Sitiimte., Feb., 1910; ag. ast. ast., immigrts., Klang, Jan., 111; passed final exam. in Siamese, Aug., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Sept., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Dec., 1914; mem., bd. of examiners in Malay for Perak, Apr., 1915; dist. offr., Tampin, 1916; sec., high commr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., and dir. food production ditto, Feb., 1922; chmn., small holdings comtes. (rubber restrictions), Nov., 1923; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Feb., 1924; ag. Br. res., Perak, Nov., 1925; ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1925; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Sept., 1926; resumed as sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1927; ag. commr., lands, F.M.S. in adn., June to Aug., 1927; represented S.S., F.M.S. and R.R.I., Malaya, at Imp. Agr. Research Conf., London, Oct. to Nov., 1927; mem., Col. Agr. Service comte., Nov., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Oct., 1928; dist. offr., Lower Perak, Feb., 1929; ag. sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1929; contr., lab., Malaya, Apr., 1930; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, May, 1930.

HAZEN, HON. SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Collegiate Schl., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis. for Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; chief just. of New Brunswick, Nov., 1917.

HAZLERIGG, GARY, O.B.E. (1920).—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A., LL.B. (class tripoe, 1900; law tripoe, 1905); dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Dec., 1908; regis., sup. ct., Ceylon, Oct., 1910; called to Ceylon bar, 1914; sec., Ceylon Coun. of legal educ., 1914; served in Belgium and France with Rifle Brig., 1915; served in C.O. from May, 1916; sec. to gov. gen., Union of S. Africa, 1919-21; employed in C.O. since 1924; temp. ag. prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

HAZLERIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, M.C.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., 1914-1915; capt., R.A.S.C., 1915 to 1920; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1920; ag. asst. land offr. in addn., 1921 to 1922 and from 1922 to 1923; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1924; treas. solr. in addn., 1926-29; ag. dep. regis. in addn., Apr., 1928 to Feb., 1929; pol. mag. in addn., July to Sept., 1929; regis., sup. ct., offl. admstr. and regis., companies, Oct., 1929.
HEAD, FREDERICK JAMES.—B. 1887; ed. Merchant Venturer Tech. Coll., Bristol; asst. supt., tels., Nigeria, 1911; engr., 1914; lieut., R.W.A.F.F., 1914-15; suptg. engr., 1922; divnl. engr., 1927.

HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925; seconded for service with Br. economic mission to Australia, 10th May, 1928 to 3rd Mar., 1929.
HEAPE, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1896; ed. Rugby and Sandhurst; asst. sec., Somaliland, Nov. 1919; asst. sec., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1929.

HEARNE, HORACE HECTOR.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; cadet, Uganda, Jan., 1916; 1st cls. mag., Aug., 1918; dist. mag., Jan., 1921; seni. mag., Nov., 1924; ag. puisne judge on various occasions, 1925-26 and 1928-30; ag. chan. of diocese, Nov., 1929; mem., H.M.'s ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1930.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e. 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resig. with his govt., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint coman. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. clk., Min. of Nat. Serv., Nov., 1917; transf., O.S.O., Mar., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.

HEATH, HUBERT HAROLD, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1865; ent. pub. serv., Barbados, 1883; junr. clk., P.O., 1885; 1st clk., 1902; acctnt., 1908; ag. col. postmtr. on several occasions, 1907-18; ch. clk., treasy., 1918; ag. col. treas., Mar. to July, 1919; ch. offr., cust., 1919; ag. comptr., cust., on several occasions, 1919-22; man., govt. savings bank, 1922; col. postmtr., 1926.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; ditto, dist. "A," 1904; ag. sen. med. offr., Montserrat, 1905, 1912, and since 1916; prea., defence reserve, 1909; ag. mem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911; mem. legis. coun., May, 1915; med. offr.-in-chge. of Montserrat recruits for the B.W.I. regt., Mar., 1916; surg.-lieut. defence force, 1916; provisionally mem. exec. coun., 1916; ag. comanr., Montserrat, on several occasions since Feb., 1918.

HEDDEN, GEORGE BRENTNALL, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1886; ed. Bedford Sch.; tel. constrn. supt. engr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, May, 1909; suptg. tel. engr., Jan., 1914; asst. engr.-in-charge, Jan., 1921; P.M.G., July, 1928; served with Cameroons Expdy. Force, 1914-16; dir., posts and tels. to C.K.F., 1915; engr.-in-obj., 1921; P.M.G., 1928.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1879; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903; prin. olk., East London, 1915; surr., Durban, 1916; oh. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918; dep. comanr., cust. and excise, 1926; comanr. cust. and excise, 1930; del. for Union govt. at Imp. Cust. Conf., 1921; cust. adviser to deleg. to Australian govt. (four shipments), 1921; mem., deleg. to Kenya to rept. on trade prospects, 1923; rep. of Union govt. at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1923; advisory mem., Imp. Conf., 1926; del. for Union govt., Imp. Conf., 1930.

HEDGES, WALTER FREDERICK, O.B.E., F.R.I.B.A.—B. 1880; served with L.N.L. Regt., France, Salonica, Egypt, Palestine, 1915-19; architect, Gold Coast hosp., 1920; Prince of Wales' Coll., Achimota, 1923; ch. arch., pub. works dept., Gold Coast, 1926; arch.-in-charge, archl. brchs., pub. works dept., F.M.S., Mar., 1928; ch. architect, P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1929.

HEIDENSTAM, OSCAR CHARLES.—B. 1877; ed. at King's Sch., Warwick; vol. clk., col. sec.'s off., Cyprus, 5th Feb., 1899; 3rd div. clk., ditto, 16th Jan., 1899; inspr. of pol., 12th Jan., 1904; sent to Egypt for training with H.M.'s troops, May, June, and July, 1904; in command of depot, 8th Aug., 1904; in command of Kyrena div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 15th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as comanr. for Papho on several occasions; passed in mod. Greek lower standard, Apr., 1906; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to high comanr., Sir. C. A. King-Harman, and clk. of exec. coun., 4th Jan. to 16th Feb., 1907; temporarily attached to ch. sec.'s off., 16th Feb., 1907; in comd. of pol., Kyrena div., gov. of pris., and dep. coroner, 24th June, 1907; asst. dist. supt. of pol., Uganda Prot., 12th July, 1907; in commd. of Entebbe pol., and supt. of cent. gaol, 10th Sept., 1907; inspr. of police, Cyprus, Apl., 1910; ch. of pol., Grenada, 22nd July, 1914; inspr.-gen., pol., Barbados, 1925.

HELLINGS, GEOFFREY STUART.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp. (foundation schol.) and St. John's Coll., Camb., Stewart of Rannoch Univ. schol., Camb., 1909; B.A., 1910 (cl. trip. ii. I); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; seconded for mil. ser., Jan., 1915; 2nd lieut., 8th (Service) Batt. D. of Cornwall's Light Infy., Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., May, 1916; lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., Sept., 1916; ag. capt., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., June, 1918; demob., Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Nov., 1919; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1920; ag. asst. prot., Chinese, Selangor, Sept., 1921; contr., lab. and prot. of Chinese, Johore, Sept., 1923; offr., ola. III., Nov., 1923; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, July, 1928; ag. sec., Chinese affr., F.M.S., in addn., Aug., 1928; offr., ola. II., prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1928; collr., estate duty, F.M.S., June, 1929.

HELPFS, EDMUND ARTHUR PLUNKETT.—B.1888; ed. Marlborough and Clare Coll., Cambridge; history tripso., 2nd cl., (1909); B.A. (1909); cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. supt. of immigts., Klang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. contriv., lab., Aug., 1913; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, May, 1914; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V, Mar., 1915; lent for service under the admntr. of German E. Africa, July, 1917; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Raub, Apr., 1920; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1920; asst. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, May, 1921; addt. to sec., rubber restriction comte., Kulim, Nov., 1923; ag. asst. adviser, Kedah, Jan., 1924; offr., cls. III, ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Nov., 1925; asst. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1925; ag. regisr., sup. ct., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Mar., 1928; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1929.

HEMMANT, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1929).—B.1880; ed. Tonbridge and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; cadet, F.M.S., 1903; ag. asst. dist. treas., Kuala Lumpur, June-Dec., 1904 and Mar.-June, 1905; passed cadet (Malay and Law), May, 1905; ag. rev. audr., Negri Sembilan, July, 1905; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Aug., 1906-Mar., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Mar.-July, 1908 and Jan.-Feb., 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, July-Sept., 1908; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Oct., 1908-Jan., 1909 and from Mar., 1909-Oct., 1910; asst. collr., land rev., Seremban, Dec., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1911-Jan., 1912; seconded, Kedah, Oct., 1914; ag. legal adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1914-Jan., 1916; seconded, Johore, Jan., 1916; ag. asst. adviser, Muar, Jan., 1916-June, 1918; collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur and regisr. of titles, Selangor, Nov., 1919-Nov., 1920; sec. to rec., Selangor, Dec., 1920-Mar., 1922; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Jan.-Apr., 1923; ag. under-sec., S. Sttlmts., May, 1923-Mar., 1924 and from Apr.-July, 1924; ag. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Mar. and Aug., 1924; gov's dep., Mar., 1924; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Sept., 1924; ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, 1930.

HEMSTEED, COMMDR. FRANCOIS RUSTAT, R.D., R.N.R. (ret.).—B. 1883; cadet, H.M.S. Conway, 1898; lieut., R.N.R., 1911; 2nd offr., Uganda marine, 1913; served E. African campaign, 1914-17; ment. in desps.; ch. offr., Uganda rly. marine 1918; lieut. commdr., R.N.R., 1919; commdr., Uganda rly. marine, 1926; prin. marine offr., Nigerian marine, 1929.

HEMSTEED, RUPERT WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1919).—2nd treasy. asst., E.A.P., 19th Sept., 1899; ch. acctnt., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1901, to 1st Dec., 1903; asst. collr., E.A.P., 1st Dec., 1904; collr., 17th Dec., 1906; offr.-in-charge, Masai reserve, 1912; senr. comsnr. (1st cls.), Jan., 1924.

HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 18th Oct., 1904; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. surg., May, 1923.

HENDERSON, JOHN, M.A., D.Sc. (N.Z.), B.Sc. (Eng.), A.O.S.M., F.N.Z.Inst.—B. 1880; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ.; instr., Reefton schl. of mines, 1903; mining geologist, geolog. survey, 1911; dir., 1928.

HENDERSON, RICHARD MCNIMIL, M.I.C.F., M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; ed. Allen Glen's Inst. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; senr. asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1912; exec. engrnr., 1920; asst. D.P.W., Hong Kong, Jan., 1928.
HENDERSON, W. W., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M. (Viot.), capt., R.A.V.C. (R. of O.).—Vety. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1913; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1924; ch. vety. offr., Nigeria, 1927.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—Foreman of Works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 26th June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., 1st Dec., 1921; on mil. serv., 7th Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1916; coy. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; serv. in Northd. Cyclists' Bttn., Northd. Fus. and Machine Gun Corps; M.C. and Bar; seconded to Zambia as prin. new vocational schl. to organise tech. educn., 1929.

HENRY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1888; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh (dux and gold medallist), and Edinburgh Univ.; Egyptian educn.; serv., 1911-15 and 1919-20; served Royal Highlanders and Royal Irish Regt., 1915-19; ment. in desps., 1919; dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 1920; M.L.C., 1926; 3rd cls., Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1928.

HENKEL, JOHN SPURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, 1st July, 1886; asst. to conservator, 1st Mar., 1890; dist. forest offr., King William's Town, 1st June, 1893; Stutterheim, 1st Aug., 1898; asst. conserv. forests, E. Conservancy, 1st Oct., 1905; asst. conserv., W. Conservancy, 1st July, 1907; prin. lecturer, forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, Oct., 1906; resig. lecturship, Feb., 1908; chmn., bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, May, 1907; asst. conserv., forests, Midland Conservancy, 1st Feb., 1909; conserv. forests, ditto, 2nd grade, 1st Apr., 1912; conserv., forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as captain with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's med.; tranfd., Rhodesian forest serv., 1st Jan., 1920.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 29th May, 1930.

HENNESSEY, PATRICK H.—M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); house surg., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1907; supernumerary med. offr., Penang, Feb., 1908; med. offr. gen. hosp., Singapore, May, 1908; ag. med. offr., Malacca, Oct., 1908; med. offr., grade II, Kuala Pilah, and med. offr., rly. construction, Apr., 1909; ag. med. offr., grade I, May, 1914; ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, in 1918 and 1919; ophthalmologist physician in addition; med. offr., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1920; med. offr. and opthal. surg., Ipoh, Sept., 1923; med. offr., Ipoh, in addn., Nov., 1925.

HENNIKER-HEATON, HERBERT.—B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1928).—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford (hons. hist., 1901); cadet, Fiji, 1902; attached to col. sec.'s off., to 1905; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in reglar.-gen.'s off., Mar.-May, 1904; ag. adjut., armed constab., musk. instr., and inspr. of pol., Feb., 1905, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., Lau, and inspr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; ch. clk., native dept., and ag. asst. native comsnr., July, 1906; apptd. asst. native comsnr., Mar., 1907; ag. chief pol. mag. and offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1910, to Feb., 1911; mem. of mining bd., Apr., 1910; comnr. of customs, Jan., 1911; mem. bd. of examiners, Feb., 1911; nom. mem. Suva schl. bd., Oct., 1911; ag. chief pol. mag., Jan., 1912; mem. native regulation bd., May, 1912; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Oct., 1913; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1914; ag. prot. of immigts. and poor law comnr., Apr., 1915, to Jan., 1916; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May and from July, 1916, to May, 1917; in comd., cov., Mauritius Vol. Force, 1916-17; col. sec., Gambia, 1917; ag. gov., June to Dec., 1918, Oct. to Dec., 1919 and July, 1920 to Jan., 1921; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1921; ag. gov., June, 1923 to May, 1924; col. sec., Bermuda, Oct., 1925; col. sec., Cyprus, Sept., 1929; ag. gov., Dec., 1929 to Jan., 1930 and May to Sept., 1930.

HENRY, HON. GEORGE STEWART, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. pub. schs., Upper Can., Coll., Univ. of Toronto, and Ont. Agric. Coll.; farmer; el. to Ont. legis. at by-el., 1913; re-el., 1914, 1919 and 1923; min. of pub. wks. and highways in Ferguson admntr., 1923.

HERBERT, PERCY FREDERICK.—B. 1887; ed. Latymer, Liverpool Inst. High Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford, B.A.; supt., schls., S. Prova., Nigeria, 1915; inspr. and schlmast., 1919; ch. in-pr., S. Prova., 1929.

HEREST, JOHN FREDERICK, C.B.E. (1919).—Ck., Cape census off., May, 1891; Carnarvon, Dec., 1891; Willowmore, May, 1895; atty.-genl.'s off., Cape Town, July, 1896; held several ag. appts.; asst. mag., Gordonia, Apr., 1897; relvg. off., Apr., 1899; ag. asst. mag., Rietfontein, Nov., 1905; confirmed Jan., 1908; ag. civ. commr. and mag., Somerset, E., May, 1908; mag., St. Marks, July, 1908; detached for special service in B.B. Prot. in connection with inroads of Hottentots ex German S.W. Africa, under Simon Kooper; received thanks of the Cape govt. and Imp. Germ. govt. upon successful accomplishments of mission; inspecting mag., S.W. Africa Prot. (with rank of Major in Union Defence Forces), Sept., 1915; dep. secy., S.W. Africa Protectorate, Mar., 1916; secy. for Protectorate, Dec., 1916; acctg. offr. in addition, Apr., 1920; ag. admntr., S.W. Africa, Oct., 1920; sec. for S.W. Africa, civ. admntr., 1921; sec. for native affrs., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1923.

HERCHENRODER, SIR FURCY ALFRED, KT. BACH. (1914), K.C. (Mauritius), 1906.—B. 1865; ed. Roy. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1888; ag. crown pros., Mauritius, 1893-8; leg. adviser, pol. mag., crown pros., conservr. of mortgages, and curator of vacant estates, Seychelles, May, 1898; judge, Seychelles, 1900; ch. just., 1903; proc. and advoc. genl., Mauritius, 3rd Apr., 1905; ag. chief judge, 3rd Sept., 1912; ch. judge, 1st Jan., 1913; ret.

HERDMAN, HON. SIR ALEXANDER LAWRENCE, KT. BACH. (1929).—B. 1869; ed. at Otago and Oamaru high schls.; barrister-at-law, 1894; entd. New Zealand part., 1902; atty.-genl., min. of justice, 1912; atty.-genl. in National ministry, 1915; resig. 1918, and apptd. judge of N.Z. sup. ct.

HERMON-HODGE, H. B.—B. 1885; ed. Winchester coll. (scholar) and Magdalen coll., Oxford; B.A., hons. in mods. and hist.; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1908; dist. offr., 1917; res., 1925; staff grade, 1930.

HEROLD, THOMAS BABY.—Cape civ. ser., 1882-1901; ch. ck. to master of sup. ct., Transvaal, Oct., 1901; asst. master, June, 1902; oomsnr. of protocols, Dec., 1904; master of the sup. ct., Oct., 1904; mem. of prisons advisory bd., Feb., 1907; dir. (chmn.), 1907-8 and 1908-9), land and agric. bank, Oct., 1907; chrmn. and gen.-man., land and agric. bank of South Africa, 1912; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 1912; man. dir., land and agric. bank, Oct., 1924.
HERRING, HERBERT RALPH.—B. 1905; ed. Ipswich and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A. (chem.) (hons.), B.A. (forestry); asst. conservator, forests, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

HEETZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.L.A.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1890 to 1900; comd. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-genl., O.R.C.; min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912; leader of opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min., 30th June, 1924.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B. 1890; asst. auditor, G. Coast, 25th Feb., 1914; ag. senr. ass't. auditor, 28th Jan. to 17th Mar., 1919; ag. ass't. col. sec., 19th Aug. to 14th Sept., 1919; jun. clk., Coll. Audit Dept., 1st July, 1920; 2nd clk., 1st Apr., 1921; audr., Gibraltar, 3rd Nov., 1922; audr., Trinidad, 18th Sept., 1926; audr. N. Rhodesia, 1930.

HEWLETT-COOPER, C. T.—Ent. "Britannia," 1889; lieut., 1898; comdr., 1909; capt. (ret.), 1921; S. African Med., 1900; commanded H.M.S. Aquarius, Aegean and Adriatic operations, 1914-17; commanded H.M.S. Andes, escort to convoys, 1918; commanded H.M.S. Marsa, 1919-20; joined col. serv. as dir. of marine, Dar-es-Salam, 2nd Oct., 1920.

HIBBERT, JOHN GROFFEY, M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hon. schl. mod. languages (French) 1913; pte., 28th County of London Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.A.O.C., Nov., 1915; capt., May, 1916; major, May, 1918; D.A.D.O.S. 15th (Scottish) Divn.; ment. in desps.; att'd. mission to Lithuania, Oct., 1919; junr. examr., clearing office (enemy debts) B. of T., Sept., 1920; senr. examr., Jan., 1921; asst. claims clk., Jan., 1922; att'd. Br. govt. att., Mixed Arbitral Tribunal, July, 1928; temp. admstv. offr., D.O., Nov., 1929; C.O., Feb., 1930; asst. ptev. sec. to S. of S. for theCols., Oct., 1930.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M.—Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, 3rd Jan., 1903 to 19th Mar., 1917; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 20th Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917.

HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundell's Schol., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Ramsgate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pol., Somaliland Prot., 22nd May, 1906; asst. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, Oct., 1925.

HIGGINS, MAJOR ROBERT HENRY CONSTABLE, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923.

HIGGINSON, ROBERT CYRIL GERARD DURANT.—B. 1884; ed. Brighton Coll.; cadet, Fiji, 1907; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1908; admitted barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1909; stip. mag., 1910; passed cadet, 1911; 2nd grade dist. commr., 1914; dist. commr., and prov., commr., Colo East, Apr., 1914; prov. commr., Mamori and Serua and dist. commr., Navua, 1915; ag. res. commr., Rotuma, Nov., 1919 to Jan., 1921; prov. commr., and dist. commr., Lomaiviti, Apr., to Dec., 1921; ag. ch. pol. mag., ag. regiar., sup. ct., ag. regiar. of titles, ag. curator, intest. and vacant estates, ag. pub. trustee, ag. offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Dec., 1921 to Mar., 1922; ch. pol. mag., regiar.-gen. and registr., titles, July, 1922; nom. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1922; commr., cust., Sept., 1925; mem. of comtes. to rep. on constitution and laws of Rotuma, Nov., 1926; seconded for sp. serv. with the W. Pacific high comsnr. and apptd. a judl. comsnr., to preside over Malaita murder trials, Solomon Is., Mar. to June, 1928; resumed duty as ch. pol. mag. etc., Oct., 1928; res. mag. Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1929.

HIGHAM, REGINALD.—B. 1879; traffic canvasser, Gold Coast rly., Feb., 1904; ch. traffic offr., Aug., 1911; ag. gen. man. for various periods, 1919-21 and in 1924, 1927 and 1929; dep. gen. man., Sept., 1928.

HIGHET, DAVID JOHN, M.I.C.E.—First asst. engrnr., Ulu Selangor extension, Oct., 1890; ch. asst. engrnr., Jan., 1894; ag. res. engrnr. for rlyws., Mar., 1894; dist. rlyw. engrnr., F.M.S.R., Jan., 1903; ag. divisional engrnr., Selangor and N. Sembilan, June, 1904; divisional engrnr., South, Apr., 1906; ag. gen. man., F.M.S.R., May, 1909.

HIGNETT, CHARLES FRANCIS.—Ed. Woking Coll., Surrey; served with Imp. Army, Apr., 1884, to Aug., 1889; 3rd clk., mag. office, Durban, Aug., 1890; sub-inspr., Zululand pol., Jan., 1891 to Jan., 1896 (three years as adjutant); asst. mag., Umlalazi sub-dist. of Eshowe, Feb., 1896; mag., Umlalazi dist., Dec., 1897; Ngutu dist., Aug., 1899; ag. mag., Eshowe dist. Apr. to Aug., 1903; Dundee dist., Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909; mag., Alexandra div., Aug., 1909; Umgeni div., Pietermaritzburg, June, 1911; Eshowe, Zululand, May, 1912; Verulam, Nov., 1915; senr. mag., Durban, Oct., 1920; judge, native high ct., Natal, Oct., 1922; war services, rebellion in Zululand, 1888-9; Anglo-Boer War, 1890-1902; Natal native rebellion, 1906.
HILL, GILBERT.—B. 1889; wireless engrnr. operator, Gambia, Feb., 1923; tel. asst. dir., Somaliland, Sept., 1926.

HILL, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1877; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; 8th clk., admtr.-gen.'s off., B. Guiana, May, 1896; 8th cls. clk., police dep., Feb., 1897; sub-inspr. of police, Dec., 1900; dist. inspr. of pol., Apl., 1903; ag. adjut. and muskty. instr., July to Nov., 1906; ag. county inspr. of pol., Nov., 1909 to Apl., 1910, and from June to Aug., 1911; ag. pay and qrtmr. from Sept., 1911; pay and qrtmr., 1st Jan., 1912; commd. paymr. and staff offr. for admtrv. duties with local forces, 1914-18; 1st cls. clk., audit dept., Apr., 1923; ag. dir., co-op. socs., July to Dec., 1923; ch. clk., audit dept., Jan., 1925; ag. audr. on various occasions, 1926-27.

HILL, HON. LIONEL LAUGHTON, M.P. (S. Australia)—B. 1881; comsnr. of pub. wks., min. of educn. and min. of industry, S. Australia, 1924-26; premier, treas. and min. of educn., 1926-27 and since Apr., 1930.

HILL, REGINALD DYKES RICHARDSON.—B. 1902; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1926; ag. prin. clk. and clk. to the ct. of policy and combined ct., Sept. to Apr., 1928; ag. prin. olk. and 2nd A.C.S. on various occasions, 1928-29.

HILL, SAMUEL RAMBAY.—B. 1841; S. African constab., 1901; Queen's S.A. med., five clasps; prison serv., E.A. Prot., June, 1905; ag. dep. supt., Sept., 1908; ast. supt., Apr., 1910; mily. serv., 1914-18; ment. in deeps.; M.C.; supt., Apr., 1917; ast. comsnr. of prisons, Kenya, Feb., 1923; ditto, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923.
HILL, WILFRID JAMES, C.D.A.—B. 1895; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; mily. serv., 1916-19; C.D.A., Edin. Agr. Coll., 1921; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika, 1925.

HILLIER, SIDNEY CHARLES.—B. 1888; London Schl. of Economics (Brunel med.); G.W.R., 1902; mily. serv., 1915-1921; ment. in deeps. (twice); G.W.R., 1921; ast. acct., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ag. dep. ch. acct., Tanganyika rly., on various occasions, 1925-29; ag. ch. acct., 1928; dep. ch. acct., July, 1930; ch. acct., 1931.

HILLYER, HERBERT KEYS, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., July, 1900; secretariat ast., col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, Jan., 1908; sec., Colombo Port Comsn., July, 1913; on war service, 1918-1919; resumed duties, 1919.
HIMSWORTH, ERIC.—B. 1905; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Feb., 1929.

HINCKS, MAURICE THORP.—B. 1885; ed. Stoneygate Schl., Leicester, and Blairlodge Schl., Polmont, N.B.; solr., sup. ct., 1908; comsnr. for oaths, 1913; on mily. serv., 1915 to 1920; junr. administrative offr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; pol. mag., Accra, Apr., 1920; ag. ch. regir., Gold Coast, on various occasions; ag. solr.-gen., Jan. to May, 1922; ch. regir., sup. ct., Nigeria, Aug., 1927.

HINDS, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1881; ed. Mily. Schl., Colchester and Colchester Grammar Schl.; acctnt.'s office, S. Rhodesia, 1904; agrl. dept., 1905; adminstr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1906; ast. native comsnr., Apr., 1910; native comsnr., Nov., 1912; ast. mag., Apr., 1922; prov. comsnr., Aug., 1929.

HISLOP, CAPT. FRANCIS DANIEL, M.A. (hons. hist.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; served Territorial Force, 1909-21; on active serv., 1914-19; served Gallipoli, Egypt, France; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; instr. in machine gunnery, 1918-19; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1919; attd., treas., Singapore, Dec., 1919; ast. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, June, 1920; resig. on med. grounds, June, 1921; cadet, Kenya, Dec., 1923.
HITCHENS, ALFRED EDEN.—B. 1880; ast. engrnr., water wks., Trinidad, 24th Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, 24th Sept., 1906; divisional engrnr., 1st Apr., 1925.

HOAL, WILLIAM GARNER.—B. 1886; ed. Normal Coll. Schl. and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., 1904; LL.B., 1908; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1909; clk. to mag., Woodstock, 1906; sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; ast. registr., prize ot., 1914; ast. registr. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Kimberley, 1917; prof. ast. to atty-gen., Pretoria, 1920; govt. atty., 1924; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1929.
HOARE, ALBERT RAWLINSON.—B.1884; govt. schlrm., Falkland Is., Aug., 1915; local audr., Apr., 1928; supt., educn. and headmnr., govt. schl., Stanley, Sept., 1928.

HOARE, Henry Samuel Malortie, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1894; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1928; attd., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1928; attd., Anuradhapura kach., April, 1928; ag. pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Jan., 1929; office asst. Jaffna kach., Feb., 1929.

HOBBDAY, Wilfred Ernest.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1913; Colombo Kachcheri, April, 1913; Trincomalee Kach., Nov., 1813; ag. off. asst. to the govt. agt., Ratnapura, May, 1914; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914; landing survr., Colombo customs, May, 1919; 4th ast. col. sec., Jan., 1922; 3rd ast. col. sec., Feb., 1922; 2nd ast. col. sec., Feb., 1922; ast. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1925; ditto, Kegalle, Feb., 1927; ditto, N'Elia, Sept., 1928.

HOBSON, Capt. Arthur Bertrand (late R.E.)—B. 1893; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Victoria Univ. (B.Sc. 1st cls. hons. chemy., 1912, M.Sc., 1913); 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1914; 25th divl. gas. off.; ast. chem. adviser, 2nd army and commdt. 2nd army gas schl.; chem. offr., Kenya, Sept., 1920; govt. analyst, Nigeria, Feb., 1923.

HOBSON, William Edward, M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1865; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the comsrs. of H.M.'s wrks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1885; transf'd to the C.O., April, 1891; ast. regiar., 1899; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900; staff-clk., first grade, 1911; supt., printing dept., Dec., 1924.
HODGE, S.O.V.—Ast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsrr., Kenya, 1926.

HODGES, Arthur Harris.—B. 1884; entd. Impl. civ. ser., and apptd. to acount. gen.'s office, G.F.O., London, 1901; ch. acount. o'mce, G.F.O. O.R.C., 1903; Australia, 1908-9; junr. ast. treas., N. Nigeria, 1910; senr. ast. treas., Nigeria, 1920; dep. treas., Nigeria, 1924; ag. treas., on various occasions in 1925, 1927 and 1928; mem., rly. prov. fund revision comte., 1926; chmn., finan. instrn. comte., 1928; mem., gen. orders revision comte., 1928.

HODGINS, Hon. Frank Egerton.—B. 1854; educ. Upper Canada Coll. and Trin. Univ.; B.C.L.; called to the bar, 1879; K.C., 1902; prominent legal practitioner in Toronto for several years; pres., Ontario bar assoc., 1908-9; legal agt. for Dominion govt. at Toronto, 1890-1896; counsel for Ontario govt. on license investigation, 1907, and on other occasions; judge of high ct. of Ontario, 1912; also local judge in Admiralty, Exchequer Ct.; now a just. of appeal.

HODGKINS, Gilbert Graham.—B. 1875; ed. N.Z. pub. schls. and Otago High Schl.; entd. N.Z. regis. gen's. dept., 1901; regis., births, etc., 1907; dep. ch. electoral off., 1913; ch. electoral off., 1928.

HODKIN, Capt. Thomas William, Fellow R. Econ. Soc., Fellow R. Statistical Soc., Fellow Inst. of Commerce, Assoc., Chartered Inst. of Secs., Assoc., Inst. of Bankers.—B. 1885; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl., Manchester Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dipl. in educn., Oxford; served in France with E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-18; attd. to gen. staff; educn. offr., 66th divn., 1918-19; instr., schl. of educn. for offrs., Catterick, 1919; instr., schl. of instrn. for offrs., Bedford, 1919-20; instr., W.O. schl. of educn., Shorncliffe, 1920-22; brig. educn. offr., 1st Air Defence Brig., Aldershot, 1922; A.E.C., res. of offrs.; examr. for W.O. in bookkeeping and accountancy; commercial master, Zanzibar, 1922; prin., Arab schl., Mombasa, Kenya, 1927.

HODSON, Arnold Wienholt, C.M.G. (1922).—Ed. Italy, Mulgrave Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub.-inspr., Bech. Prot. prot., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damara war (med. and clasp); mem. of Ngamiland mission, 1906; ag. ast. comsrr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. comsrr. and 1st cls. mag., Somaliland, 1912; H.B.M. consul. for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditto, S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zitodifor, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Jubaland, 1917-18; attd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasp); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorials); has passed Cape Univ. mag. law exam.; Somali language, lower standard; Galla language, higher standard; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Falkland Is. and their dependencies, 22nd Dec., 1926; ditto, Sierra Leone, 1931; author "Seven Years in Southern Abyssinia," "Where Lion Reign," joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HODSON, Bernard.—B. 1876; ent. Natal civ. serv., 1894; clk., mag.'s office, Dundee, 1894; clk., min., native affrs., 1895; capt. and mily. mag., Volksrust, Transvaal, 1900; prin. clk., Durban, 1905; addnl. mag., Durban, 1910; mag., Nongoma, Zululand, 1915; mag., Mpofana, 1917; mag., New Hanover, 1921; mag., Pinetown, 1921; mag., Dundee, 1926; ch. mag., Maritzburg, 1929.

HODSON, Thomas Arthur.—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham and Wadhams Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1905; seconded ast. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; ast. supt. of pol., Galle, July, 1908; ditto, Tangalla, May, 1909; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1909; office ast. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Jan., 1910; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1911; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; ast. settlmt. offr. and spec. offr. under Waste Lands Ordo., June, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; addnl. ast. col. sec., Apr., 1919; ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1919; addnl. ast. dir., educn., Mar., 1922; ag. ast. dir., educn., Apr., 1922; ag. govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1923; ast. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1924; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1925; ditto, N.W. Prov., Oct., 1926.

HOFMEYR, Petrus Jacobus Hercules, F.S.S.—B. 1877; cl. ast., Diamond det. dept., Kimberley, 1895; clk., atty.-gen.'s and col. sec.'s offices, Cape, 1898; clk., mag.'s office, Klerksdorp, 1902; ast. regr. of statistics (Transvaal), 1903; sec., pub. serv. o'man., 1912; pub. serv. inspr., 1921; pub. serv. comsrr., 1926; seconded to state alluvial diamond fields, Namaqualand as gen. man., Feb. to June, 1929.

HOGAN, Hon. Edmond John, M.L.A. (Victoria)—B. 1884; M.L.A., W. Australia, 1913; leader, Victorian party, lab. party, Apr., 1926; premier, treas. and min. of markets, Victoria, May, 1927 to Nov., 1928 and since 1929.

HOGAN, Gerald George.—B.1886; barrister, 1910 (Victoria); practised, Victoria until 1915; served Australian Field Art'y, Egypt and France, 1915-19; rank of major on retirement; ment. in desps.; stip. sp. mag., N. Territory, Australia,

HOLLINGSWORTH, Lawrence William, B.A. (Lond.), F.R. Hist. S.—B. 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. Johns' Sch., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; headmnr., teacher's training schl., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1922; author "Milangoya History" (Vols. I and II); "A Short History of the East Coast of Africa," part author "A School History of Zanzibar."

HOLLINS, Noel Clinton.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab.), 2nd cl. hist., tripes; qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; ast. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar., 1910; ag. senr. ast. col. sec., 1911 and 1912; ast. dist. comanr., Jan., 1913; dist. comanr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comanr., C. Prov., Nov., 1922.

HOLLIS, Sir Alfred Claud, K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; ast. collr., E.A.P., 12th Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., 15th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admstn. and priv. sec. to H.M. comanr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admstn., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1900-1, and Nandi expdn., 1906-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., June, 1907; on sp. serv. to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1913; ag. govr., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915 and Jan. to Mar., 1916; ec. to provisional admstn., German E. Africa (Tanganjika Territory), Nov., 1916; ag. admstn., Feb. to Nov., 1919; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. govr., Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. res., Zanzibar Jan., 1924; govr. and commdr.-in-ch., Trinidad and Tobago, 6th Jan., 1930; assumed govr., 22nd Mar., 1930; author of works on the Massi, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, John Edward.—B. 1809; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C. of G.H. (Victoria Coll.); res. maat., Grey Coll. Sch., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of exams., O.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Econ.) Lond. (Schl. of Economics), 1917; Hutchinson research medallist; lect., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-25; dir., census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1925; Union rep. at Internat. Conven. on Statistics, Geneva, 1928.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Etiya kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1926; ast. prte. sec. to gov., Mar., 1927; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, May, 1927; office ast., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1929; prte. sec. to O.A.G., Apr. to Sept., 1930.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Sydney).—Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (London) in Geology, Assoc., R. Schl. of Mines (London) in Mining. Memm., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, F.G.S. (1900)—Demonstr., mine surveying, R. Schl. of Mines, 1901; mining engnr., Dutch E. Indies, S. Africa, Siam and Gold Coast; senr. inspr., mines, Gold Coast, 1914; sec. for mines, 1922.

HOLT, CAPT. VVYYAN.—B. 1896; the Middlesex Regt. (T.A.R.O.); joined Iraq civ. admin. as ast. polit. offr., Sulaimani, Dec., 1919; A.D.C. to high commr., Iraq, Oct., 1922; pte. sec. to ditto, Nov., 1923; oriental sec. to high commr., Aug., 1926.

HONE, CAPT. HERBERT RALPH, M.C., LL.B.—B. 1896; ed. Vardean Schl., Brighton and London Univ.; enlisted, Inns of Court O.T.C., 1915; 2nd lieut., 18th Lond. Regt., July, 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Jan., 1918; attd. H.Q. 60th Divn., Sept., 1915; adjt., 1/18 Lond. Regt., Feb., 1916; served twice B.E.F., France 1916 and 1917-18; capt. R. of O. (T.A.), 1922; ast. treas., Uganda, Sept., 1920; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; LL.B. hona. Class II (Lond.) July, 1926; off. recr. in bankey. and regrist., H.B.M.'s high ct., Zanzibar, 1926; ag. res. mag., Zanzibar, Jan., 1926 to Feb., 1927; res. mag., Zanzibar, Mar., 1928; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; ag. solr.-gen., Mar. to Sept., 1930.

HONEY, DE SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1872; served in B.S.A. Co.'s expedit., to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B.C. Africa admtn., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acctng. offr., Oct., 1904; gov't. sec., Swaziland admtn., Mar., 1907; res. commr., Swaziland, Jan., 1917; gov', Seychelles, 1928.
HOOD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1893; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; war serv., France, 1916-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922.

HOODLESS, D. W., B.Sc.—B. 1887; ed. Sheffield Univ. and King's Coll., London; B.Sc. hona., Lond., 1910; Drew gold medal, mathematics, 1910; associateship of King's coll.; mast., H.M.S. "Worcester," 1911; ast. mast., Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1912; head mast., Lau Schl., 1915; J.P. for the colony, 1916; ag. head mast., Queen Victoria schl., 1918; ag. supt., schle., 1922; prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1924; ast. dir., educ., 1927.

HOOGENHOUT, NICOLAAN MARAIS.—B. 1876; ed. Boys' High Schl., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; M.A. (mod. lang.), Univ. of Cape of Good Hope; Ph.D., Kaiser Wilhelms-Universitat, Strasbourg (Alsace); prin., Kendrecht High Schl., Pretoria, 1904; vice-prin., Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1908; prin., ditto, 1921; rector, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, 1926; dir. educ., Transvaal, 1928.

HOOGENHOUT, P. IMKER.—B. 1884; ed. Stellenbosch and Cape Town, B.A. and prof. teachers' certifa., 1904 and 1906; headmr., Jeppe Educat. Inst., Johannesburg, 1908, and Hoogehout High Schl., Bethal, 1910; inspr., educ., S.W. Transvaal, 1921; ch. examr. to Trans. educn. dept., 1927; sec. for interior, 1929.

HOOPS, ALBERT LAUNCHLOT.—B. 1876; ed. King William's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1900; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1901; M.D. (stip. cond.) 1903; D.P.H., 1903; L.M. Rotunda, 1903; certif. honours, London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1904; served in S. African War as medical offr., 28th M.I., 1901-2 (several wounded, ment. in despa., medal and four clasps); house surgeon, Penang, 1904; surg. capt., Penang vols.; ag. col. surg. res., Penang, June, 1905; seconded to organise medical and prison dept. in Kedah, Mar., 1906; state surg. and supt. of prisons in Kedah to Nov., 1920; ag. adviser to govt. of Kedah, Sept., 1906, to Apr., 1907, and from July to Oct., 1907; supt. of Indian immigr., Kedah, in addition to other duties, Oct., 1910; prot. of lab., Kedah, in addition, Nov., 1917; priv. civil med. offr., S. Sttlnts., 23rd Nov., 1920; priv. coun., coll. of med., S'pore.; privs., S.S. and F.M.S. med. council; mem., leg. coun., S. Sttlnts., 1st Jan., 1923; chmn., tuberculosis comitee., June to Aug., 1923; pres., 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Singapore, Sept., 1923.

HOPE, JAMES.—B. 1899; ed. Potchefstroom Coll., Transvaal and R.M.C., Sandhurst; gaz. 2nd lieut., K.O.S.B., 1918; Br.-Gen. Serv. and Victory Medals; joined Swaziland serv., Sept., 1919; gaz. clk. to A.C. Hatikulu; ag. mast. regier. and sheriff, 1923; con. clk. to treas., N. Rhodesia, June, 1925; elk., Bech. Prot., May, 1927; passed 3rd grade Swazi language exam., 1921; passed Cape civ. serv., lower law exam., 1922; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., Apr., 1929.
HOPKINS, J. G.—Ast. dist. commr., R.A.P., Mar., 1917.

HOPKINSON, CAPT. EMILIOUS, C.M.G. (1922), D.S.O. (1900), M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.Z.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Haileybury, Trin. Coll., Oxford, and St. Thomas's hosp., Lond.; late ophthalmic house surg., St. Thomas's hosp.; clinical ast., Royal Ophthalmic hosp., Moorfields; house surg., Radcliffe infirmary, Oxford; served in S. African war, 1900-1901, as surg.-capt., 15th batt., L.Y. (ment. in despa., Queen's Med., 4 clasps, D.S.O.); late surg.-lieut., Oxford I.Y.; med. offr., W.A.F.F., Gambia, 1901-1902; prot. med. offr., Gambia, 1903; travelling commr., South Bank prov., Gambia, 1912; travelling commr., North Bank Prov., Gambia, 1921; author of "Vocabulary of the Mandingo Language."

HOPKINSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1894; cler. ast., post office, Br. Guiana, 1911; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1912; 5th cls. ditto., 1914; 4th cls. clk., treasy., 1920; 3rd cls. ditto., 1923; 3rd cls. clk., col. secretariat, 1925; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1926; sec., commrs. of currency, 1928.
HOPPS, HERBERT DAVIDSON.—B. 1876; ast. foreman, Indian State Rly., 1903; foreman, 1919; E. African Forces, 1914-19; ast. loco. supt., Tanganyika Rly., Oct., 1919.

HOPTON, REGINALD EDWARD, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.).—B. 1887; surg. probationer, R.N.V.R. (on active serv.), 1916-19; house surg., R. Infirmary, Edin., 1921; clin. astt., Grosvenor Hosp. for women, London, 1923; clin.-astt., London Lock Hosp., 1925; specialist in venereal diseases, Cyprus, July, 1927; ag. dir. health, Sept. to Dec., 1929; ag. astt. dir. health, July, 1930.

HORR-RUTHVEN, MAJOR-GEN. HON. SIR ALEXANDER GEORGE ARKWRIGHT, K.C.M.G. (1928), V.C. (1899), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1872; ed. Eton; joined 3rd batt. Highland Light Infy., 1891; attd. Egyptian Army, Soudan, 1898; commanded Camel Corps detachment at battle of Gedaref and subsequent eporns. (V.C., 4th cl. Osmanish, English and Egyptian medics with clasp, three times ment. in despa.); sp. serv. offr., Somaliland, 1903-04; mily. sec., Viceroy of Ireland, 1905-06; mily. sec., gov.-gen., Commonwealth of Australia, 1908; served European war, France and Gallipoli, 1914-18 (severely wounded, D.S.O. and bar, C.B., C.M.G., ment. in despa. five times); commanded Welsh Guards, 1920-24; commanded 1st Infy. Brig. (Guards) 1924-28; gov., S. Australia, 1928.

HORN, ARTHUR EDWIN, C.M.G. (1922), M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.); M.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); certif., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinctions).—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-1901; apptd. to W. African medical staff, 1904 and has served in G. Coast, N. and S. Nigeria and Gambia; awarded Cragg research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; reed. thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for report on cerebro-spinal fever in Northern Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for report on sleeping sickness in the Volta River dist. of the G. Coast, 1910; seconded for special service at the C.O., 1910-12; personal astt. to the P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1913; senr. med. offr. ad mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gambia, 1914-16; priv. civ. med. offr., German E. Africa, 1917-19; attd. Rockefeller comsn. for investigation of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1920; dir., med. and many services, Malaya, 1921-22; pres. elect for 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., 1923; med. sec. to Col. Advisory Med. and Sanit. Comtee. at C.O., 1923-24; junr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; consulting physician to C.O., 1928; mem., exec. comtee. of Br. Empire Leprosy Relief Assoc.; fellow R. Socv. of Med.; R. Socv. Trop. Med.; author of "Health of Europeans in W. Africa," "Health of Europeans in E. Africa."

HORNBY, CAPT. HENRY EPTON, O.B.E., F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agril. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vetv. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in desps.; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Schl. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vetv. Research Lab., Pretoria; vetv. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., vetv. services and vetv. pathologist, 1930.

HORNE, EDWARD BUTLER, O.B.E. (1928).—Ed. at Bedford Gram. Schl.; astt. collr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1904; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1909; offr-in-charge, Masai res., May, 1923; senr. comsnr. (2nd class), Kenya, Jan., 1924; senr. comsnr., 1st grade, 1926.
HORNE, EDWIN NOEL.—B. 1902; appt. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920; ag. higher grade cler. offr., 14th Apr., 1930.

HORNE, W. K.—B. 1883; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1913; war serv., 1915-19; ch. just. Tonga, 1925; ag. ch. just., Fiji and ag. ch. judicial comsnr., W. Pacific, Feb.-Sept., 1928; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1930.

HORSBURGH, HARRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1876; ed. Aske's Haberdasher's Company Schl.; C.A., Jan., 1908; astt. dir., min. of munitions, 1915; dep. contr., munitions disposal bd., 1919; dep. head, engnr. contracts, 1920; tech. offr., "Discovery Comtee," 1923.
HORSFIELD, WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1896; ed. Demstone and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cl. hons. mech. sci. tripus, 1921; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1921.

HORSLEY, BERNARD HILL, O.B.E. (1931).—D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1890; European War, 1914-18; major, 8th (service) batt., K.O.Y.L.I.; D.S.O., M.C., despa.; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1919; dist. comsnr., Somaliland, 1922.

HORSLEY, RICHARD.—B. 1893; ed. Cowley Gram. Schl., St. Helens; gen. fitter, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 13th Aug., 1919; instr. of metalwk., Accra tech. schl., 10th Apr., 1920; ag. prin., 1st Dec., 1920 to 22nd Mar., 1921; house mast., 6th Sept., 1922; head mast., junr. trade schl., 28th Apr., 1926.

HORWOOD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1895; elected to the legislature, 1894; elected as col. secn. 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1895, on the subject of confedn. with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal comsn. on Sealing Industry, 1913; pres., Newfield. centre of St. John Ambulance Assocn.; chrmn. of comtee. for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newfield. fund for hospital cots for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Comsn. established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1916.

HOSEGOOD, THOMAS WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1886; younger brother of Trinity House, master mariner; ed. St. Paul's Cathedral Choir Schl.; harbrmr. and naval agt., St. Lucia, 1918; ag. ch. of pol. on various occasions; astt. harbrmr., marine mag., etc., Hong Kong, 1927.
HOSKING, E. B.—B. 1890; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Wadham Coll., Oxford (class schl.); astt. dist. comsnr. E.A.P., Dec., 1913; dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1925.

HOUGHTON, H. E., F.R.A.S.—B. 1892; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the div. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912; attached to office of high comsnr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1920; transf. to Basutoland serv., Nov. 1923.

HOW, DOUGLAS WALSHAM.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., London Matric.; Un. of S. Africa civ. serv. lower law exam.; Basutoland 2nd grade Sesuto exam.; clerical astt., Basutoland, 5th Oct., 1910; sub-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1912; chief clk., master of ct. and regis., 1st July, 1917; served as 2nd lieut. with Royal Welsh Fusiliers in France, 1918; inspr. and staff offr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1919; dep. astt. comsnr., 1923.

HOWARD, ADDERLEY FITZALAN BERNARD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Wellington Coll.; on active serv., Durham l.l., 1914-19; ret. with hon. rank of ospt., Mar., 1919; M.C., "1914-16." star and two war medals., ment. in despatches, 1917 and 1918; col. audit dept., May, 1919; astt. audr., Nigeria, July, 1919; audr., Gambia, Oc., 1926; 2nd astt. audr., Hong Kong, Sept., 1928.

HOWARD, HON. ERATOS EDWIN.—B. 1868; ed. Iroquois High Sch., Inverness Acad., and McGill Univ.; B.A., 1895; gold medallist in math. and nat. philos.; B.C.L. (gold medallist) 1898; barrister; practised in Montreal, 1899-1919; called to the bar, Quebec, July, 1899; K.C., Sept., 1913; judge, superior ct., prov. of Quebec, 24th Sept., 1919; judge of ct. of King's Bench, 9th Aug., 1920; prof. civ. law, McGill Univ. and elective fellow of law faculty.

HOWARD, FREDERICK JAMES, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., June, 1901; 2nd cls. astt. acctnt., army accts. dept., May, 1905; selected for advancement to higher grade of scale, 1st Nov., 1911; ag. 1st cls. astt. acctnt., Oct., 1914; ag. acctnt., Nov., 1916; 1st cls. astt. acctnt., 15th Dec., 1919; personal supermyn., appt. as acctnt., 1st Jan., 1921; serv. with min. of munitions as dir. from 1915, and with disposal and liquidation comm. treas., as astt. contr. of depl. finance until 1921; O.B.E., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. prin., old style, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prin. C.O., 15th Sept., 1921; prin., 27th Aug., 1928.

HOWARD, JOHN CURTIS.—B. 1887; ed. Uppingham and Clare Coll., Camb. (exhib.) hona., nat. sci.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; 2nd lieut., 4th Lincoln Regt., Mar., 1918; served in France, Jan., 1917 to July, 1920; legal adviser, G.H.Q., France, May, 1919 to July, 1920; pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, Aug., 1920; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1921; ag. king's advoc., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1923 and Aug., 1923; king's advoc., July, 1924; title altered to atty.-gen., May, 1926; joint compilers, 2nd vol., statute laws of Cyprus, 1923; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1927-29.

HOWARD-FLANDERS, WILLIAM EDGAR.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; M.A., 1927; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-18; called to bar, M.T., 1923; mem., south-eastern circ. and cent. crim. ct.; mag., St. Vincent, June, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1928; income tax comsnr., 1928; mem., eduon. bd., 1928; ag. col. sec., July, 1928; atty.-gen., Sept., 1928; ag. ch. just., May-Dec., 1928; admstrd. govt., Feb., 1930; astt. regis., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1930.

HOWARTH, SAMUEL EDWARD JAMES.—Ed. at Buxton Coll., London; served in S. African War, Queen's and King's medals with five clasps; astt. Colony manager, field force canteens, O.R.C., Feb., 1902, to Mar., 1903; astt. acctnt., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1905; ch. acct., P.W.D., 1916.
HOWATSON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—B. 1861; dist. med. offr., Trinidad, 15th May, 1905.

HOWELL, ALLEINE GRAHAM.—B. 1871; 5th clk. corrpdg. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk. comsnr. of agriculture, 1899; sec. to W. Indian agric. conferences, 1900, 1901, 1902, 1903, and 1907.

HOWELL, CHARLES GOUGH.—B. 1894; ed. Mill Hill Schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., Cantab.; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-17; served in France and Flanders, May to Dec., 1916 and ret. on account of wounds in Aug., 1917; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1921; mem., S. Wales circuit; crown coun., Kenya, Nov., 1925; ag. solr. gen., June to Oct., 1930; mem., leg. coun.

HOWELL, E. H., M.B.E., (1927).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Mar., 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Dec., 1907; conf. clk., 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 8th Sept., 1917; deptl. astt., 16th Jan., 1928; clk. for legal instruments, 26th Feb., 1923.

HOWELL, FREDERICK ROUSK.—B. 1886; sup. clk., P.O., Barbados, Feb., 1907; jun. clk., P.O., Nov., 1907; clk. to master in chancery, Dec., 1907; 4th clk., C.S.O., May, 1909; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1919; 3rd grade offr., clerk, exec. coun. and exec. comtce., 1925.

HOWELL, JOHN BRUCE, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1867; 3rd clk., treasy., Barbados, July, 1886; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1891; 2nd clk., C.S.O., and clk. of exec. coun. and comtce., Jan., 1898; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov. of Barbados in 1902, 1904, 1906, 1907, 1910 and 1911; J.P., 1918; clk., col. secy.'s off., Aug., 1918; ag. col. sec. on various occasions 1922-25; has held various other ag. apptmnts.; man., govt. savings bank, 1925.
HOWELL, WILFRED IRVING.—Cadet, botanical and experiment statn., St. Kitts, July, 1907; foreman, Mar., 1909; agri. instr., Nevis, Apr., 1912.

HOWES, ARTHUR BURNABY.—B. 1879; ed. at Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1906; Western circuit; conveyancer, Uganda Prot., May, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Mar., 1917; dist. mag., dist. judge, and admstr.-gen., Entebbe, Uganda, Apr., 1907; dist. mag., dist. judge, and offl. rec. in bankruptcy, Kampala, Uganda, July, 1917; ag. judge of high ct., Jan.-June, 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Feb. to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Jan., 1922; ag. judge of high ct., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and June, 1923; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1927 and Sept. to Oct., 1928.

HUBBALL, JOHN CORST.—Clk., control and audit office, Cape, 1895; examr., 1895; astt. acctnt., 1903; astt. acctnt., conttrlr. and auditor-gen.'s office, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; chief acctnt., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1916; sec., office of high comsnr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1928.

HUBBARD, PERCIVAL CYRIL.—B. 1902; ed. Mill Hill and Caius Coll., Cantab.(mod. languages scholarship); B.A. hona., 1923; LL.B. hona., 1924; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1925; cadet, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., May, 1928; ag. astt. dist. offr., Malaita, Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Ysabel, Jan., 1929; ch. mag. and legal advr., Feb., 1929; ag. Br. judge, joint ct. and judl. comsnr. for New Hebrides, Feb., 1930.
HUDDEN, EDWARD LANPHIER.—B. 1902; ed. Radley Coll.; lieut., 4th Battn. Gloucestershire Regiment., T.A., 1928; cadet, Uganda Prot., 1926.

HUDSON, HAROLD.—Book-keeper, treasy., Dept., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1911; cashier, treasy., Dept., Aug., 1911; astt. treasy., Mar., 1914; 1st grade astt. treasy., Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treasy. on various occasions, 1919-30; dep. currency offr., Apr., 1929 to Dec., 1930.

HUDSON, PHILIP JAMES.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., June, 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to Colombo kach., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Balapitoya, Mar., 1921; astt. settlmt. offr., 1922; office astt., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1924; astt. astt. offr., May, 1925; astt. govt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1929.

HUDSON, LIEUT.-COL. WILLIAM, O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1880; ed. privately and King's Coll., London; S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., with 2 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; contr's staff, cent. telegrapha, London, 1902; dept., P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. P.M.G., Apr. to Aug., 1912; R.E. (signals), 1914-18; offr. in charge, civ. tels., occupied enemy territory admstrn., Palestine, 1919; P.M.G., Palestine, July, 1920.

HUGGARD, WALTER CLARENCE, K.C., B.A., LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. at Dublin Univ.; 1st honours man and senr. mod. in legal and polit. science; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1907; station mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. pulm. judge, July to Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; solr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1920; ag. pulm. judge, June-Oct., 1921; K.C., 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Jan.-Oct., 1922; atty.-gen., 1st Nov., 1922; prepared revd. edn., Laws of Trinidad and Tobago, 1923; atty.-gen., Kenya, Feb., 1926; ag. col. sec. and gov.'s dep., Aug., 1927; atty.-gen., S. Sttlmts., June, 1929.

HUGGINS, CAPT. JOHN, M.C. (1916).—B. 1891; ed. Bridlington Gram. Schl. and Leeds Univ., B.A.; 2nd lieut., Yorks Regt., Nov., 1914; Gallipoli, 1915; Egypt, 1916; France, July, 1916; capt., Sept., 1916; ment. in desp.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Klang, May, 1920; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, July, 1920; agt. to food contr., Selangor and Pahang, Oct., 1920; 4th astt. sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; passed final exam., Malay., June, 1921; dist. offr., Kajang, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. IV., Aug., 1923; recvr., Alsagoff concession, Johore, Feb., 1925; ag. 2nd astt. sec. (A.), S.S., Feb., 1926; pvt. sec. to high commr., May, 1928; ag. 2nd astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. III., Aug., 1928; ag. sec., res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1929; dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1930.

HUGHES, JOHN WHITEHOUSE WARD.—B. 1883; ed. Oakham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1905; M.A., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Kelantan, Dec., 1910; mag., Kota Bharu, Sept., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1914; ag. astt. adviser, Kelantan, Jan., 1919; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Oct., 1922; ag. Br. adviser, Perlis, June, 1923; offr., cls. I., Dec., 1923; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1926; ag. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1927; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1927; ag. commr., lands, S.S., Aug., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1928; offr., cls. IB, commr., lands, F.M.S., continues ag. as above, July, 1928.

HUGHES, LESLIE DONALD CHARLETON, M.C.—B. 1890; B.A., Oxon.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1914; attd. ch., censor's office, Apr., 1915; attd., Badulla kach., Oct., 1915; ag. office astt. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, March, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917-19; astt. dir., food prodn., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1921; astt. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Nov., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1923; astt. astt. offr., Nov., 1923; astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1928.

HUGHES, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM MORRIS, P.C. (1916).—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elec. to 1st H. of R., C. of A., 1901, and member since; min. for external affairs 1904; representative of C. of A. at Navigation Conf., London, 1907. atty.-gen., C. of A., Apr., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1915; prime min., and atty.-gen., 27th Oct., 1915; ditto, 14th Nov., 1916; ditto, 17th Feb., 1917; ditto, 10th Jan., 1918; resigned, Feb., 1923.

HULL, GEORGE HENRY, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1875; advce., sup. ct., Cape and Transvaal; mag. in various towns in Transvaal and Free State from 1902 onwards; prof. astt. to crown pros., Johannesburg, 1918; senr. civ. mag., Johannesburg, 1926; civ. commr. and mag., Kimberley, 1929.
HUMPHERSON, LESLIE HERBERT.—B. 1902; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; astt. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 7th Jan., 1926.
HUMPHREYS, J. J. H.—B. 1875; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Carmarthen; road engrnr., S. Nigeria, 1908; astt. survr., Lagos rly., 1911; engrnr., in-ch., 1926.

HUMPHREYS, LIEUT.-COL. SIR FRANCIS HENRY, G.C.V.O. (1928), K.B.E. (1924), C.I.E. (1920).—B. 1879; ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., 1900; served S. African War, 1900-01 (Queen's med. 3 clasps); ent. polit. dept., govt. of India, 1903; pvt. sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., 1904; astt. commr., Hazara, 1907; dep. commr., Bannu, 1910; Kohat, 1914; polit. agent, N. Waziristan, 1915; polit. offr. with troops, N.W. Frontier, India, 1917 (desps.); served European War in Europe as pilot R.A.F., 1918; polit. agt., Khyber, 1919; dep. foreign sec. to govt. of India, 1921; 1st envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary at the court of H.M. the King of Afghanistan, Jan., 1922; high commr., Iraq, 1929.

HUMPHRYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl., Antigua; senior Camb. certif., 3rd div. honours; cashier, treasy., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of treasy., Antigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. audit clk. and federal acctnt., Leeward Is., May to Oct., 1909, Aug., 1910, and from Apr., 1911 to July, 1912; ag. treasr. of Antigua, and federal treasr. from Dec., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

HUNT, BERTIE LEONARD.—B. 1883; ed. privately and Queen's Coll., London; trooper, 5th batt. Australian C'wealth Horse, S. African War, 1901-02; Cape Mounted Rifles, 1902-03; B.S.A. Co.'s mounted pol., 1904; various clkships., N. Rhodesia, 1907-10; accnt. P.W.D., July, 1910; accnt., London office, B.S.A. Co., May, 1913; seconded to B.S.A. Co.'s comml. branch, N. Rhodesia, 1914-21; accnt., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1921; sec., d.p.t. of lands, Apr., 1924.
HUNT, HENRY AMBROSE.—B. 1866; F.R. Met. Soc.; meteorologist, C. of A., 1906.

HUNT, WILLIAM EDGAR, B.A., C.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. Warwick Schl. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; astt. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1909; astt. col. sec., Lagos, 1910; dist. offr., (2nd cls.), 1917; res., 1924; staff grade, 1929; mem. comm. of inquiry into disturbances in S. E. Provs., 1930.

HUNTER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Archibald, K.C.B. (1898), D.S.O. (1886).—Ed. at Glasgow Acad. and Sandhurst; 4th King's Own Royal Lancaster, 1874; capt., 1882; lieut.-col., 1889; col., 1894; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Egypt, and twice wounded; gov. of Dongola Prov. and comdnt. Frontier Field Force, 1895-99; gov. of Omdurman, 1898; Egyptian medal and her orders; in command of 1st cls. dist. in India; lieut.-gen.on staff, commanded 10th div.S.Africa, 1900-1 (twice ment. in despa.) ; commanded in Scotland, 1901-3; Western Army Corps, India, 1904-7; commanded Southern Army, Indian Army, 1907-9; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar, 1910-1913.

HUNTER, James Blake, B.A. (Univ. of Toronto).—B. 1876; clk. priv. coun. off., Canada, 1899; transf. to P.O. dept., 1900; marine and fisheries, 1901; pub. wks., 1902 ch. clk., 1907; deputy min. of pub. wks., 1908

HUNTER, James Stanley.—B. 1889; ed. Hawera High Schl.; entd. N.Z. govt. serv., 1905; post and tel. dept., 1906; pub. serv. commr's. office, 1914; pvte. sec. to various ministers, 1916-24; off. sec., N.Z. rly., 1924; asst. gen. man., N.Z. rly., 1928; commr., transport, 1929; pvte. sec. to Sir Francis Bell at League of Nations gen. assem. and also at Imp. Conf., 1928.

HUNTER, John Adams.—B. 1890; ed. at Royal Grammar Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1913 (hist. tripos cls. 2, L.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; ag. supervisor, cust., Port Dickson, Dec., 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Aug.-Dec., 1917; in India studying Tamil, 1918; offr., cl. V, 26th Sept., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Klang, 5th Oct., 1918; attd. to chief sec.'s office, Jan-Mar., 1919; seconded for serv. in food control dept., 26th Mar., 1919; ag. 3rd asst. sec. to Govt., F.M.S., 7th Oct., 1920; 4th asst. sec. to Govt., 3rd Oct., 1921; offr., cls. IV., offg. dep. contr., lab., Seremban, Nov., 1921; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1922; on spl. duty, rubber census, July, 1923; offr. in ch., govt. toddy shops in adn., July, 1923; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Oct., 1926; offr., Class III, Nov., 1926; ag. sec. to rea., Selangor, July, 1927.
HUNTER, K. L.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

HUNTER, Louis Lucien.—B. 1889; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; att. to Colombo kach., Sept., 1914; addl. commr. of requests and pol. mag., Ratnapura, Oct., 1916; addl. commr. of requests and pol. mag., Panadura, Sept., 1916; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1918; pol. mag., Chilaw, Sept., 1919; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1922; asst. sttlmt. offr., Jan., 1923; addnl. asst. dir., educn., July, 1928; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Jan., 1930.
HURST, Raymond George.—B. 1877; Indian State rly., 1900; E. African rly., 1917; asst. traffic supt., Tanganyika rly., July, 1925.

HUSBAND, Charles Thomas Main, F.S.I., F.R.G.S.—B. 1891; ed.
