Box Hill Grammar Schl., Melbourne; 1st grade survr., F.M.S., 1913; served France, 1915-18; asst. supt., surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ag. supt., Selangor, July-Nov., 1920; Perak, Feb., 1926; Johore, Sept., 1927; supt., surveys, F.M.S. & S.S., July, 1929.

HUSSEY, Eric Robert James.—B. 1886; ed. Repton (Schl.), open exhib., Hertford Coll., Oxford, 1904; B.A., 1908 (lit. hum.), M.A., 1923; Sudan civ. serv. as tutor, Gordon Coll., Khartum, Sept., 1908; dist. commr., 1912; ch. inspr., educn. dept., 1918; educn. mission to Somaliland, 1920; educn. mission to Uganda, 1924; dir., educn., Uganda, 1926; educn. mission to Kenya, 1928; dir. educn., Nigeria, 1929.

HUTCHINGS, Hugh H., I.S.O. (1930)—Inspr. of govt. schls., Turks and Caicos Is., 1898-1906; J.P., 1901; ch. offr., fire brigade, 1901-19; clk. to commr. and inspr. of schls., 1907; asst. commr., Grand Turk, 1908-19; ag. audr., 1908-13; audr., 1913-19; ag. commr. and ag. judge of sup. ct., five occasions, 1911-19; ag. commr. of Cayman Is., 1912-13; commr. Cayman Is., June, 1919; commr., Montserrat, Sept., 1929.

HUTCHINS, Ernest Elwin.—B. 1886; ed. Exeter Schl.; forestry course, Edinburgh Univ., 1903-04; forest dept., S. Africa, 1904; forest demarcator, Kenya, 1909; asst. conservator, forests, Kenya, 1911; E. Africa Mounted Rifles, 1915-6; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa, 1916-21; admin. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

HUTCHINSON, George Stuart Menteth.—B. 1883; ed. United Services Coll., Westward Ho! and Royal Milly. Coll., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., unattd. list, 1902; Indian Army, 1903; capt., 1911; major, 1917; Burma Milly. Pol., Southern Shan States, 1910-14; Indian Expdy. Force, France, 1915; ment. in desps., 1915; invalided, 1917; examining offr., Miny. of Pensions, 1917; regional wounds offr., for Scotland, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; prin., Ist, 1st Oct., 1930.

HUTCHISON, Donald Maxwell Wright, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.I.C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1882; ed. Wellington Coll. and Faraday House, London; asst. elec. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1911; ch. inspr., electy. enact., F.M.S., 1915; dep. ch. engr., electy. bd., P.W.D., Jan., 1925; ag. ch. elec. engr. and inspr., electy. bd., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1926; ch. elec. engr. and inspr., electy. dept., F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ag. dir., elec. dept., June to Dec., 1928.

HUTSON, Sir Eyre, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.M.G. (1911), B.A.—B. 1864; 2nd clk. to gov. Leeward Is., Oct., 1886; asst. priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Nov., 1885; priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Mar., 1887; ditto, at Mauritius, Jan., 1890; at Br. Guiana, July, 1893; Jamaica, 1898; apptd. col. sec. and reglar.-gen., Bermuda, July, 1901; chrmn., schools comm., 1902; chrmn., St. George's harbour comm., 1903; col. sec. Fiji, July, 1908; chrmn., educn. comm., Apr., 1909; represented Fiji and Western Pacific high comm., at wireless tel. conf., Melbourne, Dec., 1909; dep.-gov., May and Oct., 1909, Sept., 1910, Mar., Sept., Oct., 1911, and June, 1912; commr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. gov. of Fiji and high commr. for Western Pacific, Aug., 1915 to May, 1916 and from June to Oct., 1918; gov., B. Honduras, Nov., 1918; gov., Fiji and high commr., W. Pacific, 1925; ret., July, 1929.

HUTTON, Frederick Augustus.—B. 1863; ed. Bedford Public Schl., S. African Coll. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., 1883; LL.B., 18/2; called to the bar, supreme ct., Cape Colony, 1892; prosecutor, special treason ct., 1901-3; judge, supreme ct., assigned to E.D. local divn., 1913; mem., special treason ct., 1915-16; mem. of rebellion enquiry comm., 1916-17; judge, Griqualand West Local Divn., Feb., 1923.

HYAMSON, ALBERT MONTEFIORE, F.R.Hist.S.—B. 1875; ed. Oxford High Schl. and Swansea Gram.Schl.; savings bank dept., G.P.O., London, 1895-98; supplementary estab., secy.'s dept., G.P.O., 1898 to Jan., 1921; dept. of infrn., F.O., Dec., 1917 to Mar., 1918; in charge of secy., miny. of infrn., Apr., 1918 to Nov., 1918; Br. war mission, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; asst. dir., immigrn. and travel, Palestine, Jan., 1921; contrlr. of lab., Dec., 1921; in charge of dept. of immigrn. and travel, Dec., 1923 to Mar., 1924; contrlr., permits, Apr., 1924; ch. immigrn. offr., Oct., 1926; sec., cent. town planning comnr., Sept., 1922; mem., economy comtee., chmn., comtee. on rates of wages for govt. employees, dep. chmn., lab. standing comtee.; author, "A History of the Jews in England," "Palestine—the Rebirth of an Ancient People," "Palestine, Old and New," "British Projects for the Restoration of the Jews," etc.

IEVERS, C.L., L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.T.M. (Lpool.).—Appd. to W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Feb., 1912; Togoland, 5th to 26th Aug., 1914; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1918; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1921.
IMBERT, A. E.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919; ch. regis., natives, Kenya, Jan., 1925.

IM THURN, SIR EVERARD F., K.C.M.G. (1905), K.B.E. (1918), C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892), M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxon; special mag., Pomeron Dist., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1890; govt. agt., N.W. dista., 1890; ag. lst el. clk., C.O., Dec., 1899; col. sec., Ceylon, 1901; admstd. govt., Ceylon, 1902; govt., Fiji, and high comnr., W. Pacific, June, 1904; ret. 1910; rep. Fiji. and W. Pacific at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; ed. of the Guiana scientific jnl. "Timehri," 1882-7; author of "Among the Indians of Guiana," 1883, and papers on the anthropology, natural history, geography, and history of Guiana; in 1884 made first ascent of Mount Roraima in the interior of Br. Guiana.

INGHAM, RAWWOOD, B.A. (Oxon).—Entered S. Sttlmts. civ. serv., cls. V. (passed in Chinese), Dec., 1912; attd. to monopolies dept., Jan., 1913; dep. collr. of land rev., Malacca, Sept., 1915; pol. mag., Malacca (in addn.), Nov., 1915; collr. war tax, Malacca (in addn.), Mar., 1917; collr., war tax, Singapore (tempy.), Mar., 1918; ag. sup. prisons, Malacca (in addn.) Mar., June, 1919; supermyn. offr., cls. IV., S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1919; asst. prot. Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1921.

INGLEDOW, CEDRIC FRANCIS.—B. 1894; on milry. serv., Mar., 1915; cadet, Cheltenham, Aug., 1920; attd. to Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1920; ag. office ass't. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1922; office ass't. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1924; ditto to govt. agt., N. Prov., 1925; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, N'Eliya, Dec., 1927.

INGLES, F. H.—B. 1878; ed. at U.S. Coll., Westward Ho!, Newton Coll., Devon, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A. Cantab., 1902; asst. dist. comnr., S. Nigeria, 6th Oct., 1906; dist. comnr., 2nd grade, 17th Feb., 1913; dist. offr., 1st grade, 20th Oct., 1919; res., 5th Feb., 1922; cls. I., grade I., admstd. serv., 1922; ag. senr. comnr., 31st Oct., 1924.

INGRAMS, WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury; served K.S.L.I., Sept., 1914 to May, 1919; wounded, Belgium, May, 1916; employed, directorate of recruiting, W.O., Dec., 1916; min. of national service, Dec., 1917; min. of lab., demob. and resettlement dept., Nov., 1918; pled. F.Z.S., May, 1919; asst. dist. comnr., Zanzibar, July, 1919; F.R.A.I., 1921; F.R.G.S., 1922; M.R.A.S., 1923; mem., Société d'Anthropologie de Paris, 1923; certif. acad. of internat. law, Hague, 1924; higher standard, Swahili, 1923; ag. dist. comnr., Aug., to Sept., 1923; ag. regis., high ct., offi. assignee and regis., patents, designs and trade marks, Oct., 1923 to Feb., 1924; 2nd asst. sec., Jan., 1925; pvt. sec. to ag. high comnr., Apr., to June, 1925; ditto to Br. res., Nov., 1925 to Aug., 1926 and from Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. pvt. sec. to H.H. the Sultan, Nov., 1926 to June, 1926; elk., prot. coun., Feb. to June, 1926; edtr., Off. Gaz., in 1924, 1925 and 1927; ag. senr. asst. sec., Mar. to Aug., 1926 and from Oct., 1926 to Feb., 1927; ag. asst. ch. sec., Aug. to Sept., 1926 and Mar. to July, 1927; elk., exec. and leg. couns., Aug. to Oct., 1926 and Mar. to July, 1927; mem. Pemba educon. comtee. and teaching of Koran comnr., 1924; mem. museum comtee., 1924 and ag. chmn., Aug., 1926; cls. IV., Order of Brilliant Star, Zanzibar 1927; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1927; chmn., museum reorganisation comtee., 1928; chmn., comsn. of enquiry, med. and health dept., 1928; part author, "Zanzibar; an account of its People, Industries and History" (1924), "Schl. History of Zanzibar" (1925), and "Aya Za Kuraani" (1926); author "Dialects of Zanzibar Sultanate" (1924), "Chronology and Genealogies of Zanzibar Rulers" (1926) and various pamphlets and articles.
INMAN, HOWARD.—B. 1887; B. of T. Certif.; inspr. of weights and measures, G. Coast, 12th June, 1912.

INNES, CECIL BENNETT ROSE.—Clk., chief acctnt., rly., 1890; census, 1891; British Bechuanaland serv.; Gordonia, Apr., 1891; relieving elk., Vryburg, Mar., 1892; elk. to P.M.G., Brit. Bechuanaland, May, 1892; sec.'s office, Apr., 1893; elk., regis. of deeds, Brit. Bechuanaland, Mar., 1894; deeds office, Vryburg, Sept., 1897; ag. regis., 20th July, 1900; elk. in charge, 1st Dec., 1901; ag. asst. magt., Laingburg, June, 1903; sub-comnr., war losses compensation comnr., June, 1903; elk. in charge, deeds office, Kimberley, Nov., 1903; rev. elk. civil comnr., Cape, May, 1907; ch. elk., Sept., 1908; receiver of revenue, East London, Dec., 1910; principle elk., recvr. of revenue, Cape Town, Nov., 1916; ch. elk., recr. of rev., Dec., 1923.

INNES, RT. HON., SIR JAMES ROSE, P.C. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1855; called to the bar of sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Feb., 1878; mem. for Victoria East house of assem., Cape, 1884; subsequently reprtd. Cape div.; atty.gen., 1st Rhodes' ministry, 17th July, 1890, to May, 1893; selected by Imp. govt. to watch proceedings in connection with trial of reform comtee. prisoners at Pretoria, 1896; atty-gen. 4th Sprigg ministry, 1900-2; deleg. for Cape Colony to attend conf. in Lond. on final ct. of appeal, 1901; ch. just. sup. ct., Transvaal, 29th Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appeal, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1910; ch. just. of S. Africa, Oct., 1914; ret., 1st Mar., 1927.

INNESS, WILLIAM JAMES DEACON, C.M.G. (1929), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Ireland).—B. 1877; ed. Exeter Schl. and St. Mary's Hosp., Lond.; house physician, Bethlem Royal Hosp.; med. offr., N. Nigeria, 1905; senr. sany. offr., Nigeria, 1914; asst. dir. of med. serv., Nigeria, 1921; dir., med. and san. services, Sierra Leone, 1924; dir., med. and san. serv., Gold Coast, Mar., 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns.
INNISS, KNOLLS ULRIC ALEXANDER, M.B., Ch. B., Edin.—B. 1882; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, 16th Aug., 1904; dist. med. offr., 1st Aug., 1912.

INSKIPP, PEROT SIDNEY, O.B.E. (Mily.).—Clt., admnstr.'s off., S. Rhodesia, 1891; ag. sec., 1892; sec. to admnstr., 1893; under sec., 1897; joined London staff, B.S.A. Co., 1900; dir., B.S.A. Co., Apr., 1919; gen. man., B.S.A. Co., S. Africa, June, 1922; mem. of pioneer corps (occupation of Mashonaland), 1890; also served with the Mashonaland horse, Matabele rebellion, 1896 (medal), and Rhodesia field force, 1900 (medal and 2 clasps); joined R.A.S.C., 1914; O.C., R.A.S.C., Harwich dist., 1914-17; S.S.O., 48th divn., France, Flanders, Italy, 1917-19; Croce di Guerra, Italy; ment. in desps.

IRBY, G. N.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Oxford and Bucks. L.I., May, 1915; invalided with rank of capt., Jan., 1918; asst. priv. sec. (apptmts.) to Lord Milner, July, 1920; to Mr. Churchill, Feb., 1921; the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. J. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.
IRELAND, FREDERICK JOSEPH.—B. 1903; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 30th Sept., 1920.

IRVINE, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.Sc. Ag., Fellow, Linnean Soc. (1927).—B. 1898; ed. Royal Grammar Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; war serv., Gordon Highlanders, France and Belgium, 1916-19; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918, R.S.c., agr., Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1920; teacher of agr. and biology, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

IRVINE, ROBERT, M.C. (1917)—B. 1894; ed. Dunfermline High Sch.; bd. of agr., Scotland, 1913; served in France, 1915-18; capt., Lancs. Fusiliers, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; govt., rice agt., Taiping, Jan., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, Mar., 1920; ag. 3rd asst., sec., Fed. sec., May, 1920; ag. 4th ditto July, 1920; pvtc. sec. to ch. sec. to govt., Mar., 1921; offr. cls. V., July, 1921; asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Apr., 1922; ag. 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1925; offr., cls. IV., July, 1925; ag. 2nd asst. sec. (A) S.S., May, 1926; ag. 1st asst. sec. (B) S.S. and clk. of couns., Mar., 1928; dist. offr., Tampin, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. III., July, 1930.

IRVINE, HON. SIR WM. HILL, K.C.M.G. (1914), LL.D., K.C.—B. 1868; called to the bar, Victoria, 1884; premier of Victoria, 1902-4; mem. H. of R., C. of A., since 1906; atty.-gen., C. of A., June, 1913 to Sept. 1914; chief just., Victoria, 5th Apr., 1918; lieut.-gov., Victoria, 11th May, 1918.

IRVING, FRANCOIS ERNEST.—B. 1880; ed. at St. Andrew's Coll. and Marist Bros. Sch., S. Africa; served in Boer War, 1901-2; German S.W. Africa, with Imp. Light Horse, 1914-15; German E. Africa, 1916; seconded to admin. of Mafia Is., as finan. offr., Nov., 1916, to May, 1919; asst. cust. dept., Zanzibar, Oct., 1919; ag. asst. ch. of cust. for various periods, 1920-22; asst. ch. of cust., Dec., 1922; ag. ch. of cust., for various periods, 1924-27, 1929 and 1930.
IRVING, HUGH CHAUFURD.—Asst. dist. commr., Somaliland Prot., June, 1914.

IRWIN, 1ST BARON (cr. 1925), HON. EDWARD FREDERICK LINDLEY WOOD.—B. 1881; ed. Eton and Christ Church and All Souls, Oxford (M.A. and fellow); major, Yorkshire Dragons; M.P., Ripon divn., Yorks, 1910-25; under sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1921; min. of educn., Oct., 1922; viceroy of India, 1926.

IRWIN, HENRY MARK, B.A.—B. 1835; ed. Haileybury and Selwyn Coll.; Cambridge exhibitioner; Haileybury exhibitioner; hona. clas. tripos, 1908; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; attached to secretariat, July to Dec., 1911; priv. sec. to ag. gov., C. L. Temple, Esq., C.M.G., June, 1912.
IRWIN, ROBERT EDWARD.—B. 1899; ed. at Christ's Hosp.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1925; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1925; asst. audr. Gold Coast, 1929.

ISAACS, RT. HON. SIR ISAAC ALFRED, P.C. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1856; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., 1899; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr. gen., 1893; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. of Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first Federal parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 1906-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 1906; ag. ch. just., 1925 and 1927; ch. just., Mar., 1930; gov. gen. of the C'wealth of Australia, Jan., 1931.

ISAACS, FRANK NOEL.—B. 1871; Jamaica civ. serv., 1890; held appts. in various dep'ts., 1891-1907; clk. of wks. and acctnt., new pub. bldgs., 1909; sec., bd. of supervision, 1910; sec. malaria comm., 1910-12; exec. offr., malaria comm., 1910-12; drought and hurricane relief offr., 1912-17; ag. prot., immigrts., 1914; man. Rio Cobro house, 1917; commr., parish of St. Catharine, 1921; sec., child welfare comm., 1921.

ISHERWOOD, ALBERT ARTHUR MANGNALL, O.B.E. (1926), M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1889; ed. St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. aspt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 9th July, 1913; loaned to polit. dept., N. Nigeria, 1915-17; polit. offr., Tanganyika adminst., 8th Oct., 1917; ag. snr. commr., Feb., 1923 to Jan., 1924; dep. dir., educn., Sept., 1924; ag. dir., educn., Oct., 1924 to Mar., 1925; ditto and mem., exec. coun., Mar. to Nov., 1927 and Aug., 1929; Swahili examr.; mem., exec. and leg. coun., Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1930.

ISLINGTON, 1ST BARON (cr. 1910), RT. HON. SIR JOHN POYNDER DICKSON-POYNDER, 6TH BART., P.C. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1913); K.C.M.G. (1911); D.S.O. (1900); J.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Harrow and Christ Church, Oxford; major and hon. col., Wilts Yeomanry; served in S. Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); late lieut. 3rd Royal Scots; mem. L.C.C., 1898-1904; M.P., Chippenham, Wilts, 1892-1910; gov., New Zealand, 1910-1912; chmn. of Indian civ. ser. comm., 1912; partly. under sec. of state for the colonies, 1914; partly. under sec. for India, 1915.
IVES, WILLIAM CARLOS, B.C.L.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schl., Pincher Creek, Alta., Coaticook, Queb., and McGill Univ.; puisne judge, sup. ct., Alta., 1914.

IVEY, LIEUT. COMMDR. A. V. P., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1894; Royal Navy, 1915; asst. lieut., R.N.R., 1917; lieut. com-mdr., 1927; Cameroons expedy. force, 1915-16; Harwich force destroyers, 1916-17; commanded Torpedo boat, 1918; commanded H.M. river gunboat "Robin" on West River, China, 1919; marine offr., Nigeria, 1920; harb. mast., Port Harcourt, 1926; prin. marine offr., 1930. GODFREY WALLACE.—B. 1891; ed. n.; B.S.A. Co., 1904-14; called to bar, emple, June, 1914; enlisted, Aug., slided; admstve. offr., Nigeria, Jan., istve. serv., Apr., 1930; dep. comsnr., J.

HENRY WALTER, M.B.E. (1930), D.Sc., 1891; ed. Univ. Coll., Cork (scholar); ., 1911; B.Sc. (biol.), 1912; agr. inspr., 914; agr. instr., 1915; ec. botanist, M.S., 1919; mem., comtese. on rubber 1923; D.Sc. by research, 1925; rep. of ac. Sc. Cong., Japan, 1926 and in Java, sm., coconut and oil palm research 1929; hon. sec., rice cult. comtese., 1930; publins. on rice, coconuts, oil ion, rubber, etc.

IN, S., M.C.—Actnt., repat. dept., Aug., 1902; rev. olk., law dept., Nov., civ. comsnr. off., Pretoria, Nov., rev. dept., Mar., 1905; prin. elk., prin. olk., inland rev. dept., Apr., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; survr., dept., Apr., 1918; ag. dep. comsnr., Sept., 1919; ch. elk., estabmsts. Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1920; sec. comsnr., Oct., 1921; ret., Oct., 1926; age of Nations, Oct., 1926.

LAN, EDWARD CLARENCE.—Ed. Har., Barbados; ent. Middle Temple, 1889; rizeman, real property, 1890; lecture equity, 1891; called to bar, 1892; at Barbados bar; elec. to H. of A., 1904-1913; mem., exec. comtese., 1909-1, mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1913; ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1921; pres., 1923; ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., 1926-1, just., Barbados, Aug.-Dec., 1929.

SON, EDWARD ST. JOHN, O.B.E. (1918) onthurst and Beaumont Colleges and College, Oxford; B.A., 1908; called to innerTemple, 1910; legal adviser, Gambia, 12; atty-gen., Nyasaland, Oct., 1918; high ct., July, 1920; pres. of lands 1920; pres., finance comsnr., 1923;, Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1924;, Ceylon, May, 1929.

SON, MAJOR FRANCIS WALTER FITTON, (1928), D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1881; ed. coll.; entd. R.A., 1899; served in S. 901-2; seconded to W.A.F.F., 30th July, ist. comsnr., Northern Territories, G. st Jan., 1907; ast. col. sec., G. Coast, r., 1911; provincial comsnr., G. Coast, r., 1915; services lent to W.O., Mar., Apr., 1919; served in France, May, 1915, 1918; ment. in despe. 3 times; O.C. forces, and senr. polit. offr., Togoland, 19; represented govt., in final hndng done and that portion of Togoland ceded by the Franco-British declrn. of 10th 19; dep. ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 1928; ch. N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1930.
JACKSON, GILDAIR.—B. 1901; ed. Edgeborough Schl., Guildford and Westminster; ast. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 12th Nov., 1924.

JACKSON, ROBERT BEST, M.B., D.P.H., T.C.D., L.M. Rotunda Hospital, Dublin, certif., London Schl. Trop. Med., math. asst., schl., and junr. modr., Trinity Coll., Dublin, senr. exh. and catechatical prizeman.—Chief med. offr., Indian Mining Asso., Jharia and Katras Coalfields, 1908-14; R.A.M.C., 1914-17; med. offr., Gen. Milly Hosp., Edmonton, 1918; med. offr., F.M.S., 20th Nov., 1920; ag. health offr., Negri Sembilan, 1921.

JACKSON, WILFRED EDWARD FRANCIS, C.M.G.—B. 1883; ed. Stonyhurst Coll. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (classical scholar), B.A. 1906; personal sec. to gov., Trinidad, 1906; priv. sec. to gov. and olk to exec. coun., Bahamas, Sept., 1906, to Oct., 1907; ast. collr., Uganda, Nov., 1907; ag. dist. comsnr., Toro, July, 1911 to Apr., 1912; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1912; ag. ast. chief sec., July-Dec., 1912; 1st ast. sec., Dec., 1912; ag. ast. chief sec., Aug., 1913 to Feb., 1914, May to Nov., 1914 and from Apr., 1915; col. sec., Bermuda, 19th Feb., 1916; col. sec., Barbados, 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, Feb., 1926; ag. gov., Apr. to Nov., 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1929; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Mauritius, Mar., 1930.

JAMAICA, LORD BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. G. C. DE CARTERET, M.A., D.D.—Curate of St. Dunstan's, Canterbury, 1889; Holy Trinity, Tulse Hill, London, 1894; St. Mary, Cheltenham, 1896; vicar of St. Paul, Southwark, 1897; Christ Church, East Greenwich, 1901; rural dean of Greenwich, 1913; ast. bishop of Jamaica, 1913; bishop of Jamaica, 1916.

JAMES, SIR FREDERICK SETON, K.C.M.G. (1929), K.B.E.(1922), C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1870; ed. at Charterhouse and abroad; ast. dist. comsnr., Niger Coast Prot., 1896; in charge at Opobo and Akwete, 1896-7; with peaceful mission to Bende in 1897; trav. comsnr., 1897; serv. as polit. offr. in expdn. ast. Ekuri;, Cross River, 1898; settled disputes in Qua country after cent. div. expdn., 1899; reed. thanks of S. of S. divsnl. comsnr., 1901; intell. and polit. offr. with Aro field force, 1901-2 (desps. medal); reed. thanks of dir.-gen. of intell. and S. of S. in connection with map of Aro operations; ag. sec. and dep. high comsnr., 1905; with Kwale patrol, Nov., 1906 (clasp); prov. comsnr. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns. of S. Nigeria on amalgamation, 1906; ag. col. sec. from Mar., 1907; dep. gov., Apr. and Sept., 1907; ag. col. sec. and deputy gov. on various occasions from 1908 to 1912; ag. gov., Feb. to Sept., 1912; astptd. adminstr. on amalgamation of N. and S. Nigeria, Jan., 1914; col. sec., Straits Settmts., June, 1916; seconded for spl. services as food contr., S. Stiltns. and Malay States, Dec., 1918; O.A.G. S. Stiltns. and high comsnr., Malay States, Aug., 1919; col. sec. in addition, May, 1919; O.A.G., Apr., 1922; col. sec., Feb., 1920. ag. oh. sec., F.M.S., June-Oct., 1920; col. sec., May, 1921; gov., Windward Is., 1924-1930.

JAMES, HERBERT BASH.—Ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner), 2nd class math. mods., 1908; 3rd class final honour schl. nat. science, 1910; ast. res., Northern Nigeria, 30th Nov., 1910; res., Dr.C., 1921.

JAMES, JOSEPH EDWARD, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schls. and Mico Coll., Antigua; ag. educnl-offr., Antigua, Nov., 1901; olk., educn. dept. and educnl. offr., Antigua, Apr., 1904; schlmaet, St. John's Training Schol., Antigua, Feb., 1913, to June, 1917; sub-inspr. schl., Antigua, Apr., 1914; sub-inspr. schl., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1919; local inspr., schl., Mar., 1927; ag. fedl. inspr., schl., July, 1928 to Jan., 1929.
JAMES, MARSHALL NICOL.—B. 1876; served under the condominium govt., New Hebrides until Mar., 1922; govt. agt., New Hebrides, Mar., 1922.

JAMES, SIR WALTER HARTWELL, KT. BACH. (1907).—Ed. in Perth at state and high schls.; admitted as barrister and solicitor, 1888; member for East Perth, 1894; represented W. Aust. at fed. conven., 1897-8; hon. minister, 1901; K.C., 1902; premier and atty.-gen., 1902; agt.-gen. for W.A., 1904-1906.

JAMISON, ROBERT, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Ireland), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1880; asst. med. offr., Hatikulu, Swaziland, 1910-13; med. offr., Swaziland, 1913; served, S.W. Africa campaign, 1914-15; prin.med.offr., 1918

JANSEN, HON. ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., LL.B., M.L.A.—B. 1881; ed. Ladysmith Govt. Schl. and Durban High Schl.; leader of Nationalist party in Natal; mem., Natal Univ. Coll. Coun.; ohmn., Saamwerk Unie van Natalse Vereeniging (the first body in S.A. to prescribe exams. in Afrikaans) for 7 years; rep. Vryheid in parl., since 1921; foundation mem. of Suid Afrikaanse Akademie vir Taal, lettere en kuns; elec. speaker, Union H. of A., July, 1924; min. of native affrs., June, 1929.
JANSZ, REGINALD BELTRAN.—B. 1878; cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1928; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1928.

JANSZ, HERBERT ERIC.—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; att. Kandy kach., Sept., 1914; att., Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Batticaloa, in add., Aug., 1915; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Batticaloa, Sept., 1915; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Jan., 1917; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1918; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1921; addnl. dist. judge, Arivawella, in addn., Oct., 1927; asst. sttlmt. offr., Sept., 1928; ag. pol. mag., Colombo, Apr. to June, 1930.
JANSZ, JONATHAN CHARLES.—B. 1871; cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., Sept., 1928; extra office asst., Galle kach., Sept., 1928; ditto to col. sec., Oct., 1929.

JARDINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. classical trip., 1910; B.A., 1910; M.A., 1914; chief sec.'s off., Cyprus, 1910; pvt. sec. to ag. high commr., July to Oct., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., 1912; ag. ch. asst. sec. on several occasions, 1912-16; passed in modern Greek, 1912; sec. to the admtn., Somaliland, 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu (3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia), 1917; in charge H.Q. services, Somaliland Expedy. Force, 1920 (A.G.S. med. and ment. in des.) ; senr. asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., July to Nov., 1925 and June to Aug., 1926; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1927; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1927 and Apr. to Oct., 1928; ch. sec., Jan., 1929; ag. gov., Jan. to Mar., 1929; gov't. dep. on several occasions since 1927; joint-editor "Hand book of Cyprus," 1913 and 1919, and author of "Mad Mullah of Somaliland," 1923.

JARDINE, ROBERT FRIER, C.M.G. (1928), O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1894; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; served European War in Gallipoli, Egypt and Mesopotamia; polit. offr., Mesopotamia, 1917; asst. divnl. adviser, Mosul prov., under Iraq govt., 1921; admstr. inspr., Mosul prov., 1923; on sp. duty in Europe in connec. with Mosul question, 1925; admstr. inspr., Sulaimani, 1925; do., Mosul prov., 1925; ditto, Basra, 1928; author "Grammar of Bahdinan Kurmanji (Kurdish)," 1922.

JARRETT, JAMES HENRY.—B. 1895; ed. Lancing Coll.; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; H.A.C., 1914; served European War, 1914-19; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 1919; seconded, judl. dept. as mag., 1922; mag., 1924; asst. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. solr. gen., May, 1927 to Jan., 1928; crown coun., 1927; atty.-gen., Grenada, 1929; asst. admstr. and ag. ch. jun., St. Vincent, from May, 1930.

JARRETT, NORMAN ROWSTONE.—B. 1889; ed. Highgate and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 3rd cls. class. mods., 1910, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1912; B.A., 1912; cadet, F.M.S., 1912; passed final exam. in Tamil, Apr., 1914; ag. asst. contrlr. of lab., Apr., 1914; passed exam. in law, July, 1914; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, Apr., 1915; ag. dist. offr., Klang, in admn., Nov., 1917; ag. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Klang, Sept., 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Apr., 1925; ag. asst. adviser, Kelantan, Dec., 1926; dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1929; offr., cls. II, 1930; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1930.

JARVIS, CYRIL CHESTER.—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; Rhodes school, 1904; Wadham Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hon. juris.); LL.B., Cape Univ.; sec., ch. just., Cape Colony, 1907; admitted Cape bar, 1910; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.F.S.; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Cape Prov., 1921; crown pros., Johannesburg, 1926.

JAYARATNE, MERENNA FRANCIS DE SILVA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd. Colombo kach., Mar., 1927; office asst., Matale kach., Jan., 1928; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1930.

JEFFERY, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1924), F.S.A.—Scholarship, R. Coll. of Art and Science, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.I.B.A., 1892; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem and the East, 1892; hon. corres. mem. of Imperial Institute of Arch. of Russia, 1897; local sec. for Cyprus Society of Antiquaries; inspr. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, 1903; relinquished appt. July, 1919; reappointed curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, Feb., 1921; author of "Summary of Architectural Monuments of Cyprus," "Cyprus under an English King," "Historic Monuments of Cyprus," and of several papers on the medieval archaeology of Cyprus and Jerusalem, edn., new edition (1929) of "An attempt at a Bibliography of Cyprus."

JEFFREY, PAY LINTUT.-COMMDR., A., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1892; ed. Glas. Univ.; R.N., 1914-19; probationer, N. Rhodesia, 1919; J.P., 1920; clk. of ct. and under-sheriff, Batche dist., 1920; asst. nat. commr., 1921; nat. commr., 1926; passed higher Chinyanga; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory 1927; asst. secy., Jan., 1930.

JEFFRIES, CHARLES J., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1896; ed. Malvern Coll.; clas. demy., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1914; 2nd lieut., 3rd Wiltsire Regt., Sept., 1915; served in France, 1916; lieut., 1917; invalided from army, Nov., 1917; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 27th Dec., 1917; appld. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., 16th July,

JENKINS, REGINALD FREDERICK.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 30th Nov., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; seconded to Empire Marketing bd., 1926; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Feb., 1928.
JENKINS, SIDNEY THOMAS.—B. 1883; ed. St. Gabriel's, Bristol; large and small scale draughtsman, cadastral and topographical surrv.; surrv. draughtsman, Zanzibar, 1926.

JENKS, J. C. A.—Ed. Bristol Gram. Schl. and Angers, France; awarded Croix-de-Guerre (Palm) 1917; Order of Saint Stanislaus with Swords (2nd cls.), and med. in desps., 1919; colonial audit serv., 1920; ast. audr., Kenya Colony, Apr., 1920; ast. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; senr. ast. audr. in charge, Jan., 1926; mem., retrenchment and rev. comtee., Zanzibar, 1927; Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar 5th cls., 1928; senr. ast. audr. in charge, Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbrs., 1929.

JENNER, FRANK.—B. 1878; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, Bechuanaland campaign, 1896-97; S. African war, 1899-1901; sub-inspr., Basuto-land Mounted Pol., 1901; inspr., 1910; supt., leper stltm., 1914; oomnr., 1920.
JENNINGS, F. G.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1919.
JENYNS, ROGER SOAME.—B. 1904; ed. Eton and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; cadet Hong Kong, Dec., 1926; ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1929.

JEPSON, ALWYN HALL.—B. 1878; ed. Mechanics Inst., Hyde; imperial postal telegraph service, 1893; S. African War, 1900-2 (medal and 5 bars); military telegraphs, Ceylon, 1903-6; acted as supt., military telegraphs, and instr. in telegraphy and telephony, Columbo; ag. overseer, Manchester, 1907; postmaster, Nyasaland, 1907; supt., telephones, 1911; offr.-in-charge posts and telegraphs, Nyasaland Field Force, 1914-17 (1914 Star, etc.); lieut., 1915; ast. postmaster-gen., 1915; ag. postmaster-gen., Apr. to June, 1917, Apr. to Dec., 1919 and from Mar., 1922 to Feb., 1923; postm. gen., Feb., 1923.

JEPSON, FRANK PRICE.—B. 1885; M.A., Pembroke Coll., Camb.; exhib., medallist, and dip. in agri. (hons. in entomology), Wye Agric. Coll., Kent, 1906; trop. disease research fund student in med. entomology, Camb. Univ., 1907; assisted in investig. by L.G.B. on flies as carriers of infection, 1908 and 1909; govt. entomologist, Fiji, 1909; offr. visits to Hawaii, 1911, Samoa, 1912, Java, 1913; ast. entomologist, Ceylon, 1919; ag. entomologist in addn., July, 1923 to Mar., 1924 and Oct. to Nov., 1925; del. to Imp. Agr. Research Conf., Lond., 1927; on sp. serv., U.S.A. (termite investig.), 1928.
JERRAM, JAMES HAVELOCK.—B. 1879; ed. Thames High Schl. and Auckland Gram. Schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. state fire insurance office, 1905; acct. 1906; dep. gen. man., 1908; gen. man., 1923.

JERVOISE, RICHARD SOMERVELL.—B. 1887; ed. Bradfield Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (hist. demy); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Mar., 1913; ag. ast. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1913; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1913; ag. ast. dist. offr., Brusa, Oct., 1914; ast. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Pasir Puteh, July, 1920; ag. judge, high ct., Kelantan, 1921; dist. offr., Tampin, June, 1922; chmn., sany. bd., Larut, Jan., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Upper Perak, Apr., 1924; chmn., sany. bd., Larut, Oct., 1924; 1st mag. and regt., sup. ct. Johore Bharu, Oct., 1924; ag. ast. audr.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1925; ch. ass't dist. offr., Kinta, Aug., 1925; dist. offr., Krian, joint examr., Malay, Perak, Oct., 1927; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1928; ag. ass't treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Sept., 1928; offr., els. II, Nov., 1928; ag. valuer-gen., F.M.S. and town planning admtr., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1929; mag., Ipoh, Nov., 1929.
JEWELL, MORTON, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899, conf. clk., 1911; deptl. ass't, 1st Apr., 1922.

JEWELL, NORMAN PARSONS, O.B.E. (1929)—M.C. (1917); M.D.; B.Ch.; B.A.O., Trin. Coll., Dub.; L.M., Rotunda, Dub.; B.A. (Moderator), Trin. Coll., Dub.; late anesthetist, Duns Hosp., Dub.; ass't med. offr., Seychelles, 1910; J.P. and chmn. local bds. of health, Praslin and La Digue; med. supt., leper asylum; med. offr., E.A.P., 1915; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Feb., 1921; ag. surg. specialist, European hosp., Nairobi, 1926.

JOBLING, GEOFFREY LIONEL.—B. 1889; ed. Rowen Schl., Brisbane; called to bar, Jan., 1926 (Gra's Inn); served German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; R.A.F., 1918-19; law dept., B.S.A. Co., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1915; ass't regtr., high ct., N. Rhodesia; ass't offl. recr. in bankcy., ass't admin. gen., ass't regtr., lands and deeds, ass't regtr. births, deaths and marriages, ass't regtr. companies, ass't custodian enemy property, ass't contr. local clearing office, ass't regtr., high ct., etc., Uganda, June, 1927; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1929.

JOHNS, FRED, F.J.I.—B. 1868; ed. Cornwall, England; on Adelaide newspaper literary staff from 1885 to 1914; leader of the first offl. Hansard staff, S. Australian parlmt. since July, 1914; author of "John's Notable Australians" (1906 and 1908), and of "Australia's Prominent People," "Who's Who in Australia" (seven editions), and "A Journalist's Jottings"; sec. of Adelaide branch of Royal Society of St. George, and hon. sec., Matthew Flinders' National Statue, City of Adelaide; also hon. sec., Princess Mary wedding gift fund, S. Australia.

JOHNSON, CHARLES WALTER TREVOR.—B. 1893; Fiji serv., 1910; col. sec's office, 1915; 1st cls. clk. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., 1920; ag. pte. sec. to ag. gov. on three occasions; seconded for serv. in W. Pacific high coms. as dist. offr., Fanning Island, Feb.-Nov., 1922; ag. ass't col. sec., 1924; ch. clk., secretariat, 1927; ag. prov. and dist. comsrs., June-Sept., 1928; ag. ass't col. sec., Nov., 1929; ass't col. sec., 1931.
JOHNSON, D. O.—B. 1873; entd. Bahamas civ. ser., 1904; Out Island comsrs. (3rd div.), 1909; 2nd div., 1910; 1st div., 1914; senr. comsrs., 1928.
JOHNSON, E. T.—Res. mag., E. Africa Prot., 1915; senr. res. mag., Kenya, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, 1924 and 1925; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1929.
JOHNSON, ELLEN M., M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1873; ass't clk., post office, 11th Sept., 1914; ch. clk., 1st Jan., 1924; ag. postmr. in 1926 and 1930; ass't postmr., June, 1926.

JOHNSON, FREDERICK ENGLAND, A.F.I.A. (Australia).—B. 1878; served in S. African war, 1899-1901 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); inspr. of native labourers, British residency, New Hebrides, 1908; in charge of acc'ts. of British residency, 1909; ag. comdt. of British div. of native constab., 1908, 1910, 1912, and from 1914, in addition to other duties; is also a dep. comsrs. of the high comsrs.'s ct. for the W. Pacific, and ag. regis. of high comsrs.'s ct., in the New Hebrides; treas. and collr. of cust., Br. Solomon Is., Nov., 1919; offr. mem., advisory coun., 1927.

JOHNSON, CAPT. GEORGE BENJAMIN, M.R.S.T.—B. 1884; ed. Ryder's Schl., Devonport and Cheltenham Training Coll. (dipl. educm.); Leicestershire Regt. and gen. staff, 1915-19; res. of offrs., T.A.; ag. vice-consul, Le Havre, 1920; headmnr., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1920; ditto and inspr., schls., 1925; ag. dir., educn. in 1920-23, 1925-26, 1928 and 1930; ch. inspr. schls., 1928.

JOHNSON, GEORGE CUNNINGHAM.—B. 1875; 2nd clk., treas., St. Kitts, Apr., 1894; 2nd clk. admstr.'s office and clk. of legis. coun., Mar., 1896; clk. to admstr. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1901; treas. and comptr., cust., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; ag. admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; offr. mem., exec. and legis. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis and offr. mem., Fed. exec. coun.; censor, St. Kitts during the war; is a J.P. for St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is. and admstrd. govt., Antigua, June, 1925; ag. comsrs., Montserrat, Feb., 1927.
JOHNSON, JOHN, M.I.M. AND C.E.—Ass't engrnr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, 1913; att'd., R.W.A.F.F., Cameroons Expdn., 1914-16; senr. exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927.

JOHNSON, JOHN COLEMAN DE GRAFT.—B. 1884; ed. Wesleyan Coll. Schl., Cape Coast; 5th grade clk., Ashanti, 1907; 4th grade clk., police dept., 1912; ass't inspr. W. and M., 1914; ass't sec. for native affrs., 23rd Mar., 1920; mem., Accra town coun., Jan., 1928.
JOHNSON, HON. SIR WILLIAM ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1862; speaker of the H. of R., C. of A., 1913-14 and 1917-23.

JOHNSON, WILLIAM JOSEPH, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1892; serv. in I.E.F., E. Africa, 1914-17; mem. in depts., 1917; transf'd to depot, 11th Mahars, India, 1917; offr. in charge, clearing house, office of field contr. of mil'y. acc'ts., R.E.F., Cairo, 1918; major, ch. acc'ts. offr., O.K.T. admstr., Palestine, 1919; treas., Palestine civ. admtn., 1st July, 1920; dep. treas. on abolition of post of finan. sec., May, 1922; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and advisory couns., June to Sept., 1923, June to Sept., 1924, Jan., Apr. and May, 1925, May to Sept., 1926 and Jan. to June, 1928; mem., comttee, on pub. security forces, 1923; mem., comttee, on establishment of permanent and pensionable cadre for gen. clerical serv., 1925; mem., road bd., 1926; mem., rly. bd., 1928; currenry offr., Jan.-June, 1928.

JOHNSTON, ALEXANDER.—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schl., and St. Francois Xavier Coll., Antigonish, Nova Scotia; entd. local legis. of Nova Scotia, April, 1897, as repres. for Cape Breton Co., Nova Scotia; reeig., 1900; elec. to H. of C., 1900; re-elec., 1904; dep. min. marine and fisheries of Canada, June, 1910.
JOHNSTON, H. LINDSAY.—Govt. statist., S. Australia, 1916.

JOHNSTON, JOHN ROOKE.—B. 1898; ed. St. Ninians, Moffat and St. Bees Schla. and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, 23rd Dec., 1917; seconded to 2/1st K.A.R., 23rd Dec., 1920; adminis. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Sept., 1923.

JOHNSTON, SIR REGINALD FLEMING, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.M.G. (1928), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; M.A., Magdalen Coll., Oxford (1901) Gray prizeman, Edin. Univ., 1924; prov. acc. Lord Rector's essay, Edin.; hons. in English lit., mod. hist. and constitutional law, Edin.; exhibr., Magd. Coll., Oxford, 1894; ment., hon. causa Sta. hope Essay, 1898; B.A., 1898; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; ag. clk. of couns., 1899; privte. sec. to gov., 1900-01; ag. astt. ool. sec., 1900-04; J.P., June, 1902; sec. to govt., Weihaiwei, 1904; list. offr. and mag., ditto, 1906; seconded for serv. as tutor to Manchu Emperor (ex-Emperor of China), 1919-26; recd. from Emperor button of highest offl. grade and sable robe, 1922; warden, Summer Palace, Peking, 1924; brought Emperor into safety in legation quarter, Peking, Nov., 1924; sec., Br. China indemnity delegation, 1926; oomnr., Weihaiwei, 1927-30.

JOHNSTONE, ANGUS COLIN DUNGAN.—B. 1889; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and in Germany; passed Army entrance exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1908; higher grade staff, G. Cent. Rly., 1909-12; served, Balkan War, with Br. Red Cross Unit attd. Turkish Forces, 1912-13; lieut., 5th (Res.) Batt. West Yorks Regt. (T.), Nov., 1914 to Apr., 1915; astt. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, 10th Sept., 1913; lieut., W.A.F.F., and attd. Gold Coast Regt., 1915; seconded Br. Forces, Togoland, May, 1915; offr., cls. II, polit. serv., Jan., 1920; dist. comnr., Ashanti Akim, Sept., 1922.

JOHNSTONE, BANNER CARRUTHERS, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1882; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Cantab.); passed R.G.S. survey course, 1908; astt. supt., surveys, Ceylon, Oct., 1909; astt. collr. (afterwards astt. dist. comnr.), Zanzibar Prot., Apr., 1913; ag. 1st astt. sec. and clk. of coun., Nov., 1914 to June, 1915; ch. land attltud. offr., Jan., 1916; seconded for active serv., in German E. Africa, Aug., 1916; on mil. serv., France, Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; ag. dist. comnr., Pemba, July, 1919 to Apr., 1920; dist. comnr., Pemba, Nov., 1921; ag. senr. comnr., Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1926; 3rd cls. Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1920; ag. senr. comnr. from Oct., 1926 to Apr., 1927; mem., leg. coun.; prov. comnr., 1929.
JOHNSTONE, JAMES MONTAGUE CHOLMELEY.—Knt. prison dept. (England and Wales), Aug., 1921; dep. astt. supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1924; astt. supt., 1927.

JOHNSTONE, JOHN WILKIE.—B. 1884; solr., England, 1906; astt. polit. offr., G.E.A. (Tanganika Territory), Nov., 1918; ag. mag., Apr., 1919 to Feb., 1921; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; res. mag., Apr., 1929.

JOHNSTONE, SIR ROBERT STEWART KT. BACH. (1915).—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in history and political science), 1889; LL.B., 1894; M.A., 1895; Holt scholar, Gray's Inn, 1895; called to the Irish bar 1886; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut., 3rd (Militia) Batt. Manchester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. comnr., Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary comm., 1890; stip. and circuit mag. and judge of ct. of com. pleas., Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1902; ch. just., Grenada, 1909; ret., 1914.

JOMARON, ADOLPHE CHARLES.—B. 1893; ed. at University Coll. Schi. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (class. schi.)—B.A., 1920; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914; lieut., 1915; capt., 1917; ag. major, 1918; wounded, 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920, attd. to astt. advisor's office, Muar, Johore; 2nd astt. advisor, Muar, Johore, Jan., 1922; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., cls. V., June, 1922; seconded for serv. in Kelantan as ag. dist. offr., Pasir Puteh, July, 1923; ag. supt., lands, Kelantan, astt. state audr., ohmn., conservancy bd., Khota Bharu and coroner, June, 1924; offr., cls. IV., June, 1925; 3rd astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; ag. temp. dist. offr., Larut and regiar, titles, Perak, Nov., 1927; sec. to rivers comnr. in adrn., Dec., 1927; ag. 2nd astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., 1929.

JONES, CHARLES BENJAMIN.—B. 1882; 6th cls. clk., treasy dept., S. Nigeria, 1905; 6th cls. clk., 1906; 4th cls. clk., div. comnr.'s off., Bonny, 1906; prov. comnrn.'s off., E. Prov., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., Jan., 1907; head clk., sol. gen.'s off., E. Prov., Sept., 1909; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1912.

JONES, CHARLES ERNEST.—B. 1892; B.A., B.Sc., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. to Kegalla kach., Dec., 1914; Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1915; Kegalla kach., Jan., 1916; censor's off., Apr., 1916; on milly duty, 1918; returned to censor's off., June, 1919; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1919; sec., retrenchment coman., Jan., 1922; addnl. astt. dir., educn., Dec., 1923; dist. judge, N'Ellya, Apr., 1925; astt. govt. ast., Mannar, Nov., 1925; astt. col. treas., Sept., 1928; astt. govt. ast., Matale, June, 1929.

JONES, G. A.—B. 1889; appt., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907; astt. sec. to Oversea Prize Disposal Comtee., Mar., 1917; ag. sec. to ditto, Jan., 1918; promoted, under O.in-C. of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 14th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

JONES, GWILYMN ARTHUR.—Agric. diploma, Univ. Coll., Bangor, N. Wales, with 1st cls. in agric.; awarded Wm. Griffith prize, 1906-8; holder of certif. in forestry; ag. agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schi., 1909; astt. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, Aug., 1909; in charge of agric. schi., Dominica, Aug., 1909 to Jan., 1910; astt. sec. to permanent exhibtn. comtee., 1909; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 1913; chemist, agric. dept., Dominica, Apr., 1915, and a director of the Victoria museum, 1915.
JONES, LLANELLYN A. F.—Astt. dist. comnr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; junr. astt. sec., Feb., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., 1915; dist. comnr., June, 1916; ag. senr. comnr., Kenya, 1926 and 1928.

JONES, MALCOLM LUDLOW, O.B.E. (1919).—Served at Bd. of Trade (Labour Dept.), 1897-1901; chief clk., Emigrants' Information Office, 1901; visited Canada on behalf of E.I.O., 1907; chief clk., Oversea Settmt. Office, 1919.

JONES, PERCY SYDNEY TWENTYMAN.—B. 1876; ed. at Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; White prize for classics; Archbishop's Greek testament prize; B.A., Cape, 1896; LL.B., Cape, 1898; called to bar, Aug., 1898; took silk, 1924; edited "Liquor Laws of Cape," and jointly with H. O. Buckle "Magistrates' Court Practice of S.A."; judge, Aug., 1926.
JONES, ROBERT NOBLE, C.B.E. (1928).—B. 1864; barrister and solr., N.Z., 1890; judge, native land ct. and dist. land regr., 1903; chief judge, native land ct., 1919; under sec., native dept., 1922.

JONES, SAMUEL BENJAMIN, M.B.E. (1924), M.A. (Durh.), B.A. (Lond.), Inter. LL.B. Exam. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glasg.)—B. 1874; ed. Antigua Gram. Schi.; med. offr., and mag., Anguilla, 1918; thanked by exec. coun. of St. Kitts for checking outbreak of small pox in Anguilla, 1921; med. offr., dist. No. 2. St. Kitts, 1924.

JONES, STANLEY WILSON.—B. 1884; B.A. (Manchester), 1st cls. hons. hist. (1908); cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1912; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Nov., 1914; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1916; asst. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1917; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, June, 1918; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu; Jan. 1920 dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1920; ag. di t. offr., Klang, Aug., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1923; local examr. in law and Malay, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1923; attd. to federal secretariat on sp. duty, Aug., 1924; local examr. in Malay, Selangor, Sept., 1924; ag. commr., trade and cust., Johore, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1928.

JONES, WILLIAM JOHN ANDREW, B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1889; ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Mar., 1913; dist. commr., Dec., 1918; dep. prov. commr., Jan., 1924; prov. commr., July, 1927; sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1930.

JONES-BATEMAN, REGINALD.—B. 1894; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1919; attached to Kurunegala Kach., Dec., 1919; office asst. to supt. of census and dir. of statistics, Oct., 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Dec., 1926; asst. stltm. offr., Aug., 1927.

JORDAN, ARTHUR BENJAMIN.—B. 1890; ed. Nottingham High Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford (class. exhib.)—B.A., Oxon., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed final exam in Cantonese, Apr., 1916; ag. asst. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, June, 1916; 2nd lieut., Malayan Vol. Infy., Dec., 1916; passed F.M.S. govt. exam. in law, and passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V, offr., as asst. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1916; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Nov., 1917; asst. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1918; lieut., Malayan Vol. Infy., Dec., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Raub (Bentong), Feb., 1919; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1921; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Deo., 1923; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; prot., Chinese, Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1926; ag. chmn. sany, bd., Kinta, Mar., 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, July, 1929; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., F.M.S., Mar., 1930.
JORDAN, A. H.—B. 1903; ent. C.O., 17th Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 14th Oct., 1921.

KEIR, ALEXANDER, M.A., U.D.A.—B. 1884; ed. Fordyce Acad. and Aberdeen University; M.A. (Aberdeen), 1906; diploma, agr., Cambridge Univ., 1913; educ. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., 1906; inspr., schls., Selangor, 1911; head, Malay training coll., Matang, 1913; inspr., schls., Perak, 1920; repd. British Malaya at Imp. confere. on educn., 1923; ch. inspr., English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1923 to Sept., 1924; inspr., schla., Perak, Sept., 1924; del. for British Malaya to Imp. Educat. Confere., June to July, 1927; ag. ch. inspr., English schls., S. Sttlmtns. and F.M.S., sept., 1:28.

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD, O.B.E. (1927), Commdr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd lieut., 7th Lancs. Fusils., Aug., 1914; capt., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; seconded, gen. staff, G.H.Q., E.E.F., July, 1915; Bimbashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. offr., Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Sudan, Aug., 1916; Darfur Expd., and first inspr., Umkedas, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. and Bar); 2nd cls. mag., 1916; dist. comnr., Eastern Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. Arabic exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; pub. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory adminstrn., Dec., 1919; 1st astl. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to Q.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1925; astl. dist. comnr., N. dist., 1st Oct., 1925; dep. dist. comnr., Jerusalem divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publications, “Darfur, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan,” “The Pageant of Jerusalem,” joint editor, “Handbook of Palestine, 1922”; edr., “Gen. Regs. of the Govt. of Palestine.”

KELLAGHER, GEORGE BANNERMAN.—B. 1889; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919; cadet, S.S., 1913; attd. res. councillor’s office, Penang, Dec., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Feb. to Mar., 1914; attd. G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1914; ag. 4th mag. and astl. dist. judge, Nov., 1914; passed cadet, Feb., 1917; supernumy. offr., cls. V., Feb., 1917; on mil. serv., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.E., Dec., 1917; B.E.F., France, Apr., 1918; wounded Oct., 1918; lieut., R.E., June, 1919; demob., Feb., 1920; supernumy offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. astl. offr. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1921.
KELLIE, GEORGE GORDON.—Seconded from London postal serv. and apptd. 2nd astl. dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Jan., 1924; postal astl. dir., Somaliland, Apr., 1926; ag. D.P.T., June-Oct., 1927.

KELLY, SIR HENRY GREENE, KT. BACH. (1906).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; honorman and prizeman in hist. and political science; B.A. and LL.B.; called to the bar, King’s Inn, Dub., Mich. Term, 1884; apptd. a puisne judge in the Niger Territories, 1891; acted on several occasions as chief justice; chief justice, 1899; chief justice of Southern Nigeria, 1900; ret., 10th Feb., 1908.

KELLY, HON. HUGH THOMAS.—B. 1858; ed. St. Michael’s Coll., Toronto, and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1908; pres., York Co. Law Assoc., 1910 and 1911; chmn., Toronto pub. lib. bd. for 3 years; a gov. of Toronto Univ., 1906-1912; jud. of high ct. of Ontario, 1911.

KELLY, PERCY JAMES.—B. 1876; M.B., Ch.B., Glasgow, 1906; house surg., W. Kent general hosp., Maidstone, 1907-8; employed by govt. of Bengal on famine duty, 1907; certif. Lond. schl. of trop. med., 1908; apptd. to W. African med. serv., G. Coast, 1908; med. offr., Hong Kong, 1910; med. offr. to Victoria gaol and visiting med. offr. to Tung Wa hospital; med. offr. in charge of infectious diseases hosp., Kennedy Town; registr., med. coll., Ceylon, 1912; on military duty, 1916-1918; surg. gen., B. Guiana, 1922.

KELLY, ROBERT ALSOB, A.S.A.A., J.P.—B. 1841; ed. George Heriot's Schol., Edinburgh; clk., inland rev., 1898; S.A. war, 1900-02 (Queen's m.d.), 5 clasps); ass't acot., N. Rhodesia, 1911; assist. audr., 1913; ag. audr., 1914-15 and in 1919; lieut., N.R. Rifles, 1916; ag. adjt., 1917-18; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, 1921; ag. treas., 1922; ag. treas. and mem., exec. coun., 1923, 1927 and 1928; ag. comnr., taxes, 1928; treas., Gold Coast, 1928.

KEISICK, OSCAR RICHARDSON.—Ag. cashier, savings bank, Antigua, Aug., 1906; apptd. as ditto, Jan., 1908; ag. acotnt., savings bank, Jan., 1908; apptd. as ditto, Sept., 1908; ag. acotnt., treasy., May to Aug., 1909; junr. audit clk., June, 1909; ag. govt. offr. (acotnt.), treasy., Dominica, Apl. to Aug., 1910; ag. chief audit clk. and federal acotnt., Dec., 1914 to Feb., 1915, and May to July, 1915; 3rd customs offr., treasy., Antigua, Apl., 1915; clk. to treas.; clk. to postmaster, cashier and acotnt., savings bank, elk. to exec. coun., Virgin Is., July, 1916; ass't treas., colr., cust., postmr., etc., Montserrat, Apr., 1924; mem., leg. coun., Montserrat, July, 1924; J.P., Nov., 1924.
KELVIN-STARK, DAVID.—B. 1906; ed. Taunton; B.A. (Lond.), 1928; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1928.
KEMP, GEN. THE HON. JAN C. G.—M.L.A., Union of S. Africa since 1920; min. of agr., 30th June, 1924.

KEMP, SIR JOSEPH HOESFORD, KT. BACH. (1927), C.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1918).—B. 1874; B.A., Cape Univ.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; land office work, New Territory, 1899; registr., land court, 1900; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1900 to 1904; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1904; ass't sec., sanitary bd., 1904; deputy-registr. and appraiser, sup. cl., 1904; ag. registr., sup. cl., off. admstr., off. trustee, and registr. of companies, 1907; ag. off. recvr. in bank, Apl., 1907, to Apl., 1908; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1908 to 1909; head of san. dept., 1908; registr., sup. cl., off. admstr., off. trustee, and registr. of comp., 1909; crown solr., 1911; ag. puisne judge, 1913; ag. atty.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., 1915; ag. ch. just., Apr.-July, 1930; ch. just., Aug., 1930.

KEMP, JOHN TURNBULL.—B. 1892; ed. Christ Coll., Brecon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); war service, E. Africa 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Tembe; ass't dist. comnr., Sierra Leone, 15th Jan., 1915.

KEMPE, SIR JOHN ARROW, K.C.B. (1910), C.B. (1900), B.A.—B. 1846; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; clk., treasy., 1887; priv. sec. to chancellor of excheq., 1874-80, to Sir R. Lingen, 1880-81, to Lord F. Cavendish, 1881; mem. of comnr. on agric. and dairy schol., 1887-88; prin. clk., treasy., 1888; dep. chmn., bd. of cust., 1894; mem. of royal comnr. on elec. communts. with lighthouses and light vessels, 1892-97; ass't comptir. and auditor, 1904; comptir. and auditor-gen., 1905; hon. auditor of certain crown colonies and protectorates, ag. on behalf of S. of S. for the cols.: retired, 1911.

KEMPE, JOHN ESHERING.—B. 1888; ed. Radley Coll. and University Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. hon. sch. mod. hist.); B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1913; ag. ass't dist. offr., K. Kangar, Oct., 1912; ass't dist. offr., K. Kangar, Feb., 1915; ag. 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, Mar., 1918; offr., cls. IV, 1st Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Pekan, Feb., 1919; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Pahang, Apr., 1919; seconded for serv. as ag. comnr., lands, Trengganu, Feb., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Upper Perak, Dec., 1926; relief ass't. s.c. to govt. F.M.S., Mar., 1927; clk., fedl. coun. in addn., Apr., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Larut and reglar. titles, Perak, Nov., 1927; fedl. examr., Malay, Jan., 1928; ag. inspr., prisons, F.M.S., and supr., convict estab., Taiping, in addn., Apr. to Dec., 1928; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928.
KENDRICK, EDWARD ROBERT, A.M.L.C.—B. 1888; ass't engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1913; senr. exec. engnr., 1927; ag. ass't D.P.W., in 1927-28.

KENMUIR, THEODORE JAMES.—B. 1878; apptd. treasy., Natal, Jan., 1896; acotg. offr., Natal treasy., Mar., 1901; acot., July, 1903; senr. clk., July, 1906; ag. ch. acot., July, 1909; prin. clk., May, 1910; transf., Union treasy., Oct., 1910; transf. to head office, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Apr., 1911; recvr. of rev., Kimberley, July, 1911; head office I.R. dept., Pretoria, Jan., 1915; sp. grade prin. clk. ditto, Apr., 1918; survr., Jan., 1920; ch. clk., income tax, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Apr., 1920; recvr. of rev., Pretoria, Mar., 1921; ch. clk., gen. admins., I.R. dept., Pretoria, Dec., 1923; ch. rev. offr., ditto, Sept., 1929; recvr., rev., Cape Town, 1930.
KENNAN, MYLES.—B. 1894; clk., Basutoland, 1913; war serv., 1915-18; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1926.
KENNAN, THOMAS BERETON, M.C.—B. 1891; cler. asst., Basutoland, 1910; sub-inspr., pol., 1913; inspr., 1919; served in European War; comnr., 1929.

KENNEDY, F.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apl., 1917; passed London matric., June, 1917; on mil. service from 17th Sept., 1917, to 19th March, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher gr., 1st June, 1928.

KENNEDY, HON. ROBERT, M.A., L.L.M. (N.Z.).—B. 1887; ed. Southland Boys' High Schol. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; admitted barrister and solr., 1909; examr. in law to N.Z. Univ.; pres., N.Z. law soc'y., 1924-26; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1929.

KENNEDY-SKIPTON, GEORGE STACY.—B. 1898; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st class schol., 1919; triple senr. mod., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; ass't head, as'y. dept. and sec., sany. bd., 1924; ass't sec., Chinese affrs., 1926; asst. supr., impts. and exports, 1926; asst. treas., 1927.
KENNEDY, W.—Vet. offr., R.A.P., May, 1910; dep. chief vet. offr., Aug., 1914; ch. vet. offr., Kenya, Apr., 1921.

KENNIFIE, VICTOR, B.E. (SYD.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E., (AUST.), M.L.M. AND CY. E.—B. 1896; ed. Sydney High Schol. and Sydney Univ.; asst. eng., P.W.D., N.S. Wales, 1916; dist. eng., P.W.D., Fiji, 1921; ag. asst. comnr. of wks., 1927; ag. comnr. of wks., chmn., cent. road bd., Apl. to Dec., 1927; mem., leg. coun., Fiji May to Dec., 1927; ass't D.P.W., Cyprus, Mar., 1930; ag. D.P.W., July to Oct., 1930.

KENT, HENRY TUFFLEY MONBY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Elstow schol. City and Guilds Coll.; instr., tech. schol. F.M.S., Sept., 1914; ag. timber suptd., F.M.S. rlya., Dec., 1914; contr., timber supplies, F.M.S. rlya., Jan., 1919; ditto, forest dept., S. Stiltns. and F.M.S., Jan., 1923; sent to report on timber utilization problems in India and Burma, May, 1924; on spl. duty, Madison, U.S.A., Nov. to Dec., 1926.
KENT, HON. Jas. M.—K.C., B.A.; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1901; mem. of exec. coun. and min. of just., 1907; judge, supreme ct., 1916.

KENWORTHY, MAJOR H., R.E. (ret.), O.B.E.—B. 1873; ed. privately; mech. and civ. engrnr., specializing in land reclamation and irrig., anti-malarial drainage; serv. in France, Belgium and Italy, 1915-20 (twice ment. in desp., chev. Crown of Italy); C.R.E., Toronto; tech. adv., disp. bd.; dental. Genio Marina for anti-malarial reclam. and drainage; supt., P.W.D., Seychelles 1921.

KERMACK, STUART GRACE, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1888; advoc.; coman. in R.F.A. (T.F.), 1910-19; serv. in Gallipoli and Egypt, 1915-18; dist. judge, Sudan, 1918; govt. advoc. and regiar. of companies and industrial prop., Palestine, 1920.

KERR, DAVID IRVINE—B. 1878; clk. to pol. mag., Grenada, 1899; rev. offr. (various grades) 1902-15; 2nd clk., treas., 1918; ch. rev. offr., 1919; ag. ch. of pol., 1920 and 1924; ch. clk., P.W.D., 1921; supt., of prisons and recorder of meteorological obsns., 1922; seconded for war service (Egypt and E. Africa), 1915-19; J.P., 1919.

KERR, ROBERT ARTHUR.—B. 1870; ed. pub. sch.; Swellendam; served Matabele war and rebellion and seriously wounded in latter; formerly mem., dep. ch., and ch. of com. prov. coun., Transvaal and pres. senate; mem., Union senate since 1921.

KERR-PEARSE, MAJOR B.A.T., C.M.G. (1929) (formerly Rifle Brigade).—B. 1871; A.D.C. to gov. gen. of Australia (Lord Northcote), 1906-07; priv. sec. to gov. of West Australia (Sir G. Strickland), 1909-12; priv. sec. to gov. of Tasmania (Rt. Hon. Sir W. Ellison-Macartney), 1913-16; priv. sec. to gov. gen. of Australia (Rt. Hon. Sir R. Munro Ferguson), 1915-16; mil. sec., 1916.

KERSHAW, HENRY VALDOR—Capt., 19th Batt., London Regt., adjutant Nairobi defence force; serv. d. as lieut. in 2nd Middlesex Regt. in S. African War, 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps); asst. traffic supt., Jamaica govt. rlyw., Apr., 1906; travelling auditor, Lagos rlyw., Apr., 1908; ag. chief acctnt., Sierra Leone govt. rlyw., Mar., 1909; asst. acctnt., Lagos rlyw., June, 1909 to Dec., 1910; asst. chief acctnt., Uganda rlyw., 23rd Oct., 1912 to 31st Mar., 1915; dep. chief acctnt., Uganda rlyw., 1st Apr., 1915.

KIBBLE, EDMUND DAVIES, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Highgate Schl. and City & Guilds (Engnr.) Coll., S. Kensington; asst. engrnr., pub. wks. dept., F.M.S., Dec., 1907; ex. engrnr., pub. wks. dept., F.M.S., 1914; senr. ex. engrnr., 1923; ag. state engrnr., Kedah, Nov., 1923; senr. ex. engrnr., F.M.S., July, 1924; asst., govt. town planning dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1924; ag. state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1927; state engrnr., Pahang, June, 1927.

KIDD, MAJOR GEORGE MONTGOMERY, B.A., Dublin, M.C.—B. 1889; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Sept., 1914; seconded mily. serv. as temp. 2nd lieut., R. Irish Fusiliers, Mar., 1915; temp. capt., ditto, Aug., 1915; ag. maj., R. Inniskilling Fusiliers, Jan., 1915; demob., Apr., 1919; returned to duty, F.M.S., Nov., 1919; asst. to advr., Kedah, Jan., 1920; asst. agr., food contr., S. Kedah, Feb., 1920; agt., food contr., Kedah, Sept., 1920; dep. supt., census, Kedah, Oct., 1920; ag. asst. to advr., land, Kedah, June, 1921; sec., sany. bd., Alor Star, Sept., 1922; sec., rubber restn. comtee., Kedah, Oct., 1922; asst., rubber restn. off., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1925; chmn., assess. comtee., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; ag. coman., lands, Trengganu, Mar., 1926; ag. dist. offr., Tampin, Oct., 1926; dist. offr., Tampin, May, 1927; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Nov., 1928; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929.
KIDD, CAPT. W. R., M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—Asst. dist. coman., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

KIDSON, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919), M.A., D.Sc., F.Inst.P.—B. 1882; ed. Nelson Coll. and Canterbury Coll., N.Z.; on staff, Australian Commonwealth meteorological bureau, 1921-27; asst. dir., 1923-27; dir., meteorological services, N.Z., 1927.

KIDSON, NOELAN S. B.—B. 1885; served in Connaught Rangers, 1914-19; 2nd asst. mast., Boys' Gram. Schl., Fiji, Mar., 1920; clk. of peace, Rewa, Sept., 1920; ag. dist. coman., Rewa and ag. prov. coman., Naitaisiri, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. coman. and prov. coman., Colo East, Apr., 1921; govt. sec., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., Nov., 1922; on sp. duty as judl. coman. for W. Pacific, Apr., 1924; has acted as offr.-in-charge at headqu. on several occasions; ag.-res. coman., Dec., 1925 and Oct., 1928; offr. mem., advisory comm., 1927; judl. coman. (temp. and provisional), Nov., 1928.

KILBY, WALTER WHATELEY.—B. 1890; ed. Reading Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; White scholar, 1908-12; 2nd cls. class. mods.; 3rd cls. lit. hum.; asst. dist. coman., Gold Coast, 29th Apr., 1914; ag. dist. coman., Oct., 1914, to June, 1915, and from Nov., 1915, to Dec., 1916.

KILPIN, SIR ERNEST FULLER, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Cape, June, 1876; priv. sec. to Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, premier and col. sec., Feb., 1878, to June, 1880; clk. assist. of the house of assem., June, 1880; clk., 16th July, 1897; has served as sec. on the following Cape govt. comans.: Dorthesia, 1877; war expenditure, 1881; Liesbeek municipality, 1883; diamond laws, 1887; liquor laws, 1889; lighthouses, 1890; fisheries, 1892; scab., 1893; defence, 1896; and acted as priv. sec. to premier, the Hon. Sir T. C. Scanlen, Feb. and Mar., 1883; sec. to the Anglo-German coman. on Angra Pequena and W. Coast claims, 1886; author of parl. agents' manual, Cape, 1902, 2nd edtn., 1906; of S. African Union and private bill legislation, 1908, and editor of civil service list, 1889-1910; prepared standing rules and orders for the legis. coun., Rhodesia, 1898; has frequently been consulted on parl. procedure in South Africa; at the invitation of the O.R.C. govt., assisted in the inauguration of parl. institutions there in Dec., 1907; was examiner for shorthand under civ. ser. comans., 1889-1910; is a J.P. for the Cape Province; chfr. sec., S. African National Convention, 1918-1909; retired, 1910.

KILPIN, RALPH.—B. 1887; ed. Diocesean Coll., Rondebosch, S. Africa; clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1905; clk. of papers, Union H. of A., 1910; 2nd clk.-asst., 1917; clk.-asst. and acctnt., 1920; admitted sworn translator (Eng. and Dutch), sup. ct., of S. Africa, 1912; assessor apptd. by admistr., Cape exec. comtee. elections and elec. of senators since 1910; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-17; recd. vote of thanks of leg. assembly, S.W. Africa, for preparing standing rules and orders and assisting in inauguration of assembly, 1926; author of "The Old Cape House."
KIMBERL, ALFRED HENRY.—B. 1875; ed. at Otago Boys' High Schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. pub. serv., 1895; held various positions in P.W.D., includg. land purch. offr.; und.-sec. of mines, 1920.

KIMBER, GUTHRIE.—B. 1906; ed. Per-e Schl. and Cambridge; nat. sci. schl., Clare Coll., 1924-27; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripos, pt. I, 1926; 2nd cls., pt. II, 1927; B.A., 1927; appld. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 19th Oct., 1928.

KINDER, F. T.—Asst. engnr., Singapore rlyw., Mar., 1900; asst. supt. of wks., S. Stiltna., Mar., 1903; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Mar., 1908; asst. supt. of wks., Penang, Apr., 1909; asst. engnr., Jan., 1910; exec. engnr., prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1910; exec. engnr., supernum., Penang, Jan., 1912; ag. exec. engnr., Malacca, June, 1913; exec. engnr., supernum., Penang, Mar., 1915; ag. exec. engnr., Singapore, July, 1919; exec. engnr., grade II., Aug., 1919; ag. exec. engnr., grade I., Malacca, 28th Jan., 1920, and 3rd Mar., 1920; ag. dep. exec. engnr., Penang, 20th Feb., 1920, and 26th Mar., 1921; ag. exec. engnr., P.W.D., Singapore, 14th Feb., 1921.

KING, CLEMENT HAMPDEN.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ag. 5th cls. clk., police dept., B. Guiana, June, 1893; gradual prom. to prin. clk.; dist. inspr. of police, 1901; county inspr., Dec., 1908; ag. adjt., July, 1907 to Mar., 1908, Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909, and from Oct., 1911 to Apr., 1912; adjt. and staff offr., local forces, Oct., 1914 to Aug., 1919; capt., B. Guiana Militia, Oct., 1914; major, Aug., 1919; ag. deputy inspr.gen. and supt. of Georgetown fire brig., July to Oct., 1914; ag. deputy inspr.gen. on various occasions; dep. inspr.gen. pol. and supt. fire brigade, 20th Aug., 1919; comnrs. pol., Sierra Leone, 13th June, 1921.

KING, SIR FREDERICK TRUBY, Kt. Bach. (1925), C.M.G. (1917), M.B., B.Sc.—Founder of New Zealand Plunket Sooy, and famous for his work in connection with infant-life protection; dir. of divn. of child-welfare, N.Z. health dept., 1921; inspr.gen., mental hosps., 1925-27.

KING, GEORGE HOWARD.—Ag. clk., treas., St. Kitts, 1894; ag. rev. offr., Nevis, 1895; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, 1895; ag. clk., regar's office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1899; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1905; 1st clk., P.O., and sub-inspr.of schls., St. Kitts, 1906; ag. postmr., St. Kitts, 1914; postmr., May, 1915.

KING, JOHN EDMUND.—B. 1900; entered C.O., Sept., 1916; served with R.N.V.R. from 30th Sept. to 19th Dec., 1918; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apl., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler.offr., 4th July, 1928.

KING, NAPLETON WALTER.—B. 1876; cler. asst., surg.gen's office, Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; 6th cls. clk., off. of inspr. of prisons, 1896; 6th ditto, June, 1897; 4th cls. offr. of cust., Jan., 1906; asst. supt., alms house, June, 1912; asst. supt., prisons and asst. to inspr., Sept., 1916; ag. supt., prisons and mag., Bartica, Oct., Dec., 1917; ag. supt., Onderneeming induc.schl. and immigrn. agt., No. 4 dist., Dec., 1918-Jan, 1919 and from Nov., 1920-Feb., 1921; dep. to D.P.W., June, 1919; ag. man. dir., col. steamer serv., Mar.-Dec., 1921; traffic supt., col. transport dept., Jan., 1922; supt., prisons, inspr. of ditto and mag., Bartica, June, 1922.

KING, SYDNEY NOEL.—B. 1897; ed. Dover Coll. and Bedford Schl.; served in R.F.A., Jan., 1916-Jan., 1920 (comnrs., Aug., 1916); cadet, S. Stiltna., Mar., 1920; ag. asst. to senr. dist. offr., Butterworth, Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1920.

KING, THOS. HENRY.—B. 1883; ed. at Exeter Gram. Schl.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1904; passed in Cantonese, 1907; attched. to Punjab pol., 1907; passed in Hindustani, 1907; ag. asst. supt. of pol., 1908; J.P., 1908; ag. supt. of imports and exports, Dec., 1909; asst. emigrn. offr. in addition to pol. duties, 1910; passed in Punjab, 1910; ag. asst.-supt. of police, 1911; mem., bd. of exams., 1912; asst. supt. of police, 1912; ag. dep. supt. of police and fire brigade, 1913; dep. supt., pol., 1921; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1921 and June-July, 1925; dir., criminal intelligence, 1923; ag. capt. supt., pol., Mar. to Nov., 1929; dep. inspr.gen., pol., Seot., 1930.

KING, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM LYON MACKENZIE, P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1906), M.A., Toronto Univ., LL.B., Toronto Univ., Ph.D., Harvard Univ.—B. 1874; apptd. dep. min. of labour and editor of "Labour Gazette," 1902; registr. of boards of conciliation and investigation, 1907; sworn of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of labour, 1909; defeated at g.e., 1911; special investigator, Rockefeller Foundation, 1914; chosen leader of the opposition, H. of C., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Prince Co., P.E.I., 20th Oct., 1919; re-elec. for N. York, Ont., g.e., Dec., 1921; prime minister, Dominion of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921, also pres. of the coun. and sec. of state for external affairs; represented Canada at Imp. Conf., 1923; prime min., 1926-30; leader of opposition, 1930.

KINGDON, DONALD, K.C., M.A., LL.B.—Ed. at Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tem., July, 1905; legal asst. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Jan., 1907; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Mar. to July, 1912; atty.-gen., Uganda, Oct., 1912; ag. chief just., Apr., 1917, to July, 1918; atty.-gen., G. Comt., Sept., 1918; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1919; compiler of 1930 revd. edn., G. Coast laws, 1920; atty.-gen., Nigeria, Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; compiler, revd. edn., Nigeria laws, 1923; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Oct., 1928 to Feb., 1929; ch. just., Sept., 1929.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Chelt. Coll.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1879; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high comnrs., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. comnrs., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec., Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; admnstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high comnrs., Cyprus, 16th Apr., 1904; represented Mediterranean Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

KINGSBURY, ALLAN NEAVE, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—Sometime asst. pathologist, Middlesex Hosp. and bacteriologist, Middlesex Hosp., venereal diseases clinic; pathologist, City of Lond. Maternity Hosp.; lect., R.F.A.; ag. govt. pathologist, Penang, 1923; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, Kuala Lumpur, 1924; on sp. duty in India and Ceylon, 1924; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research and ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., 1925; professor of bacteriology, King Ed. VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, 1926; bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Research, F.M.S., Apr., 1927; ag. dir. ditto, Aug., 1927; dir., ditto, Oct., 1927; awarded N. Persian Forces memorial med. for re-search, Oct., 1927; author of many medical and scientific publications.
KIPPING, RUPERT HARRY, M.B., Ch.B. (Leed.).—B. 1904; late jun. house surg., Darlington Genl. Hosp.; locum house surg., Sheffield Roy. Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1929.
KIRBY, A. C.—Aast. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., ec., 1913; 2nd grade rea., Nyassaland, Jan., 25; 1st grade rea., Mar., 1925.

KIRBY, ARTHUR HENRY, B.A. (Cantab.), O.B.E. (1879)—B. 1879; lecturer in science, Antigua, under the Imperial dept. of agric., 1903 to 1909; scientific ast., in the Imperial dept. of agric., and charge of publications, 1909 to 1913; ast., rector of agric., Southern Provs., Nigeria, 1913; r. of agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; author publications on tropical agric., educn. and topical hygiene.

KIRK, JAMES BALFOUR.—B. 1893; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edin Univ., M.B., L.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Oxford), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), F.Z.S. (Lond. and Scot.), Vans Dunlop scholarship bot. and zool., Edin. Univ., 1911, rtif. (with distinc.), L.S.T.M., 1922; 2nd lieut., F.A., 1914-16; lieut., R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917; pt., 1918; astl. pol. offr., Hai., 1918-19; civ. rg., Arabistan, 1919; astl. M.O., Baghdad, 1920-21; bacteriologist, R. Inst. Pub. Health, 20-21; demonstr., L.S.T.M., 1921; M.O.H., P. ols., Mauritius, 1922; astl. dir., med. and health pt., Mar. to Dec., 1927; dir., ditto, Dec., 1927; author of "Hints on Equipment and Health for tending Residents in the Tropics," and several articles on med. and hygienic subjects.

KIRKHAM, G. H.—B. 1881; S. African nstbl., 1901; served S. African War, 1901-02 (wounded); Transvaal pol., 1908; adt., 4th S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1914; S. African Rebellion, 1914; capt., 1st S. African Infy., Overseas Brig., 1916-18; German W. Africa campaign, 1914-16; ment. in desps., France, 16-17 (twice wounded); M.C., Apr., 1917; ing's pol. med.; dep. comsrr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1918; comsrr., 1929.

KIRKHAM, V. H.—B. 1881; ed. Portsmouth Grammar Sch., Munic. Coll., Portsmouth and sch. of Agr., Camb. Univ., B.Sc. (Lond.), 1903; I.C., 1906; F.I.C., 1908; dipl. agr. (Camb.), 1910—Govt. analyst, E. Africa Prot., 1911; dir. em. research, Kenya, 1919; dir., agr., Zanzibar, 1923 and govt. chem., 1925; offl. mem., leg. coun., 26.
KIRKPATRICK, IVONE.—Cadet Sarawak civ. r., Limbang, Aug., 1892; extra offr., Simanggang, n.n., 1993; res. 2nd cls., Mar., 1894; upper Rejang, May, 1899; res. 2nd cls., Sadong, ib., 1901; Kapit, 1905.
KIRSOPE, GEORGE DOUGAL—Astl. collr. st., E.A.Prof., Jan., 1914; collr., cust., Kenya ib., Jan., 1920; comprtr., cust., Zanzibar, Mar., 23; ag. treas. for various periods, 1924, 1926, 28, 1929.
KIRWAN, ARTHUR CONINGABY—Jun. clk., l. sec.'s off., Falkland Is., 1st Jan., 1907.

KITCHING, A. E.—Ed. Oundle Schol. and suc. Coll., Cambridge; astl. dist. comsrr., A.P., Apr., 1914; as-t. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; dist. offr., June, 1926; ast. sec., nativ. affrs., Apr., 1929; astl. sec., nativ. affrs., 1930.

KITTERM MASTER, SIR HAROLD BAXTER, B.E. (1928), C.M.G. (1928), O.B.E. (1918), 1st Shrewsbury and Christ Ch., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1920-07; astl. dist. comsrr., A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. comsrr., 1915; fr. in charge, N.F.D., 1916; secy. to admtrn., Somaliland, Aug., 1921; gov. and commdr-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 1926.

KLOSS, CECIL BOEN.—B. 1877; ed. Dulwich coll.; curator, state museum, Selangor, May, 1908; ditto, Perak, Oct., 1908; astl. dir., museums and inspr., fisheries, F.M.S., Feb.-Nov., 1910; astl. dir., museums, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; astl. dir., museums, and inspr., fisheries, F.M.S., in 1916-17 and 1920-21; dir., Raffles museum and library, S.S., Oct., 1923; dir., museums, S.S., and F.M.S. and dir., Raffles library, S.S., Aug., 1926; author of "In the Andamans and Nicobars" (1903), and numerous zoological and anthropological papers.

KNAPMAN, GEORGE WILLIAM—S.A. War; Transvaal civ. serv., 1900; ch. clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; office supt., secretariat, E.A.P., 1911; lieut., K.A.R., 1917-19; estab. offr., secretariat, Kenya, 1923; Queen's S.A. med., Br. War and Victory meds.

KNIBBS, STANLEY GEORGE CETHOYS, F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Sydney Gram. Sch., New South Wales; dipl. in all subjects, Hawkesbury Agril. Coll., 1908; land survr., Fiji, 1911; land survr., Victoria and N.S.W., 1912; crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1913; ag. supt., pub. wks. in addn., 1918; astl. dist. offr., Shortlands during 1919; reg. of land titles in addn., 1919; dep. comsrr. for W. Pacific, 1919; dep. for natives in lands coman., 1920 and 1921; astl. res. comsrr. during 1920; comsrr. of currency (temp.), 1920-24; qualified as civ. engrnr. to shires and municipalities of N.S.W., 1923; comsrr. of 'ands and crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1924.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS.—Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk. supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan, 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914.

KNIGHT-BRUCE, G. K.—B. 1891; ed. Radley Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A. (2nd cls. hon.), schl. of jurisprudence, 1913; astl. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Deo., 1913; ment. in desps., 1918; astl. dist. comsrr., 1920; astl. res. mag., Mombasa, 1923; res. mag., Uganda, 1925; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1925; astl. judge, Zanzibar, 1926; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; astl. judge, Zanzibar, 1927, 1928 and 1929; astl. atty.-gen., 1930.

KNOBLAUGH, CHARLES TRAPPE.—B. 1871; ed. at pub. schl., Caledon and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk., atty-gen.'s office, Cape, 1890; astl. acct. and bookkeeper, A.G.O., 1901; prin. clk., master's office, Cape, 1922; ch. clk. and astl. mast., sup. ct., O.F.S., 1923; mast., sup. ct., 1926.
KNOLLYS, LANCELOT ERskINE.—B. 1886; ed. Dover Coll. and S.E. Agric. Coll., Wye, Kent (lip. agric.); astl. supt., cotton cult., Uganda, 1909; astl. dist. comsrr., 1914; 1st grade admin. offr., 1925.

KNOLLYS, WILFRED ERskINE.—Ed. King's Schol., Bruton, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1906; clk., colonial audit branch, E. and A. dept., Oct., 1906; astl. aud., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Nov., 1907; astl. aud., Uganda Prot., Aug., 1909; senr. astl. aud., E. Africa Prot., June, 1915; senr. astl. audr.incharge, German E. Africa prov. admin., Apr., 1917; audr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919.

KNOWLES, CHARLES HENRY, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1878; ed. Burton-on-Trent gram. schl. and Mason Univ. Coll., Birmingham; res. master, agric. schl., St. Vincent, Jan., 1902; supt. of agric., Fiji, 1905; M.L.C., 1912; chmn., agric. experiments comtee., 1916; chmn., food supply comtee., 1917; astl. gov't. entomologist (conjoint), 1917 and 1919-21; J.P., 1917; M.E.C. (temp.), June, 1920; prov. supt., agric., G. Coast, May, 1921; dep. dir., agr., Aug., 1924; dir., agr., Dec., 1924.
KNOWLES, JOHN WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1877; joined Trinidad govt. serv., 1894; warden, counties Nariva-Mayaro, 16th June, 1922; warden, St. Andrew and St. David, 1st Dec., 1925.
KNOX, Rt. Hon. Sir ADRIAN, P.C. (1920), K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1918), K.C. (1906), LL.B.—Ed. at Cambridge; chief just., high ct., Commonwealth of Australia, 1919; ret., 1930.

KOTZE, Hon. Sir JOHN GILBERT, Kt. BACH. (1917), K.C., LL.B. (LOND.).—B. 1849; ed. South African Coll. and Lond. Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1874; sole judge of the high court of the Transvaal, 1877 to 1881; one of the commrs. under the convention of Pretoria to settle claims for losses and injuries suffered during the Boer War of 1880-81; chief justice of the Transvaal, 1881-1898; chmn. of bd. of examrs. in literature and science, Transvaal, 1890-1898; recd. the Portuguese honour of Knight Grand Cross of the Order of the Conception, 1896; atty.-gen. and member of exec. and legis. couns., S.
