Rhodesia, 1900; ag. admstr., S. Rhodesia, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, 1903, and judge pres., Eastern dists. ct., 1904; chmn. of the couns. of Rhodes Univ. Coll.; examr. in law and jurisprudence to the Univ. of O. of G. H., 1903-1908; puisne judge, C. of G.H. Provl. Div., 1st Apr., 1913; judge, president, 14th June, 1920; judge of appeal, appellate divn., June, 1922.

KRAUSE, FREDERICK EDWARD TRAUVOTT.—B. 1868; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Amsterdam, London, Cambridge Univs.; B.A. (Hons.), Cape, 1888; LL.D., Amsterdam, 1893; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Cape, 1906; advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa and high ct., Rhodesia; state prosecutor and ag. atty.-gen., Witwatersrand, 1896-1900; sp. commit. and mil. gov., Witwatersrand, 1900; chmn., mining regs. couns., 1907-9; mem., Johannesburg town couns., 1906-9; M.L.A., Vrededorp, 1907-10; chmn., inquiry comm., Johannesburg; mem., prop. representation couns.; mem., Witwatersrand cent. schl. bd.; K.C. (Union of S. Africa), 1912; ag. judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1st Apr., 1923.

KRIGE, Hon. CHRISTIAN JOEL, M.L.A.—B. 1868; intern. B.A.; atty.-at-law; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; rep. Caledon in parl. since 1904; spkr., H. of A., U. of S. Africa, 1915-24; ch. whip, S.A. party for several years prior to elec. as spkr.

KRUGER, JOHANNES JACOB.—B. 1888; on active serv., E. Africa and overseas, 1915-29; govt. printer, 1927; Union R. of O. with rank of major; mem., Union tender bd. and govt. rep., coun., Pretoria tech. coll.

KUIT, ALBERT.—S. Africa Repub. govt. and Z.A.S.M. rly. serv. till Br. annexation; served Jameson raid and Boer war on Boer side; finan. and estate agt., Amersfoort (T'vaal) town coun., J.P. and practically all public bodies, 1902-25; rly. commr. since 1925; pub. debt, commr. since 1928; a commr. of oaths for Union.

KUSCHKE, HERMANN F. G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal sec., Stofberg Gedenk Schl., 1916-24; organising inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1924; ch. inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1926.

LABORDE, ARTHUR LIONEL CRIGHTON.—B. 1876; supernumerary, col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 3rd Jan., 1893; acted as clk. to gov. in ex. comtee. and to the exec. coun., May to Oct., 1896; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., Lagos, Apr., 1897; ag. ch. registr., July to Aug., 1901; ag. col. postmtr., 29th Aug., 1901, to 31st Mar., 1902, confirmed in the appt.; ag. astl. col. sec., Aug., 1905, to Apr., 1906; dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; ag. provl. sec., Cent. Prov., May to June, 1907.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, C.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regist., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., administrator's office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1887; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several commrs. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admstr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. admstr. and col. sec., May to Nov. 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada, for various periods, 1915-20; admstr., govt., Sept.-Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.

LACEY, MAJOR ALFRED TRAVERS.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Schl., Norwich and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripos; hon. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; dir., educn., Nyasaland Prot., 1930.

LAFERLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., LL.D.—B. 1887; ed. Flores's Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903; capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elem. schls., Malta, Aug., 1920.

LAFFER, HON. GEORGE RICHARDS.—M.P. for Alexandra, S. Australia; sometime chmn. of commtes.; commr., of crown lands and imgrn. and min. of repatriation, 8th Apr., 1920; commr. of crown lands and imgrn. and min. of irrigation and repatriation, 1922-24; speaker, H. of A., 1927-30.
LA FONTAINE, SYDNEY HUBERT, O.B.E. (1930), D.S.O., M.C. (1916).—Aest. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1910; district commr., Kenya, Oct., 1918.

LAGDEN, SIR GODFREY YEATMAN, K.C.M.G. (1897), K.B.E. (1927), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1861; clerk, G.P.O., London, 1869-77, after compet. exam.; clk. to sec. to govt., Transvaal, 1878; priv. sec. to admstr., 1878-81; sec. to exec. coun., 1880-81; priv. sec. to Sir W. Bellairs and Sir E. Wood, while administering govt., 1881; sec. to Transvaal sub-comrn. for compensation claims, 1881-82; engaged as war correspondent, Egyptian campaign, 1882-3, present at all engagements, including Tel-el-Kebir and capture of Cairo; on special financial duty, G. Coast, 1883; sec. and acctnt., Basutoland, 1884; asst. commr., 1885; established magistracy in Masupha's country, 1886; acted as res. commr., May to Nov., 1890; ag. Br. comsnr., Swaziland govt. comte., of adminstr., Apr., 1892; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 1893-1901; comsnr. for native affairs, Transvaal, Aug., 1901; mem. of ex. and leg. couns.; chmn. of S. African intercol. native affairs comm., 1903-4; retired, 1907.

LAING, GORDON DACOMB, B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B. (St. And.), D.P.H. (Johannesburg)—B. 1899; ed. Merchiston Sch., Pietermaritzburg, Madras Coll., St. Andrews, and St. Andrews' Univ.; senr. lect. in anatomy, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1924; asst. pathologist, S. African Inst. for Med. Research, 1927; asst. health offr. for Union of S. Africa, 1928; med. offr., health, Pretoria munici., Nov., 1929; published some papers on anatomical and anthropological subjects in the Journal of the S.African Assocn. for the Advancement of Science.

LAING, MALCOLM BUCHANAN.—B. 1890; ed. Forest Sch., Essex; asst. commissary, B. Guiana, Oct., 1909; seconded as ch. clk., immigr. dept., Apr., 1913; mem., govt. standing bd. of examiners in Hindi, etc., Nov., 1913; immigr. agt., Aug., 1920; capt. B. Guiana Arty. (Militia), Jan., 1921; senr. 1st cls. clk., col sec.'s office, Aug., 1920; clk. to exec. coun. and leg. coun., July, 1928.
LAMACQ, FRANK CONSTANT.—B. 1903; apptd. cler. offr., London telephone service, May, 1921; transferred to C.O., July, 1923.
LAMB, F. M.—Senior staff survr., E.A.P., May, 1907; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1910; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Jan., 1911; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1918.

LAMBERT, C. E.—B. 1900; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. clk., cust., Dec., 1919 to Jan., 1920; R.I.C., Feb., 1920 to Feb., 1921; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921 to 7th July, 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1923; seconded as regnr. on staff of high comsnr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Great Britain, 15th Sept., 1928.

LAMBERT, SIR HENRY CHARLES MILLER, K.C.M.G. (1919); C.B. (1910).—B. 1868; ed. at Eton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; 1st class in classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schls., 1891; apptd. 2nd class clk. in the C.O. after open compet. exam., Feb., 1892; mem. of comte., emigrts.' information office, 1894; chmn. of the comte., 1897-1907; acted as priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain for the purposes of the comte. of the H. of C. on South Africa, 1897; 1st class clk., Nov., 1898; visited Canada, 1903; visited Australia, on behalf of E. I. O., in connection with emigration questions, 1905; prin. clk., Jan., 1907; acting officer, C.O.; asst. under-secr. of state, 4th June, 1916; sec. to the Impl. Confess.; senr. crown agt. for the cola., 1st Dec., 1921; agt. under sec. of state for the cola., 30th July, 1924 to 4th Aug., 1925.
LAMBERT, H. E.—Tempy. asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

LAMBERT, R. W.—Asst. auditor, E.A.P., Mar., 1912; asst. dist. comsnr., May, 1914; 1st grade admstive offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1926; ag. prin. asst. comsnr. for local govt., lands and stintl., 1928.

LAMBORN, WILLIAM ALFRED STEDWELL, O.B.E. (1930), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1877; ed. Oxford Preparatory Sch., King's Sch., Canterbury, and Middlesex Hosp.; asst. med. offr., St. Mary's Infirmary, London, 1899 to 1905; asst. med. offr., British Guiana, 1905; asst. med. offr., Oxford County Asylum, 1906; med. offr., Nigeria Bitumen Co., 1908; govt. entomologist, S. Nigeria, 1913; entomologist, Imp. Bureau, 1914; temp. med. offr., Tanganyika, 1917; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1919; asst. med. entomologist, F.M.S., 1920; med. entomt., Nyasaland, 2nd Dec., 1921.

LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, creat. 1880), SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1896).—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903-7.

LAMONT, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1885; vice-prin., Schl. of Agr., Grootfontein, Middelburg (Cape), dep. of agr., South Africa; prin., schl. of agr., Elandsburg, Mulders Vlei, 1920; asst. ch., divn. of agril. educn. and extension, 1925; ch., divn. of economics and markets, 1926; mem., agril. educn. comsn., 1921 and fruit export enquiry comte., 1924; chmn., wine expt. comsn., 1927; ag. under-secr. for agr., 1927; seconded for sp. duty, expt. of Union tobacco, 1928.

LANCETOT, CHARLES, K.C.—B. 1864; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Laval Univ.; private tutor; called to the bar 1885; K.C., 1899; special law offr., Quebec, 1891; dep. atty.-gen., Quebec, 1905; mem. of coman. for revision of code of civil proceed., 1893-97; comsnr. for revision of statutes of Quebec, 1909-10; published "Traité théorique et pratique du droit criminel"; "Manuel du juge de paix"; "Annotated Criminal Code."
LANDALE, ERIC NORMAN.—B. 1905; ed. Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, May, 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929.

LANE, CHARLTON ADELBERG GUSTAVUS.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1911; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 1913; ag. dist. comsnr., 1918; asst. secretariat, 1918; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1919; senr. asst. sec., 1920; dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 1923; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; rea. mag. Kenya, 1929.

LANG, ERNEST PHILIP HENRY.—Ed. Monmouth; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1905; pvt. prac., Hong Kong, 1905-15; war serv., 1915-19 (capt.); asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, Sept., 1928; dep. registr., sup. ct., Oct., 1928; ag. offl. recr. and regtr., trade marks and letters patent in addn., 1930.

LANG, HON. SIR FREDERIC WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1916).—B. 1852; mem. of H. of R., New Zealand, 1893-1905 and 1906-22; chmn. of comtees., 2nd Aug., 1912 to 26th June, 1913; speaker from 26th June, 1913 to 1922; M.L.C., 1924.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A. of New South Wales; treas. in Storey and Dooley govt.; premier, N.S.W., 1926-27.

ANGLELEY, EDWARD RALPH.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab); war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; passed lower stand. exam. in Temne; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 29th Apr., 1914; ag. asst. col. sec. in 1916 and 1918; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., May to Oct., 1919; dist. comsnr., 21st Aug., 1922.

LANGSLOW-COCK, E. A., M. INST. M.M., M.I. MIN. E.—Ed. Marlborough and Truro Schl. of Mines; I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900; inspr., mines, Pahang, 1903; inspr., mines, Perak, 1908; inspr., mines, N. Nigeria, 1910; ditto, Nigeria, 1914.

LANGSTON, STEPHEN HORATIO.—B. 1877; ed. Westminster Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxon. B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1900; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Jan., 1902; passed cadet, May, 1903; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Dec., 1903; ag. asst. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1904; ag. asst. dist. treas., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1904; ag. dist. treas., Seremban, Mar., 1906; 2nd ast. dist. offr., Tapah, June, 1905; ag. dist. treas., Raub, Aug., 1905, and July, 1906; offr., cls. V, Jan., 1906; ag. ast. dist. offr., Raub, Mar., 1906; ag. ast. collr. of land rev., Seremban, Aug., 1906-Feb., 1908; ag. ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1909; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Jan., 1910; ag. dist. offr., K. Langat, June, 1911; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1912; dist. offr., coast, Neg. Semb., Apr., 1912-Apr., 1913; dist. offr., Tampin, Apr., 1914; censor, Penang, S.S., June, 1916; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Oct., 1916; ast. dist. judge, Penang, Oct., 1916; ag. ast. regis. of impts. and expts., in addn., Aug., 1917; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1918; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Batang Padang, Aug., 1920; mag., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1920 and June, 1921; ag. collr. of land rev., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1920; ag. temp. as 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, in addn., May, 1921; ag. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1921; on ap. duty as town planning admstr., Apr., 1922; valuer-gen., Jan., 1923; town-planning admstr., Ipoh, Dec., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1926-27; offr. cls. I.B., dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, July, 1927.

LANKTREE, CHARLES JOSEPH DANE, Barrister-at-Law, Kings Inn, Dublin.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., Sept. 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct. 1921; attd. Colombo knch., Nov., 1921; ag. office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1922; office ast. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1924; ditto to dir. of med. and sany. serv., Oct., 1929; addnl. ast. dir., educm., Nov., 1926; admstve. sec. to dir., elec. undertakings, July, 1928.

LANSDOWN, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., LL.B.—Clt., C.G.R., Mar., 1889; clk., C.S.C.O., Capetown, Oct., 1893; atty.-gen.'s off., Nov., 1894; mag.'s clk., Aliwal N., Feb., 1898; pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Aug., 1898; ag. chf. clk. to sol.-gen., Grahamstown, May, 1901; ag. ast. mag., Port Elizabeth, Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Oct., 1901; addnl. legal adviser, July, 1907; prof. ast., atty.-gen.'s off., Capetown Apr., 1912; law adviser, dep. of justice, Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Cape Prov., Dec., 1926; senr. law adviser, 1927.

LASCELLES, SIR ALFRED GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1913), B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; b. 1857; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1886; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. cts. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. ct. Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1895; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. ct. Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1898; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; chief just., May, 1911; retired, 1914.

LASSALLE, CHARLES FRANCIS, M.D., Edin., M.B.C.M. (Hona.), Edin., D.P.H., Oxford.—B. 1871; ed. Univ. of Edinburgh; postgraduate work at London schl. of trop. med., 1902; the Polyclinic, London, 1902; Univ. Coll., London, 1902; Lister inst. of Preventive Medicine, 1912; mem. of B.M.A.; mem. of coun. of med. bd., Trinidad and Tobago; apptd. govt. med. offr., Trinidad, Nov., 1894; port health offr. and med. inspr. of immigrants, Trinidad, Jan., 1910; ast. med. offr., inspr. of health, Dec., 1920; has acted as ast. M.O.H., res. surg. at col. hosp., and also as D.M.O. at various centres; dep. surg. gen. and med. inspr. health, 1931; author of "Notes of lectures on Midwifery for Midwives," etc.

LAST, JOSEPH SPURRIER.—B. 1887; on active serv., Egypt and Palestine, 1915-1917; ast. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, Sept., 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Pembe, Dec., 1926 to Apr., 1927; ag. ast. ch. sec., clk. to exec. and leg. couns. and edr., offl. gazette, July to Sept., 1927; ag. ast. ch. sec. in 1928, 1929 and 1930; cls. IV, Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar.

LATCHFORD, HON. FRANCIS ROBERT, B.A., K.C.—B. 1856; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; elec. to Ontario legis., 1899; re-elec., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, chancery divn., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908; ch. just., 2nd divnl. ct., sup. ct., 1923.

LATHAM, GEOFFREY CHITTY.—B. 1887; ed. Winchester Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford, B.A. (class. hona., 1910); probationer, N.W. Rhodesia, Oct., 1910; ag. pts. sec. to admstr., Nov., 1910 to May, 1911; ast. native comsnr., N. Rhodesia, July, 1912; seconded to N. Rhodesia pol. as temp. 2nd lieut., Aug., 1915; temp. lieut., Feb., 1916; native comsnr., July, 1916; temp. capt., July, 1917; Croix de Guerre (1917); hon. capt., Aug., 1919; ag. ast. mag., Barotse dist., Nov., 1919; seconded as inspir. schls. (European) and ast. to sec. for native affrs., June, 1920; ag. dir. native educn., Apr., 1925; dir. native educn., Apr., 1926.

LATHAM, HON. JOHN GREGG, C.M.G., K.C., M.A., LL.B., M.F.—B. 1877; ed. State Schls., Scotch Coll., Melbourne and Melbourne Univ.; called to bar, 1904; formerly lecturer, Melbourne Univ.; during European War was lieut.-commdr., Intelligence Branch; attended, with min. for navy, Imp. Confce., 1918; assist. sec. Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confce., Paris, 1919; Br. sec. to inter-allied comm. on Czech-Slovak affrs. at confce.; M.H.R. (C'wealth) for Kooyong (Vic.); atty.-gen., C. of A., Dec., 1925; a rep. of C'wealth at Imp. Confce., 1926; leader of opposition H. of R., 1928.

LA'CHLAN, STUART, B.S.C. (FORESTRY).—B. 1891; ed. Royal High Schl., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; ast. conserv., forests, Nigeria, 1914; conserv., forests, 2nd grade, 1918; sec. conserv., forest, 1927.

LAUDER, JOHN ILES.—2nd clk., regis. and prov.-marshal's offr., Dominica, Jan., 1901; ag. ast. mast., Dominica gram. schl., May to Aug., 1906; govt. offr., treasy., Sept., 1906; clk. to comsnr. and clk. of couns., Montserrat, Oct., 1906; educnl. dist. offr., Aug., 1907; deputy coroner, Dist. "I." Mar., 1911; ag. curator, botanic station, Jan. to Feb., 1912; J.P., Mar., 1912; ag. mas., Dist. "I," in 1911 and 1912; ag. offr. in charge of prison discipline, May to June, 1912; mem. of Montserrat defence req., Nov., 1906 to June, 1912; ast. rec-gen., Gambia, June, 1912; ag. rec-gen., on various occasions, 1912-17; ag. master of govt. vessels, Oct., 1912 to Oct., 1913; provnl. mem. exec. and legis. couns., on various occasions, 1912-18; mem. navigation and pilotage bd., May, 1916; ag. curator of intestate estates, June to Oct., 1914; offr.-in charge, secretariat, on various occasions, 1916-18; ag. ast. col. sec., n. 1918 and 1919; food cont., June to Dec., 1918; dep. compt., cust., G. Coast, May, 1919; ag. compt., in 1919 and 1920; contrlr. enemy pro erty, in 1919, 1920 and 1921; comptr., cust., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1922; ag. col. treas., Feb. to Apr., 1929; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Oct., 1926; gov't. dep., lst to 8th Oct., 1926; comptr., cust., Gold Coast, Feb., 1928.
LAURENCE, C. G. W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.
LAVILLIE, LOUIS VICTOR JOSEPH.—B. 1888; ed. at Bedford Schl., and Corpus Christi Coll.

LAW, CHARLES EWAN.—B. 1884; ed. High Schl., Croydon and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Campbell-Foster prizeman, criminal law, Middle Temple, 1903; called to bar, 1906; Burma bar, 1905; staff capt. (temp. Brit. serv.), Burma divn., Mar., 1917; Volunteer Long Service med. (India), Feb., 1919; maj. (temp. Ind. army res. offfrs.) comdg. 15th Mech. Transport Coy., July, 1919; released from serv., rank capt., I.A.R.O., Feb., 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1923 to Oct., 1924; mem. legis. coun., May, 1923 to Oct., 1924; ag. atty.-gen. and mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1924; Swahili (L.S.), Sept., 1923; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1926; ag. judge, Kingston ct., on various occasions, 1925 to 1927; judge, Kingston ct., Feb., 1927; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions 1926, and 1929; mem., standing comm. of res. maga., June, 1926; mem., comm. of enquiry into admnstr. of prisons, Sept., 1926; puisne judge, Uganda, Oct., 1930; mem., ct. of Appl. for Eastern Africa.

LAW, Y. P., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1888 astt. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1915; ag. inspr. of vernac. schls., 1915 and 1917; mem., bd. of exams., 1919-22; inspr., vernac. schls., 1919 and 1921; hon. sec. bd. of educn., in addn., 1920-22; inspr., vernac. schls., New Territories, 1922; apptd. mem., bd. of exams., 1924; ag. sen. inspr., vernac. schls., for urban districts in addn., 1925; inspr., vernac. schls., 1927.

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, G.C.S.I. (1911); G.C.I.E. (1906); K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; dep.-adminstr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; adminstr., Matabeleland, Dec., 1898; ag. adminstr., Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high commr., 1903; chmn. of ex. coun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C.; gov. of Madras, 1905.

LAWRANCE, MAJOR A. S.—Major, reserve of officers; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps, King's medal with 2 clasps, ment. in desps.); served with 6th Batt., K.A.R., Somaliland, 1905-1911 (African General Serv. medal with clasp "Somaliland 1908-1910," ment. in desps.); astt. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1911; dist. commr., Somaliland, Sept., 1914; comdt., Somaliland Camel Corps, Oct., 1913; operns. against Mullah, 1913; ment. in desps.; A.G.S., med. with clasp; ag. consul, Harar, 1915 and 1919; operns. against Mullah, 1920; ment. in desps., D.S.O.; admstd. govt. on various occasions, 1921, 1925-29; sec. to govt., 1926.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR CHARLES TREVOR, O.B.E. (1926)—B. 1879; ed. St. David's, Reigate and at Shrewsbury Schl.; N. Nigeria Regt., 1900-05; A.D.C. to Gov., S. Nigeria, 1906; junr. astt. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ag. astt. prov. commr., Cent. Prov., 1912; pvtc. sec. to gov. gen., 1914 and 1915; commanded Nigeria Land Cont., 1914-15; 1st astt. sec., S. Prov., 1916; attd., Nigeria Regt., 1917-19; sec., petroleum pool bd., 1918-19; ag. sec., S. Prov., 1919, 1920 and 1921; prin. astt. sec., 1921; commr., Br. Empire Exhib., 1923; ag. lieut.-govr., S. Prov., 1927; staff grade, 1928.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR ERNEST HARRY THORNE, O.B.E. (1919).—Capt., reserve of offrs., 4th Batt. Connaught Rangers; served in S. Africa with 20th Batt. I.Y., 1900-1901; 2nd lieut., 5th Batt. Connaught Rangers, Oct., 1901; seconded to remount dept. and served in United States and S. Africa, 1902 (Queen's medal and four clasps); passed promotion to capt., 1903; schl. of musketry, Hytha (distinguished), 1904; A.S.C. transport course, Aldershot, 1904; army physical training, Aldershot, 1905; instr. of musketry, 5th Connaught Rangers, 1906; instr. of signalling, Aldershot, 1905; army veterinary course, Aldershot, 1906; passed for maj., 1906; mil. schl. of instr., Chelsea, 1903 and 1907 (special); dist. subj. of pol., Uganda, June, 1907; R.I.C. course, 1909; ag. commr. of pol. and prisons, Uganda, Mar. to Sept., 1910, Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1912, and from Mar.-Sept., 1914; astt. commr. of pol. and prisons, 1st Apr., 1911; offr. in ch., defence measures, Uganda, on outbreak of War with Germany with temp. rank of major; provost marshal, Uganda, Aug., 1914; commdt., Uganda Vol. Res., Aug., 1914; commdt., Uganda Pol. Service Batt., Dec., 1914; served in G.E. Africa till 15th Jan., 1917 (ment. in desps.); major, res. offfrs., Jan., 1917; ag. commr. of pol. and prisons, Uganda, Sept., 1917 to Oct., 1918, and from Apr. July, 1921; mem., Uganda Development Comm., 1921; commr. of pol. and prisons, 12th May, 1922.

LAWSON, HON. H.S.W.—B. 1875; M.L.A. for Castlemaine, Victoria, 1899; has sat continuously in State Parlmt. since 1899; commr. of crown lands and survey, Dec., 1913 to Nov., 1915; afterwards atty.-gen., solr.-gen. and min. of public instrn., premier and ast.-gen. 1918; premier and min. of agr., 1920-24; now senator for C'wealth of Australia.

LAWSON, HENRY WILFRED, M. INST. C.E., M.I. STRUCT. E.—B. 1883; Bara-Kano and Nigerian rly., 1907-1929; astt. ch. engrnr., 1928; seconded a res. engrnr.in-ch., Port Harcourt harbr. constrn., 1918-1929; astt.-ch. engrnr., Nigerian rly., 1928.
LAWTON, JOHN GILBERT.—B. 1887; ed. Harrow and Christ Church, Oxford; astt. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1928.
LAWSON, R. A.—Astt. traffic man., Uganda rly., Apr., 1919.

LEA, LESLIE.—Ed. privately and Univ. Coll. Schl., Grey's Inn, 1922; elk. acct., fencing dept., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1908; conf. elk. to commr. for lands, 1909; conveyancing elk., land dept., 1910; ag. conveyancer, 1913; astt. regnr., crown lands, 1916; musketry instr., K.A.R., 1916 (recd. thanks G.O.C.L. of C.); regnr. of titles, 1919; dep. recorder of titles, 1919; ag. prin. regnr. crown lands, 1920; dep. regnr.-gen., titles and ch. controlling rev. authority, 1920-21; law offr., legal adviser, etc., St. Helena, Aug., 1923; col. audr., mem., bl. of commrs., crown property, Sept., 1923; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, 1925; commrs. for preparation of new edn. of laws, 1925.

LEA, WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West Ham Tech. Inst.; employed by Messrs. Perry & Co., Ltd., contractors, Bow, 1900 to 1914; dist. engrnr., Br. Guiana, 3rd Dec., 1914; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

LEACH, FRANK, M.C., B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1889; on mily. serv., July, 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd. to the secretariat, June, 1921; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1922; asst. stltm. offr., Oct., 1923; office asst. to dir., med. and sanry. services, Apr., 1929; asst. stltm. offr., Jan., 1930.

LEACH, JOHN VINCENT, M.B.E. (1924).—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll., England; admitted solr. sup. at., Jamaica, 1881; res. mag., 1888; has frequently been specially appointed to conduct important inquiries and to report thereon.
LEADBETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LEAVITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., High Schl., Bellaire, Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; M.S. For., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv. as student ast., 1903; apptd. forest ast., 1904, with successive appts. as ch. of boundaries, ast. ch., office of organization, ch., office of organization, dist. forester, and ast. forester; ch. forester, comsn. of conservn. and bd. of rly. commrs., Canada, 1912; ch. fire inspr., bd. of rly. commrs., 1921; mem., soc. of American Foresters; successively, pres. and sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engrs.

LECHMERE-GUPPY, GARETH EVERARD.—B. 1874; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 20th May, 1895; seconded for special serv. to water authority and sewerage bd., 1904-1905; ag. inspr. of imigrts. in 1908, 1910, 1912 to 1913; statistical clk. and librnr., col. secretariat, Trinidad, 15th July, 1915; sec. income tax comtee., etc., comtee., tenders comtee., and civ. serv. comtee.; J.P. for the col. and Tobago; dep. sub-intendant crown lands, Dec., 1920; sub-intend., crown lands, Jan., 1925.

LECHMERE-GUPPY, P. (Jnr.).—Supernumerary clk., audit offr., Trinidad, 1889; 4th clk., sub-treasy., San Fernando, 1st Jan., 1892; landing waiter, customs, 1st May, 1894; clk., col. treasy., 1st Mar., 1898; acted as warden, Tacarigua, in 1902, 1904 and 1905; sub-revrr., postmaster, collr. of cust. and harbr. mast., Tobago, 16th Oct., 1917; collected fresh-water fishes of Trinidad for British Museum.
LERC, W. J., M.I.M.E.—B. 1889; ed. St. Bees Schl., Cumberland; colliery man., Nigeria, 1914.

LEDEROKR, CLAUS MARCUS.—Served in S. African Constab., 1901-7 (S.A. war med.); Bech. Prot. police, from 1907; sub-insp., Bech. Prot. police, 1st Apr., 1914; asst. res. mag.; passed Cape civ. serv. lower law exam. and 2nd grade Sochuan exam.; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1919; ag. res. mag., 1921; res. mag., 1921; passed Cape civ. serv. higher law exam., 1923; ag. asst. res. commrs., 1930.

LEE, FRANK G.—B. 1903; ed. Brentwood Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. English tripso, 1923; 1st cls. historical tripso, part II, 1924; foundation schl., 1924; apptd. to C.O. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; temp. ag. prin., 26th Sept., 1930.

LEE, H. N., O.B.E. (Mily.), 1918.—Ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl. and King's Coll. London; articles and exams., Inst. of Chart. Accts., 1906-11; aud. dept. audt. 1913; asst. audr. E.A.P., Jan., 1914; E.A. Expedy. Force, 1914-19 (twice ment. in desps.); senr. audr. astdr., Kenya, 1920; ag. audr., Kenya, 1921; temp. dutv Somaliland Prot., 1924-26 and in 1928; ag. audr. Kenya, 1926-27; prin. ast. audr., Jan., 1928; audr., Zanzibar Prot., Nov., 1928.
LEE, JOHN HENRY BURKILL.—B. 1906; ed Univ. Coll., Leicester; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Dec., 1928.
LEE, LIONEL JAMES, A.M.I.M.E.—Ed. R.N.E. Coll., Keyham; asst. engrnr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1913; prin. engrnr, 1927.
LEE, HON. SIR W. H., K.T.M.G. (1922), KT. BACH (1920).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.

LEES, AUBREY TREVOR OWNSHALL.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Royal Mily. Academy, W.oolwich; ent. R. Arty., Jan., 1919; served with 2nd R.W. Fus., Ireland, June, 1920; attd. with Arab and Kurdish levies, Iraq and Kurdistan, June, 1922; attd. air hd. qrs., 'Iraq (air staff intell., spec. serv.), June, 1924; adminstr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1926; pvtc. soe. to Br. res. and ag. ast. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. and edr. of councils, Sept., 1927; temp. attd., C.O., 1928; admteve offr., Palestine, 1929.

LEES, HENRY BERRY, M.C., M.I.C.E., M.I.M. and Cy. E., F.S.I.—B. 1880; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1905; ag. prov. engrnr., N. Prov., 1908; extra ast. dir. of pub. wks., 1909; 2nd ast. dir., pub. wks., 1912; ag. ast. dir. of pub. wks., 1913 and 1920; on active serv. with R.E.'s in France, Belgium, Italy, and with the army of occupation on the Rhine, 1915-19; major, R.E., M.C., Legion d'Honneur, Croix de Chevalier, twice ment. in desps.; comsnr. of wks. and mem., legis. coun., Fiji, 1920; comsnr. of water supply and drainage, 19.0; chmn., cent. road bd., 1920; dir., pub. wks. and mem., advisory coun., Palestine, 1923; chmn., cent. housing comm., 1923; apptd. adviser to H.M.G. under concessions protocol to treaty of Lausanne in connection with certain pre-war Turkish concessions, 1923; chmn., road bd., 1926; D.P.W. and M.L.C., Ceylon, Apr., 1927; chmn., pub. wks. advisory bd.

LEESE, CHARLES WILLIAM.—Ast. dist. commrs., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1906; transf'd. to treasy. dept., 1907; acted as supervisor of customs, 1908, and as junior ast. sec. in 1911; served as finan. offr., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; dep. treas., 1921; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1921 and June to Dec., 1923; treas., G. Coast, 1924; ag. col. sec., Sept.-Nov., 1925 and from Oct., 1926 to Mar., 1927; gov.'s dep., on various occasions in 1926 and 1927; treas., Nigeria, Oct., 1928.

LEESE, CAPT. ERNEST BENJAMIN, O.R.R. (1930).—3rd Batt., E. Yorks Regt., res. of offr.; served in I.V., in S. African War, 1900-1901 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps); subaltern in G. Coast Regt., July, 1905; capt., Jan., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov'r. of G. Coast, Nov., 1910 to Feb., 1911; trav. comm., Gambia, Nov., 1911; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1915-16.

LEFEBVRE, PHILIP LLEWELLYN.—B. 1875; ed. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Brighton Coll., Sussex; clk., G.P.O., Cape Town, Feb., 1896; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., Glen Grey, Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., East London, Sept., 1896; clk., sol.-gen.'s off., Grahamstown, Apr., 1899; clk., atty.-gen.'s off. (treason branch).

LEGAT, CHARLES EDWARD.—B.Sc.; joined forest dept., Cape Colony, May, 1898; ast. to supt., George, Apr., 1899; supt., Concordia plantation, Jan., 1901; dist. forest offr., Hanover, Aug., 1901; transf. to Transvaal govt. as ag. forestry ast., Apr., 1902; conservator of forests, Transvaal, Oct., 1904; 1st grade conservator, Transvaal, Apr., 1912; chief conservator of forests, Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1913.

LEGGE, CHARLES ARTHUR LIONEL.—B. 1879; clerical ast., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th class offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th class clk. pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut., B. G. Artillery, 1901; attached to No. 32 Coy. R.G.A., Portsmouth, July, 1904; cert. in depression range finding, July, 1904; Hythe musk. ccert., Aug., 1904; sub-inspr. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Oct., 1904; promoted capt., B. Guiana Artillery, and seconded, Dec., 1904; mem. local health authority, St. Andrew's parish, Grenada, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. of pol., Grenada, from June, 1907.

LEGGE, CYRIL HENRY STHELWOOD.—B. 1883; joined B. Guiana civ. serv., June, 1901; clk. in surg. gens. office, cent. bd. of health, cust. and atty-gens. office; commissary, May, 1906; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1914 to Dec., 1916; stip. mag. 2nd grade, Dec., 1916; ast. mag., 1st grade, Mar., 1921; passed final bar exams, 1912; called to bar, Lincolns Inn, July, 1922; passed govt. Hindi exam.

LEIGH-CLARE, HARRY JAMES LEIGH.—B. 1891; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Feb., 1920; astd., Colombo kach., Nov., 1920; astd. office of food contr., Apr., 1920; ag. office ast. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1921; office ast. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Apr. and Sept., 1922; ag. ast. govt. agt., Kandy, May, 1922; ditto to govt. agt. Cent. Prov., Apr., 1922; ag. ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Mar. to Sept., 1922; addnl. landing survr., cust., Colombo, Oct., 1924; 2nd landing survr., Nov., 1924; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1926; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1927; landing survr., cust. Mar., 1928; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Mataura, Dec., 1929; ast. govt. agt., Mataura, Feb., 1930.

LEMBERGER, MAURICE.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., 23rd Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 26th Aug., 1918 to 27th Feb., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; trans. to C.O., 1st Sept., 1923.

LENTON, HENRY JOHN.—Cape teleg. serv., 1st Sept., 1898; clk., staff branch, 21st Jan., 1902; 1st cls. clk., ditto, 1st Apr., 1912; senr. clk., ch. clk.'s office, 1st Apr., 1919; prin. clk., ditto, 18th Dec., 1919; 2nd grade, ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1921; ast. sec., 1st Nov., 1923; ag. P.M.G., May to Oct., 1924 and from 3rd July, 1925; P.M.G. and chmn., civ. air bld., 23rd Apr., 1926.

LEONARD, HUGH GOODWIN RUSSELL, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1880; cadet, F.M.S., 1901; passed cadet, 1905; ag. ast. dist. treas., Kuala Lumpur, 1905; ag. dist. treas., Raub, Apr., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. sec. to res.-gen. and priv. sec. to res.-gen., Aug., 1906; ag. ast. supt. of 1n'tr'ta., Nov., 1906; ag. harm't', Port Swettenham, in addn., Aug.-Oct., 1907; bonus exam. in Malay, 1907; ag. emigrn. agt. in India, Mar., 1909-Mar., 1912; ag. dep. contr. of lab., Feb., 1913; bonus exam. in Telegu, 1913; ag. 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, Apr.-Aug., 1915; ag. dep. contr. of lab., Sept., 1915-Aug., 1919 and June, 1920; offg. ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1921; chmn., European unemployment comte., in addn., Apr., 1922; ag. treas., F.M.S., Nov., 1924; astd., fedl. sect. for sp. duty, Sept., 1925; contr. rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; finan. commsr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Aug., 1926; ag. finan. adviser and treas., F.M.S., Mar., 1927; offr. 1s. la. finan. advr.and treas., F.M.S., June, 1927; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Mar., 1930; treas., F.M.S., 1931.

LESLIE, ALLAN WALLACE.—Ag. clk., Estcourt magistracy, Natal, 1889; 3rd cls. clk., Newcastle, Jan., 1890; clk. of ct. and Zulu interp. (2nd cls.), Alexandra, May, 1891; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1898; transf. to Newcastle, Jan., 1899; mag., Ndwandwe, July, 1900; Krantzkop, Apr., 1904; Richmond, Oct., 1906; mag. for col. of Natal, Oct., 1906; ast. under-sec., Dec., 1907; ag. prin. under-sec., Apr. to Nov., 1909; ag. under-sec. for just., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1911; chief clk., dept. of just. 1st Apr., 1912; mag., Dundee, Nov., 1915; mag., Durban, May, 1920; judge, native high ct., Natal, 1st Oct., 1920.

LETHAM, CHARLES KINGSLEY, B.Sc. (agr.)—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Sch., Capetown; B.Sc. (agr.) Transvaal Univ. Coll. Pretoria, Univ. of S. Africa, 1922; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LETHEM, G. J.—B. 1886; ed. at Mill Hill, Sch., Middlesex, and Edin. and Grenoble Univs.; M.A. and LL.B. (with distinction) Edin.; mem. of Lincoln's Inn; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1911; res., Aug., 1924; ag. sec., N. Provs., Apr., to Nov., 1927; ag. sec., native affra., Nov. 1927 to Sept., 1928; staff grade, Mar., 1928; sec., N. Provs., Apr., 1929.

LEWETY, MAJOR A. W.—Ed. St. Paul's and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A. (Cantab.); Middlesex Regt., European War, 1914-18; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; aide-de-camp and pvt. sec., Gambia; pol. mag., Gambia, Dec., 1929; ag. legal adviser, and prov. mem., exec. and leg. couns., June-Nov., 1930.

LEWIS, ALFRED DALE, B.A. (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), M.I.M.E., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1881; P.W.D., India, 1906-07; circle engrnr., irrig. dept., Cape and Union of S. Africa, 1907-15; on mily. serv. as major, R.E., in France, Egypt, Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ag. dir. of irrig., 1920; dir. of irrigation, 1921; author of "Irrigation in America."

LEWIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1890; linotype operator, govt. printing press, Zanzibar, Nov., 1921; ag. govt. printer, Oct., 1922 to May, 1923; ast. govt. printer, Nov., 1923; ag. govt. printer for various periods in 1925, 1927, 1929 and 1930.

LEWIS, FREDERICK JAMES.—Tempy draughtsmen, survr.-gen.'s office, Natal, Mar., 1892; draughtsman, July, 1896; draughtsman and computer, Sept., 1898; ag. examnr. of diagrams, Jan., 1908; examnr. of diagrams, Jan., 1909; professional ast., survr.-gen.'s office, Natal Prov., Apr., 1912; survr.-gen., Natal Prov., 1st Oct., 1915.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl.of practical engnr.; jr. ast. engrnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rly., 1909; ast. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1912; dist. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1913; senr. dist. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1923.

LEWIS, L. H.—B. 1885; elk., acct.'s office, G.P.O., O.E.O., 1903; elk., P.O., savings bank Transvaal, 1904; elk., sec.'s office, P.O., Union of S. Africa, 1914; seconded to adminstr., S.W. Africa Prot., 1915-17; 1st grade elk., treas., S. Africa, 1917; senr. elk., 1919; prin. elk., 1921; sec., prov. finances commn., 1922-23; sec., educn., adminstr. commn., 1923.

LEWIS, Hon. Sir Neil Elliott, K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1901), B.A.—B. 1858; atty.-gen. in Dobson-Herry ministry, Tasmania, 1892-4; premier and atty.-gen. in Lewis ministry, 1900; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as min. without portfolio, Jan., 1901; again premier of Tasmania, 1909-1912; treas., and min. for mines, Apl., 1916.

LEWISON, Irvine Boyce.—B. 1884; ed. Marine Brothers Sch., Johannesburg; elk., survey dept., Kenya, 1911; survr., survey dept., Kenya, 1912; asst. survr., Zanzibar, 1922; ag. survr. for various periods, 1923-26 and 1928.

LEY, Major Cuthbert Hilliab, O.B.E. late R.E., F.R.Met.Soc.—B. 1872; comen. in R. Engnr., 1892-1907; ret. from army, 1907; army serv. in Jamaica, 1896-99; S. African war, 1899-1900 (Tugela Heights and relief of Ladysmith); A.D.C. to inspr.-gen. of fortifications and engnr., 1900-03; geodetic survey of S. Africa, 1903-05; topographical seen., gen. staff, 1906-07; survey of Fiji, 1908; ordnance survey of Gt. Britain, 1912; served during war at Southampton in depts. of fortifications, miny. of munitions, and directorate of arty.; dir., Palestine survey, Dec., 1920.

LEY, Henry Coates.—B. 1898; elk. to gov., Leeward Is., Nov. 1920; 1st elk. treas. and cust. and shipping master, Falkland Is., Apr., 1924; also postmtr., Apr., 1928; ag. treas. and collr., cust., man., savings bank, recr. of wrecks and regsr., shipping, June 1928; ch. acctnt., treas., Cyprus, Sept., 1930.

LEYH, Stanley George Hoare, Pay-Lieut. R.N.R., O.B.E. (Mily.)—B. 1893; ed. East Ham Tech. Coll., A.S.C. (T.F.), 1908-12; Royal Navy, 1913-20; staff of comdr.-in-chief, Grand Fleet, 1914-17; admity. war staff (allied naval coun.), 1918-19; staff, comdr.-in-chief, China station, 1919-20; "1914-15 Star," Brit. War Med., Victory Med.; librarian, col. sec.'s office, Singapore, 4th Oct., 1920; ag. office asst., ditto, 15th Mar., 1921; office asst., col. sec.'s office, Singapore, 1st Jan., 1922; offr. in-ch., records revision in addn., 1st Feb., 1924.

LIESCHING, Percival.—B. 1895; ed. Bedford Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (scholar); lit. hum. (D.) 1920; pvt., 1st batt. H.A.C., 1914; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., Feb., 1915; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Tank Corps, Oct., 1918; served in France, 1914, 1917-18; E. Africa, 1916; ment. in desps., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 26th July, 1920; joint sec., col. survey comtee., 1920; sec., comtee. on priv. enterprise in trop. Africs, 1923; pvt., sec. to perm. under sec. for dominion affrs., 6th Aug., 1925; temp. ag. prin. 6th Jan., 1927; seconded to staff of high commn. for Gt. Britain in Canada, 15th Sept., 1928.

LIGHT, Joseph.—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1921; attd., Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1922; ditto, Kegalla Kachcheri, Feb., 1923; office ast. to ast. govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1925; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1927; ditto, Jaffna, Apr., 1928; ast. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Jan., 1929; ast. attnt. offr., Jan., 1929.

LIGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out Islands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd elk., col. sec.'s office, 1910-12; sec., bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; sec., pol. commn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. accnts., May-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. commn., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; elk., grade I., audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., in 1929 and 1930.
LILLEY, Cyril Charles, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; capt., Worcestershire Regt.; ast. dist. commn., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920.
LILLEY, Ronald, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Bedford and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; col. audit dept., Apr., 1919; ast. audr., Tanganyika, July, 1930

LINDSAY, James.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1906; late res. physician, Grampian sanatorium for consumption; late clinical ast., Royal Victoria Dispensary for Consumption and diseases of the chest, Edin.; joined W.A.M.S., Apl., 1910.
LINDSAY, K. G.—Asst. dist. commn., Kenya, June, 1921.

LINDSELL, Harold Offley.—B. 1884; ed. at Bradfield Coll. (exhibitioner) and All Souls Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Litt. Hum.), 1907; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1909; dist. offr., 1917; res., 1925; res., Kano, 1929.

LINDSELL, Roger Edward.—B. 1886; Ed. at Uppingham schl. (scholar) and King's Coll., Camb. (scholar); class. tripes, part I., cls. I., div. 2; called to bar, 1930; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd ast. regar-gen., 1911; J.P., 1912; passed law exam., 1913; ag. 1st ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. 2nd ditto, 1913; 3rd ditto, but continued to act as 2nd ditto, 1914; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1915; ag. chief ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915 to 1916; ag. ast. P.G., 1916; ag. ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1916; ag. ast. supt., imports and exports dept., 1918; ag. dep. regar., sup. ct., 1918; ag. P.M.G., 1923; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1921, 1922 and 1923; 1st pol. mag., on various occasions, 1922-30.

LINERHAN, William.—B. 1892; ed. Christian Brother's Coll. and Univ. Coll., Cork (class. schol. and exhib.), 1910-13; B.A. (1st cls. class., 1913); post grad. scholarship, 1913-14; M.A. honors, class., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., 1916; 2nd ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Jan.-April, 1919; ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, Apr., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Krian, 1920-21; ast. dist. offr., Lower Perak, May, 1922; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, and regar., titles, Pahang, local examr., law and Malay, Pahang and supt., prisons, Lipis in addn., Feb., 1927; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1928.

LINNELL, Capt. Thomas Forrester, R.E. (res. of offr.), A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Denstone Coll., 1899-1904; educated pupil, civ. engnr., 1905-09; lieut., Royal Engineers, S.R. of O., 1910; temp. ast. engnr., P.W.D., Br. E. Africa, 1911; transf'd to Uganda rly., Apr., 1914; war. serv. on Uganda rly. and in German E. Africa, 1915-18; "1914-15 Star," Victory and Gen. Serv. meda.; dist. engnr., Kenya and Uganda rlys., June, 1924.

LIVERPOOL, Earl of, Rt. Hon. Arthurb Foljambe, P.C. (1917); G.C.B.; G.C.M.G. (1914); G.B.E. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1912), M.V.O., (1900); Viscount Hawkesbury of Kirkham; Baron Hawkesbury of Haselbech; B. 1870; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1891; lieut., 1893; capt., 1897; staff capt., Dublin district, July, 1890 to Dec., 1901; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; major, 1907; state steward and chamberlain to Earl of Aberdeen, K.T., lord lieutenant of Ireland, 1906-08; gov. of New Zealand, 9th Sept., 1912; assumed govt., 19th Dec., 1912; governor-general of New Zealand, 1917-20.

LLEWELLYN, JOHN LIONEL BRETHERTON LLEWELLYN, M.A. Oxon. (hons. hist.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ast. dist. comntr., E.A.P., 22nd Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comntr., N. Frontier, Sept., 1915-June, 1920; A.G.S. med., "Jubaland, 1917-18"; ag. dist. comntr., Jubaland, Nov., 1922-May, 1924.
LLOYD, EDGAR BRINLEY.—B. 1894; ed. Aberavon; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1919; ag. land ast., 1920; ag. regis., crown lands, documents and titles, 1921.

LLOYD, J. D.—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Tiverton, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; class. trip., cls. II., div. I.; Blundell exhibtr., and major scholar of Sid. Sus. Coll.; B.A., 1906; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrts., 19-8, to 1909; passed cadet, 1910; ag. Ist ast. regis.-gen., 1910; J.P., 1911; ast. postmaster-gen., 1912; 2nd ast. regis.-gen., 1913; ag. dep. regis., acctnt. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1913; ast. P.M.G., 1914; ag. dep. regis. and acctnt., sup. ct., 1914; attached to imports and exports dept., 1916; ag. supt., ditto, 1916; seconded for mil. serv., 1917; comntr. in Chinese Labour Corps, 1918; offr. recvr. and regis. of trade marks and patents, 1919; custodian of enemy property, and contrlr. of local clearing office, 1920; admstr. of Austrian and Bulgarian property in addn., 1920; census offr. in addn., 1921; custodian of enemy pr. pty., etc., and dep. regis., sup. ct., 1922-23; supt., impts. and expts., 1923; commended by S. of S. for repta. on opium smuggling, 1924.

LLOYD, RUPERT CORDEN.—B. 1870; clk., contr. and audr.-gen.'s dept., 1889; ag. civ. comntr. and regist. of deeds, Kimberley, 1898; ch. examr. of accts., law dept., 1910; acctnt., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1910; olv. comntr., res. mag. and dep. admstr., marital law, Barkly East, 1912; olv. comntr. and res. mag., Barkly West, 1906; comntr., Robben Island, 1909; civ. comntr., and mag., Mafeking, 1913; Sonerset Est., 1915; inspgr. mag. and inspr. of prisons, 1916; civ. comntr. and mag., Oudtshoorn, 1919; ch. mag., Cape Town, 1923; pub. serv. comntr., 1926.

LLOYD, T. I. K. B. 1896; ed. Rossall Schol. and Caius Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1914); cadet, Royal Mil. Acad., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1916; served in Egypt and Palestine, sent in depts., 1917; apntd. under re-construction scheme, ast. prin., minv. of health, July, 1920; att. to C.O. as ast. prin., Dec., 1921; ag. prin., D.O. 4th Sept., 1929; seconded for serv. as sec. to Palestine comntr., 1929.

LLOYD, WILLIAM BUTLER.—B. 1880; ed. Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; M.A.; cal'd to the bar, 1905; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913; mag., Rodriguez, 1916; mag. for occupied territories of German East Africa, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1919; pol. mag., S. Leone, June, 1922; circuit judge, Mar., 1924; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1924 to May, 1925 and from July, 1928; puli-ne judge, sup. ct., Niger, '90.

LLOYD, HON. SIR WM. FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Owen's Coll., Manchester and Durham Coll. of Science, Newcastle-on-Tyne; bachelor of law, London Univ., 1894; D.C.L., Victoria Univ., Toronto, 1901; vice-principal, Bishop Field Coll., St. John's, Newfld., 1891; editor, "Evening Telegram" 1904; M.H.A., Trinity dist., 1904; leader of the opposition, H. of A., 1916; with Sir Edward (now Lord) Morris formed a National Govt., becoming attorney-general, 1917; ag. prime minister, 1917; prime minister, 1918.

LLOYD-BLOOD, CAPT. L.I.N., M.C., B.A., Barrister-at-law.—B. 1896; ed. Tonbridge and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., 5th R. Dublin Fusiliers, Jan., 1916; France, 1916-18; ment. in depts.; dep. regis., sup. ct., Kenya, Oct., 1920; regis., July, 1921; ast. atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Aug., 1924; ag. atty.-gen., in 1926, 1926, 1927 and 1929.

LOCK, GEORGE ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Sidney-Sussex Coll., Camb., 1895 (schol.); senior optime, maths. tripoe, 1898; 1st cls. mech. sciences tripoe, 1899; ent. C.A. as tech. ast., 1905; dep. head, wks. dept., 1913.

LOCKHART, CHARLES RAMSDALE.—B. 1892; mil. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Gallipoli, Egypt, and with K.A.R. in E. Africa); twice ment. in depts.; ast. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; ag. senr. ast. treas., May, 1921; senr. ast. treas., 1st Apr., 1923.

LOCKHART, SIR JAMES HALDANE STEWART, K.C.M.G. (1908); C.M.G. (1888); F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.; LL.D. (Hong Kong Univ.).—B. 1858; Edin. Univ.; Greek medallist, etc.; apptd., after compet. exam., to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1878; attached to the C.O. for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; supt. opium revenue, Mar., 1883; ast. col. sec. and ast. auditor-gen., Aug., 1883; ag. regis. gen., 1884 and 1885; regis.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; mem. exec. coun., 1891; chairmn. bd. of examiners in Chinese, 1891; ditto govern. body Queen's Coll., dep. regis., sup. ct. in prize, 27th Nov., 1914; 1894; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1894; col. sec. and regis.-gen., Mar., 1895; special comntr. to report on the extension of the bdry. of H. Kong, 1898; representative of Gt. Br. to delimitate bdrys., for both of which recd. thanks of S. of S., 1899; rector of coll. of medicine for Chinese, 1895-1902; comntr. of Weihsiaiwei, 20th Jan., 1902; assumed govt., 3rd May, 1902; ret., 1920.

LOCKHART, KENNETH SPENCER.—B. 1890; copyist, admstr.'s office, Dominica, 1908; 2nd clk., regis.'s office, 1910; 2nd rev. offr. and harbmr., Nevis, 1913; ag. acctnt., treasy., 1917; is also sec. to quarantine bd., and clk. to cent. bd. of health; govt. trade agt. to enquire into and rep. on conditions re W.I. labourers at Santo Domingo, Nov., 1918; rev. offr., St. Kitts, May, 1920; ag. audit. clk., Sept., 1920; 2nd clk., treas. dept., July, 1921; capt., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force; ag. 1st clk., treasy., May-Sept., 1922; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1922; income tax commr., 1924; prin., Oct., 1929.

LOCKYER, SIR NICHOLAS COLSTON, K.T. BACH. (1926), C.B.E., I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1855; entd. treasy., N.S. Wales, 1868; collr. of cust. and first comntr. of taxation, N.S.W., 1896; compt.-gen. of custs., C. of A., 1911; mem., Inter-State comntr., July, 1913; retd.

LOGAN, SIR EWEN REGINALD, Kt., Bach. (1928).—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., B.A., 1891; M.A., 1897; student, Inner Temp., Jan., 1894; called to bar, Nov., 1899; joined Middx. and N. London sess. and cent. crim. ct., 1900; serv. in Boer war as 2nd lieut., 65th squad., I.Y., 1901; Queen's medal and four clasps; mines dept., Transvaal govt., Aug., 1901, to Apr., 1902; asst. res. mag., Transvaal, May, 1902 to May 1904; mag. E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1905; chief just., Seychelles, 1914; admstd., govt. from July, 1916 to Mar., 1917 and from May to Oct., 1918; drafted in conjunction with Alfred Gallé, Esq., barrister-at-law, code of civil procedure for Seychelles; read. thanks of sec. of state; puisne judge, G. Coast, June, 1920, with seniority from Mar., 1920; senr. puisne judge, Dec., 1923; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Nov., 1923 and Feb. to Mar., 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, Feb., 1925; chmn., bd. of educn., Aug., 1925; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, May, 1927.

LOGAN, WILLIAM MARSTON.—B. 1889; ed. Bristol and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (Hulme exhibr., Bridgeman prizeman, B.A.); asst. dist. commnr., East Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; sec., cent. comm. of supplies, 1914-15; hon. lieut., unattached list, 1915-17; ag. dist. commnr., 1918 and Dec. to July, 1921; ag. senr. ass't sec., Sept. to Nov., 1920 and July, 1921 to June, 1924; 1st grade adjmstr. offr., June, 1924; senr. ass't sec., Dec., 1924 to Mar., 1925; ag. ass't col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1925 and in 1926; sec., local govt. comm., 1926; ag. ass't col. sec., 1927; prin. ass't commnr. for local govt., lands and sttmt., 1928; ag. comm., June, 1928.

LOGIE, HON. WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.B. (1918).—B. 1866; ed. Queen's Univ., Kingston, Canada; B.A. (1st cl. hons. in classics), 1887; M.A., 1888; LL.B., 1892; barrister, Canada, 1890; bencher, Upper Canada, 1911; entered Vol. Militia, 1883, and is maj.-gen. comdg. 2nd Div., Toronto; puisne judge of high ct., div. of Ontario, and a judge of Appellate div., 1918.
LOLTGEN, W. G.—B. 1894; on mil'y serv., 18th May, 1916 to 18th Feb., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Mar. 1920 to 27th June, 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 27th June, 1922.

LONDON, GEORGE ERNEST.—B. 1889; ed. at Warwick, and Downing Coll., Camb. (schol.); 1st in nat. sci. tripus, 1910; hist. tripus, 1911; B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; offr., cls. V, 1914; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 1916; on active serv., 1916-19; lieut., Glouc. Regt.; offr., cls. IV, 1919; sec. to res., Pahang, 1920; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; ag. state treas., Perak, July, 1924; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Aug., 1924; dep. contr., rubber expts., S.S. and F.M.S. and dep. supervisor, rubber F.M.S., Nov., 1924; ag. contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1926; ag. ass't treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Apr., 1926; ag. commnr., lands, F.M.S., June, 1928; ag. collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur in addn., June-July, 1928; ag. finan. commnr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Jan., 1930 sec. to res., Perak, Sept., 1930.

LONG, CAPT. HERBERT EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Kendrick Schl., Reading; B.E.F., Egypt, Gallipoli, Salonika, Mar., 1915 to Feb., 1918; India and Aden, Mar., 1918 to April, 1921; dist. pol offr., Somaliland, July, 1921; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Jigiga, Sept., 1927.

LORAM, CHAS. TEMPLEMAN.—B. 1879; M.A., LL.B., Ph.D.; asst. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., schl., 1896; asst. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Natal, seconded, May, 1901; asst. inspr. of nat. schls., Feb., 1906; asst. inspr. of schls., July, 1906; inspr. of schools, 1910; chief inspr., native educn., May, 1918; native affrs. commnr., 1920 ch. inspr., educn., Natal, 1922.

LORD, GERALD FRANCIS.—Clk., P.M.G.'s dept., Natal, 1901; inspr. of educn., Transvaal, 1901-2; civil serv. examr., Natal, 1902; clk., lands and works dept., and rly. and harbs. dept., 1903; senr. clk., 1906; prin. clk., 1910; ag. sec., rly. and harbs. dept., in 1908 and 1909; prin. clk., admstr.'s dept., Natal Prov., 1910; chf. clk., Feb., 1918; ag. prov. sec. Jan. to Apr., 1925.

LORD, LIEUT.-COL. J. E. C. C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D.—Ent. Tasmanian civ. ser., 1886; commanded 40th Batt. Tas. A.I.F. in European War from Feb., 1915 to Aug., 1919; commands 12th Inf. Brig., Australian Mil. Forces; A.D.C. to the gov.-gen.; commr. of pol., Tasmania.

LORNE, JAMES, C.M.G. (1931), M.A., B.Sc. Edin.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1901; ag. head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. asst. regist., sup. ct., and mag. and sheriff, Malacca, Apr., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Jan., 1904; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Jan., 1908; ag. temp. 3rd mag., Penang, Jan., 1910; ag. dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1910; offr., cls. IV., May, 1911; ag. collr., land rev. and regist. of deeds, Singapore, Aug., 1911; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1916; asst. dist. judge (civil) in addition, Jan.-Feb., 1917, July-Aug., 1920 and Nov.-Dec., 1920; supernumy. offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; offr. mem., leg. coun., May, 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., and regist. of deeds, Singapore, Nov., 1921; ag. under sec. S. Sttmts., Apr., 1922; commnr., lands, S.S., Sept., 1922 and June, 1926; dep. pres., munie. commnr., Singapore, Mar., 1925; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1926; Br. res., Dec., 1926.
LOUDOUN, WILSON ANDERSON.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls., Chatham, New Brunswick; asst. audr.-gen., 1902; audr.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1907; now comptroller-general.

LOUW, ANDRIES DE ROOS ECKARD.—B. 1873; clk., cust., Cape Town, 1893; examr., offr., 1896; prin. clk., E. London, 1912; survr., Durban, 1915; ch. clk., Cape Town, 1916; collr., eust., E. London, 1928; Port Elizabeth, 1929; collr., cust. and shipping mast., Durban, 1930.

LOUW, ERIC H.—B. 1891; ed. Beaufort West High Schol. and Univ. of Stellenbosch, S. Africa: B.A. and LL.B. (Cape); called to bar, 1916 and practised at sup. ct., Grahamstown; el. M.P. for Beaufort West, 1924; first S. African commr. to U.S.A. and Canada, 1925; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, Mar., 1929; min. plenipotentiary, Washington, Oct., 1929.

LOUWRENS, HILLIGARD MULLER.—B. 1868; ed. Normal Coll., Cape Town, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and law classes, S.A. Coll., Cape Town; admitted as atty. and notary, sup. ct., Cape Town, 1892; pract. as atty., 1892-1903; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1906; pract. at sup. ct. bar, Cape Town, 1906-23; K.C., 1922; ag. waterct. jge., 1923; jge., sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope local divn., 1924.

LOVAT, 14TH BARON (CB. BEFORE 1440) MAJOR-GEN. SIMON JOSEPH FRASER, K.T. (1914). K.C.M.G. (1919), K.C.V.O. (1908), C.B. (1903), C.V.O. (1903), D.S.O. (1910).—B. 1871; ed. Port Augustus Abbey and Oxford; late major 1st Vol. Batt., Queen's Own Cameron Highlanders and liut.-lt., 1st Life Guards; served S. Africa as hon. maj. commd. Lovat's Scouts (desps., D.S.O., C.B.); served European war, Gallipoli, France and Flanders (K.C.M.G., desps.); vice-chmn. and chmn., comm. on agr. research and admin. in the non self-governing dependencies, 1924-27; partly under sec., D.O. and chmn., Overseas Settlement Comm., 1927-1928; chmn., Overseas Settlement Comm., 1st Jan., 1929.

LOWE, CAPT. HERBERT JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vet. Coll., Dublin; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1914; capt., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1915; war serv., France, 1914-18; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1918; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923; ag. dep. ch. vetv. offr., May to Dec., 1926.

LUCAS, SIR CHARLES PRESTWOOD, K.C.B., (1912), K.C.M.G. (1907), C.B. (1901), B.A.—B. 1863; ed. at Winchester Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; open exhibitioner; Ist cls. in mods., 1873, and 1st class in final classical schools, June, 1876; Chancellor's prize for Latin essay, 1877; called to the bar by the Soc. of Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1888; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1877; priv. sec. to Sir R. Herbert, Dec., 1881; Earl Granville, Feb., 1886, and again to Sir R. Herbert, Aug., 1896; also chairman of comitee of emigrants information officer till 31st Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., Feb., 1892; priv. clk., Sept., 1896; asst. under-sec. of state, June, 1897; head of the Dominions div., 1907, in which capacity he visited Aust. and N. Zealand, 1909; regis. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1909-1911; ret., 1911; author of "Historical Geography of the British Colonies," "The Canadian War of 1812," "A History of Canada, 1763-1812," "Greater Rome and Greater Britain," etc.
LUCAS, HON. SIR EDWARD, KT. BACH. (1921).—M.L.C., S. Australia 1900-1918; agt. gen. for S. Australia in London, 1918-24.

LUCAS, FRANK ARCHIBALD WM.—B. 1881; ed. St. Augustine and Mareset Brosch. Schls., Johannesburg, S. African Coll. (now Univ., Cape Town), Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Cape); B.A. (honors) Oxon.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa; K.C., 1924; mem., Transvaal provin. coun., 1914-17; mem., econ. and wage coman., 1925; mem., native econ. coman., 1930; chmn., Union wage bd. since 1926.

LUCKETT, EDWARD HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Apr., 1915; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd. to Anuradhapura kach., June, 1921; attd., Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1922; attd., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1922; pvt. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to the gov., July, 1924 to Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1926; ditto, Kandy, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Feb., 1929.

LUCIE-SMITH, SIR ALFRED VAN W., KT. BACH. (1911).—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limassol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1896; ag. oh. just., 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900; ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 1908; pres., W. Indian ct. of appeal, 31st May, 1920; ret., 1924.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN ALFRED, O.B.E., V.D.—B. 1888; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1905-06; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc. and solr., S. Sttlmts., 1910-14; on mily. serv., 1914; twice ment. in desps.; ag. pres., dist. ct., Trinidad, 1920; asst. mag., Port of Spain, 1920-23; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1924; passed exam. in med., Greek, Dec., 1925; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1926-27; ag. crown pres., Nov., 1926; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1927; puisne judge, Trinidad, 1929.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN DUDLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Berkhamstead schl.; entd. civ. serv. of Jamaica, 1909; 2nd cls. clk., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 1920; sec., Westmoreland hurricane relief coman., 1912; sec. foot and mouth disease coman., 1922; ag. priv. sec. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Sydney Olivier) 1912; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Leslie Probyn), 1920-23; prin. clk., col. secretariat, 1929; war serv., R.F.A. (France and Flanders) 1916-18; A.D.C. to C.R.A., 68th Div., 1918; W.O. staff, 1919; edir. of "The School Garden in Jamaica" and "Elementary Hygiene."

LUDINGTON, NORMAN JOHN, M. Sc., Durham Univ.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attd., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1911; ag. pol. mag., Trincomalee, July, 1912; ag. office astt. to gov't. agt., E. Prov., Mar., 1913; ag. office astt., Kurunegala Kachcheri, Aug., 1913; ag. astt. govt. agt., Puttalam and Chilaw, Apr., 1914; resumed duties as office astt. to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1914; pol. mag., Negombo, May, 1915; Panadura, June, 1915; Negombo, Aug. 1915; Galle, Apr., 1916; Colombo, July, 1921; astt. comanx., excise, July, 1923; astt. attitl. offr., Nov., 1926; agt. contr., Indian immigr. lab., Dec., 1929.

LUGARD, 1ST BARON (CR. 1928), RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK JOHN DEALTRY LUGARD, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1896), D.S.O. (1887).—B. 1868; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st coman., 9th foot, May, 1878; captain, Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880; medal: Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1895; medal clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches; Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabakhi; adminsd. Uganda; went to Borgu in comd. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apr., 1895, Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Brd Charterland Co.; apptd. comanx. and comdt., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; high comanx. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, Mar., 1912; gov.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913; assumed govt., Jan., 1914; retired, 1919; Br. mem., Permanent Mandates comanx. since 1922; author of "The Dual Mandate in Br. Tropical Africa" and other works.

LUKE, HARRY CHARLES, C.M.G. (1926), Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1884; ed. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mod. hist., B.A., 1906; M.A., 1910; B. Litt., 1919; priv. sec. to the gov'r. of Sierra Leone (Sir L. Probyn, K.C.M.G.), 1908, and A.D.C., 1909; ag. astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar. to July, 1909; priv. sec. to the gov'r. of Barbados, Feb., 1911; attached to C.O., May to July, 1911 (Coronation medal); priv. sec. to the high comanx. of Cyprus (Sir H. Goold-Adams, G.C.M.G.), and clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., Cyprus, 1912; ag. comsrr., Paphos, 1917; comsrr., Famagusta, 1918; sec., Cyprus defence comte., Aug., 1914; served in H.M.S. Doris on Syrian Coast, 1914-1915; political offr. to Admirals Lord Wester Wemyss and Christian and govt. sec., Mudros, Feb., 1915 to June, 1916; (lieut.-comdr., and ag. comdr., R.N.V.R.); recd. thanks of admty.; Italian med. for milly. valour; political offr. to Admiral of the Fleet Sir J. de Robeck, Constantinople and Black Sea, Aug., 1919; Br. ch. comsrr. in Georgia, Armenia and Azerbaijan, Apr. to Sept., 1920; asst. gov. of Jerusalem, Oct., 1920; mem. of commsn. on the Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, and on the Jaffa disturbance, 1921, and finan. comsrr. of the Patriarchate, 1921-24; col. sec., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; ag. gov. on four occasions; ch. sec., Palestine, July, 1928; lieut. gov., Malta, Aug., 1930; passed in Turkish and modern Greek (hona.); author of "A Bibliography of Sierra Leone," 1910 and 1925; "Cyprus under the Turks," 1921; joint editor, "Handbook of Cyprus," 1913 and 1920; "Handbook of Palestine," 1922 and 1930.

LUKE, SIR JOHN PEARCE, KT. BACH. (1921), O.M.G. (1917), M.P., A.M.I.M.E., J.P.—Ed. Penzance, Cornwall, and privately Wellington, N.Z.; M.P., 1908-11 and 1917-28; mayor of Wellington, 1913-21; sl., life mem., N.Z. returned soldiers' association, and holds Medaille de la Reconnaissance Francaise (3rd cl.) for war services.

LUKE, S. E. V., B.A., 1927.—B. 1906; ed. St. George's, Harpenden and Oxford, Braeigirdle exhib., Wadham Coll., 1924; 2nd cls. mod. hist., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. inspr., taxes (inland rev.), 1927; apptd. after further compet. exam., junr. clk., House of Commons, Feb., 1930; transfd. to C.O. as asst. prin., Sept., 1930.
LUKIN, LIONEL OSCAR.—B. 1868; puisne judge (central), supreme ct., Queensland, Dec., 1910-26; judge, fed. conciliation ct., 1926.

LUMLEY, JAMES MADDY, LS O. (1930).—B. 1878; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne; served R.H.A., 1895-1901; S. African war, 1899-1902; wounded; Queen's medal, four clasps; King's medal, two clasps; S. African constab., 1901-08; pub. pros., and just of the peace, Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1904-08; asst. supt. of pol., E.A.P., 1909; served in E. Africa, 1914-18; King's pol. medal, 1919; sup't., pol., Kenya, 1919; ag. asst. comsrr., Kenya pol., various periods, 1920-24; asst. comsrr., pol., 1925; dep. comsrr., pol., 1926; ag. comsrr., pol., 1927.

LUNN, WILLIAM, M.P.—B. 1873; contested Holmfirth divn., 1912; M.P. for Rothwell divn. of Yorks since Dec., 1918; parly. sec. to D.O.T., 1924; parly. under sec. for the cols., June, 1929; parly. under sec. for dominion affrs., Dec., 1929; chmn., Oversea Settlement Comte., Dec., 1929.
LUNNON, GEORGE JOSEPH.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LUSCOMBE, LAURENCE GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. Heles schl., Exeter; served in S. African war, 1901-02; clk., Swaziland adminstr., Sept., 1902; asst. acctnt., Dec., 1909; acctnt., Apr., 1910; finan. secy., 1918; finan. sec., Basutoland, Sept., 1927.

LYALL-GRANT, R. W., M.A. (Aberdeen), LL.B. (Edin.)—Vans Dunlop schol. in law, Edin. Univ.; called to Scottish bar, 1903; practised till 1909; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1909; ag. judge, high ct., Sept., 1909 to June, 1910, and from Aug., 1912 to May, 1913; judge of high ct., Aug., 1914, and mem., H.B.M. Ct. of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chmn., native rising coman., 1915; chmn. of claims bd. for Nyasaland, under Br. prota. defence O in C., 1916-19; judge advce. to Nyasaland-Rhodesia Field Force, 1917-18; atty.-gen., Kenya, July, 1920; mem. exec. and leg. councils; puisne just., Ceylon, Feb., 1926.
LYDEKER, O. J.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comsrr., Kenya, 1926.

LYNCH, FRANCIS CHRISTOPHER CHISHOLM.—B. 1884; ed. at pub. schls., priv. tuition, McGill Univ.; assoc., Eng. Inst., Canada; assoc. mem., Town Planning Inst. of Canada; gen. engnr., 1902-03; apptd. to interior dept., 1906; asst. aupt., rly. lands br., 1911; supt., nat. resources int. br., dept. of int. Can., Dec., 1911.

LYNCH, FRANK PIERS, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1883; ed. Downside Coll., Somerset and Trinity Coll., Dublin; asst. dist. comsrr., S. Nigeria, 1908; polit. offr., Okpoto Patrol and ag. dist. comsrr., 1912; polit. offr. to No. 1 column, Udi Patrol, 1914-15; polit. offr. to Umunze (Awka) Patrol, 1915; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1915; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1925; ag. sec., S. Provs., 1930.
LYNDE, WALTER MICHELLS, F.S.I. (Eng.).—B. 1883; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; quantity survr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1910; in charge sp. wk., Kabete, 1918; contracts offr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1923; ag. D.P.W., 1926.

LYON, WILLIAM ROY.—B. 1889; ed. at Montjoy Schl., Dublin; 2nd lieut., 6th batt., City of London Regt. (T.F.), Feb., 1909; Hythe certif., musketry and mach. gun, 1910; lieut., 1910; asst. dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone, Nov., 1912; ag. dist. comsrr. for various periods, 1914-16; capt., 1914; major, 1916; served with Nigeria Cavalry Corps in E. Africa, 1917; attached, 4th batt., Nigeria Regt., as O.C. details, Lagos, 1st Apr., 1918 to 20th May, 1918; dist. comsrr., 2nd ol., 29th Nov., 1917; passed lower standard native language (Temne).

MAARTENSZ, LEWIS MATTHEW.—B. 1876; barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple; addtnl. crown counsel, Ceylon, Apr., 1903; crown counsel, July, 1906; addtnl. dist. judge, Kandy, Oct., 1910; ditto, Colombo, Apr., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Apr. to Oct., 1916, Apr.-Dec., 1920, Mar. to Nov., 1923 and from June, 1924; coman. of assize, Apr. to June, 1926; ag. puisne just., July to Sept. and from 30th O't., 1926; dist. judge, Colombo, Jan., 1927; ag. puisne just., June, 1927.

MAASDORP, HON. SIR ANDRIES FERDINAND STOCKENSTROM, KT. BACH. (1904).—B.A., Lond., 1869; barrister-at-law, Inn. Temp., Nov., 1871; mem. of the house of assem., Cape Colony, 1874 to 1878; solr.-gen., Cape Colony, Aug., 1878 to May, 1897; mem. of special (treason) court, Cape Colony, Oct., 1900, to June, 1902; ch. just. of the O.R.C., June, 1902; ret., Jan., 1920.

MACABBE, CAPT. VICTOR LEOPOLD, M.C., Chevalier de la Legion d'Honneur.—B. 1889; joined "Fort Garry" Regt. 18th Canadian Reserves, Aug., 1914; served in France as com-nr. to Tank Corps, Dec., 1916; left Army, July, 1920; ment. in despa.; admstve. serv., N. Provs., Nigeria, 1920; transfd. prisons dept., S. Provs. as supdt., prisons, 1924; ag. inspr., prisons, 1923; dep. dir., prisons, 1926.

MACALISTER, GEORGE HUGH.—B. 1879; ed. Charterhouse, Cambridge and Guy's Hosp.; B.A., 1901: M.A., 1906; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., 1905; M.B., B.Ch.(Cant.), 1906; M.D., D.P.H.(Cant.), 1909; M.R.C.P., 1927; house phys. and asst. bacteriologist, Guy's Hosp.; asst. bacteriologist, Lister Inst., 1910; pathologist, Impl. Bact. Lab., Mukteswar, U.P., India, 1914; capt., I.M.S., 1916; prin. and prof. of clin. med., Singapore Coll. of Med., since 1918; mem. and sec., S.S. & F.M.S. Med. Coun. and Dental Bd.; mem., coun. and senate, Raffles Coll.; edr.-in-ch., Malayan Med. Jnl.; rep. Malaya govt. at Internat. Rabies Conf., Paris, 1927; on sp. serv., Gold Coast and Sierra Leone, 1927; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services in W. Africa; author various papers on med. and pathological subjects.

McCALL, Lieut.-Col., Frederick Johnson, C.B.E. (1929). M.C., M.R.C.V.S. — Veterinary offr., E.A.P., Mar., 1910; served in Great War, Oct., 1914 to June, 1918; M.C., 1917; 1914-15 Star, British War Med., twice ment. in desps.; major, A.D.V.S., 2nd divn., E. Africa Force, May, 1916; A.D.V.S., G.H.Q., E.A. Force, Aug., 1917; ch. vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919; dir. vety. services, Apr., 1927.

MACCALL, Thomas Vernon, B., 1884; ed. Vickery's schl., Portsmouth, and Edin. Univ.; joined London Metro. Police, Feb., 1905; served in C.I.D., New Scotland Yard; resig. Mar., 1911; served in B. of T. labour exchanges, June, 1911 to Mar., 1914; asst. supt. of police, B. Honduras, Apr., 1914; J.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, July-Aug., 1914; visiting just. dist. prisons, 1914-19; dep. atty.-gen., Corosol sessions, sup. ct., Sep., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, Feb.-Mar., 1915; comsnr. of sup. ct., Apr., 1915; 2nd lieut., B. H. Voors., Dec., 1915; atty., Corosol; lieut., Jan., 1917; served in Cayo Exped. Force, on Guatemalan frontier, Apr. to July, 1916; capt., B.H.T.F., May, 1917; dep. atty.-gen., Corosol sessions, sup. ct., Dec., 1918; ag. dist. comsnr., Corosol from Nov., 1918.

McCALLAN, E.A.—B. 1874; ed. Ontario agr. coll.; clk., post office, Bermuda, 1903; clk., works dept., 1904; rev. offr., 1907; agric. asst., 1913; dir. of agric., Bermudas 1920; del. to Washington 1920 and 1928; mem., immigr. lab. bd.

McCALLUM, Angus, M.C.—Ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin. and Aberdeen Univ.; capt., B.E.F., France, Aug., 1915 to Aug., 1918, M.C. and bar. ment. in desps.; lieut., 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1918 to June, 1922; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1923.

MCCOWAN, HUGH WALLACE.—B. 1885; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana (Guiana schl., 1905), and St. John's Coll., Cambridge (maths. tripos, 1908; asst. mast., Aldenham, 1909 (temp.); asst. mast., Wellingborough Grammar Schl., 1910 (temp.); maths. and sci. mast., Boys High Schl., Salisbury, Rhodesia (ag. prin., 1913), 1911; headmnr., Boys' Schl., Grenada, B.W.I., 1917; inspr., schls., S.P., Nigeria, 1923; asst. dir., educn., S.P., Nigeria, 1927; ag. dep. dir., May-July, 1929; asst. dir. in ch. S. Prova., Apr., 1930.
MOCRACKEN, ALBERT VICTOR NEILSON.—B. 1886; dep. regiar. and asst. marshal, sup. ct., Trinidad, Jan., 1925.

MCCRAE, JOHN, Ph.D., F.I.C., M.S.P.A., F. Chem. Soc., Pestly Schol., Ehrhardt prizeman—Asst. chem., dept. of agr., Pretoria, 1903; ag. govt. chemist, col. sec.'s office, 1906; app't confirmed, 1907; dept. of int., 1912; govt. analyst, 1917; examr., physiochem. and chem., Transvaal Pharmacy Bd., 1916; examr., chemery., Univ. of S. Africa, 1920; transf'd. to dept. of agr., 1st Apr., 1923.

MCCREA, HON. CHARLES, K.C.—B. 1877; ed. high schls., Queen'n Univ., Kingston and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; barrister; el. to Ont. legis., 1914; re-el. 1919 and 1923; min. of mines in Ferguson adminstr., 1923.

MCDERMID, JOHN SHEWAN.—B. 1881; tech. instr., tech. schl., Accra, Gold Coast, 13th Dec., 1911; ag. prin. on several occasions, 1913-20; seconded for serv. on s.s. "Marina" for operns. in Togoland and Cameroons, 18th Aug. to 22nd Oct., 1914; asst. censor, 27th Oct., 1914 to 2nd Mar., 1915; headmast., junr. trade schl., 1st Dec., 1921.
MACDONALD, ALLAN RONALD—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1929; att'd., secretariat, Jan., 1930.

MACDONALD, HON. DANIEL ALEXANDER.—B. 1858; ed. privately and at gram. schl., comol. coll. and Prince of Wales coll., P.E.I.; admitted to bar, P.E.I., 1883; Manitoba bar, 1885; practised law in Manitoba; judge, ct. of King's Bench, Manitoba, 1906.

MACDONALD, HON. EDWARD MORTIMER, K.C., LL.D.—B. 1865; ed. Picton Acad. and Dalhousie Coll., N.S.; barrister; mem. of N.S. legis., 1897-1904; H. of C., 1904-17; re-el. to H. of C., 1921; min. of national defence in King adminstr., 1923.

MODONALD, F. H. E.—Asst. supt. pol., B. Honduras, July, 1919; ag. supt., pol., July, 1919 to July, 1920; supt., Belize fire brig., Oct., 1919; on mily. service in Egypt, Mesopotamia, and India, 1915-19 (ment. in desp.); Meritorious Ser. Medal, 1918; dist. comanr., Br. Honduras, 1928.

MACDONALD, HON. HECTOR Y.—B. 1876; ed., common schls. of Nova Scotia, and Univ. of St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Antigonish, N.S. (B.A., 1900); studied law in the law schl. of Dalhousie Univ.; called to bar of N.S. 1903; practised in Sydney, N.S., removed to Regina, 1906; called to bar of Sask., 4th May, 1906; law clk. in atty.-gen's. dept.; city solr., Regina; K.C., 1913; justice, ct. of King's Bench, Sask., 2nd Mar., 1918.

MACDONALD, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1858; ed. coll. inst., Stratford, Ontario, Toronto Univ., Osgoode Hall, Toronto; called to Ontario bar, 1889; Brit. Columbia bar, 1897; K.C., 1906; bencher, Brit. Columbia Law Society, 1906-7; elec. to legis., Brit. Columbia, 1903; re-elec. 1907; leader of the opposition, 1903 to 1909; ch. just., ct. of appeal, Brit. Columbia, 1909.
McDONALD, JOHN, D.F.C., B.Sc. (hons.).—Myologist, agrl. dept., Kenya, Sept., 1920.
McDONALD, JOHN.—B. 1881; senr. postmr., Kenya and Uganda, 1921; col. postmr. and supt. of tels., Fiji, 1925; P.M.G., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1927.

MACDONALD, HON. JOHN ALEXANDER, P.C. (Can.).—B. 1874; min. of pub. wks. and highways, P.K.L., 1923-26; dir., national serv. and hon. fuel and food control admtr. for P.R.I. during war; 1st el. to P.E.I. legis., 1908; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, 1926; min. without portfolio in Meighen adminstr., 1926; min. without portfolio in Bennett cabinet, 1930.

MACDONALD, JOHN WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1883; ed. Timaru Main and Hihi Schls., N.Z.; barrister; ent. dept. of justice, N.Z., 1900; asst. solr., pub. trust office, 1906; solr., 1909; asst. pub. trustee, 1917; pub. trustee, 1920; also comanr., rural intermediate credit, 1928.

MACDONALD, W. G. MACLEOD.—Imp. post office, 1899; transf'd. to combined post and tel. serv. of E. Africa and Ugandas, 1908; dep. postmr., 1910; ch. clk. and storekeeper, 1913; postmr., 1913; in charge radio tels., Lake Victoria area, 1914; 1st cls. postmr., 1918; asst. postmr.-gen., 1922; ag. P.M.G., 1926.

MACDONALD, W. M., O.B.E. (1926), M.R.C.S. (England), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B.1870; ed. Liverpool coll., and St. Bart.'s Hosp., London; house physician, St. Bart.'s, 1896; ag. med. offr., Montserrat, 1897; ditto, Antigua, 1899; med. offr., Montserrat 1901; med. offr., burgher refugee camp, Klarksdorp, Transvaal, 1902; med. offr., Antigua, 1904; med. offr., St. Kitts, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts, 1906; med. supt., hosp., etc., St. Johns, 1913; med. offr. of health, St. Johns, 1914; surgeon-lieut., Antigua defence force, 1914.

MACDONELL, Sir PHILIP JAMES, Kt. BACH. (1925), B.C.L., M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Clifton Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schol.; 1st cls. hist. schl., 1894); of Gray's Inn, utter barrister; Bacon schol.; I. of C. prizeman, constitutional law; called to the bar, 26th Jan., 1900; pub. pros., Transvaal, Apr., 1906; asst. crown pros., Transvaal, Feb., 1907; legal adviser and pub. pros., N. W. Rhodesia, Oct., 1908; legal adviser and pub. pros., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1911; ag. judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, Apr. to Oct., 1914, Apr. to July, 1916 June to Nov., 1917; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, 13th Sept., 1918; ohmn., Native Reserves Comans., 1924-25 (Fast Luangwe) and 1926-7 (Railway Dist.); ch. just., Trinidad, 1927; ch. just., sup. ct., Ceylon, 1930.

McDONNELL, MICHAEL FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1882; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and St. John's Coll., Camb. (scholar), B.A., 1904; pres., Cambridge Union, 1904; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1908; asst. dist. comanr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; pol. mag. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Apr., 1913; legal adviser, Gambia, July, 1918; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918; attv.-gen., Apr., 1920; ag. ch. just., circuit judge and judge of ct. of appeal on various occasions, 1919-23; ch. just., Palestine, 1927.

McDOUGALL, ARCH.—B. 1889; ed. West of Scot. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; civ. engr., P. and W. Anderson, rly. contractors, 1906-11; asst. engnr., Caledonian Rly., 1911-12; M.I.E.S., 1914; engr., way and works dept., Demerara Rly. Co., Br.
