Hong Kong, 1919; lect. on path. and med. jurisprudence, Hong Kong Univ., 1919; Milner research fellowship, L.S.H. and T.M., 1925; lect., trop. disease, Westminster hosp. med. schl., 1926; examr., D.M.T. and H. Eng., 1927; med. sec., col. med. research comte, 1928.

SCOTT, HERBERT SEPTIMUS.—B. 1873; ed. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; M.A.; inspr. of schools, Transvaal, 1902; examr., 1908; sec. to educat. dept., Transvaal, 1911; dir. of educ., Transvaal, 1924; dir., educ., Kenya, 1928.

SCOTT, JOHN, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admg., the govt., Nov., 1903; offr. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1904; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1905; Mannar, Apr., 1906; pol. mag., Hatton-Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1907; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1910; ditto, Hambantota, May, 1911; dist. judge, Negombo, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1913; asst. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, Sept., 1913; addtl. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1914; attached to censor's off., Apr., 1915; censor, Sept., 1915; prin. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1924; col. sec., S. Stlitms, 1928.
SCOTT, MARGARET.—Ed. Sherbourne Schl. for Girls; V.A.D. nurse, Brit. R. Cross, 1915-19; housekeeper's dipl., Edin. Sch. of Domestic Econ., 1920; matron, Achimota, 1925.

SCOTT, R. B., M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1914; ment. in desps., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr. of health, Apr., 1924.

SCOTT, THOMAS.—Ck., aud. office, Transvaal, Feb., 1901; inspr., Jan., 1904; prin. ck., rev. div., treasy., Oct., 1904; rev. inspr., Mar., 1906; prin. ck., audit office, Dec., 1908; chief inspr. of expenditure audit, control and audit office, Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; ag. asst. contr. and audr.-gen., Feb. to Aug., 1921 and May to Oct., 1922; asst. contr. and audr. gen., Oct., 1927.

SCOTT, WALTER RICHARDSON.—B. 1899; ed. Durham, 1922; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1922; passed final exam. in Chinese, 1924; asst. supt. pol., 1924; J.P., 1925; attd. Indian pol. and passed in Urdu 1926; passed Punjabi, 1928; prty. sec. to O.A.G., in addn., 1928; ditto to gov. in addn., Jan. to Mar., 1929; hon. A.D.C., to gov., 1929.

SCOTT-HALL, CAPT. EDWARD ALAN GRAHAM, R.E., R. of O., B.Sc., M.I.M. Cy. E.—B. 1883; asst. survr., roads, Gold Coast, 1908; senr. engrnr., 1910; war serv., G.C., Def. Force, 1914; 63rd divn. (B.K.F.), 16th divn. (M.K.F.), 1915-19; twice ment. in desps.; prov. engrnr., Gold Coast, 1919; senr. pub. health engrnr., 1926; asst. D.P.W., Nigeria, 1927.
SCRAGG, JOHN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; schol., 2nd cls., philos., politics and economics, 1925; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

SCRIVENER, RUPERT FREDERIC.—B. 1890; assoc., R. Schl. of Mines, dipl., Imp. Coll. Science, assoc., I.M.M., assoc., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy; survey work, England and Alaska, 1911-14; comand, serv., 1914-20; capt., 1918; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Mesopotamia, India and Palestine; commanded, 29th Rly. Coy., Sappers and Miners, I.A.; asst. ch. engnr., Palestine rlys., 1920; engnr., ways and wks., Palestine rlys., 1924.

SCRIVENOR, JOHN BROOKE.—B. 1876; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; served on geol. surv. of the U.K. in 1902-03; geologist, F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. senr. warden, mines, F.M.S., in addn., Mar.-Sept., 1908; served in the Army in England and France from Dec., 1916 to end of war as 2nd lieut., R.E. signal service; on sp. duty for state of Kelantan, May, 1924; dir., geological survey, F.M.S., Dec. 1927.
SCRIVENOR, T. V.—B. 1908; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1930; apptd. temp. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930 prior to serv. as an admstr. offr., N. Rhodesia.
SCULLIN, JAMES HENRY.—El. to Commonwealth H. of R. for Corangamite, Victoria, 1910; defeated, gen. el., 1913; M.H.R. for Yarra, Victoria, since 1922; prime min. of the C'wealth of Australia, 1929.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH, M.B.E.—B. 1888; clk. to res. commr., New Hebrides, 1902; inspr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. asst. to res. commr., 1911-12 and 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 1915, to Dec., 1922; ag. asst. to res., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., constab., Aug., 1922; dep. commr., W. Pacific (New Hebrides), Nov., 1922; in ch. of accts., May to Aug., 1923; ag. asst. to res. oomsnr., May, 1923 to Aug., 1924 and from 18th Aug., 1927; dist. agt. and lab. inspr., Apr., 1923; agt., Efate dist. and commdt., pol. (Br. divn.) 1st Apr., 1925.

SEALY, GORDON COURTLAND MILLER.—B. 1863; clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1889; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confid. clk. to the admstr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., surg-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.; 4th class clk., treasy. dept., 1st June, 1901; 3rd class clk., ditto, 14th May, 1906.

SEALY, JAMES ERNEST CAMERON.—B. 1871; 2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to oomsnrs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treasy., June, 1893; postmtr. of Tobago, Dec., 1895; clk. to sub-recev., Tobago, Jan., 1899; clk. to warden, Tobago, Sept., 1899; ch. clk. and cashier, treasy., Tobago, Feb., 1904.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cls. hist. tripes, pt. I, 1914 and pt. II, 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; asst. prin., C.O., 1922; pvt. sec. to parly. under sec. of State, D.O., 23rd Sept., 1929; ag. prin., D.O., 1st Oct., 1930.

SEDWICK, N. A.—B. 1890; ed. at Univ. Coll. Schl., London, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb.: B.A., London and Camb.; cadet, S.S.lims; civ. serv., Dec., 1913; dep. to supt., govt. monopolies dept., Jan., 1914; in charge of govt. opium factory, May to Aug., 1914; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. asst. supt., govt. monopolies dept., 1916; passed cadet (Malay), Mar., 1917; attached to col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasin, Malacca, Sept., 1917; collr. of war tax, Singapore, July, 1918; supermyn. offr., cts. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. treas. and comrn. of stamps, Penang, Dec., 1920.

SEED, CHARLES WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1898; ed. St. John's (R.C.) Coll., Womersley, Surrey; Artists' Rifles, 1916; 2nd lieut., 12th London Regt., 1917; served in France, 1918-19; (G.S. and Victory Meds.); col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; ag. senr. asst. audr., July-Sept., 1926; asst. audr., Nyassaland, Dec., 1926; ag. senr. audr. audr., June, 1927 to July, 1928 and Oct., 1928 to Sept., 1929; audr., Seychelles and Somaliland, July, 1930.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macclesfield Grammar Schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mily. serv., Cheshire Regt., in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; higher divn. clk. under reconstruction scheme, and apptd. to air miny., 28th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as asst. prin., 9th Jan., 1922; sec., managing comtee., imp. bureau of entomology; pvt. sec. to parly. under secy. of state for the Cola. (Mr. Wm. Lunn, M.P.), 10th June, 1929.

SEELY, MAJOR-GEN. RT. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carbineers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (deeps., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; recd. gold medal (French govt.) for saving life at sea, 1891; parly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1903; chmn. of comtee. to inquire into organisation of crownagentsoffice, 1908; under-sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; joint parly. sec. to ministry of munitions, 1918; under-sec. of state for air, Jan., 1919.
SEEBULT, JOSEPH EMANUEL.—B. 1881; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., Jan., 1916; asst. censor, 1914; censor, Apr., 1918; man. sec., agric. bank, Jan., 1928.

SEEBULT, LEO GABRIEL, M.I.C.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; asst. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., June, 1917; gen. asst. to asst. D.P.W., Jan., 1925.
SEIGNORET, G. B.—Clk. to postmr., Dominica, 1889; govt. offr., treasy., 1893; ch. clk., registr.'s off., 1895; ag. postmr., 1896-7; clk. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treasy., 1901.

SELBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1905), THE RT. HON. WM. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1869; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cola., June, 1896; chmn., Pacifico cable comtee., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtee., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov. 12th, 1900; high comrnr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.

SILDON, ARTHUR ARNOLD.—B. 1889; ed. Charterhouse and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A.(hons.); asst. dist. comrnr., B.A.P., Dec., 1913; capt., A.R.A., 1917-18; sec., famine comtee., Nairobi, 1919; personal asst. to ch. native comrnr., 1920; 1st grade admstive offr., 1926; H.M.'s ag. consul., S. Abyssinia, 1928.

SELLS, MARTIN PERBONET, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E. M.I.Loco., E.—B. 1887; ed. Trent Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; junr. astt. loco. supt., Lancs and Yorks. Rly.; temp. major R.E., 1917; supt., light rly., France, 1917-1919 (twice ment. in despatches); dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika Terry. rly., 1919; ag. ch. mech. engr., Tanganyika Terry. rly., 1920; ch. mech. engr., Gold Coast rly., 1922; ch. mech. engr., Nigerian Rly., 1926.

SELWYN-CLARKE, PERCY SELWYN, M.C., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Eng.) with distinc., D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), barrister-at-law.—Ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp. and Lond. Univ.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1919; med. offr., health, W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; senr. sany. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1924; awarded Langley Memorial Prize, 1924; dep. dir., sany. serv., May to Aug., 1927; del. for Gold Coast to Yellow Fever confee., Dakar, Senegal, 1928; visited U.S.A. and Canada at invitation of Rockefeller Foundation, 1928; asst. dir., sany. serv., June, 1929; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; awarded Médaille d'Argent des Épidémies for work in connection with outbreak of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1929; ch. health offr., F.M.S., 1930.

SEMPER, D. H., I.S.O. (1930).—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1883; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treasy., Oct., 1902; fed. treasy., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. reg., Leeward Is., and reg., and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, reg. of titles, reg., of friendly societies, and reg. gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. reg., and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, and reg. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1908; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1906; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.I.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911; escheator-gen., 1911; offr. in charge of prison discipline, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911; addtl. mag., Dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916; mag. and coroner dist. D., 30th Nov., 1921.
SEMPILL, CHARLES IGNATIUS, M.M.—B. 1825; Australian Forces, 1914-19; asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1920.

SEMPILL, J. HOWARD, M.R.E. (1925)—B. 1877; ed. Stirling High Schol.; ch. of pol., Bermuda, 1914; ch. of pol. and provost marshal, 1916; ditto and marshal, sup. ot. in admv., 1920; supt., pol., Br. Honduras, 1927; ag. supt., prisons, May to Sept., 1930.

SEMPLE, J. M., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., T.C.D., L.M. (Rotunda).—Sandhurst, 1915; lieutenant, R. Irish Rifles, 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922; med. offr., Zanzibar, Oct., 1924; ag. S.S.O., July, 1925 to Oct., 1926; ag. D.D.S.S., Feb. to Aug., 1928; and Nov., 1928 to Aug., 1929.

SENEVIRATNE, DIONYSIUS BARTHOLOMEW, M.M.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Nov., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Bo'aloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala Kach., Nov., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamwura, June, 1922; office astt., Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1923; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1926; astt. govt. agt., Ratnapura and addnl. astt. govt. agt., Kegalle, Jan., 1929; dist. judge, N. Kilya, Jan., 1930; ditto, Anuradhapura, Sept., 1930.

SENEVIRATNE, LEOPOLD JAMES DE SILVA, LL.B., Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1923; atttd., Matare kach., Dec., 1923; ditto, Jaffna kach., July, 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Puttalam, Mar., 1926; ditto, Pt. Pedro, May, 1927; astt. attnt. offr., Apr., 1929.

SENNITT, OEDRIC WILLIAM ARTHUR.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S. Stlitms., 1914; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, 1915; astt. censor, 1916; astt. regitr., impts. and expts., 1917; astt. supt., govt. monopolies, 1920; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., 1920; astt. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Raub, 1921; supernumy. offr., cl. IV., 28th Nov., 1921.
SERGEANT, JOHN.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief astnt.; pub. wks. dept., E. Africa Prot., 30th May, 1905.

SERTSIOS, B. D.—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1909; astt. king's advoc., June, 1924; title changed to astt. attty.-gen., May, 1925; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to May, 1927; puisane judge, Oct., 1927.

SETON, CLAUD RAMSAY WILMOT.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk, Laheham, Mar-gate and Univ. Coll., London; admitted solvr., sup. ct., England, 1910; on mily. serv., 1914-19; M.C., twice ment. in despa.; called to bar, Grays Inn, 1928; pres. dist. offr., Jaffa, Palestine, 1927; judl. adviser, Transjordan, Oct., 1926.

SEVERN, SIR CLAUD, K.B.E. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (Cantab.), LL.D., Hong Kong (1920).—B. 1869; temp. clk. in librarian's dept., Foreign Office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Stlitms., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1895; passed in Malay and law, 1898; astt. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; astt. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; astt. sec. to res-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr. to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906 to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to high commr., F.M.S. and priv. sec. to gov., S. Stlitms., May, 1907; sec. to high commr., Malay States, Feb., 1911; col. sec., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; admnstd. gort. on various occasions, 1912-13, 1916-19, 1921-22; ret., 1926.

SEVIGNY, HON. ALBERT, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.L.; advoc., city of Quebec; unsuccessful candidate, legis. assem., Quebec, bye-elec., 1907; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 3rd Mar., 1921.

SEYMOUR, ALFRED WALLACE.—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schl. and St. Andrew's Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1906; off. astt. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apl., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1911; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1912; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1913; ditto, Matale, Sept., 1915; on mil. duty, June, 1916; dist. judge, Chilaw, Aug., 1919; astt. govt. agt., Kegalla, Mar., 1920; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., May, 1921; reglar. gen., Dec., 1924; col. sec., Fiji, Dec., 1926; ag. gen., Fiji and high commr., W. Pacifico, Nov., 1927 to Apr., 1928 and from Apr. to Nov., 1929; gov's. dep., Jan. to Feb., 1930.

SEYMOUR-HALL, CHARLES CYRIL WILLIAM, F.R.S.A., F.R.G.S., F.R. Met. Soc., F.R.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut. R.A.F., 1917; ment. in despa., 1919; capt., H.M.A.Z. 31, 1919; demob., 1919; astt. dist. commr., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd astt. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. res., 1923-4; ag. astt. admstr.-gen., 1928-27; ag. astt. sec., pvt. sec. to Br. res., clk. of couns. and edfr., offl. gaz., 1923; ag. prov. commr. (with seat on leg. coun.) 1929 and 1930.

SHANNON, C.M.—B. 1869; astt. astnt., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1888; 5th cls. clk., G.P.O., June, 1889; ditto, treasy., June, 1890; 4th cls. clk., ditto, July, 1893; govt. offr. (mining regns.) and J.P., Apr., 1897; 3rd grade land surr., lands and mines dept., Aug., 1902; 3rd cls. clk., ditto, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Aug., 1908; 1st cls. clk., audit dept., Aug., 1913; ch. clk., P.W.D., July, 1920; supt., acts., corres. and stores, P.W.D., May, 1921; P.M.G., Feb., 1924.

SHARP, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. Coll. Schl. and King's Coll., London; comptr. and acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., 1907-12; acct., postal dept., S. Nigeria, 1912-14; acct., posts and tels. dept., N. and S. Provs. of Nigeria, 1914-19; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, 1920; on mily. serv., London Rifle Brig., 1908-12; Nigerian Land Contg., 1914-18; ch. acct., P. and T., Nigeria, 1920; ag. dep. P.M.G. in 1928-30.

SHARPE, SIR ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambezi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. commr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy commr., 1st Apl., 1896; and again acted as commr. and consul-gen., 16th Apl., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; commr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infi. N. of Zambesi, 15th July, 1897; recd. Jubilee medal, 1897; commr., commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; recd. Coronation medal, 1902; of Nyasaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; represented E. African Prot. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

SHARPE, EDMUND.—B. 1882; ed. Arnold House, N. Wales, Haileybury and Edinburgh University; probationer, N.K. Rhodesia, Nov., 1905; astt. native commr., Apr., 1906; native commr., Dec., 1910; astt. mag., Apr., 1916; ag. mag. on various occasions, 1919 to 1927; mag., June, 1927; J.P.; prov. commr., Apr., 1929.
SHARPE, H. B.—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya Colony, Apl., 1919; ag. dist. commr., 1926.

SHAW, GEORGE ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; scholar and senr. moderator in classics; vice-chancellor's prizeman in English prose; B.A.; LL.B.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1900; collr., land rev., Krian, 1901; mag. 1st cls., Perak, 1903; offr., Cls. V., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1906; collr., land rev., Lower Perak, 1908; adviser, land office, Kedah, and supt. of mines, 1911; offr., Cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; supernumy, offr., Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; lent to W.O., directorate of prisoners of war, Aug., 1916; adviser, land off., Kedah, 19th July, 1919; ag. commr., lands and mines, Johore, May, 1923; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1924; on sp. duty, Burma, Oct., 1925; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Dec., 1925; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Sept., 1927; gen. advr., Johore, Feb., 1929.
SHAW, JOHN HENRY DE BUROH, B.L.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton Schol., Dalkey, Dublin and Corrig Schol., Kingston; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 25th May, 1921.

SHAW, JOHN VALENTINE WISTAR.—B. 1894; ed. Repton Schol.; service with R.E., 1914-19; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1928; seconded for duty at C.O., Mar.-Sept., 1930.

SHAW, SIR WALTER SIDNEY, KT. BAOR.(1921).—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb., 1908, to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admstd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1911 to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., B. Honduras, 1912; puisne justice, Ceylon, Aug., 1914; ag. chief just., June to Oct., 1916; ch. just., S. Stltms., 23rd May, 1921; ret., 1926.

SHEEDY, CAPT. FREDERICK JOHN, M.B.E., B.So., M.R.C.V.S.—Ed. Clongowes, Queen's Coll., Cork; late capt., R.A.P. forces; ent. army, A.V.C. 1911; vety. offr., K.A.P., July, 1914; served throughout Great War, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1919 (E. Africa Mounted Rifles and E.A.V.C.); adjutant, E.A.V.C., 9th Jan., 1916; M.B.E. (mil.), 1918, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med.; dep. ch. vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; ag. ch. vety. offr., on various occasions 1920-21, 1923-24 and 1926; dep. dir., vety. services, Apr., 1927; ag. dir., vety. services, Jan. to Aug., 1929; prin. vety. offr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1930.

SHEEDY, ANDREW JOSEPH.—B. 1879; joined S. Stltms. pol., Jan., 1902; ag. 3rd cls. inspr., Singapore, Aug., 1904; 3rd cls. inspr., Penang, Mar., 1906; 2nd cls. inspr., Penang, Aug., 1907; 1st cls. inspr., Penang, July, 1908; ag. ch. inspr., Singapore, Aug., 1909; ag. ch. detect. inspr., Singapore, Apr., 1910; ch. detect. inspr., Singapore, July, 1910; ag. ast. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; ast. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; ag. supt. pol., Singapore, Oct. and Dec., 1920; senr. ast. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1925; dep. commr., pol., F.M.S., July, 1925; ag. supt. and ch. pol. offr., Penang, Dec., 1925; ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Sept., 1926; commr., pol., Johore, Nov., 1927; ch. pol. offr., S'pore., Sept 1928.

SHELDON, ROBERT PAUL.—B. 1880; ed. Harrow and in Germany; ag. vice-consul, Ghent, 1905; ag. vice-consul, Havre, 1906-7; ag. consul, Bordeaux, 1907; ast. collr., Zanzibar, 27th Dec., 1907; sec. to first minister, 1909-10; collr. (now dist. commr.), Pemba, 16th Oct., 1912; ag. mag., 30th Aug., 1915; ag. ch. sec., 5th Mar. to 17th Nov., 1919; senr. commr., Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 1923; 4th cls., Order of the Aliyeh and 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar; M.L.C.

SHELLEY, MALCOLM BOND.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1901; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1902; ag. ast. supt., immigrts., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. sec. to res.-gen., 1907; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1910; dist. offr., Batang Padang, 1911; commn. in M.S. Vol. Rifles, 1911; ag. ast. regt., sup. et., Ipoh, 1912; ag. dist. offr., Larut, 1914; ag. inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., 1914; capt., M.S.V.R., 1915; ag. dep. pub. pros., Perak, 1916; ag. mag., Ipoh, 1920; ag. assig. and pub. trustee, 1922; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, 1924; ag. treas., S.S., 1924; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, 1926; ag. dir., educm., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1926; chmn., trade statistics comttee., Aug., 1926; contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1926; treas., S.S., 1929.

SHELSWELL-WHITE, GEOFFREY HENRY.—B. 1897; ed. Whitgift Schol.; served with R.F.A. in India, Mesopotamia and Persia, 1916-18; ast. pol. offr., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; personal att. to Sir Arnold Wilson, ag. civ. commr., 1918; Iraq rebellio n, 1920; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to res., ag. 1st ast. sec., secretariat, elk. to prot. coun. and edr., off. gaz. at various periods; higher standard Swahili, 1925; pres., Pemba Swahili exam. bd., 1929; dist. regir., Pemba, 1930; officiated as res. mag., Pemba, 1930; called to bar, 1930.
SHELTON, C. F., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.D. (Lond.), B.S.—Civil surgeon, Serbian red cross, 1913; surgeon lient., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1921.
SHERGOLD, F. V.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., O.O., Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 1918 to 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SHERIDAN, JOSEPH.—Ed. at Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Ireland, 1907, went Connaught circuit; judcl. clk., Nyassaland Prot., July, 1908; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. judge of high ct., Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1911, to Mar., 1912; apptd. ast. to atty.-gen., Apl., 1912; ag. atty.-gen., Aug., 1912; town mag., R.A.F., 1913; ag. puisne judge, July, 1919; 3rd puisne judge, Kenya, Apr., 1920; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.
SHERINGHAM, GEOFFREY.—2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1899; resigned, 1908; war serv., Boer War, 1899-1902, France and G.E.A., 1916-18; K.A.R., 1918-19; ast. pol. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1919.
SHIELD, CHARLES GROFFEY.—B. 1896; on mily. service (France) 1914-18; on staff, 1918-19; priv. secy. to gov., and clk. to couns., Seychelles, 1920.
SHIELDS, W. P.—Labour inspection offr., K. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

SHIELS, DR T. DRUMMOND, M.C., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.B., Ch.B.); served European War as offr. R. Scotz.; in command with rank as capt. one of trench mortar batteries of 9th (Scottish) Divn. (severely wounded); was a mem., Edinburgh town coun.; M.P., East Edinburgh since 1924; mem., ap. coman., Ceylon constitution, 1927; parly. under sec., India Office, June, 1924; parly. under sec. for the Cola., Dec., 1929.

SHILL, A. T.—B. 1896; on active serv. with Rifle Brig. and Dorset Regt., 1915-17; wounded; Imp. cust. and excise dept., 1919; also dep. supt., mercantile marine, rear. of wreck, regar. shipping, Lascar transfer offr., etc. asst. rear. gen., Gambia, Aug., 1928.

SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. elk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; minor staff clk., 1911; regar. of col. laws, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., promotions, 19th Nov., 1928.

SHIRCORE, JOHN OWEN, C.M.G. (1926), M.B., Ch.B.—Med. offr., Nyassaland, 1908; transf. to Uganda, 1909; re-transf. as med. offr., Nyassaland, 1910; med. offr., E.A.P., 1912; dep. prin. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; ag. P.M.O., Mar.-Dec., 1924; P.M.O., Dec., 1924; dir., med. and sany. services, 1926.

SHIRLEY, EVILYN PHILIP SEWALLIS, O.B.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Columbus Coll., Rathfarnham, Dublin and R. Mily. Coll., Camberley; joined R. Irish Fusiliers, Dec., 1918; N. Persia and Iraq, 1920; seconded, K.A.R., Jan., 1923; 2/1st K.A.R.; Tanganyika and Somaliland Camel Corps; 3rd grade comsrr., Somaliland, Sept., 1929.

SHORTT, ADAM, C.M.G. (1911); M.A., F.R.S.C.—B. 1869; ed. at Walkerton High Schl. and Queen's Univ., Canada (B.A., 1883, M.A., 1886) and Glasgow and Edin. Univer.; asst. prof. of philosophy, Queen's Univ., 1886; lect. and prof. of polit. science, 1889-1908; civ. ser. comsrr., Canada, 1908-1917; chmn. of historical manuscripts publication board, Oct., 1917; writer on history, banking and economics; del. to internat. congress of history of America, Rio de Janeiro, 1922; mem. preparatory comsrr. for econ. confere., L. of N., Geneva, 1926.

SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newchurch Grammar Schl., Nottingham Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; prin. asst. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on orig. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. clk., store dept., I. O., Oct., 1900; transf. to judl. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to permitt. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; asst. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. asst. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924.

SIDEBOTHAM, JOHN BIDDULPH, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Stanhope exhib., open class exhib., 1911, schol., 1912); 1st cls., theolog. tripos, pt. I., 1914; 2nd lieut., Home Counties R.E. (T.F.), 1914; lieut., 13th June, 1916; serv. in France, 1914-15; wounded, Apr., 1915; demob., 1919; ent. inland rev., Somerset House, 7th Feb., 1920; transf. to C.O. as asst. prin. under reconstruction scheme, Dec., 1922; sec., managing comttee, bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1925; sec., East African guaranteed loan advisory comttee., 1927; pvt. sec. to parl., under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 15th Sept., 1928; pvt. sec., permitt. under sec. for the cols., 8th July, 1929, ag. prin., 24th Jan., 1930.

SIDNEY, HON. SIR THOMAS KAY, KT, BACH. (1930), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1863; ed. pvt. schls., Otago Boys' High Schl., and Otago Univ., N.Z.; barrister and solr., 1889; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1901-28; atty-gen. and leader of legis. coun., 1928.

SIDLEY, WILLIAM HENRY FRANCIS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1908; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; county inspr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until disbandment of that force, July, 1922; depot inspr. (1st cls.), Jamaica constab., Dec., 1922; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1923; ag. inspr.-gen., 1st June to 14th Oct., 1925.

SIKES, HOWARD LECKY, B.A., B.E. (hons. and ext.), R.U.I., M.I.C.E., F.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Bootham Schl., York; asst. engr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1907; res. engr., Mombasa water wks., 1911; engr. in ch., Magadi water wks. constrn., 1913; res. engr., Magadi rly., 1915; lieut., E.A. Pioneers, 1916; capt., 1916; govt. hydraulic engr., P.W.D., Kenya, Jan., 1919; dep. dir., pub. wks., Kenya, 1921; D.P.W. and offl. mem., leg. coun., 1923.

SILVA, POTHOUGWE OSMUND SAMUEL EDRESINGHE, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Puttalam kach., Mar., 1927; attd., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1927; ag. pol. mag., Kalutara, Nov., 1928; office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Aug., 1929.
SILVESTER, J. M.—Asst. dist. comsrr., Kenya, E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. comsrr., Kenya, 1926.

SIMMONS, BENJAMIN ROBERT—Clk. in conservator of forest's office, E. conservancy, Transvaal, 1898; ag. dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1902; dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1906; ditto George, 1914; 2nd grade conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1929.

SIMMONS, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1877; ed. at Malvern and Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1900; in charge of treasy., Seremban, May, 1901; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Aug., 1901; ag. sec. sany. bd., Seremban, Apr., 1902; ag. inspr. of schls. in addn., 1904; 1st asst. collr. of land rev., Seremban, Jan., 1905; ag. asst. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, May, 1909; comsrr. of cust., Johore, Dec., 1910; comsrr. of lands and mines, Johore, Oct., 1914 to June, 1920; sec. to comsrr. of enquiry, Trengganu, Sept., 1918; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1921; attd. to col. secretariat, Singapore, June, 1921; 1st asst. sec. A., S. Sttlmts., June, 1921; ag. finan. comsrr., Johore, Dec., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., S. Sttlmts., May, 1922; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Kinta, July, 1923; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1924; ag. Br. advisor, Trengganu, May, 1925; Br. advr., Trengganu, Sept., 1926; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1928; Br. res., Negri Sembilan, June, 1928; ag. Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1929 to Apr., 1930.

SIMMONS, HON. WM. CHAS.—B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1895; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; resig., 1908; puisane judge of sup. ct., Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.
SIMMS, HENRY, M.V.O. (1917), A.M.I.C.E. (1913).—B. 1881; sany. engr., Gold Coast, 1908; sany. engr., Sierra Leone, 1912; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1919; D.P.W., Jamaica, 1926.
SIMPSON, JOHN.—Imp. tel. serv., Jan., 1892; transf., post office, Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1895; surrv., posts and tels., Mar., 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Aug., 1921.
SIMS, JAMES HUGH.—B. 1878; sub.-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1906; inspr., 1913; asst. commr., 1.18.

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN.—B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mem. of swine comm. which visited Europe in 1909; unsucc. cand., legis. ass., P.E.L., 1908; el. to H.C., g.c., 1917; re-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admin., 29th Dec., 1921.
SINCLAIR, HON. SIR J. R., KT. BACH. (1918).—B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schl., N.Z.; barrister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and since 1918; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Com., 1912.

SINCLAIR, ST. CLAIR OVERBECK, M.A., F.I.C., D.Sc.—Cik., atty.-gen's. office, 1893; clk., col. sec's. office, 1894; analyst, 1897; transf. dept. of agr., 1907; transf., dept. of int., 1911; gov't. analyst, Cape Town, 1918; transf., dept. of agr., 1st Apr., 1923; chief of divn. of chemistry, 3rd Mar., 1925.

SISSETT, SIR HERBERT KORTRIGHT MCDONNELL, KT. BACH. (1927).—Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Temp., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; practised at bar, Jamaica, Mar., 1898, to Apr., 1903, during which period acted as res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; as priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, May to June, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. gov'r., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apr., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, as res. mag., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, as res. mag., Manchester, Apr. to June, 1904, as res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1904, for two and a half months in 1905, and from June to July, 1906; regar.-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. commr. of Belize, Apr., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, 8th Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie riot, 1913, and was highly commended by S. of S.; ag. atty.-gen., 1919-20; seconded, puissant judge, Jamaica, 1920; 1st puissant judge, 1921; ch. just., Br. Hond., 1922; compiled Br. Hond. sup. ct. rules, 1926; apptd. arbitrator by gov'ts. of U.S.A. and Guatemala in case of P.W. Shufeldt v. the gov't. of Guatemala.

SITTAMPALAM, CATHIVAVELU, B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1923; attd., Kalutara kach., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office asst., Puttalam kach., Aug., 1926; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1928; ditto, Puttalam, Oct., 1929; asst. gov't. agt., Kurunegala, Nov., 1929.

SKEEKEN, DONALD TRENCH, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1883; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., F.M.S., grade III, May, 1912; med. offr., grade II, Dec., 1912; war service, 1915-19; med. offr., Batu Gajah, Sept., 1919; senr. med. offr., Negri Sembilan, May, 1921; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, Feb.-Oct., 1924.

SKEETE, 'COLL. CARRINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; 3rd class hon. nat. sci. tripod pt. I., 1922; dipl. course, Schl. of Agr., Cambridge, 1922-23; asst. dir., agr., Barbados, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., agr., in 1921 and 1925; asst. dir., sci. and agr., Sept., 1925; ag. dir. sci. and agr., on various occasions, 1920-29.

SKINNER, ANTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Schl.; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-inspr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apr., 1915; o/c cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. inspr. of schl., Leeward Is., Apr., 1918 to Feb. 1920; clk. to commr., clk. of couns. and educnl. distr. off., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., Antigua and supt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. ast. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns. Leeward Is. on various occasions 1921-24; senr. bursar, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, 1927.

SKINNER, H. AVERY.—B. 1871; ed. Hurstpierpoint and Ardingly Colls.; asst. acctnt., treas., N. W. Rhodesia, June, 1907; ag. acctnt., N. Rhodesia, on various occasion, 1917-21; asst. audr., Feb., 1921; ag. audr. on various occasions, 1921-30.

SKINNER, LESLIE ERNEST.—Gloucestershire R.F.A., 3rd Apr., 1903 to 9th July, 1910; B.S.A. Pol., 22nd Aug., 1910 to 21st Aug., 1913; K.A.P. Police, 17th Sept., 1913; asst. supt., 1st Apr., 1915; staff offr. to inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Kenya Colony and Uganda Prot., 1st May, 1920 to 28th Feb., 1922; qualified in senior captain's course, Schl. of Musketry, Hythe, Sept., 1921; supt. pol., Nyasaland, 11th Nov., 1922; ag. ch. commr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, 17th May, 1923 to 31st Jan., 1924; asst. commr., pol., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; ag. commdt., pol. and gov', prisons on various occasions, 1925-30.

SKINNER, ROBERT BURNELL.—B. 1893; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; held various minor appts., L'Edward Is., 1911-20; ch. clk. to admr., and clk., couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and clk., couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; asc., loco bd., May, 1926; mem., Roseau town bd., Apr., 1927; commr., income tax, May, 1927; pvt. sec. to Sir Eustace Fiennes, Nov., 1927 to June, 1929; ag. treas., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; offl. mem., exec. and leg. coun., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Montserrat, Feb., 1930; ag. treas. and offl. mem., exec. and leg. coun., May to Sept., 1930.

SKINNER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER GEORGE.—B. 1865; joined N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1879; offr.-in-charge, Lyttelton prison printing office, 1849; overseer, govt. printing office, 1906; supt., 1909; govt. printer, 1923.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; served nine years in 91st Highrs.; certif. of mil. eng., 1896; on Boer prisoners of war staff as warrant offr., S. Africa and St. Helena, Nov., 1899 to Oct., 1902 (Queen's med. and clasp); inspr. of pol. gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helena, Mar., 1903; served in various offl. capacities in St. Helena, Antigua, Montserrat; and Dominica, 1906-14; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Islands; ag. mag., Dist. F., May-Aug., 1914; capt. (2nd in command), Dominica Defence Force, 4th Aug., 1914;

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CROIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1906).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirioi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. commr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. commr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1896; ag. res. commr., Dec., 1900; res. commr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SMALL, ALEXANDER SYM.—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1912; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., Krian, Jan., 1916; ag. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1916; ag. asst. adviser, Endau, Johore, Sept., 1918; ditto Muar, Johore, June, 1920; ag. asst. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1922; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1923; town-planning admstr., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1926; ag. finan. commr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. II, finan. commr. and audr.-gen., Johore, June, 1927.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical asst., astt. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical asst., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; ag. astt. M.O.H., 1916; ditto, in addition to other duties, 1916; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examiner in Physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Lai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr. govt. civil hosp., 1923; med. offr., lunatic asylum, 1923; ag. port med. offr., 1924; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, med. offr. in charge pub. mortuary, Kowloon and Kowloon pub. hosp., 1925; med. offr. in charge, Victoria hosp. for women and children, 1927; ag. dep. dir., med. and sanry. services, 1928.
SMART, L. M.—B. 1889; astt. astt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., 26th Mar., 1927.
SMARTT, FITZPATRICK FORBES PERCY.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; astt. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921.

SMIT, HAROLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., 1st Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores commr., 1910; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. o.k., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. inspr., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., 19th May-31st Oct., 1924.

SMIT, JACOBUS STEPHANUS.—B. 1878; D.T.D. (Dekoratie Trouwe Dienst) Anglo-Boer War; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; pte. sec. to Gen. Smuts as col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; to Gen. Botha as premier, Transvaal, 1907 and to Hon. J. de Villiers as atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1908; astt. mag., Transvaal, 1909; astt. mag., Union civ. serv., 1910; resig., 1915; el. mem. for Klerksdorp, Union of S. Africa legis. assem., 1920, 1921 and 1924; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1925; admstr., Transvaal, Mar., 1929.

SMITH, LINUT. ARTHUR BERTRAM, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. R. Masonic Schl., Bushey; sub-lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; ag. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Baltic submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Russian Order of St. Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian Order of St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with swords), 1916; commanded submarine C 14 (N. Sea), Mar. to Nov., 1918, and various surrendered German submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; port offr., Dar-es-Salaam, Mar., 1919; harbmr. and supt. of lighthouses, Trinidad, 27th Feb., 1926.

SMITH, B. O.—Cler. astt., post. dept., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; sec., leave and pensions coman., 1920; sec., deptl. salaries comtee., 1921; gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk. and sec. currency comanrs., July, 1923; transf'd. to col. secretariat, Apr., 1925; acctant., sup. ct., Oct., 1925.

SMITH, CHARLES BERNARD.—B. 1887; ed. Bedford House Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford (M.A.); supt. educn., N. Nigeria, 1913; attd., Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; senr. supt., 1919; ag. dir., educn., N. Provs., 1926; astt. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1927; ag. dep. dir., educn., Nigeria in 1928 and 1929; dep. dir., ditto, 1930.
SMITH, CLIVE ALEXANDER.—B. 1903; ed. Malton Grammar Schl.; R. Navy, 1919; sub. inspr., Jamaica constab., Dec., 1924; 3rd cls. inspr., May, 1930.

SMITH, HON. DAVID STANLEY, LL.M.—B. 1888; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); on serv. with N.Z. exped. force, 1917-18; barrister, solic., and notary pub. till apptd. judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1928; examr. in law, univ. of N.Z., 1920-27; author of "Adoption of Children in N.Z." in Journal of Comparative Legislation, Oct. 1921.
SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., Dec., 1913; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
SMITH, EMILE HAMMEL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., Dec., 1912.

SMITH, ERNEST GARDINER, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.; prizeman in constit. law and hist.; astt. dist. comanr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1908; astt. pol. mag., Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., Jan., 1914; transf'd. to legal dept., Sept., 1914; ag. mtrl.-gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921, and Aug., 1921 to Mar., 1922; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, Aug., 1922; puisane judge, G. Coast, June, 1925; compiled 1928 edition Laws of Gold Coast, Ashanti, etc.

SMITH, ERNEST JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. E. London Tech. Coll.; mily. serv., Gold Coast Volunteers, 1914-18; astt. acct., constrn., Gold Coast rly., 1920; ch. acct., 1922; dep. ch. constrn. acct., Nigerian rly., 1929; astt. ch. acct., 1930.

SMITH, FRANCIS GORDON—B. 1886; ed. Bromsgrove Schl.; solr., Jan., 1909; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, July, 1921; legal dept., N. Rhodesia, 1913; on active serv. in East Africa with N. R. Police, July, 1915 to July, 1918; held various appts. in legal dept., N. Rhodesia during B.S.A.Co's admtn., including regsr., high ct. etc., and astt. legal adviser and pub. pros.; astt. atty.-gen., Oct., 1924; solr.-gen., Kenya, Sept., 1926; atty.-gen., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1927; ag. judge, high ct., Sept., 1927 to Mar., 28; mem., exc. and leg. couns.

SMITH, F. M. URLING.—B. 1876; ed. at St. John's, Leatherhead, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; schoolmaster in England, Scotland and Wales from 1900 to 1906; served in ed. dept., Egypt, 1906 to 1910; supt. of Nassarawa schools, N. Nigeria, 28th Sept., 1910; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1913; dir. of educn., Northern Provs. 24th Aug., 1919.

SMITH, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1914), C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1858; entd. war office, 10th Apr., 1878; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 24th Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; astt. to ch. sec., Dec., 1883; ag. dir. of survey, Aug., 1886 to Feb., 1887; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1886 to Nov., 1891; ag. ch. sec., and mem. of exec. coun., May to Oct., 1888, and on other occasions; comanr., Papho, Nov., 1891; registr.-gen. and offl. mem. of legis. coun., June, 1895; has served also as mem. of comtee. of management, Cyprus museum, 1886; sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comtee., 1886; sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comtee., 1887; mem. of land registra. comanr., 1891; pres. mun. comanr., Papho, 1892; mem. of ecclesiastical corp. comanr., 1896; mem. of mun. comanr., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of gen. hosp. bd., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of bd. of agric., 1896; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf. under conven. with Turkey, Mar., to Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903, and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; Br. del. of Evcaf., July, 1905; ag. recr.-gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1905, 1906, 1907 and 1909; col. sec., Mauritius, June, 1910; administered govt., Apr., to Nov., 1911; gov., Nyasaland, May, 1913; assumed govt., Sept., 1913; ret., Dec., 1923.

SMITH, CAPT. GEORGE SCHLIM.—B. 1871; ed. Rathmines Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 5th batt. R. Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1895; hon. capt. in army, 1900; astt. inspr., Hausa force (G. Coast batt.), Jan., 1897; sub.-inspr., Br. Guiana pol., Feb., 1898; dist. inspr., Oct., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir J. A. Swettenham, 1902-04; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, 1904-06; astt. dist. supt., pol., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1906; astp., pol., Apr., 1914; comdt. pol. and gov. of prisons, Zanzibar, May, 1920-26; pte. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron, gov., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; 1914-15 Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory med.; King's pol. med., 1924.
SMITH, HARRY ALSTON.—B. 1886; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1913; sub.-inspr. Basutoland mounted pol., 1919; war serv., 1914-18; inspr., 1930.

SMITH, HARRY LESTER.—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., O.R.C., May, 1903; clk., col. sec.'s office, O.R.C., Aug., 1903; clk., gov'ts. office, O.R.C., 1907; clk., gov.-gen.'s. office, Union of S.A., May, 1910; prin. clk., July, 1924; ch. clk., Nov., 1926; ag. sec. to gov.-genl., Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1927; ch. clk., off. of high comanr. for S.A., Jan., 1928.

SMITH, HERBERT DENSHAM.—B. 1882; entd. Impl. civ. serv. and apptd. to acct. genl's. dept., Admy., 1901; astt. acct., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1910; acct., P.W.D., Sierra Leone, 1914; transf'd. to treasy., Sierra Leone, as astt. treas., Jan., 1916; ag. senr. astt. treas. for various periods in 1917, 1918 and 1919; ag. ch. acct., rly., May to Aug., 1922; ag. treas. and currency offr., 1919 and 1923; recr. gen. and currency offr., Gambia, Jan., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns.
SMITH, HUGH MIDDLETON BRICE, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. at Pocklington Schl. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; astt. res., N. Nigeria Jan., 1909; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Oct., 1917; ag. mag. in 1921 and 1922.
SMITH, JAMES.—B. 1882; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub-insp., Basutoland mounted pol., 1917; inspr., 1926.

SMITH, JAMES ALFRED, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1871; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1891; promoted to higher grade, 1902; ast. sec. to col. regns. comttee., 1907-8; staff clk., 1st grade, 1915; staff offr., Apr., 1922.

SMITH, JAMES LANDETH.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, 13th July, 1891; draftsman, survey offr., Sept., 1893; warden, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1896, to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, 19th Aug., 1901; ast. supt. of wks., 1st Apr., 1902; comntr. of crown lands, 28th June, 1904; survr. of crown lands, 1904; ag. supt. of wks., 14th June, 1906, to 16th Jan., 1907; supervisor, govt. cotton ginnery, 1st Oct., 1912; ag. sup. pub. wks., St. Vincent, 4th Oct., 1913; col. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; supt., pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevs., Sept., 1920.

SMITH, JOHN.—B. 1883; ed. New Vety. Coll., Edinburgh (M.R.C.V.S.), and Liverpool Univ. (D.V.H.); vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; capt.; reapp., Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921-May, 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. coun.; ag. sec. for agr., Apr., 1925; J.P.; dir., animal health, Nov., 1929.

SMITH, JOHN NOEL.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks. Light Infantry, 1909; seconded for serv. as ast. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; attd., Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, Mar., 1915; station mag., N. Prova., Mar., 1919; dist. offr., Jan., 1921; ag. senr. res., Sept., 1923.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; entd. regyay. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1919; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923; ag. higher-grade cler. offr., 1st Mar., 1930.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEA.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to comntr., Turks I., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regir. of shipping) comntr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regir. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. ast. comntr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of countr., 1913; ag. ast. ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. ast. postmr.-gen., 1914; seconded for serv. as British postmaster at Shanghai, 1914; ag. postmr.-gen., 1915; seconded for military service 1916-19; ag. deputy regir. and appraiser, 1919; ag. supr. Victoria Gaol, 1919; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; read. Chinese order of Wen-hu, 5th cls., 1920; qualified in Pekinese, Mar., 1920; supt., impts. and expts., 1921; attd., Geneva opium conf., 1923; head of sany. dept., 1924; P.M.G., Oct., 1923; 2nd ast. col. sec. and dep. clk. of countr., 1930.

SMITH, P. C., M.I.Munic.K. (Lond.)—B. 1879; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil with Messrs. Kelites and Fosbrooks, archs. and survrs., Leicester, for five years, and remained for two years as ast.; joined staff of Messrs. Johnson and Langley, contrs., Lond.; ast. to Mr. C. F. Wike, M.I.C.E., city engnr., Sheffield, 1902; town engnr., Rainy River, Out., Can., 1910; town engnr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engnr., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1915.
SMITH, PETER HAMBLIN, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1900; ed. Denstone coll. and Lincoln coll., Oxford; ast. dist. comntr., Sierra Leone, 5th Sept., 1923.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON.—B. 1898; ed. Chatham House, Ramagate; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C., Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Indian Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Aden Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919.

SMITH, ROBERT, LL.B., Barrister and Solicitor.—B. 1886; ed. Pub. Schls., Nanaimo, B.C.; Osgoode Hall and Toronto Univ. (LL.B., 1912); called to bar, Ontario, 1912; called to bar, Br. Columbia, 1913; M.L.A., Vancouver, Br. Columbia.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. higher grade cler. offr., 29th Oct., 1930.

SMITH, STEPHEN HENRY, C.B.E. (1929), F.R. Aus. H.S.—B. 1865; ed. Grafton Superior Pub. Sch.; inspr., continuation schls., N. S. Wales, 13th June, 1912 to 29th Feb., 1920; ast. under sec., educn., dept., N.S.W., June, 1920; dir., educn., N.S.W., 1923.

SMITH, STEPHEN JOHN.—B. 1887; N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1903; internal affrs. dept., 1913; sec., Samoan admnstr., 1916; ob. clk., external affrs. dept., since 1920; ch. clk., Cook Is. dept., 1920-28; sec., 1928.
SMITH, HON. SYDNEY GEORGE.—B. 1879; ed. public schl., New Plymouth, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. house of reps., 1918-25 and since 1928; chmn., comtees., 1928; min., lab. and immgrn., 1930.

SMITH, WALTER JOHN.—B. 1884 ast. supt. wks., P.W.D., S. Stiltna, Jan., 1908; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1909; ex. engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1917; ex. engrnr., grade III, July, 1908; ag. state engrnr., Kedah, Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; sen. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. state engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1927 to May, 1928; senr. exec. engrnr., Kinta, June, 1929.

SMITH, WILLIAM HARDEN, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (T.C.D.), I.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (R.O.P. and S.I.), D.T.M. (Bengal).—ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; surg. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1918; med. offr., Zanzibar, Nov., 1925; ag. D.D.S.S., 1930.

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Rustatschol., 1897; Abbott univ. schol., 1899; B.A., 1st cls., class. tripes, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, history, 1902; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., 1st Dec., 1903; ast. audr., Nyasaland, 1st Oct., 1904; audr., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; examr., exchequer and audit dept., 17th Aug., 1910; audr., Seychelles, 23rd Mar., 1911; 1st divn. clk. (2nd cls.), col. audit dept., 1st Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., 5th Aug., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., 29th Jan., 1915; wounded, 8th Mar., 1917; relinquished coman. owing to wounds, and returned to col. audit dept., 29th Sept., 1918; audr., Uganda, 29th Dec., 1923; audr., Kenya, 1927.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.D., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chmn., cent. bd. of health and coroner, 1903-29; author of several medical and scientific works.

SMITHERS, ROBERT FRANCIS.—B. 1875; clk., atty.-gen.'s office, Natal, 1902; secy., tender bd., 1907; senr. clk., dept. of just., 1910; mem., pub. serv. comm. of enquiry, 1917; pub. serv. inspr., 1921; mem.-sec., pub. hosp. enquiry commn., 1926.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Ebbden scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripos, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in comd. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comttee. of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; in command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. of British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at peace confere. at Paris, 1919; prime min. and min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa, 1919-24; M.P. for Standerton, 1924.

SMYLY, SIR PHILIP CRAMPTON, KT. BACH., (1905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1896; atty.-gen., 1896; ch. just., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1911; ret., 1929.

SNELGAR, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1890; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Kings Coll., London; clk., bd. of educn., 1906; clk., C.A., 1910; clk., land dept. E.A.P., 1912; ag. office supt., 1917 and 1919; land ast., 1919; sec., land tenure commn., 1920; warden of mines, 1920; regis. of titles, etc., 1922; ast. land offr., 1922.

SNELL, EDWARD WALTER.—B. 1879; ast., post office, Kingwilliamstown, Mar., 1900; ast., G.P.O., Cape Town, Nov., 1900; clk., accts. branch, July, 1903; 1st cls. clk., Apl., 1912; income tax offr., Pretoria, Sept., 1914; senr. clk., income tax offr., Cape Town, July, 1915; senior clk., rev. office, Johannesburg, Mar., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Jan., 1921; ch. clk., income tax, head office, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Aug., 1921; ast. commn. inland rev., Sept., 1929.

SOLOMON, MICHAEL CLAUDE.—Copyist, med. dept., Jamaica, Oct., 1885; 3rd cls. clk., 1888; 2nd cls. clk., 1891; 1st cls. clk. and sec., cent. bd. of health, 1908; ch. clk., 1920; sec. to commn. on reorganization of med. serv., 1920.

SOLOMON, NEVILLE STAFFORD VICTOR.—B. 1874; clk., G.P.O., Bahamas, 1891; tel. dept., 1892; treas. and cust., 1895; supervisor, cust., 2nd gde., Gold Coast, 1902; ag. ch. regis., sup. ct., 1906; finan. ast., treas. and cust. dept., Nigeria, 1907; supervisor, 1st gde., cust. dept., 1910; collr., 1917; adm. reporting offr., various ports, Nigeria, during great war, 1914-18; ret. on pension, 1922; ast. postmstr. (temp.), Bahamas, Jan., 1924; resigned, Nov., 1925; recr. gen. and treas., and mem. exec. coun., Bahamas, 1928; has held various ag. appts.
SOLOMON, SAUL.—B. 1875; ed. Bedford Grammar Schol. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; M.A.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1900; K.C., Union of S. Africa, 1919; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1927.

SOLOMON, HON. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH. (1907), M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at S. African Coll., Cape Town and Cambridge Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1876; raised to the bench, Kimberley, 1887; afterwards transferred to Eastern Districts Court in same capacity; puisne judge sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appellate div. of sup. ct. of South Africa, May, 1910; ch. just., 1st Mar., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, KANDIAH.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. Kalutara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colombo Kach., Dec., 1922; office ast. to rubber contr. Mar., 1923; office ast., Galie kach., Aug., 1926; 6th ast. col. sec., Nov., 1926; 4th ast. col. sec., May, 1928; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1928; addnl. ast. contr., Nov., 1928.
SOMERS, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1896; served Dardanelles and E. Mediterranean with H.M. Transports, 1914-18; govt. pilot, Kenya, 1924; ast. port offr., Zanzibar, 1929; ag. port offr., Aug., 1929.

SOMERVILLE, JAMES, O.B.E.—Clk., traffic dept., Cape, 20th Mar., 1897; clk., C.T. M. office, Cape, 1st Oct., 1898; ch. clk., admtr.'s office, Transvaal, 1st Dec., 1900; sec., refugees and dept., 1st Nov., 1901; sec., Transvaal immigrn. office, 1st Aug., 1903; ch. clk., lands dept., 1st May, 1904; under sec. for lands, Union of Africa, 1st June, 1917; sec. for lands, 1st Apr., 1921.

SOPER, JOHN ROBERT PHILPOTT, B.A. (hons.), Cantab.—B. 1904; ed. Tonbridge Schol. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (sohol.); headmaster, agr. prim. schl., Zanzibar, Sept., 1926; seconded for duty as agr. offr., Nov., 1926; agr. offr., Jan., 1928.

SOUTHBOROUGH, 1ST BARON (creat. 1917), RT. HON. SIR FRANCIS JOHN STEPHENS HOPWOOD, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; ass't. law clk., B. of T., 1886-1888; astl. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec., rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1895; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comttee. of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic commn., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions commn., 1906; mem. of commn. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal commn. on electoral reform, 1909; regis. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development comm., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHORN, WILFRED THOMAS, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1906; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr. customs, Colombo, July, 1909; ast. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to govt., Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd ast. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prin. ast. col. sec., July, 1920; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port comm., Dec., 1923; mem., leg. coun., 1924 and exec. coun., 1925; col. see., Hong Kong, May, 1926; O.A.G., in 1927, 1928 and 1929.
SPARROW, WILLIAM LA BART.—B. 1904; pol. probationer, Nov., 1924; seconded to Weihaiwei as junr. dist. offr., Jan., 1928.

SPEARMAN, BARUCH, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Camb.), D.T.M. and H., certif., London Schl. of Trop. Medicine (with distinction).—B. 1877; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1912; capt., Uganda med. serv., Sept., 1916; med. offr. of hth., Zanzibar, Nov., 1920; recd. thanks of ag. gov., Uganda, for services in connection with outbreak of plague at Jinja, Nov., 1914; ment. in desps., 1919; M.O.H., Zanzibar, Sept., 1920; senr. med. offr. of health, Zanzibar, Jan., 1922; ag. D.M.S.S., on various occasions, 1922-23, 1925-26, 1928 and 1930; D.D.S.S., Jan., 1926; 3rd cls., Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1929.

SPEED, SIR EDWIN ARNEY, KT. BACH. (1911).—M.A.; LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtnr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripos, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripos, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.

SPENCE, ARNOTT VERN.—B. 1893; 3rd clk., treas., St. Vincent, Nov., 1910; ag. rev. and excise offr., June, 1915 to May, 1916; 1st cls. rev. offr. and port offr., May, 1916; ag. warden, Leeward dist., June, 1917 to June, 1919; 2nd clk., govt. office, July, 1919; ag. ch. clk., govt. office, clk. ex. and leg. couns., and med. regiar., Apr., 1923 to Sept., 1927; ch. clk. etc., Sept., 1927.

SPENCER, CYRIL EDWARD.—B. 1873; entd. col. ser., Cyprus, 1st Mar., 1891; in secretariat from Apr., 1892, to May, 1898; also clk. to legis. coun., Mar., 1894, to May, 1898; priv. sec. to high comsrr. on several occasions, 1898-1900; inspr., mil. pol., 1st May, 1898; ag. dist. comit., July, 1900, to Jan., 1904; apptd. in comd. of diva. of pol., Jan., 1904; also gov. of pris. and ast. to dist. comsrr., and dep. coroner; ag. dist. comsrr., Apr. to Dec., 1905; passed in mod. Greek, higher standard, 1904; and in Turkish, lower standard, 1906; transf. to B. East Africa, as ast. dist. comsrr., Nov., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. dist. comsrr., Mombasa, from July, 1907; dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Apr., 1912; ast. ast. sec., secretariat, Jan., 1919; clk. of councils, Jan., 1919; ag. ast. chief sec., Jan., 1919; comsrr. of prisons, Kenya, Oct., 1923.

SPIEBER, ROY GODFREY BULLEN, M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Colet Court and St. Paul's; probationary ast. supt. pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1909; ast. supt., 1911; supt., 1915; on war serv., 1915-19; wounded, Oct., 1918; returned to Ceylon pol., 1919; supt., grade I., 1923; comsrr. pol., Kenya, June, 1925.

SPIELLER, JOHN WYATT, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Schl. (Som.); served in engng. dept., G.W.R., 1896 to 1910; joined engng. dept., Crown Agents, 1910; services lent to Min. of Munitions, 1916; tech. adviser to dir., Munitions Overseas Transport, 1917; returned to C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engnr., 1919; visited Ceylon, F.M.S. and S. Sttlmts., 1920; ch. engnr. (designs), 1922; visited Br. Guiana and Trinidad, 1922; acted for one year as gen. man., F.M.S. rly., and ch. engnr., 1926.

SPIO-GABRAH, JOSIAH.—B. 1878; 2nd ast. mast., tech. schl., Accra, Gold Coast, 1st Jan., 1899; 1st ast. mast., 19th Nov., 1902; 3rd grade teacher, 1st Jan., 1907; prin. teacher, 3rd Nov., 1912; ast. headmast., 1st Apr. 1920; headmast., 1st Apr., 1922; insp., schls., 1st Apr., 1923.

SPOONER, MAJOR JOHN CHARLES GARTH, M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Marlborough Coll.; jr. ast. engnr., F.M.S. rly., 1903-06; ast. engnr., F.M.S. rly., 1906-12; dist. engnr., grade II., 1912-14; dist. engnr., grade II., 1914; on active serv., 1915-19; dist. engnr., grade I., Ipoh, Jan., 1920; sen. dist. engnr., Ipoh, Jan., 1921; ag. engnr. constrn., F.M.S. rly., Sept., 1925 to Feb., 1927; sen. dist. engnr., Johore Bahru, Mar., 1927; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1928; engnr. ways and wks., June, 1929.

SPROULE, PERCY JULIAN, B.A., Camb.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1896; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. ast. registr. sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904; ag. dep. pub. proc., May, 1905; ag sol.-gen., Penang, May-Aug., 1906; dep. pub. prosecutor, Sing., Aug., 1906; ag. solr.-gen., Penang, Mar., 1908; ag. regir., supreme ct., Penang, Dec., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., July, 1909; ast. gen., Apr., 1911; ag. judl. comsrr., F.M.S., Jan., 1912; solr.-gen., May and Oct., 1912; ag. temp. judge, sup. ct., July—Sept., 1912, and Nov., 1912; puisne judge, Dec., 1913; pres., toddy comm., Selangor, Oct., 1916; ag. ch. judl. comsrr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. ch. just., S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1920; ag. senr. puisne judge, Feb.-Apr., 1921; ag. ch. just., S.S., 16th June, 1922; ag. ch. judl. comsrr., F.M.S., June, 1924.
SQUIRE, REV. CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1888; ed. Repton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); aupt. educn., S. Prova., Nigeria, 1927; prin. govt. coll., Ibadan, 1928.

STACE, WALTER TERENCE.—B.A., Trin. coll., Dublin; B. 1886; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 29th Oct., 1910; attached to the Galle Kachcheri, 1st Dec., 1910; office ast. to govt. agt., Galle, March, 1912; pol. mag., Chilaw, May, 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1915; priv. sec. to govt. and extra ast. col. sec., Sept., 1915; ast. censor, Dec., 1916; censor, May, 1918; dist. judge, Negombo, Apr., 1920; ast. sttlm. offr., Jan., 1922; ag. attlt. offr., Mar.-Nov., 1926; mayor of Colombo, Oct., 1927 to June, 1928.

STAFFORD, FRANK EDMUND.—B. 1895; ed. R. Grammar Sch., Guildford; Queen's Regt., 1914-19; joined Iraq civ. admin., Feb., 1919; ag. asst. rev. sec., Oct., 1919; supervising offr., high commn., Oct., 1920; asst. to finan. adviser to high commn., June, 1921; dep. fin. sec. to high commn., Jan., 1923; ag. finan. sec., Apr., 1923; finan. sec., June, 1923.
STALLWORTHY, HON. ARTHUR JOHN.—B. 1877; ed. State schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. parl., 1928; min. of health, 1928.

STANFORD, COL. THE HON. SIR WALTER ERNEST MONTMOR, K.B.E. (1919), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1892), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1860; mag.'s elk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Gcaleka war, 1877-8 (medal and clasp); mem. native laws and customs commn., 1880-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1 (med. and clasp); on special service to Pondoland, 1884; ch. mag. Griqualand E., 1885; negotiated treaty with Pondos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1898; comd. forces in E. Griqualand, Boer War, 1899-1902 (despa., 2 meda. and 2 clasps); ditto and ch. mag., July, 1904; mem. of S. African native affairs commn., Sept., 1903, to Jan. 1905; holds rank of colonel in Cape colonial forces; ret., May, 1907; M.L.A. for Tembuland, 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909; mem. of senate, Union of S. Africa, 1910-29; dir., war recruiting, Union of S. Africa and commissioner for returned soldiers, 1918-19.
STANLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR LYULPH, K.C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1875; ed. Oxford Univ. (B.A.); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; M.P. for Eddisbury div. of Cheshire, 1906-10; gov. of Victoria, 1914-20.

STANLEY, SIR HERBERT JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1905; asst. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov. gen., 1913; resident commn., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high commn. for S. Africa, 1918; first gov. and commdr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924; gov. of Ceylon, July, 1927; high commn. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, 1931.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.B.E. (1913), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1921.—Served with 1st Border regt. occupation of Crete, 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (med. and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjt., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expdn., 1901, (med. and clasp); ag. trav. commn., Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. commn., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. eng., Chatham, 1905; resig. commn., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in natural language; dist. commn., S. Leone, 1910; prov. commn., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, on various occasions, 1918-20 and 1922-23; gov.'s dep. on several occasions.

STANTON, AMBROSE THOMAS, C.M.G. (1929), M.D. (Toronto), F.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Cambridge).—B. 1875; senr. house surg., registr. and demonstr., Lond. Sch. of Trop. Med., 1906-07; bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1908; on sp. duty in Siam, Indo-China and Netherlands R. Indies, 1915-16; dir., govt. labs., F.M.S., 1920; ch. med. adviser to S. of S., 1926; chmn., col. advisory med. and sanry. comm., 1926; chmn., managing bureau hygiene and trop. disease, 1926; mem. col. med. research comm., 1927; Cragg's research prize, L.S.T.M., 1911; Biset Hawkins gold med., Royal Coll. of Physicians, London, 1926; Mary Kingsley Memorial Med., Liverp. S.T.M., 1929; author of "Etiology of Beriberi," "Malayan Culicidæ," "Meloidoisiv," etc.
STAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., Mar., 1923.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1885; mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., O.C., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922; ag. higher grade cler. offr., 17th Feb., 1930.

STARK, WALTER JOHN KIRKPATRICK.—B. 1887; ed. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and at Aberdeen Univ. (M.A.), 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, Mar., 1911; ditto, Ulu Langat, May, 1911; ditto, Klang, May, 1912; passed cadet, Aug., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. supt. of imgrts., Penang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. contr., lab., Dec., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Mar., 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1913; asst. contr., lab., Seremban, Apr., 1914; ag. mag., Seremban, in addn., Feb. to Mar., 1915; asst. contr., lab., Klang, Sept., 1915; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, in addn., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. dep. contr., lab., Sept., 1917 and June, 1918; dep. contr., lab., Klang (offr., cls. IV.), 1st Jan., 1919; emgrn. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Coconada, Sept., 1920; ag. emgrn. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Madras, to Oct., 1920; sup't, emgrn. depot, Negapatam, Mar., 1922; mag., Ipoh, July, 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Dec., 1926; ditto, Klang, Oct., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Nov., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1928; mag., Ipoh, Oct., 1928; ag. contr., lab., Malaya, Aug., 1930.

STARNS, CORTLANDT.—B. 1864; served in N.W. rebellion, 1885, as ag. adjutant, 65th Montreal; Inspr., R.N.W.M.P., 1st Mar., 1886; adjutant, 1891-97; aspt., 1909; asst. commn., 1st Dec., 1919; commn., 1923.

STATEN, OSCAR WILLIAM—Asst. res. mag., Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1902; ag. res. mag. and res. mag., Barberton, 1902-11; res. mag., Heidelberg, 1911-16; inspgr. mag., 1916-21; ch. pub. serv. inspr., 1921; sec., pub. wks., Jan., 1925.
STATHAM, HON. SIR CHARLES ERNEST, K.T. BACH (1926).—Mem., N.Z. parl. since 1915; speaker since 1923.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS, O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., asst. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, Oct., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. habmr., and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; regir. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; asst. cable censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, Jan., 1919 to Mar., 1920; refugee commn., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; regir. of patents, Apr., 1920; ch. collr. of cust. and excise, Oct., 1921; inspr. of fisheries, 1921; Médaille de la Reconnaissance Francaise, 3rd o., 1922; ag. consmr., Larnaca for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; regar. of companies, July, 1922; ch. supt., Port of Larnaca, June, 1922; regar. of companies, July, 1922; regar. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; regar. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir. cust., excise, and trade, Palestine, July, 1924; ag. regar.-gen., Aug. to Dec., 1924; regar., trade marks and patents, Apr., 1928; ag. regar., companies and co-op. societies, Apr.-Dec., 1928.

STEAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1889; ast. gen publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of immigr. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.

STEDMAN, HUGH JOHN HARRY, O.B.E.(1929), M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Weymouth Coll. and trained at Royal Naval Depot, Portland; ast. engrn., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1905; ag. exec. engrn., Kisumu, Mombasa, 1907; in charge, Thika rly. survey and constrn., 1910-11; ag. exec. engrn., Nairobi and Naivasha, 1912-16; seconded to 3rd batt., K.A.R., 1915 ("1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory meda.); exec. engrn., P.W.D., 1917; D.P.W., Zanzibar, 1925; Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, cls. III., March, 1928.
STEED, F. L.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920, apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., O.S.O., July, 1922.
STEEL, CECIL RICHARD, M.R.O.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. London S.H.T.M. (distinc.) D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—Mily. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925.

STEELE, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1899; admitted Grenada bar, July, 1899; mag., dist. F., Dominica, Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and regar.-gen., dist. E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920.

STEBB-MAITLAND, SIR A. H. D. R., BART. (creat. 1917).—B. 1876; ed. Rugby (scholar and exhibitioner); Balliol Coll., Oxford (classical scholar); Eldon scholar, 1899; 1st cls. class. mods., 1897; 1st cls. class. schls., 1899; 1st cls. final law schls., 1900; sec., junr. treas. and pres., Oxford Univ. Soc'y.; special consmr. to royal comm. on Poor Laws, 1906-7; M.P. (C.) East Birmingham, since 1910; chrmn. of Unionist party organisation, 1911; parl. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 31st May, 1915; ditto, 11th Dec., 1916; additional under-sec., F.O., and additional parl. sec., B. of T., in charge of the work of the dept. of overseas trade, 1917-1919; min. of lab., 7th Nov., 1924.

STEPHENS, MAJOR FRANCIS TRANT, O.B.E. (1919), M.C., King's Police Med.—Served in S. African war, 1900-02; joined B.S.A. pol., Rhodesia, 1903; comand., 1907; served with Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for service in E. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in desps.; ch. consmr. pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Nyasaland Prot., July, 1920.

STEPHENS, JOHN EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1869; ed. St. Olave's Schl., York and Royal Univ. of Ireland; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1894; mag., H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar, Jan., 1912, and mag. of the ct. of H.H. the Sultan of Zanzibar, Feb., 1912; ag. asst. judge, Aug., 1915 to Apr., 1916 and from Feb. to Aug., 1918; 3rd puisne judge, Kenya, Feb., 1925; editor-in-ch. of "The Manual of Naval Law and Court-martial procedure" (4th edn.); ast. editor of 11th edn. of "Addison on Contracts"; author of works on "Demurrage," "Freight," "Charter parties," etc.; contributor to the Encyclopedia of the Laws of England, Journal of Comparative Legislation, etc.

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERARD.—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in Laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cls., class. mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1923; on military service, 28th Aug., 1914, to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; apptd. under re-construction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ast. priv. sec. to S. of S., 18th June, 1920; ast. prin., 1st Mar., 1923.

STERLING, HERBERT HARRY, LL.B.—B. 1886; ed. Christchurch (N.Z.) Boys High Schl., Otago Univ. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. rlys. dept., 1901; mem., rlys. bd., 1924; ret., 1926; gen. man., rlys., 1928.

STEVENS, GEORGE ALEXANDER, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Schl., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation schol. in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd cls. hons., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STEVENS, HON. HENRY HERBERT.—B. 1878; ed. at Peterboro, Ont.; sl. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e., 1911; re-el., 1917; min. of trade and commerce, 1921; min. of cust. and excise in Meighen cabinet, 1926; re-el., g.e., 1926; min. of trade and commerce in Bennett cabinet, 1930.
