TOLMIE, ALEXANDER WILLIAM.—B. 1884; ed. Kincardine high schol., Toronto Univ.; apptd. to Canadian govt. exhibn. commsnr., 1908; Canadian govt. exhibn. commsnr., Aug., 1919.

TOLMIE, HON. SIMON FRASER.—B. 1867; ed. Victoria High Schol., Ontario, and Vet. Coll., Toronto; farmer and breeder of live stock; pres., Dominion Holstein Breeders, B. Columbia Vet. Assoc., B. Columbia Holstein Breeders; chf. inspr. of health of animals branch, B. Columbia; B. C. representative on live stock comms.; elec. of Canadian H. of C., g. e., 1917 and 1921; min. to agric. in fed. govt., Aug., 1919; resig. portfolio on defeat of admtn., in general election, Dec., 1921; premier, Br. Columbia, Aug., 1928.
TOMKINSON, C.—Ast. dist. commsnr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1915; seconded as lieut., Carrier Corps.

TOMLINSON, GEORGE JOHN FREDERICK, C.B.E. (1928), M.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-law.—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse and Univ. Coll., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1903-04; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1907; seconded to Gold Coast as dir., educn., 1909; reverted to N. Nigeria, 1911; 3rd ols. res., 1911; ag. res., 1912; 2nd cl. offr. (change of title), 1914; 1st cl. dist. offr., 1918; ag. sec., N. Provs., 1919; ast. sec., native affrs., 1921; ag. sec., do. 1921 and 1925; staff grade (supernay.), 1925; ret., 1928; asst. under sec. of state, C.O., 1st Oct., 1930.

TOMLINSON, THOMAS FOWLER, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, and Leeds University; asst. engr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1911; served with R. Engnr., France, 1916-19; res. engr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1920; temp. D.P.W., Virgin Is., 1925; dist. engr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1927; ch. engr., govt. rly., Trinidad,

TOMLINSON, SIR THOMAS SYMONDS, K.T., B.A. (1926).—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B. 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, May, 1907; asst. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Jan., 1911; ag. judge, Feb. to Nov., 1916; judge (now ch. just.), 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil admtn. of the occupied terry. of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921; prepared revised edn. of the laws of Zanzibar, 1922; and els., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1923; ret., 1928.

TOMSON, GRAHAM.—B.1887; ed. Elmdon Hall and Dover Coll.; inspr., plantations, Zanzibar, Apr., 1921; agrl. offr., 1924; ag. asst. dir., agr., May-Oct., 1926 and Jan.-April, 1926; game ranger, Tanganyika, Sept., 1926; ag. game warden, Jan. to July, 1927; man., plantations, Zanzibar, July, 1927; ag. asst. dir., agr., Feb., Mar., 1930.

TONGE, LIEUT. COMDE. C. G., M.B.E., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1878; ed. Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; naval transport offr., Aug. to Dec., 1914; lent for serv. in gunboats on Lake Nyasa, Jan., 1915; senr. naval offr., Lake Nyasa and Ida offr., marine transport dept., June, 1919; Royal Naval Res. Offr.'s Decoration.
TONGUE, ERIC DAUNCY, B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Taunton Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol. and exhib.); asst. dist. commr., Uganda, October, 1913; dist. commr., Aug., 1923; lab. commr., May, 1930.

TOPP, WM.—B. 1884; Gambia govt. serv., 1901-07; S. Nigeria govt., 1907-08; N. Nigeria govt., Oct., 1908 to Sept., 1910; transf'd. to the Gambia, Sept., 1910; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1911; ch. clk., June, 1913; sp. cls. apptmnt., Jan., 1921; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1924; offr.-incharge, secretariat, July-Aug., 1925; J.P. and commr., ct. of requests, July, 1925.

TOPPIN, CHARLES SAMUEL.—B. 1866; ed. at Diocesean schl., Waterford, Ireland; served as book keeper and acctnt. with W. Australian land co. (Great Southern rly.), 1887 to 1897; on taking over of line entd. govt. ser. as sub-acctnt., rly. dept., 1st Mar., 1897; asst. acctnt., 1st July, 1900; ag. ch. acctnt., 1st July, 1903; audgen. for State of W. Aust., Feb., 1904; also dep. aud.-gen. for Commonwealth, Feb., 1904, to Jan., 1906.
TORY, JAMES CRANSWICK.—B. 1862; ed. common schl., Guysboro Acad., Wesleyan Theological Coll., Montreal; Nova Scotia legislature, 1911; re-el., 1916; lieut.-gov., Nova Scotia, 1st Oct., 1925.

TOTHILL, FRANCIS JAMES.—Extra asst. to postmtr.-gen., Ceylon, Oct., 1898; asst. inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1899; asst. sec., cent. irrigtn. bd., Jan., 1900; sec., ditto, and office asst. to dir. of irrigtn., May, 1900; asst. acctnt., gen. treas., Jan., 1907; ag. finan. asst. and acctnt., gen. treas., on various occasions, 1911-16; ag. paymr., Ceylon volr. force, Aug., 1914; ag. staff offr. in add. Apr., 1915; ag. finan. asst. and acctnt., gen. treas., Nov., 1915; 1st asst. acctnt., Apr., 1916; ag. finan. asst. and acctnt., Apr. to Nov., 1921; finan. asst. and acctnt., July,

TOTHILL, JOHN DOUGLAS.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's Schl.; Ontario Agr. Coll. (Toronto Univ.); B.Sc., Agr., 1910; Cornell Univ., U.S.A.; Graduate Schl., 1913, 1914; Harvard Univ., D.Sc., 1922; agt. and expert, U.S. Bureau of Entomology, 1910-11; entomologist, entomological branch, Ottawa, 1911-24; seconded as dir., Levuana campaign, Fiji, for two years, Nov., 1924; ag. dir., agr., Fiji, May, 1925; dir., Levuana campaign and agr., 1926; dir., agr., Uganda, June, 1929.

TOUGHER, ROBERT ALEXANDER HUNTER.— B. 1891; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst and Queen's Univ. of Belfast, B.A. (1914); army, 1914-19; warser., Dardanelles Egypt and Syria, ment in desps.; capt., gen. R. of O., 1919; soldr., Ireland, 1920; asst. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

TOUSSAINT, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1879; ag. off. asst., Matare Kach, Ceylon, Jan., 1917; sec., Buddhist temporalities ord. commr., and extra off. asst. to dir., educn., Nov., 1918; ag. off. asst. to col. audr., Apl., 1919; cls. V. of civ. serv., July, 1919; ag. asst. col. audr., Mar., 1920; ag. sec. to food contr., July, 1921; addnl. asst. commr. of stamps, Sept., 1921; cls. IV. Mar., 1926; asst. commr., stamps, Mar., 1930.

TOVEY, DUNCAN LOHRMANN PATTON.—B. 1903; ed. Clifton Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A.; asst. priv. sec. (appts.) to S. of S., Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Dee., 1926; to Lord Passfield, June, 1929; apptd. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930.

TOWNER, H. V., Amoo Mem., Inst. Civ. Engrns.—Aust. supt. of wks., S. Stilimta., Mar., 1901; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Oct., 1904; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, Apr. to Sept., 1902, and from Nov., 1904, to Mar., 1908; exec. engrnr., Dec., 1909; ag. dep. col. engrnr., Penang, Jan., 1910; exec. engrnr., Singapore, Mar., 1915; on sp. duty in Malacca on road reconstruction, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. col. engnr. and survr.-gen., Malacca, Feb., 1919; exec. engnr., grade I., Malacca, Dec., 1919; dep. col. engnr., S. Stilimta., Feb., 1921; ag. col. engrnr., Mar., 1921.

TOWNLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1892; served with field coy., R.E.'s (T.), in Egypt, France, Belgium and Germany, 1914-19; asst. govt. printer, Nigeria, May, 1920; senr. supt., Aug., 1926; ag. govt. printer, May, 1927.
TRAFFORD, H. H.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1913; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, 1925.

TRAFFORD, JOSEPH LOUIS.—B. 1888; ed. Beaumont Coll.; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commr., June, 1915, and from July, 1916 to July, 1917; asst. dist. commr., 1914; ag. dist. commr. for various periods, 1915-20; ag. asst. col. sec., June to Aug., 1920; dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1921; asst. col. sec., 11th Oct., 1922.

TRATMAN, DAVID WM., C.M.G. (1930). —Ed. at Clifton Coll., schlr. of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1900; B.A., 1904; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1904; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. asst. land offr. in New Territories, and registrar of land ct., 1907; mem. of land ct., 1907; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., 1909; 2nd asst. registrar-gen., 1909; ag. supt. of imports and exports, 1910; 1st asst. registr.-gen., 1911; ag dist. offr., N.T., 1911; ag. head of sanitary dept., 1912; asst. dist. offr., N.T., (South), 1912; ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affairs, 1914; ag. dist. offr., N.T., 1915; ag. head of sanitary dept., 1915; head of sanitary dept., 1917; supt. of imports and exports, 1917; local priority authority, 1918; ag. col. treas., 1920 and 1922; dist. offr., N. Territory, 1920; ag. col. treas., 1924; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., 1925; 1st cls. cadet offr., ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of councils, 1926; title changed to prin. asst. col. sec., 1930.

TRAVERS, MICHAEL F.—B. 1897; ed. St. Mary's and O'Connell's Schls., Dublin; boy clk., Irish land comm., Dublin, 8th May, 1913; asst. clk., board of educn., 22nd Apr., 1916; war serv., R. Irish regt., 19th Apr., 1916 to 5th Dec., 1919; appdtd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1921; transf. to C.O., 7th Apr., 1926.

TREE, FRANCIS THOMAS, B.A., Dublin.—B. 1887; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Jan., 1911; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, June, 1911; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., sp. res., Nov., 1915; lieut., Sept., 1916; attd. R.E. for research in poison gas, Mar., 1918; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Sept., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, May, 1920; ag. asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, Jahore, Oct., 1922; offr., cls. INT., Nov., 1922; ag. asst. advr., Muar, May, 1924; commnr., lands, S.S., 1930.

TREMLET, FREDERICK THOMAS GEORGE, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; 2nd lieut., Jamaica Militia, 1902; lieut., 1903; attached Lancs. Fusiliers, 1903; sub-inspr. constab., Jamaica, 1903; ag. inspr., ditto, 1906-1911; studied finger-print system, Scotland Yard, 1906; J.P., 1909; inspr. of constab., Jamaica, 1912; supt., Spanish Town fire brigade, 1911-1912; passed mil. schl. of instr., Chelsea, 1912; transf. to Mauritius pol., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, 1916; capt. and adjt., Mauritius vol. force, 1916; capt. in army, 1917; raised and comd. Mauritius labour batt., 1917; comd. Mauritius vol. force, Mar. to Dec., 1919; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, May, 1917, and Mar. to Dec., 1919; major, 1920; ag. inspr.-gen., supt., prisons and O.C., Mauritius Vol. Force, Feb. to Sept., 1922; dep. commnr., pol. and prisons, Uganda, Nov., 1922; ag. commnr., pol. and prisons, on many occasions, 1922-28.

TREMLET, CAPT. JOHN DANIEL SCHWEDER, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Harrow Schol. and R. Vety. Coll., London; war serv., Gallipoli and Franco, 1915-18; M.C., 1917; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923.
TRENCH, ARTHUR DONALD LE POE, O.A.O. (dip.).—Senr. coffee offr., agrl. dept., Kenya, Oct., 1916.

TREVOR, IVAN BERNARD.—B. 1900; ed. Tyttenhanger Lodge, Scarsdale; Ro-sall exhib., 1915 (not taken up); cadet, Midland rly., 1917; traffic asst., Kowloon-Canton rly., Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; ag. traffic supt. and ch. storekeeper in addn., 1926 and 1927; ag. ch. acct., 1928; passes Cantonese, 1928; ag. traffic man., and ch. storekeeper in addn., 1930.

TREWIN, CAPT. ALGERNON BURNETT.—B. 1884; ed. the Royal Naval Schol. and Sandhurst; ent. Army (Essex Regt.), Jan., 1905; instr. of signalling, 1906; seconded, K.A.R., Uganda, 1908; coy. comdr., 1911; seconded, Zanzibar armed constab., 1912; ent. Uganda admin., July, 1914; adjt., Uganda vol., Aug., 1914; commn., gen. res. of offrs., 1919; dist. commn., Uganda, Aug., 1923.

TROWBRIDGE, EDMUND.—B. 1881; ed. pub. model and high schls., Toronto, Ontario; asst. clk., exec. coun., 1906; sec., Alberta coal commn., 1907; sec., Alberta pork commn., 1908; dep. prov. sec. and regiar. of companies, prov. of Alberta, 1910.
TRUELOVE, A. C.—Asst. engrnr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, 1914; retrenched, 1918; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1919; senr. exec. engrnr., 1928.

TRUMP, PHILIP, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Exeter Schol. and King's Coll., Lond., civ. engrnr; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Selangor, Aug., 1910; ex-engnr., Grade III, 1919; ex-engnr., malaria advisory, Jul., July, 1921; ex-engnr., whs. and bridge, Kuala Lumpur, May, 1923; ag. senr. ex-engnr., Johore, May, 1924; ditto, Cocto, Pahang, Oct., 1926; senr. ex-engnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1927; ag. state engrnr., Trengganu, Apr., 1927.
TRUPHET, JOHN SYDNEY.—B. 1883; ed. Roan Schol. and King's Coll., London; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1901; asst. head, gen. stores dept., 1919; dep. head, shipping dept., 1927.

TRUSTED, HARRY HERBERT, K.C.—B. 1888; ed. Ellesmere Coll. and Trin. Hall Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1913; served overseas (Duke of Cornwall's L.I. and staff, East Indies), 1914-19; puisne judge, Leeward Is., 1925; atty-gen., 1927; commnr. to revise the federal acts, Leeward Is., 1928; K.C., Leeward Is., 1929; atty.-gen., Cyprus, Dec., 1929.

TRUTER, SIR THEODORUS GUSTAFT, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1873; ed. Boys Pub. Schol., George; Normal Coll., Capetown; Diocesan Coll., Dordbosch; entd. Cape cvn. ser., 1st Dec., 1892; chief examr. of accts., 1890; lieut., Robert's Horse, 1899-1902 (medal and four clasps); asst. mag., Pretoria, 1st Apr., 1901; mein., spec. criminal ot.; mag., Ermerlo, 1st Dec., 1904; mag., Standerton, 1st Apr., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, 1st July, 1910; commnr., S. African pol., 16th Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa; ret., 1928.

TUCKER, KEITH RAVENSCROFT, F.I.A.A., certificated acctnt.—B. 1890; articled pupil to Messrs. Yarrow & Co., Glasgow, 1906-8; entd. engineering dept., office of crown agts. for the colonies, Mar., 1908; transf. to col. audit dept., cent. office, June, 1913; asst. auditor, G. Coast, Sept., 1913; asst. audr., Nyassaland Prot., June, 1916; despatched Pretoria, on mily. audit duties, Oct., 1918 to Apr., 1919; served with Togoland Field Force, Aug.-Sept., 1914 (ment. in desps.); lieut., G. Coast Volks., Apl., 1915; hon. temp. capt., Nyassaland Field Force, 1919; asst. auditor, Nyassaland Prot., June, 1916; ag. auditor, Apl., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dep. treas., June, 1921; dep. treas., Dec., 1921; ag. treas., Apr., 1923; treas., 14th Jan., 194.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate Institn. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. and inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; 1st cls. both years certif. exam.; parchment certif., 1897; head master, govt. boys schl., St. Helena, 1944; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1945; hon. sec. and treas., govt. lace schl., 1908; supervisor of govt. schls., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911; govt. rep. on Hu-sey Charity bd. of educn., 1911; J.P. and coroner and mem., bd. of health, 1912; lieut., St. Helena Rifles, Sept., 1914 to Nov., 1916; lieut., R.A.S.C., Apr., 1917 to Nov., 1919; inspr. schls., Leeward Is., Feb., 1920; chmn., gen. purposes comtee., Antigua bd. of educn.; chmn., St. Kitts bd. of educn., 1922; asst. dir., educn., Jamaica, 1930.

DUOKER, RICHARD WILLIAM ETHELBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Ed., (Cape town).—B. 1892; ed. Wilsons Grammar Schol. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., nat. sci. tripes pt. I (hons. chem., and zoology) 1913; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripes pt. II (zoology) 1914; asst. S. African museum, Cape town, 1914-21; entomologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, 1921-23; entomologist, Barbados, 1928; publs. include various papers on Araneae and Acari, and economic papers on control of Acariad and insect pests.

TURBETT, IVAN JOLIFFE TUPKELL.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1913; cadet, Fiji, 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1915; ag. crown solr., 1916; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1917; pol. mag., Gambia, 1918; ag. legal adviser, 1919-23 and in 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct. on various occasions; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1926 ag. atty.-gen. in 1926, 1927 and 1928.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Saak., 23rd Sept., 1907; also to Sak. legis., 1907; re-elected, 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ot. of appeal, Saak., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNBULL, DAVID OLIVER, D.S.O.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; diploma, H.C.V.S., Dec., 1903; lieut., A.V.D., Sept. 1904 to July, 1908 and Nov., 1914 to Nov., 1915; capt., Nov., 1915; major, Apr., 1916 (A.D.V.S., 27th divn.); lieut.-col., 1918 (A.D.V.S., 16th Corps); despatched, 1916, 1917, 1918; French Croix de Guerre, Mar., 1917; D.S.O., June, 1917; N. Rhodesia vety. dept., Sept., 1920; ag. ch. vety. offr., N. Rhodesia on two occasions in 1927; ch. vety. offr., Nyasaland, 1930.

TURNER, EDWARD PHILLIPS, F.R.G.S.—B. 1865; ed. Hutchins Schol., Hobart (Tas.) and pvt. tutors; survey work in N.Z. and Tasmania; entd. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1894; insp. of scenic reserves, 1907; sec., royal comm. on forestry, 1913; mem., royal comm. on scenery of Wangannu river, 1914; sec. of forestry, 1919; dir., forestry, 1928.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1880; clk., post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. (medal and 5 clasps); depot sergt-major, R.N.W. mounted police, Canada, 1908-1916; coronation medal, 1911; lieut., Bahamas police and defence force, 1916; capt., 1917; major and offir. com. local forces, comdt. of police, provost marshal, and inspr. of prisons, June, 1919.
TURNER, CAPT. H. W.—Port offr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1915.

TURNER, JAMES POGSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., regir.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; commr. to administer oaths, St. Kitts, 1899; clk. to mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1906; 1st clk., regir.'s off., Antigua, 3rd May, 1906; commr. to admist. oaths, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. to trustees, sup. ct. library, June, 1906; clk. to regir. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; ag. regir. and prov. marshal, Dominica, Oct., 1910, to Feb., 1912; ditto, Antigua, and ch. regir., Leeward Is., 17th Feb. to 31st March, 1912, 26th Apr. to 20th Nov., 1914, and from 4th May to 6th Nov., 1915; dep. coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, Apr., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; regir. and prov. marshal, Antigua, and chief regir., 1st Sept., 1917.

TURNER, LEWIS JAMES BARNETSON.—B. 1886; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1908; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Gampola, Nov., 1911; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., May, 1913; asst. commr. of excise, Nov., 1913; asst. commr., S. Div., Jan., 1914; ditto, N.W. Div., Aug., 1916; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Oct., 1917; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1918; asst. commr. of excise, N.W. divn., and asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1919; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1919; sup. of census, July, 1920; dir. of statistics in addn., Sept., 1920; designation changed to dir., statistics and office systems, Oct., 1926.

TURTON, NAVILLE.—B. 1889; ed. at Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons.), 1911; M.A., 1915; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; asst. audir., S. Leone, 1912; ag. audir. on four occasions; volunteer, R.G.A., Sierra Leone; asst. to the atty.-gen., Uganda, 1921; 1st cls. mag. and judge in addn.; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, June, 1924; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; chmn., income tax commr., 1924; mem., educ. bd., 1925; ag. col. sec., 1925 and 1926; ag. ch. just., 1926, 1928, 1927 and 1928; admst. govt., Sept., 1926; solr.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1928; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1929; and Aug., 1930 to Feb., 1931.

TUTE, RICHARD CLIFFORD.—B. 1874; ed. St. Peter's Royal Schol., York and London Univ.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; passed open compet. exam. for Indian civ. serv., 1898; dist. and sessions judge, Allahabad, Bareilly, Meerut and other dists., from 1907; served throughout European war, retiring in 1919 with rank of major, R.G.A.; dist. judge, Galilee dist., Palestine, 1919; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1920; pres., land ct., Samaria, 1921; pres., land ct., Jerusalem, 1926; author of "A Commentary on the Ottoman Land Code," and (as joint author) a metrical translation of Omar Khayyam.

TWELLS, JOHN.—Ed. King's Schol., Canterbury and at Jesus Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., 1907; examir., E. and A. Dept., July, 1909; asst. auditor, S. Leone, Nov., 1909; asst. auditor, E. Africa, Prot., July, 1912; senr. asst. mast., educn. dept., Kenya, 1919; ag. head mast., Nairobi Schol., June to Dec., 1920; senr. asst. mast., Apr., 1921; ag. head mast. in 1924, 1925 and 1927.

TWEMLOW, GEORGE BROWNBILL.—B. 1897; ed. at Rossall Schol.; mily. serv., 1916-20; botanical with rank of capt.; re-enlisted, 1920-23; joined botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1923; asst. supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1927.

TWOSE, LAWRENCE RENDLE.—B. 1891; clk. to mag., dist. A, 1915; sec. to fire brig. commr., 1916; lieut., H.M. forces, 1916-19; ag. A.D.C. to gov., Leeward Is., on occasion of visit of Prince of Wales, 1920; clk. to commr., clk., couns., dep. coroner, educn. dist. offr., Montserrat, 1920; J.P., chmn., prison justices, 1924; mag., coroner, regir.-gen., sup. ct., 1924; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1925.

TYLER-SMITH, H. A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1887; B.Sc. (Eng.) with 1st cls. hons., civil engnr., Liverpool Univ., 1909; 2nd lieut., R.E. (sp. res.) 1910; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1911; exec. engnr., 1917; capt., R.E. (S.R.), 1917; D.P.W., Gambi Deo., 1920; asst. D.P.W., Nigeria, 1930.
TYNDALL, EUSTACE JOHN.—B. 1896; ed. Beaumont Coll., Windsor, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 9th July, 1924.

TYRRELL, Francis Graeme, C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1876; ed. Pembroke coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1900; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the governor, Aug., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admstg. the govt., Apr., 1902; off. astt. to gov't. agt., Cant. prov., Jan., 1903-Dec., 1904; ag. astt. gov't. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1904; seconded supt. of pol., N.W. prov., Aug., 1906, cent. prov., Mar., 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1908; astt. gov't. agt., Matale, Feb., 1909; dep. collr. of customs, Nov., 1911; on mil. duty, Jan., 1915; ag. gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1919; gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1921; prin. collr., cust., Apr., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. contr. rev., Mar., 1927; contr., rev., June, 1927; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1927-30.

TYSER, Sir C. R., Kt. Bach. (1909).—Ed. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In. Tem., 1873; asst. ag. judic. comnrs. and leg. advdr., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., Leewardes, 1886-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrena, Cyprus, 1896; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; ch. just., 1906; ret., 1919; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 1894; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's Evkaf Law, 1899; and of the Mejalli, 1901.

UNWIN, Arthur Harold, D.Oec., M.E.F.A. F.R.G.S., M.C.S.F.E.—Forestry branch, dept. of interior, Canada, 1903-4; asst. conservator and senr. conservator of forests, Nigeria, 1904-17; office of woods, timber supply dept., England, 1917-19; prin. forest offr., Cyprus, 1921; rep., Cyprus and Palestine; at Empire forestry conf., Australia and N.Z., Sept.-Nov., 1928; rep., Cyprus at internat. cong. of experimental forest stations, Stockholm, July-Aug., 1929.
USHER, Capt. C. G., M.C.—Ast. dist. comnrs., Kenya, May, 1920; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927.

USHER, Walter Whitbread.—B. 1886; ed. at Marist Bros. Coll., Uitenhage; elk., Swaziland, 1910; registr. and master, sp. of. Swaziland, and Sheriff, 1916; dep. astt. comnrs., Mbabane, 1919; served in Anglo-Boer War, 1901, S.W. campaign, 1914-15 and Flanders, 1916-18.
UTTLEY, Denys Plurat, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. St. John's, Leatherhead and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; col. audit dept., May, 1930; astt. audr., Tanganika Territory, Aug., 1930.

VAITHIANATHAN, KANTHIAH, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1923; astt., Colombo kach., July, 1923; ditto Ratnapura kach., May, 1924; ag. officemast., ditto, Sept., 1924; extra office astt., ditto, Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1925; ditto, Avissawella, February, 1926; office astt., Galle kach., Nov., 1926; addnl. astt. gov't. agt., Hambantota, Dec., 1928; office astt., Colombo kach., Mar., 1929; addnl. astt. gov't. agt., Nov., 1929; astt. col. sec., Mar., 1930.

VAN DER MEULEN, Sir Frederick A., Kt. Bach. (1919); O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1875; ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons. in mod. hist.), 1896; M.A., 1900; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1900; West circuit; astt. dist. comnrs., Sierra Leone, 1907; solr.-gen., 1908; ag. atty.-gen. and also ag. puisne and circuit judge on various occasions, 1908-1911; ch. mag., Gambia, 1913; puisne judge, Nigeria, Oct., 1919.

VAN DER SPUY, Adrian Johannes, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.P.H. (Cape Town).—Ast. health offr. for Union of S. Africa, and D.D.M.S., Union Defence Forces; lt.-col., S.A.M.C.; late H.S. and H.P. Western Infirmary, Glasgow; capt. S.A.M.C. (overseas); O.C. mily. hosp., Wynberg, and Roberta Heights.
VAN DER STER, Willem Cornelis.—Dir., secondary triangulation, Union of S. Africa; 25th Aug., 1919; dir., trigonometrical survey, Union of S. Africa, 1st Oct., 1920.

VAN DE VELDE, Marcel Arthur Maurice.—Ed. at Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; elk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., May, 1900; tempy. astt. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., Mar., 1903; astt. audr., Uganda Prot., June, 1903; astt. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., 1904; local audr., Uganda Prot., Nov., 1909; audr., Uganda Prot., May, 1910; audr., Kenya, Oct., 1923.
VAN HEERDEN, Senator the Hon. H.C.; M.L.A.—Formerly chmn. of comtees; min. of agric., Union of S. Africa 1913-19; pres. of the senate since 1921.
VAN LANGENBERG, William James Albert.—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1929; astt., Ratnapura kach., Jan., 1930; astt., Anuradhabura kach., May, 1930.

VAN NIEKERK, Christiaan Andries.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schl., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; commdt. for Kroonstad in Anglo-Boer War (wounded); del., peace confce., Vereeniging, 1902; mem., compensation comn., till 1903; mem., leg. assem., O.F.S., 1907; leg. assem., Union., 1910-24; mem., pensions grievances comn., chmn., sp. pensions bd., 1925; mem., Union senate since 1924; prea., 1930.

VAN REENEN, Reenen Jacob, B.A., C.E.; Assoc. M. Amer. Soc. C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. at S.A. Coll., Cape Town; at graduation obtained Victoria scholarship, proceeded to Lehigh Univ., Pennsylvania, U.S.A.; astt. engr., irrigtn. dept., Cape Colony, Sept., 1909; ditto, Union of S. Africa; supt. of roads and local wks., O.F.S. Prov., May, 1912; mem. of Union Drought Investigation Comn., 1920-24; chmn., Drought Investigation Comn. of S.W. Africa, 1923-24; mem., irrig. finances comn., 1924-25; chmn., sp. irrig. comn., 1926; mem., Angola bdly. comn., May-July, 1926; chmn., irrig. comn., Aug., 1926; mem., natural and historical monuments comn. since 1923.

VAN RENEN, Andries Christoffel.—Appointed 7th Jan., 1890 to the law dept., Cape of Good Hope; astt. mag., Sept., 1901; mag., Feb., 1908; senr. relieving mag., Sept., 1912; mag., Port Elizabeth, Jan., 1930.

VAN RYNEVELD, Col. Sir Pierre Herbert Andreas, K.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918), M.C. (1916), B.A., B.Sc.—R. 1891; ed. Grey Coll. Schl., Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein, Imp. Coll., Univ. of London; 2nd lieut., 7th Loyal N. Lancs. Regt., Aug., 1914; heut., R.F.C., Apr., 1915; detachment C.O., Senussi campaign, Egypt, 1915-16; flight comdr., Palestine, 1916; ditto, Salonika, 1916; squad. comdr., London night defences, 1916-17; squad. comdr., No. 17 sqd., B.E.F., 1917; severely wounded; wing comdr., XI. (Army) Wing, 2nd Army, 1917-19; ditto, Army wing, Rhine army of occupation, 1919; ment. in desig. six times, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med., Order of the Nile, 4th cls., Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur, France, Croix d'Officier, Ordre Leopold avec Croix de Guerre, Belgium; S. African liaison offr., Air Miny., 1919-21; lieut.-col. (temp. col.), Permanent Force Union Defence Forces, 1st Feb., 1920; director of air services, U.D.F., 1st Feb., 1920.
VAN SCHALKWIJK, JAN ZACHARIAH.—B.A.; inspr. of schls., O.F.S. Prov., Union of S. Africa, Nov. 1917.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, L. M. A. N., B.A. (Cape), Ph.D. (Amst.).—B. 1888; ed. Normal and S. African Coll., Cape Town, also in Holland and Germany; teacher, Cape Prov., seven years; organising inspr., Union dept. of educn., 1921.

VAN WAKKEL, WILLIAM.—B. 1874; degree civ. engnr., tech. univ., Delft, Holland, 1902; ast. drainage engnr., Pretoria mun., 1906; ast. engnr., irrigtn. dept., Pretoria, 1908; hydrographic ast., 1909; ast. hydrographic survr., 1913; hydrographic survr., 1916.
VAN ZEYLEN, FREDERICK CORNELIS.—B. 1885; mech. and elec. engnr., draughtman, harbr. engnr.'s office, Nassau, Bahamas, 1923; ast. civ. engnr., 1925; dep. civ. engnr., 1925; D.P.W., May, 1928.

VAN ZIJL, HENDRIK STEFANUS, L.L.B. (Camb.), B.A., L.L.B. (Cape).—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1903; M.P., Cape of Good Hope, 1905-10; parly. draftsman, Union of S. Africa, 1910-15; K.C., 1919; judge, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1920; chmn., delimitation comm., July, 1927.

VAREY, JAMES ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1879; apprentice, city engnr.'s office, Leeds, 1898; ast. engnr., ditto, 1900; ditto, waterworks engnr.'s office, Leeds, 1905; irrigtn. engnr., irrigtn. dept., Ceylon, 1907; irrigtn. engnr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, 1913; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, Feb., 1920.

VASKESSEY, HENRY HARRISON.—B. 1891; clk., Western Pacific high comm., Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., June, 1915; clk. in ch., accts., W. Pacific Sect., 1919; ch. clk. and acctnt., Apr., 1920; ag. sec. to W. Pacific high comm., for short periods in 1920, 1922, 1926 and 1927-29.

VASSALL, HENRY HOLLAND.—B. 1887; ed. Bedford Grammar Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; ast. res., Nyasaland, 1911; res., 1919; admstr. offr., 1st grade, Dec., 1923; ag. prov. comsmnr., Jan., 1929 to June, 1930.

VASSALLO, EDWIN P., L. S. & A.—B. 1891; ed. Flore's Coll. and Malta Univ.; grad. as land survr. and architect, 1914; elec. for 7th divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for pub. wks., 16th Aug., 1927; edr. "The Malta Times and Progress" until Aug., 1927.

VASSALLO, S., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Ed. Malta Lyceum and Malta Univ.; M.D., 1913; F.R.C.S. (Edin.), 1928; govt. exhib., for science, 1906-1907; ditto for medicine, 1909-1913; post-graduate work at Univ., Coll. Hosp. and London Hosp.; clinical ast., St. John's Hosp., W., and Royal Ear Hosp.; supernumerary med. offr., Leeward Is., 1914; D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (Duncan med.); temp. coman., R.A.M.C. (ment. in deeps.); med. offr., Uganda, 1919; res. surg. offr., Zanzibar Prot., 1926.

VAUGHAN, JOHN HENRY, M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (hist. tripus), 1914; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-21; M.C., Arras, 1917; ast. dist. coman., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to Br. res. and clk. of the coun., July, 1924; ag. regis., high ct., 1927; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1929; res. mag., July, 1929.
VEITCH, HON. WILLIAM ANDREW.—B. 1870; mem., N.Z. H. of R., since 1911; min. of lab. and min. of mines, 1928-30; min. of transport, 1929-30; min. of rly., 1930.
VENABLES, OSWALD ERIC.—Cadet, S. Stltms., Nov., 1914; on active ser., June, 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, Sept., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Dingdingo, Feb., 1922.

VENN, HARRY NORMAN.—West Australian service, 1895-1900; S. African war (2 medals, 5 clasps); Imperial army pay dept. in Australia, 1902-03; ch. clk. and acctnt., dist. hosps. dept., Transvaal, Aug., 1903; pub. health dept., Transvaal, 1904; acctnt., col. sec.'s dept., 1905; acctnt., dept. of interior, May, 1910; ag. undersec. for interior, Union of S. Africa, 1915; undersec. for the Interior, Dec., 1919; comanr. for immigrn. and Asiatic affrs., and prot., Indian immigrn., Natal, May, 1927.

VENNING, GUY, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., and assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of 2nd div. of the div. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf'd to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903; comp. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; staff offr., 9th Jan., 1928.

VENOUR, LOUIE STEPHEN DOUGLAS HAMILTON.—B. 1886; ed. pvtly.; capt., 6th batt., Middx. Regt.; ast. comanr., pol., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. comanr., pol., Aug., 1915; ag. staff instr., in 1916 and 1919; comanr., pol., Jan., 1917; King's Pol. Med., 1923; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., in 1925, 1927, 1928 and 1929; ag. comanr., pol. G.I.D., June to Nov., 1928; senr. comanr., pol., 1929; ag. dir., prisons in 1930.

VERITY, JOHN.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, copyist, treasy. and oust., Br. Honduras, 1908; 3rd cls. clk., regis. gen.'s office, 1910; 2nd cls. clk., atty. gen.'s office, 1912; resig., 1913; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1918; dep. clk., etc., St. Catharine, Jamaica, 1918; clk., cts., Trelawny, 1920; ag. res. mag. for various periods, 1922-26; res. mag. for various periods, 1922-26; res. mag., Hanover, 1926; ag. judge, Kingston et al., 1927.
VERNEY, FRANK ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1874; vet. offr., Natal, 1901-08; prin. vet. offr., Basutoland, 1908.

VERNON, ROLAND VENABLES, C.B. (1924).—B. 1877; ed. at Clifton and Oxford; schol. of Balliol, 1895-9; 1st els. moda., 1897; prox. acc. Hartford schlr.; Craven schlr., 1897; Jenkyns exhib., 1899; 1st els. lit., hum., 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. inseco.'s dept., inland rev., Nov., 1899; 2nd els. clk., C.O., 13th Aug., 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot, comanr., 1903; mem. managing comte. of E.I.O.; ast. priv. sec. to Lord Elgin., Jan., 1908; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 16th Apr., 1908; reed. S. African Union medal, 1910; ag. 1st els. clk., 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Denman, gov.-gen. of Australia, 1911-12; 1st els. clk., 9th Nov., 1912; visited and reported on British Solomon Islands, Nov.-Dec., 1911; represented H.M.G. on Anglo-French New Hebrides confere., May-Aug., 1914; transf'd to treasy., Oct., 1914; lent to Miny. of Munitions, June, 1915; ast. gen. sec., 1916; visited France and Belgium on sp. serv., Feb., 1917; ast. sec. to treasy., May, 1918; dep. acct.-gen., B. of E., Apr., 1920; lent to C.O., Middle East dept as ast. sec., finan. and economic, Apr., 1921; visited Egypt, Palestine and Trans-Jordania on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv. at Lausanne for Turkish Peace Treaty, Nov. to Dec., 1922 and June, 1923; transf'd to permanent staff of C.O. as ast. sec., Jan., 1924; seconded for service as finan. adviser to govt. of Iraq, 1925; returned to C.O., 1st Oct., 1928; mem. Palestine Cur. Board, 1928.
VERSCHAFFELT, PAUL DESIRÉ NESTOR, C.M.G. (1930), LL.B., F.I.A.N.Z.—B. 1887; grad. in law, Victoria Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. pub. serv., 1904; asst. pub. serv. comntr., 1921; pub. serv. comntr., 1923.
VETCH, CAMPBELL FOSTER.—B. 1886; ed. Belford and South Eastern Agrl. Coll., Wye; asst conserv., forests, S. Nigeria, 1911; conserv., 1915; senr. conserv., 1928.

VIADER, RENE, M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; ent. civ. serv., Mauritius, Nov. 1899; registr., col. sec's office, May, 1919; finan. asst., P.W.D., Mar., 1923; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1926; 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1926; ag. astt. col. sec., 1925-30.

VICKERS-HAVILAND, L. A. W.—B. 1895; licent., R. Fusiliers, 1914-18; seconded, 2nd K.A.R., 1918; asst. polit. offr., 1920; admstive offr., 2nd grade, 1922; asst. dist. offr., 1926; passed law exam., 1923; higher Swahili, 1924; attld., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

VICTORIA, RIGHT REV. GERARD HEATH LANDE, Lord Bishop of Victoria, Hong Kong.—B. 1861; ed. at Trin. Coll. and Ridley Hall, Camb., B.A., 1884; M.A., 1888; D.D., 1907; consec. at Lambeth Parish Church, 29th June, 1907; curate of St. Bride's, Liverpool, 1884 to 1888; vicar of St. Benedict's, Liverpool, 1888 to 1896; of Litherland, 1896 to 1905; of St. Cyprian's, Liverpool, 1905 to 1907; chrmn., Litherland schl. bd., 1898 to 1903; chaplain to Liverpool dist. lay readers, 1892 to 1905.
VIDAL, M. R. R.—Asst. dist. comntr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912; 1st grade admstive offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924.
VIMPANY, HAROLD RAVENSCROFT.—B. 1870; clk., Basutoland, 1907; treas. clk., 1913; clk. to asst. comntr., 1915; dir., indu. schl., 1916.
VINCENT, GEORGE ALVES, M.B.E. (1931), M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1872; asst. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Trinidad, 16th Mar., 1901; supt., ditto, 1st Apr., 1909.
VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY, L.M.S.S.A., Lond.—B. 1896; ed. Colet Court, St. Paul's schl., St. Mary's Hosp., and London Univ.; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1915.

VISCHER, HANNES, C.B.E.—B. 1876; ed. Switzerland and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1903; res., 1906; dir. of educn., 1908-18; lieuq., Gen. List, 1915; capt., 1915; major, 1916; Rtoile Noire, 1917; Legion d'Honneur, 1918; Crown of Italy, 1918; Crown of Belgium, 1918; C.B.E., 1918; demob., 1919 with rank of major in G.R.O.; mem. and sec., S. of S.'s advisory comittee, on native educn. in Trop. Africa, Nov., 1923.

VISSER, DANIEL HENDRIK, J.P.—B. 1877; entered Cape pub. serv., 23rd Mar., 1894; asst. mag. and ag. civil comntr., 1920-06; clk.-asst., Cape House of Assembly, 8th Apr., 1907; clk.-asst., House of Assem., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1910; clk., House of Assembly, 1st Oct., 1920.
VISSER, DR. THOMAS CHRISTOFFEL—Mem., Union assem., to 1929; mem., Union senate, 1930; chmn. of countees, 1930.

VIVIAN, ARCHIBALD COMLEY, A.R.S.M., B.A., D.Sc., M.I.M.M.—B. 1884; ed., St. Paul's Schl. and Royal Schl. of Mines; A.R.S.M., 1906; inspr. mines and ag. sec. for mines, Gold Coast, 1912; seconded for war serv., 1917-19; Cambridge Univ. (King's Coll.), 1918; B.A. (Cantab) by research, 1919; dept. sci. and ind. research, 1919; sp. research, 1920-22; nat. phys. lab., 1922; D.Sc. (Lond.), 1926; mines dept., North Rhodesia, 1927; sec. for mines, Sept., 1927.

VLIELAND, CHARLES ARCHIBALD.—B. 1890; ed. Exeter Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (schol.); ist cls. hon., nat. sci., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, 1917; 3rd astt. sec., fedl. secretariat, 1919; astt. dist. offr., 1929-21; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, 1922; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., June, 1925; attld., col. secs. office, Singapore, Nov., 1926; ag. 1st astt. sec. (A) Singapore, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1926; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, July, 1927 to Sept., 1928; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Apr., 1929; supt., census, Malaya, Mar., 1930.

VOIGT, FREDERICK CARL MARTINUS, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1879; clk., dept. of just., 1901; pvt. sec. to min. of nat. affra., 1910; senr. clk., nat. affrs. dept., Pretoria, 1912; dept. of just., Feb., 1913; astt. mag., Krugersdorp, 1916; prof. astt., atty-gen's office, Bloemfontein, 1921; ditto, Cape Town, 1926; pub. serv. inspr., 1928; astt. prov. sec., Cape adminstr., 1930.
VOLLET, DENYS HENRI RENE.—B. 1898; served in Great War; med. offr., Basutoland, 1927.

VOLNEY, JOSEPH HENRY LOUIS.—B. 1875; entd. pub. ser., St. Lucia, Feb., 1892, as supernm. clk., post off.; ag. 4th clk., treasy., Apr. and May, 1893; ag. rev. offr., June to Nov., 1893; clk., immig. offr., Feb., 1894; 4th clk., treasy., Apr., 1894; ag. 3rd clk., in 1895, 1896, and 1897; 3rd clk., treasy., Jan., 1898; ag. 2nd clk. and treasy. acctnt., May to Oct., 1898; ag. 2nd clk. and cashier, treasy., Aug., 1900, to Apr., 1901; 2nd clk., regiar.'s off., Apr., 1901; ag. clk., Aug. to Oct., 1901; ag. audit clk., Aug. to Nov., 1902; transf'd. on temporary duty to pub. wks. dept.; ch. clk., regiar.'s dept., Apr., 1903; ag. regiar. on several occasions, 1908-1915; ag. mag., dist. III, Mar. to May; astt. mag., dist. I, Sept. to Dec., 1913, and from Aug., 1914; ag. marshal of the Royal court (Admiralty jurisdiction) from Sept., 1914.
WADDEL, WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1931).—B. 1868; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. state advances, dept., 1895; astt. supt., 1904; dep. supt., 1907; supt., 1922.

WADDINGTON, E. J., O.B.E. (1919).—Astt. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. prov. comntr., Jubaland, Nov., 1919; senr. astt. sec. secretariat, Kenya, Feb. to Sept., 1923; clk. of couns., Sept., 1923; astt. sec., govn', confee., 1926; rea. comntr., Mombasa, 1928.

WADE, A. DE V., O.B.E. (1931).—Ed. Lancing Coll., exhibtr. and Keble Coll., Oxford; scholar; M.A.; astt. di-t. comntr., E. Africa Prot., March, 1912; ag. dep. ch. native comntr., Kenya, 1926; prim. astt., native affrs. dept., Jan., 1928.

WADE, CECIL HENRY, O.B.E. (1929), B.A. (hons.), 1909.—Ed. Lancing Coll. (exhibtn.) and Keble Coll., Oxford; 3rd grade res., Nyasaland, 5th Aug., 1911; asst. ch. sec. July, 1920; 1st asst. sec., June, 1921; examr. in Chinyanja language; ag. ch. sec., May, 1926; ag. treas., July to Dec., 1927; ag. ch. sec. May 1929 to Jan., 1930 and Apr., 1930.

WADIA, SIAXAX HIRJI.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1915; attd., Jaffna Kach. June, 1915; addl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn., Oct., 1916; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov. Oct., 1916; finan. asst. to chrmn., munici coun., Colombo, Jan., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1920; pol. mag., Avissawella, June, 1920; ag. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, July, 1921; asst. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; addnl. asst. contr., rev., Aug., 1925; asst. comsrr., stamps, Jan., 1926; asst. contr., rev., Nov., 1926; ag. asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, Mar., 1928.

WAIAPO, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. WILLIAM WALMSLEY SEIDWICK, M.A., D.D.—B. 1858; ed. Maidstone Gram. Sch. and Christ Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1881; served in marine batt. with ships at Suakin, 1884-5 (Egyptian medal with Suakin clasp and Khedive's bronze star); vicar of Waikari, New Zealand, 1901-3; of Akaroa, 1903-4; of St. Luke's, Christchurch, 1904-13; Bishop of Waiau, 1914.

WAINWRIGHT, WILLIAM FREDERICK, O.B.E.—B. 1881; Indian pol., 1899; accompanied Sir Charles Yate on polit. mission to Meekan, S. Persia; transf. to Bengal pol., 1901; on sp. famine opera., 1906-08; serv. in Gt. War, Egypt and Palestine, 1916-20; major, Territorial Reserve (Yeomanry); dep. dist. comdt. of pol., Palestine, 1920; dist. supt., pol., Palestine, 1926.

WAIT, WALTER ERNEST, C.M.G. (1930).—B. 1878; ed. Fettes and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1902; addl. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., and addl. coman. of requests and pol. mag., Mannar and Puttalam during pearl fishery, Feb., 1905; asst. land settm. offr., May, 1905; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Dec., 1910; asst. land settm. offr., Feb., 1911; on mil. duty, Aug., 1914; pol. mag., Colombo, May, 1915; asst. govt. agt. Puttalam and Chilaw, Sept., 1916; deputy-colr. of customs, Colombo, Apr., 1919; settlement offr., June, 1920; ag. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; contr., Indian immigr. lab. in Ceylon, Apr., 1923; ag. contr., rev., Dec., 1925; mem., leg. coun.; prin. collr., cust. and food contr., Mar., 1927; ag. col. treas., Mar. to July, 1927; prin. collr., cust. and food contr., July, 1927; inspgr., offr., mag's. cts., Nov., 1929; ag. conservator, forests, July, 1930.

WAKEFIELD, ARTHUR JOHN, B.Sc., N.D.A., N.D.D.—B. 1900; ed. Brewood Grammar Sch., Harper Adams Agr. Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; 2nd lieut. Shropshire Light Infy., 1918; vety. dept., N. Rhodesia, 1923; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; senr. agr. offr., 1930.

WALCOTT, SIR HENRY BARCLAY, KT. BACH. (1930).—C.M.G. (1913), C.B.E. (1926); B. 1886; excise and customs offr., St. Lucia, 1884; treasy. acctnt., 1890; ag. ch. clk., treasy., 1891; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., St. Vincent, 1892; ag. treasy. acctnt., Grenada, 1892; ch. clk., audit office, Dec., 1893; ag. postmr., 1895-6; ag. ch. of pol. and excise, 1897; 2nd audit clk., col. sec.'s off., Br. Honduras, 1897; auditor, Feb., 1898; ch. asst. treas., Gold Coast, Jan., 1900; ag. treas., and mem. ex. and legis. coun., dir., pub. offr. & guarantee fund, mem. bd. of educn., July, 1900, to Jan., 1901, and May to Oct., 1902; ag. commr. of police and supt. of prisons, 1901-1902; pres. Accra town coun., 1901-1902; treas. and collr. of cust., regar. of shipping, pres., quarantine bd., chmn. poor relief comittee., mem. of bd. of educn., mem. ex. and leg. couns., Br. Hond., Mar., 1903; J.P., Apr., 1903; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1905-08; collr. of cust. and detaining offr., M.S. Act, Trinidad, 1909; colony's del. at conferees. on Canadian reciprocity, Ottawa, 1913 and 1920; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1918, Mar.-Nov., 1919, July-Aug., 1920 and from Jan., 1921 to Jan., 1922; treas., Trinidad, Jan., 1924; ag.-gov., July to Aug., 1924; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1924 and Dec., 1925 to Feb., 1926; chmn., agrl. bank, 1925; ret.
WALKER, A.—Laboratory supt., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1918.
WALKER, ARTHUR FRANCOIS GREGORY, M.C.—B. 1884; asst. supt., prisons, Ceylon, Jan., 1910; inspr. gen., prisons, Feb., 1922.

WALKER, JOHN C.—4th batt. Yorkshire regt.; Coorga Nagpore rifles; served with Yorks. I.Y. in S. Africa (two medals with 5 clasps); asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1905; mentioned in despatches, 1906; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1910.
WALKER, REGINALD PLUMER, A.M.I.T.—B. 1891; travelling audr., F.M.S. Rly., 1914-18; asst. acct., 1918-25; dep. acct., Jan., 1926; ag. chief acct., F.M.S. Rly., Feb., 1926-Jan., 1927.

WALKER, WILLIAM HENRY, C.M.G. (1917); I.S.O. (1912); B.A.—B. 1884, apptd. 3rd class olk., gov't. gen'l. office, Canada, 1887; promoted to 2nd cls., 1892; 1st cls., 1898; ch. clk., 1908; chf. clk., dept. of external affairs, 1909; asst. under-secretary, for external affairs, 1912.

WALL, JAMES HORNE DARRELL.—B. 1871; clk. and storekeeper, Holberton Instit., Antigua, Nov., 1891; shortd. reporter to W. Ind. Roy. Coman., 1897; aud. def. force accnt.; ag. ch. clk. to aud.-gen., Leeward and fedrl. accnt., June, 1899; also clk. to trade and income tax comsrs., Sept., 1899; ch. clk. to aud.-gen. and fedrl. accnt., May, 1900; ag. treas. of Antigua and federal treas., May-Oct., 1909, and Aug., 1910; ag. inspr. of scolls., Leeward Is., Apr., 1911, to Aug., 1912; also ag. treas. of Antigua, and federal treas., Mar.-Apr. and Aug.-Sept., 1912; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., May to Aug. and Oct.-Nov., 1914; auditor-gen., Tonga, Mar., 1915.
WALLACE, CYRIL R., L.R.C.F., L.R.C.S., L.M. (Ireland).—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-18; medical officer, Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918.

WALLACE, CAPT. DAVID BUAN, M.C., M.P.—B. 1892; ed. Harrow and Sandhurst; joined 2nd Life Guards, 1911; adjt., 1915-18 now capt., res. of offr.; served in France, 1914-18; wounded; three times ment. in desps.; on staff of mily. attaché, Washington, 1919; A.D.C. to govt. gen., Canada, 1920; M.P. for Rugby, 1922-23; priv. sec. to Mr. Amery as 1st Lord of the Admty., 1922-3; M.P. for Hornsey, 1924; partly priv. sec. to Mr. Amery as S. of S. for the Colonies, 1924.

WALLACE, GEORGE BRUNTON, B.Sc. (agr.), Ph.D.—B. 1899; ed. George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh; mily. serv. R.G.A., 1918-19; B.Sc., 1922; Ph. D., 1925; sen. demonstr. in agr. and forest entomology, univ. of Edinburgh, 1922-23; asst. in plant pathology, bd. of agr. for Scotland, 1924-25; microscopept. game pres. dept., Tanganyika territory, 1926; mycologist, dept. of agr., 1927.
WALLACE, Sir Lawrence Aubrey, K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. 1910.—Acting admstr., N.E. Rhodesia, 15th May, 1907; ag. admstr., N.W. Rhodesia, Jan., 1909; admstr., Northern Rhodesia, 1911; ret., 1921.

WALLACE, William Thomas Ewart.—Ed. privately and at St. George's Cathedral Coll., Cape Town; Cape govt. postal tel. serv.; transf., B.S.A. Co's serv., 1889; lieut., Mashonaland Defence Force (Matabele War), 1893; transf., B.S.A. Co.'s admstrive serv., 1894; resig. after Jameson raid, 1906; lieut. and adjt., B Squadron, Lord Loch's Horse, intelligence offr., 4th and 8th Mounted Infy. Brig., A.P.M. 6th Mounted Infy. (De Lisle's), capt., 4th Batt. "The King's" (Liverpool Regt. (S. Africa), 1899-1902; asupt., R.E. tel. constrn. party, S. Nigeria, 1903-05; seconded for serv. under C.O. as asst. asupt., tels., Uganda and E.A.P., 21st Dec., 1905; asst. P.M.G. and asst. tel. engr. for the Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1912; transf., dep. P.M.G., Gold Coast, 12th Mar., 1913; ag. P.M.G. on various occasions; major (2nd in commd.), 13th (Service) Batt., "The King's" (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; censor, Accra, 1915-16; special military service, Palime, Togoland, Feb., 1916; served, headqrs. supply staff, min. of munitions, Aug., 1916 to Jan., 1917; ag. engineer-in-chie, postal tels., Gold Coast Nov., 1923 to May, 1924.

WALES, Dudley J. C.—B. 1895; ed. Merchant Taylors Schl.; served with H.A.C. in France, 1914-15; 2nd lieut., Middlesex Regt., 1915; W. African Regt., Sierra Leone, Nigeria, 1916-17; transf., 2nd K.A.R. and served in E. Africa 1918-21; Somaliland campaign, 1920; dist. pol. offr. Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

WALSH, Geoffrey, C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1884; Imp. cust. serv., Apr., 1906; collr. of cust., Mombasa, May, 1913; seconded to occupied territory of German E. Africa to organise cust. serv., with rank of capt., 1916-17; in charge of cust. dept. of occupied area, German E. Africa, Jan. to July, 1917; dep. comsnr., cust., Kenya and Uganda, Jan., 1920; comsnr., cust., Kenya and Uganda and mem., leg. coun., Kenya, Apr., 1923.

WALTER, A., F.R.A.S.—Joined magnetic and meteorological dept., royal observatory, Greenwich, 1892; asst. director, Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Apr., 1897; statistical adviser to Mauritius Royal comsnr., 1909; census comsnr., Mauritius, 1911 and 1921; dir., Royal Alfred Observatory, May, 1911 to 1921; ag. asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Nov. to Dec., 1918; food, trade and export contr., Mar., 1920 to Jan., 1922; ag. prot. of imigrts. and poor law comsnr., Jan., 1922; mem. of coun. of govt., 1922; headed deputation to India for the re-opening of emigr. to Mauritius, 21st Dec., 1922; statistician, Kenya, 1926; statistician to govt.'s confee., 1926; author of "Sugar Industry of Mauritius"; Insurance of crops against cyclones; editor, Mauritius almanac.

WALTERS, John Radley.—B.A. Cantab.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; att. Kandy kach., 15th Dec., 1911; att. Galle Kach., May, 1912; sg. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1912; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1915; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, July, 1917; pol. mag., Karunegala, Dec., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, July, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Feb., 1924; dist. judge, Matare, Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Mar., 1927; ag. govt., agt., Badulla, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1929.

WALTON, B. S.—B.A. (Oxon.); cadet, S. Stiltna, civ. serv., 29th Nov., 1913; ag. asst. dist. judge and 4th mag., Singapore, 23rd Mar., 1914; asst. censor, 23rd Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., Singapore V.R., 28th Jan., 1916; ag. asst. supt. govt. monopolies, Singapore, 2nd Nov., 1916; supernumerary offr., cls. V., 25th Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), 17th Dec., 1917; ag. asst. supt. govt. monopolies, Penang, 5th Aug., 1919; supernumy offr., cls. IV., 29th Nov., 1920.

WALTON, Hon. Sir Edgar Harris, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898, and in Union parlmt. since 1910; treas.-gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald; high comsnr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1921-25.

WALTON, Sir George O'Donnell, K.T. Bach. (1925).—B. 1871; matriculated London Univ., 1891; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1893; practised at the bar, Barbados, 1894 to 1902; acted on several occasions as pol. mag. and judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "F," Barbados, Dec., 1902; lieut. of M.I., Barbados volunteers; ag. chief just. St. Lucia, 18th May to Oct., 1908, and from March to Oct., 1911; mag. and cor., dist. "C," St. Kitts, Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1911-15; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, May, 1915; ag. adminstr. on several occasions in 1916 and in 1916 and 1917, ag. chief just., from Oct., 1916 to Mar., 1919; atty.-gen., B. Honduras, Mar., 1919; ag. chief justice, May to Nov., 1919; ch. just., Grenada, July, 1921; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, Apr., 1926.
WARD, C. E.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 7th Aug., 1909; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1918.

WARD, Espine Francis, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1912; trans., Gambia, 1918; trans., G. Coast, 1920; on special service for C.O., Oct., 1915 to Feb., 1916; temp. lieut., gen. list, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1916; attached Cameroon Expdy. Force, 1914-15; priv. secy. to ag. gov., Gambia, 1918 senr. med. offr., July, 1927; author of "Beri-Beri, Aetiology, Symptoms, and Treatment." "Favourite Prescriptions" (2nd edition), "Deaths from Lightning Stroke" (Br. Med. Jnl.)

WARD, Howard Hyland.—B. 1884; ed. pub. schls. and cent. business coll., Stratford, Ont.; ch. clk., operating dept., bd. of rly. comsnrs. for Canada, 1911; ch. clk. and asst., 1913; dep. min. of labour, 1923.

WARD, Joseph Corbet, O.B.E.—Lieut. (afterwards capt.) I.Y., S. Africa, 1900-02 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1902-04; Br. E. Africa, Sotik expdn. (med. and clasp), 1905; prin. regier., docs. land dept., E.A.P., 1905; attd. 3rd K.A.R., 1914; post. commdt., lines of commun., 1915-18; staff offr. for Br. E. Africa, 1918; temp. liut.-col. (deeps. 3 times, 3 war medals); staff offr. and intell.-offr., Kenya, 1920-25; sec., office of H.M. Eastern African Dependencias, London, 1926.

WARD, LENNOX, K.C., B.A. (lit. and phil.), LL.B., 1908 (Univ. Cape of Good Hope).—Called to bar, 1909; prof. astt., atty.-gen., Cape, 1910; prof. astt., solr.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., mandated territory, S.W. Africa, 1919; solr.-gen., 1925; atty.-gen., Natal, 1926.

WARD, LEONARD KEITH, D.Sc. (Adelaide), B.A., B.E.—Ed. Sydney Gram. Schl. and Brisbane Gram. Schl.; entd. univ. of Sydney, 1897, as Queensland govt. exhibiter, and graduated in arts, 1900, and in engineering, 1903; in service of Broken Hill Proprietary Mining Co., till Oct., 1903; lecturer in geology, mineralogy, petrology and mining geology, Kalgoorlie schl. of mines, W. Australia, 1903-7; astt. govt. geologist and inspr. of mines, Tasmania, 1907-11; govt. geologist, S. Australia, Jan., 1912; also dir. of dept. of mines.

WARD, PERCY HAROLD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (London).—B. 1876; ed. Loughborough Grammar Schl. and Guy’s Hosp.; dist. surg., Murrayburg, Cape Colony; civ. surg., S.A. Field Force, S.A. War; med. offr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1st March, 1909; 2nd lieut., N.R. Rifles, Dec., 1915; lieut. 1st Nov., 1916; capt., N.R. Med. Corps, Oct., 1917; ag. prin. med. offr., N. Rhodesia on various occasions, 1920-28; prin. med. offr., July, 1928; dir., med. and sanv. services, Apr., 1930.
WARD, ROBERT PERCY.—B. 1868; ed. Wanganui Coll. schl., N.Z.; New Zea. and dept. of just., 1884; held various offices prior to 1921, in which year apptd. departl. inspr.; under-sec. of just., 1928.

WARD, WILFRED ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. privately and at Christ’s Hosp.; served in France with Civil Service Rifles, 15th London Regt., as lance-corp.; 2nd lieut., 3rd Lance Fusiliers, May, 1915; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, and served with them in France, Salonica and Palestine; M.C. at taking of Jerusalem and promoted capt.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; gov’t. rice agt., Batang Padang, Sept., 1920; ag. 2nd astt. dist. offr., Tajah, Mar., 1921; ag. astt. dist. offr., Batang Padang, May, 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; astt. dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1922; offr., cl. V., Nov., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Apr., 1923; offr., cl. IV., Nov., 1923; ag. sup’t., lands, Kota Bharu, Feb., 1925; dist. offr., Port Dickson, Apr., 1926; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1927; ag. astt. adviser, lands, Kedah, July, 1927; offr., cl. III., Nov., 1928; dist. offr., Temerloh, July, 1929; 2nd astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Feb., 1930.

WARD, WILLIAM ERNEST FRANK, B.A., B.Litt.—B. 1900; ed. Mercer’s Schl., London, Dulwich Coll. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; open hist. schol., Lincoln Coll., 1918; 2nd cls. hon., B.A., mod.hist., 1922; B.Litt., 1923; Goldsmith’s exhib., mod.hist., 1921; Ridley Hall, Cambridge; mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

WARDE, HARRY MONTAGU JOHN.—B. 1873; ed. at Epsom Coll., entd. Crown Agents’ office, Sept., 1892; astt. head of miscellaneous dept., Oct., 1900; chief cashier, July, 1914; head of gen. stores dept., Dec., 1920; head of pay and checking dept., Nov., 1921; head, gen. stores dept., Oct., 1924; mem., fair wages advisory comtce., 1930.

WARDLE, BRIAN.—B. 1891; ed. King William’s Coll., Isle of Man; Indian pol., 1911-22; King’s Pol. med.; supt. in charge, C.I.D., Zanzibar, 1924; imigrn. offr., May to Nov., 1925, Aug., 1926 to Feb., 1928 and from Aug., 1928; ag. astt. commdt., Apr.-Aug., 1930.
WARDLE, D. S.—Temp. astt. treas., Kenya, June, 1920; astt. treas., July, 1923.

WARD-PRIECE, HENRY LEWIS, B.A., Barrister.—B. 1888; ed. Caterham Schl. and St. Catherine’s Coll., Cambridge; astt. dist. comntr., S. Nigeria, 1912; astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1924; astt. sec., S. Provs. secretariat, 1926; cls. I, grade I, adm. serv., 1929.

WARDROPER, PERCY REDDELLA, M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1892; B. S. A. pol., 1912; clk., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1914; seconded to N. Rhodesia pol., 1915; lieut., N. Rhodesia pol., Jan., 1917; capt., Jan., 1927; comntr., pol., Dec., 1926; oh. inspr., prisons, Apr., 1927.
WARNE, OSWALD HORNBY.—B. 1891; ed. Rossall Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; lieut., South Staffordshire Regt.; astt. dist. comntr., Ashanti, G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914.

WARNER, BRODICK ASHTON.—B. 1888; ed. Winchester Coll.; astt. dist. comntr., Uganda Prot., 1912; lab. comntr., 1922; 1st grade administrative offr., 1923; senr. astt. sec., 1924; dep. prov. comntr., 1929; late capt., 5th batt., City of London Regt. (T.F.); capt., Territorial Force Reserve.

WARREN, CHARLES HY., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E.—B. 1873; ed. Maritzburg Coll., Natal; govt. rly., 1889; constrn. and surveys, 1889; underground mining, 1894; govt. rly., 1896; dist. engr., C.S.A.R., 1905; surveys, irrign. dept., 1909; circl. engrnr., ditto, 1917; sup’t. engrnr., irrign. dept., 1925.

WARREN, EDGAR HERBERT, M.B.E. (1930).—Served with 28th L.Y., S. African war, 1902 (medal with 2 clasps); S. African constab. (civil), 1903-1908; lieut., Transvaal H.A., 1907; clk. in customa, Nyassaland Prot., 1908; offr. of customs, 1909; also imigrn. offr., 1913; principal imigrn. offr., May, 1914; astt. contr., cust., Apr., 1920; ag. comptr., cust., July, 1922; comptr., cust., Mar., 1926.

WARREN, ERNEST, D.Sc. (Lond.)—Demonstrator of zoology at Univ. Coll., Lond., 1894; life mem. of convocation of the Univ. of Lond., 1898; astt. lecturer and museum curator at Univ. Coll., Lond., 1899; elected fellow of Univ. Coll., 1898; astt. prof. of zoology at Univ. Coll., 1900; mem. of the faculty of science, Univ. of Lond., 1902; dir. of Natal govt. museum, 1st Feb., 1903.
WARREN, FREDK. DRUMMOND.—B. 1881; sec. inspr., Basutoland, 1914; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1922; war serv., 1915-19.

WARREN, PHILIP DAVID, C.M.G. (1908); Assoc. Inst.C.E., F.R.G.S., F.Met.Soc.—B. 1851; astt. survr., Ceylon, 8th Feb., 1878; dist. survr., 1st Oct., 1879; office astt. to survr.-gen., 16th June, 1888; astt. survr.-gen., 26th Mar., 1897; survr.-gen., 23rd June, 1904; M.L.C., Ceylon, 1907-N; ret. 1910.

WARRINGTON, J. C.—B. 1887; educ. Marlborough and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., 1906; served in France with R.E. (Signals) 1914 to 1918; twice ment. in despr.; French Croix de Guerre; astt. dist. comntr., G. Coast, 1919; dist. comntr., 1st Apr., 1924.

WATERALL, LEONARD SAXTON, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul’s Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cl. class. tripes. 1907; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1911; cadet, N.E. Rhodesia, 1908; astt. native comntr., 1911; native comntr., 1912; passed law and higher Chiemba; ag. Br. vice cons., Katanga, 1914; inspr., Rhodesian natives, Katanga, 1914; war serv., comntr., Rifle Brigade. 1914; France and Belgium, 1915-16; Egypt, 1917; O.C., prisoner-of-war camps, Palestine, 1919; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1918; ag dist. polit. offr., 1919-20; passed higher Swahili, 1922; law, 1924; dist. offr., 1924; dep. lab. commnr., 1926; ag. lab. commnr., 1926 and 1929.

WATERLAND, HAROLD.—B. 1888; ed. partly, and Harper-Adams Agr. Coll.; dipl. agr.; M.R.A.S.E., sci. mast., govt. gram. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1911; censor, 1914; asst. chem., Impl. dept., agr., St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1914; sen. asst., agr. dept., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1916; ag. dir., agr., various periods, 1916, 1918, 1920; del., Imp. Entomol. Conf., 1920; agr. dept., Zanzibar, May, 1922; ag. asst. dir., agr., in 1926, 1928 and 1929; asst. dir., agr., July, 1929.

WATERMEYER, ERNEST FREDERICK.—B. 1880; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch Boys Sch., Bath Coll. and Calais Coll., Cambridge; maths. schol., Calais Coll., 1899; maths. tripes, 1902; law tripes, 1903; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1904; pres., income tax sp. ct., 1919; ag. puisme judge, Cape Prov., divn., sup. ct., 1920; puisme judge, C.P. divn., sup. ct., 1922.

WATERS, BERNARD DAVID.—B. 1879; telegraphist, Cape Colony, May, 1902 to Aug., 1906; postal asst., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1905; ch. asst., Apr., 1912; sec., post office, May, 1913; ag. P.M.G., Jan., 1925 to May, 1926; dep. P.M.G., Nov., 1926.

WATERS, HAROLD BERTRAM B.A., Oxon.—B. 1896; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Guildford and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; served in The Queen's (Royal West Surrey) Regt. and Signal Serv., R.E., 1914-19; asupt., agric., Nigeria, Mar., 1921; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to report on oil-palm industry, 1926; dep. asst. dir., agric., Nigeria, Apr., 1927; commr., Canadian national exhib., 1928; dep. dir., agr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1928.

WATERS, HORACE FRANK, M.C., V.D., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Perse Gram. Schl., Camb.; asst. engr., P.W.D., Perak, Oct., 1908; seconded for mily. serv., May, 1914; 2nd lieut., 2/16th batt., London Regt., Feb., 1915; lieut., Sept., 1915; temp. capt., mach. gun corps., Jan., 1916; temp. major, July, 1916; instr., genl. hdqrs., mach. gun schl., B.E.F., May, 1917; ex. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Nov., 1917; corps mach. gun offr., VIII, corps hdqrs., B.E.F., May, 1918; demob., Mar., 1919; col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Med., Sept., 1921 and Dec., Sept., 1926; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Aug., 1922; ag. senr. ex. engr., Kinta, Jan., 1924; lieut., M.S.V.R., June, 1924; ag. ch. hydrau. engr., F.M.S., July, 1926; senr. ex. engr., Oct., 1926; capt., M.S.V.R., Apr., 1927; senr. exec. engr., Kinta, Jan., 1927; ag. state engr., Perak, Jan. to June, 1928; state engr., Johore, 1929.

WATKINS, OSCAR FERRIS, C.B.E. (1918) D.S.O. (1916).—Ed., Marlborough and All Souls, Oxford, B.A., M.A., 1910; served with Oxfordshire Lt. Infy. (43rd) during S. African War, Dec., 1899 to 1901 (Queen's Med. and 4 clasps); S. African constab., Dec., 1902 to Apr., 1904; col. sec.'s office, Transvaal, May, 1904 to Oct., 1907; sec. to Pretoria sewage comm. and govt. laboratories commn.; asst. dist. commn., Br. E. Africa, 16th Jan., 1908; dist. commn., Apr., 1914; seconded for Coast Land arbitrn., Sept., 1913 to Aug., 1914; seconded as intell. offr. to organise carrier transport D.A.D.T., E. African Forces, 1915; dir., mil. labour, 1916; ag. ch. native commn., Aug., 1920 to Aug., 1921; offi. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1920 to Feb., 1922; mem., land tenure commn., 1920; mem., central bd. of health, 1921; mem., native punishments commn., 1921; chmn., labour bureau commn., 1921; dep. ch. native commn., Aug., 1921; senr. commn., Jan., 1928.
WATKINS-PITCHFORD, H. C., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H.—B. 1900; ed. St. George's Coll., Surrey; St. Thomas's Hosp. Med. Schl.; E. Africa med. serv., Zanzibar, Feb., 1925.

WATSON, ANDREW D.—B. 1882; ed. common schls., Baddeck Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1905; ent. civ. serv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., 1906; actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst.of Actuaries, Lond., 1909; Assoc., Actuarial Soc. of America, 1913; Fellow, 1919; Contrib. to the jnl. of the Inst. of Actuaries, and the transactions of the Actuarial Soc.

WATSON, DONALD FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1883; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Seychelles, 1899; dispenser and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. to local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery comtece, 1900; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; ag. treas., collr. of customs, chmn., marine bd., and chmn., profiteering comtece., July, 1920 to Apr., 1921; contr., local clearing office, June, 1921; treas., postmr., collr. of cust., commr. of income tax, man. of savings bank, regis. of shipping, Apr., 1923.
WATSON, ERNEST SIBBLES, B.Sc. Tech.—B. 1902; ed. Cent. High Schl., Manchester and Coll. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc., Tech., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

WATSON, HON. HERBERT GORDON, I.S.O. (1922).—Clk., gov. of Zululand's office, 1896; ag. sec. for Zululand at different periods, 1896; olk., gov.'s office, Natal, Dec., 1896; transf. Cape civ. serv. and apptd. clk., gov.'s office, Mar., 1901; ch. clk., July, 1904; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1904 to Apr., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admatg. govt., major-gen. E. S. Brook, C.B., July, 1904 to Feb., 1905; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1907; priv. sec., major-gen. Sir Henry Scobell, K.C.V.O., Dec., 1909; asst. clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; sec. to prime min. and olk., exec. coun., Feb., 1916; clk., exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, 1922; admstr., prov. of Natal, Feb., 1923.

WATSON, JAMES, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. 1902, B.Sc. 1904; European mast., Raffles Institution, Singapore, 1906; science mast., 1910; inspr. of schls., Pahang, F.M.S., 1915; headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, 1920; inspr., of schls., Penang, 1921; ch. inspr. of English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., 1923.

WATSON, JAMES GILBERT.—B. 1889; ed. Kgl. Preuss. Forstakademie, Eberswalde, Germany; supt., govt. plantations, F.M.S., Feb., 1913; asst. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Oct., 1913; dep. conserv., forests, research, F.M.S., Nov., 1926.
WATSON, P. C. M.—Treasy. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924; prin. asst. treas., 1930.

WATSON, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1868; M.E.C. and M.H.A., Newfoundland; ed. at St. John's and Rugby; elected for Trinity, 1897, 1902, 1908 and 1909; mem., exec. coun.; i.d. sec., and partly, head of dept. of post office, telegraphs, educn., rly., pub. health and pub. charities,

WATSON, WILLIAM LAW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. Grammar Schl. and Robert Gordon's Coll. (Abdn.); loco. dept., G.N.S.R., 1899; loco. dept., G.W.R., 1905; engnr. design dept., Crown agents, 1912; min. of munitions, 1915; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engnr. (mech.), 1928.

WATT, ALEXANDER STUART.—B. 1884; ed. Edin. Acad., Edin. Instn., Blair Lodge and Edin. Univ.; writer to the signet, 1909; capt., 5th Batt. Royal Scots; ast. dist. commr., G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Dec., 1915; attached G. Coast Regt., 1915; with E. Africa Exped. Force, July, 1916.

WATT, SIR THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1867; ed. at priv. schls. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a solr. to Scottish cts.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commdg. Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educn., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of pub. wks. and interior, 1916; min. of pub. health, 1919; min. of posts and telegraphs and of public wks., 1921-24.

WATT, Rt. Hon. WM. ALEXANDER.—P.C. (1920); M.H.A.; treasur. of Victoria Jan., 1909; premier and treas., 1912, and again in Dec., 1913; resig. premiership to enter H. of R., C. of A., to which he was elected in Sep., 1914; min. for works and rly., 1917; ditto, from 10th Jan., 1918; treas., 27th Mar., 1918; min. for trade and cust., 13th Dec., 1918; acting prime min., 1919; resig. office, 1920; speaker, H. of R., 1923; ret. from politics, 1929.

WATTS, BERTRAM T., O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. Wellingborough Schol. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., F.R.G.S.; inspr., Egyptian govt. survey, 1905-08; inspr. Egyptian govt. survey, 1912; or sp. surveys in the Sudan for irrign. wks.; dir. sp. irrign. surveys, Mesopotamia, for Turkish govt., 1908-11; ast. dir. surveys, Cyprus, 1912-20; ag. regnr. gen., 1913, 1918, 1919 and 1920; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf., 1919, 1923 and 1926; regnr. gen., Cyprus, 1920; passed in mod. Gk. and Turkish; title altered to dir., land registration and surveys, July, 1927; dir., surveys, land offr. and commr., mines, Uganda, 1930.

WATTS, OLIVE.—2nd lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; capt., June, 1918; ment. in desps. and wounded, European War; acctnt., treas., Cyprus, 1920; acntnt. to pub. custodian and Cyprus contr. in addition, Apr., 1921 to May, 1923; passed exam. in Greek, 1921; ag. ast. treas., for various periods in 1924, 1926 and 1927; ag. ast. sec., col. sec's. office in 1926 and 1927; ag. dep. treas., July, 1927 to Jan., 1928; dep. treas., Jan., 1928; ag. treas., June to Oct., 1928.

WATTS, GUY TRACEY.—B. 1892; ed. Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, Apr., 1914; war serv., 1915-18; crown counsel, Nigeria, Mar., 1925; pol. mag., Calabar, 1928; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1930; chmn., bd. of educn., mem., leg. coun.; ag. ch. just., Sept., 1930.

WAUTON, Capt. EDGAR BENTON, R.E. (S.R.).—B. 1883; ed. Uppingham; ast. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1909; astd., Nigerian Regt., Cameroon, 1914-15; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1917; cls. 1, grade 1, admstive serv., 1928.

WAYLAND, EDWARD JAMES.—B. 1888; ed. Central Foundation Schol., City of London Coll., R. Coll. of Science; nat. sci. studentship (C.L.C.), 1906; nat. schol. (R.C.S.), 1907-10; post grad. & Marshall research schol., 1910; A.R.C.S., M.I.M.M.; F.G.S., London, etc.; ast. min. surv., Ceylon, 1912; war serv. in 254 Tunnelling Coy., R.E., as forward demolition reconnaissance offr. (IVth Army); ag. O.C., 3rd Water Boring Sec., R.E., apptd. to Uganda, Oct., 1918; dir., geological survey, 1928.

WAYNE, RICHARD ST. JOHN ORMEROD.—B. 1904; schol. of Eton, 1917; Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1923; M.A., 1930; ast. sec., col. sec's. office, Cyprus, May, 1927; ag. pvtv. sec. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1927-29; passed exam. in mod. Greek, June, 1928; ag. ch. ast. sec., May-Aug., 1930.

WEATHERHEAD, ARTHUR EVELYN.—B. 1880; served in S. African constab., 1900-1907; S. African war, Queen's medal with 5 clasps; dist. supt., Uganda police, 1908; ast. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., 4th Apr., 1910; ag. dist. commr., 1914; dist. commr., 1919; ag. prov. commr., 1924; lab. commr., Uganda, 1st Jan., 1925; ag. prov. commr., Sept., 1925; prov. commr., 2nd grade, 1927; prov. commr., 1st grade, 1927.
WEAVING, L. A.—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921.

WEBB, CECIL JOHN.—R.N., 1909-11; marine offr., S. Nigeria, 1914; war serv., Cameroons Expdy. Force; H.M.S. "Minotaur" flagship; second cruiser squadron, 1916-17; commanded H.M.S. "Waterfly" and "Firefly," 1917-18; 1st lieut., H.M.S. "Venus," flagship of E. Indies squadron, 1918-19; returned to marine dept., Nigeria, 1919; prin. marine offr., 1928.

WEBB, CAROL RICHARD, O.B.E. (1926), M.C.—Ast. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; traffic man., Tanganyika Territory rly., Oct., 1919; gen. man., govt. rly., Sierra Leone, June, 1923; gen. man., Palestine rly., 1930.

WEBB, AMBROSE HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Clifton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. schol.); 1st Victoria prize man. and John Brooke school, King's Inns, Dublin, 1908; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1921.

WEBB, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1888; ed. Christ Coll., Blackheath and Guy's Hosp.—M.B. (hons.); B.S., Lond.; D.P.H., Lond.; M.R.C.S., (Eng.); L.R.C.P., Lond.; med. offr., Uganda, 1913; sp. serv., venereal diseases, 1913; temp. capt., Uganda med. serv., 1914-16; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1918-19; senr. med. offr., 1923; spec. offr., med. supt., Mu'ago hosp., 1926; dep. dir., med. serv., Uganda, 1928.

WEBBER, ARTHUR FREDERICK CLARENCE.—B. 1873; ed. Merton Coll., Oxon., B.A., 1896; called to bar, Inn. Temp., Feb., 1896; ag. stip. mag., 1898, and March to Oct., 1900, Br. Guiana; stip. mag., Esseguibo, Oct., 1900; ditto, Whim Correntyne, July, 1902; ditto, Berbice judicial dist., 1904; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1906, to Jan., 1907, and in July, 1909; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, Sept., 1909.

WEBLEY, H. L.—B. 1884; ed. pvtvly., Bloxham; E. African pol., Feb., 1912; served in European war, 1915-19; ast. commr., pol., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1919; seconded to act as commr., pol., inspr., prisons and sheriff, Gambia, May,

WEBSTER, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1889; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa provisional admnstr., Oct., 1916; adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922; ag. senr. comanr., Mwanza dist., Feb. to Nov., 1923; ag. senr. comanr., Dar-es-Salaam dist., Jan. to Dec., 1925; dep. prov. comsnr., Jan., 1926; ag. prov. comanr., E. Prov. Mar., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1926; ag. sec., native affrs. and mem., exec. coun., Jan. to Apr., 1927; ag. prov. comanr., Feb., 1928; prov. comanr., junr. grade, May, 1929.

WEBSTER, G. W., M.B.E. (1913).—Ed. at Rossall; served with Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 16th Nov., 1901; 2nd cls. res., 23rd Aug., 1909; 1st cls. res., 1918; senr. res., 1920.
WEBSTER, GUSTAVUS WILLIAM, M.B.E.—B. 1876; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1901; 2nd cls. res., 1909; senr. res., 1920.
WEBSTER, H. G.—Asst. treas., Kenya, July, 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1923; ch. storekeeper, P.W.D., 1929.

WEDDERBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN.—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matara, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; asst. land settm. offr., Jan., 1911; ag. addtl. asst. col. sec. in addition to own duties, July, 1912; ag. asst. col. sec., July, 1912; asst. land settm. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordoe.", Aug., 1912; asst. censor, Aug., 1914; asst. land settm. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordoe.", Oct., 1914; asst. censor, Nov., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1915; asst. settm. offr., July, 1920; ag. settm. offr., Mar. to Nov., 1921 and from Apr., 1922 to June, 1924; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1925; addnl. govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1928; govt. agt., S. Prov., Apr., 1929.

WEDGE, CAPT. CYRIL NORMAN.—B. 1896; schol., mod. hist., St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge, Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., Mar., 1915; served in Gallipoli (Suvla Bay), 1915; "14-15" Star, Br. War and Victory Meds.; seconded 1st K.A.R., 1918; served in Portuguese E. Africa and Nyasaland, 1918-20; capt. and adjt., 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyika Territory, 1920-23; pay and qrtmr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, January, 1923.

WEERAKOON, WILLIAM AUGUSTUS.—B. 1878; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1899; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Sept., 1900; Batticaloa, Nov., 1901; Kurunegala, May, 1902; Jaffna, Mar., 1904; Ratnapura, May, 1904; pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1908; Point Pedro, Aug., 1908; addtnl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, N.W. Prov., Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Panadure, Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Negombo, June, 1915; pol. mag., Jaffna, Aug., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Dec., 1916; pol. mag., Puttalam, Sept., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1920.

WEGERTER, WILLIAM FREDERICK, A.M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1897; ed. Crossley Schl., Halifax; Fleetwood L. & Y. & L.N.W. Joint rly., marine dept., 1914; draughtsman, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L. & Y. rly., 1919; power plant supt., loco. works, C.M.E. dept., Horwich L.M.S. rly., 1922; loco. man., loco. dept., F.M.S. rly., July, 1928.

WEIGALL, Lieut.-Col. Sir William Ernest GEORGE ARCHIBALD, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1874; ed. Wellington Coll., Royal Agric. Coll. (gold medallist); major (ret.), 3rd Batt. Northants Regt.; served in S. Africa, 1902; M.P. (U.), Horncastle Div., Lincs., 1911-1920; capt. Lincolnshire Yeomanry, and lieut.-col. and inspr. Q.M.G. services, Eastern Comd., 1914-17; mem. of national salvage council; dep. chmn., surplus govt. property disposal bd.; mem. of select comte. on national expenditure; mem., national agric. coun.; pres. agric. organisation soc.; dep. chmn., federation of county agric. comtees.; J.P., Kent and Lincs.; gov., S. Australia, 27th Mar., 1920; assumed govt., 9th June, 1920; resig., 1922.

WEIR, Hon. ROBERT, P.C. (Can.), B.A.—B. 1882; ed. Clinton Coll. and Toronto Univ.; major, Canadian militia; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., July, 1928; min. of agr. in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-el. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.

WEIR, JOHN DALZIEL, M.I.E. (S.).—B. 1887; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; on mil'y. serv., 1914-16 (Mons Star); asst. loco. suptd., Nigerian rly., 1917; dist. loco. suptd., 1922; wks. man., 1926.

WEISBECKER, ARNOLD.—Clk., census dept., Cape Prov., 17th Apr., 1891; P.W.D., 29th Feb., 1892; admstr.'s office (Cape Prov.), 1st July, 1910; ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1911; ch. local govt. inspr. and ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; prov. secy. and ch. local govt. inspr., 1st Sept., 1922.

WEISBERG, HYMAN, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1880; 1st cls. math. tripos.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1917; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1917; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, May, 1919; asst. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu, May, 1920; ditto, Kuala Langat, May, 1922; mag., Seremban, July, 1922; ag. state treas., Perak, Dec., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Upper Perak, July, 1924; ag. state treas., Perak, Aug., 1924; temp. ag. chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Matang, in addn., May, 1926; ag. dist. offr., Larut and regiar., titles, Perak, 1927; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, October, 1927; ag. pub. trustee and offl. admstr., F.M.S., Oct., 1928; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, in addn., Apr. to June, 1929.
WELBY, H. E.—Asst. dist. comanr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. comanr., Kenya, Jan., 1920.
WELCH, T. B.—B. 1898; ed. Brighton Coll.; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1923; asst. divn. supt. (loco.), 1927.
WELCH, THOMAS BURGES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.B.B.S., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1881; med. supt., Chacachacare leper settlement, Trinidad, 23rd Sept., 1925.

WELLINGTON, Bishop of, Right Rev. THOMAS HENRY SPROTT, M.A., D.D.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; Hebrew prize, 1877; B.A., 1878; (Respondent) 1887; Hebrew Testamur, 1879; Divinity Testimonium (1st cls.) and Church Formularies Prize, 1879; M.A., 1882; B.D. and D.D. (jure dignitatis), 1911; deacon, 1879; priest, 1880; curate of Holy Trinity, Kingston-on-Hill, 1879-1882; St. John the Evangelist, Waterloo Road, 1882-1886; vicar of St. Barnabas, Auckland, 1886-1891; examining chaplain to Bp. of Auckland, 1888-1891; vicar of St. Paul's, Wellington, 1892-1911; examining chaplain to Bp. of Wellington, 1892-1911; consecrated Bp. of Wellington, 6th June, 1911.

WELLINGTON, ARTHUR ROBERTSON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. and D.P.H. (Camb.).—B. 1877; med. offr., Sarawak, Sept., 1905; med. offr., port health office and med. offr., quarantine station, Singapore, 10th Sept., 1908; health offr., Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, 13th May, 1909, health offr., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1911; senr. health offr., F.M.S., 5th Mar., 1919; offi. del. to 4th cong., Far Eastern Asso., Trop. Med., Java, Aug., 1921; also offi. del., 5th cong., S'pore, Sept., 1923; offi. del., L. of N. Eastern Health Socce., S'pore, Feb., 1925; malaria res. offr. in addn., May, 1925; offi. del., 6th cong., F.E.A.T.M., Japan Sept., 1925; offi. rep., L. of N. interchange of health offrs. in Japan, Korea and Manchuria, Nov., 1925; offi. mem. advisory coun., L. of N. Eastern Health Bureau, S'pore, Jan., 1926; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1926; offi. del. for F.M.S. and S.S. at Internat. Health Confce., Melbourne, Dec., 1926; on offi. duty, Panama, Feb., 1927; ch. health offr., F.M.S., Feb., 1927; offi. del., Far Eastern Asso., Trop. Med., India, Dec., 1927; offi. mem., L. of N.
