Eastern Health Bureau, Delhi, Dec., 1927; ag. prin. med. offr., June, 1928; dir., med. and sani. services Hong Kong, 1929.

WELSH, ALEXANDER SIMPSON, K.C.—B. 1881; Cape civ. serv., 1899; B.A. (Cape), 1904; LL.B. (Cape), 1907; professional assist. to crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1914; prof. assist. to atty.-gen., Cape, 1918; crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1921; law adviser, 1926; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1929.

WELSH, GORDON JAMES.—B. 1894; ed. Cathedral Schl. and Simon Langton Schl., Canterbury; war serv., 1914-19; India, Aden and Mesopotamia, as pvt., 1/4th Batt. The Buffs and 2nd lieut., The Queen's Own Royal West Kent Regt.; clk. to govr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; ch. offr., 1923; seconded to Somaliland as ass. sec. to govr., Mar., 1929; ag. dist. commr., Burao dist., Aug., 1929.

WELSH, WILLIAM THOMSON.—B. 1873; clk. to mag., Cape Town, Nov., 1892; Bedford, Dec., 1892; Kimberley, Jan., 1894; Klipdorp, April, 1895; Barkly West, July, 1895; Beaconsfield, Jan., 1896; Prince Albert, Mar., 1896; Jansenville, May, 1896; ass. reg. clerk., high ct., Kimberley, July, 1896; clk. to mag., Victoria East, Aug., 1900; ass. res. mag., Paarl, Aug., 1901; King William's Town, Aug., 1904; Middelrift, Aug., 1907; res. mag., Libode, July, 1908; Mganduli, May, 1910; Kokstad, Feb., 1916; ass. chief mag., Transkeian Territories, and res. mag., Umtata, Apr., 1919; ch. mag., Transkeian Territories, Sept., 1920.
WERTE, ALBERTUS JOHANNES.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; teacher at Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-19; M.P. for Kroonstad, 1921-26; admstr., S.W. Africa, Apr., 1926.

WESSELS, SIR JOHANNES WILHELMUS, Kt.—Ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town, Downing Coll., Cambridge, Middle Temple; admitted to Cape bar, 1886; Transvaal bar, 1887; legal adviser to Lord Roberts, 1900-01; judge, sup. ct. Transvaal, 1902; judge pres., Transvaal prov. divn., 1920; judge of appl., 1923.

WESTON, HARVEY CHARLES, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1894; ed. Chatham House Schl., Ramagata, Goldsmiths Training Coll., Univ. of Lond., Glasgow Univ., and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; war serv., 1914-19; ch. ass't engrmr., Zanzibar, 1921; res. engrmr., Dar-es-Salam, 1925; prin., Acro Tech. Schl., May, 1929.

WHATELEY, MAJOR H. H., O.B.E., M.C.—Served with Royal Engineers in France, 1914-19 (twice ment. in despa.); ass't to ch. engr. and sec. to govr., public works dept., Iraq, 1920; ass't adviser to min. of communications and wks., Jan., 1921; adviser to min. of communications and wks., Apr., 1922.

WHEBBELL, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; joined Royal North West Mounted Pol., Canada, Dec., 1912; proceeded overseas with 1st Canadian contingent, Sept., 1914; joined Irish Guards, Oct., 1914; transf'd. to 1st Guards Brig. Machine Gun Coy., Sept., 1915; served throughout Great War with Guards Divn., 1914-19; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. Medals, Meritorious Serv. Med., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgian), ment. in despa., Apr., 1917; rejoined R.N.W.M. Pol., Canada, May, 1919; ass't comdt., pol., Bahamas, 21st Apr., 1921; ag. comdt., June, 1921 to Apr., 1923 and from May to Dec., 1924; comdt. of pol. instr. of prisons, provost marshal and ch. of fire dept., 20th Aug., 1926.

WHEELER, LEONARD RICHMOND, B.A., B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1888; science master, Letchworth Schl., 1909-12; Gram. Schl., Antigua, 1912-14; ent. col. serv. as science master, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad Dec., 1914; coman. in 1st batt., B.W.I.R., from 1st Trinidad contingent, Sept., 1915; pilot, R.A.F., 1917, served in Egypt and Salonica, 1916-19; returned to Trinidad, 1920; comanr. for Boy Scouts, Trinidad, 1920; transf'd. to S.S. and F.M.S. as European mast., Nov., 1921; science and maths. mast., Malay Coll., Kuala Kangsar, Dec., 1921; vice-prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Tangjong Malim, Aug., 1924; ass't instr., schl., Perak, July, 1926; instr., schl., Malacca, Sept., 1926; ditto, Pahang, Mar., 1928; author of "Flora of Antigua," "Scouting in the Tropics," "The Modern Malay," etc.
WHEELER, CAPT. RICHARD CHAMBERLAIN, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., Kenya, July, 1913; senior veterinary officer, Apr., 1920.

WHINERAY, SIDNEY BUCKINGHAM.—B. 1879; ass't. acct., Baro Kano rly., N. Nigeria, 1911; Nigerian rly., 1913; sp. investgn., accts., miny. of munitions, 1918; ass't. acct., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ag. dep. ch. acct., 1923 and 1928-29.

WHISKARD, GEOFFREY GRANVILLE, O.B. (1923).—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1909; ent. H.O., 1911; ass't. prte. sec. and subsequently prin. pte. sec. to successive secs. of state, 1915-20; ass't. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin Castle, 1920-22; ass't. sec., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924; vice-chmn., Oversea Settlement C'tee., 4th Sept., 1929; ass't. under sec. of State, D.O. in addn., 1st Jan., 1931.
WHITCOMBE, SIDNEY F.—B.1899; ent. C.O., July, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., ass't. clk., C.O., 1917; on mil. serv., Jan., 1918 to Apr., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

WHITE, ALEXANDER HAROLD, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1889; ass't. collr. (now ass't dist. comanr.), Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; ag. 2nd ass't sec. and clk. of coun., June, 1915; ag. 1st ass't sec., Oct. to Dec., 1915; ag. mag., His Highness' ct. for Zanzibar, 1918 (Brilliant Star, 4th cls., July, 1919); ag. dist. comanr., Feb. to July, 1919; ass't sea., German E. Africa, provisional admtn., July, 1919; dist. political offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. mag., Dar-es-Salam, Apr. to July, 1922; seconded for sp. serv. as Br. commr., Anglo-Belgian bndy. comm., (Ruanda-Urundi), Aug., 1922 to Aug., 1925; prov. commr., Jan., 1926.

WHITE, ARTHUR V.—B. 1871; ed. Woodstock Pub. and High Schls., and Univ. of Toronto (M.K.); engrnr. with several prominent firms; lect. in median. drawing and machine design, Toronto Tech. Sch.; later with dept. of pub. wks., Can.; consulting engrnr., Coman. of Conservation, Can., until its abolition, 1921; consulting engrnr., representing Can. to the internat. joint commn., Lake of the Woods investigation under the boundary waters treaty, 1911; consulting engrnr. to Hydro-electric Coman., Ontario, 1922; has written extensively on water power, elec. development and fuel supply.

WHITE, Rt. Hon. Sir WILLIAM THOMAS, P.C. (1920) ; K.C.M.G. (1916), B.A.—B. 1866; ed. High Schls., Oakville and Brampton, Ont., Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate of the Ontario law schl.; apptd. manager National Trust Co., Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently general manager and vice-pres. of the same institution; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of finance in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; disto in National govt., Oct., 1917; re-elec. at g.e., Dec., 1917, for Leeds-Brockville; resig. portfolio, July, 1919.
WHITE, W. V. C.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., 8th Sept., 1914 to 14th Dec., 1915; discharged through wounds, 17th Feb., 1916; ent. Miny. of Food, 18th Mar., 1918; appt. cler. offr., O.O., 24th Apr., 1922.

WHITEHEAD, FRANK EDRED, O.B.E. (1926), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); certif., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med.—B. 1880; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; late hon. surg. County Hosp., Huntington; med. offr., Sierra Leone, May, 1913; Cameroons Exped. Force, Aug., 1914 to Feb., 1916; med offr., Somaliland, June, 1916; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Nyassaland, July, 1923; ag. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1922; dir., med. and sanry. services, 1923.
WHITEHEAD, JOHN.—B. 1894; ed. High Schl. and Tech. Coll., Greenock; associate mem., of Inst. of Naval Architects; naval architect, Nigerian marine dept., 1928.
WHITEHEAD, LOUIS HAROLD.—B. 1871; chief clerk to atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1st Apr., 1906.

WHITEHORN, ROGER HERBERT, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1911; attd. col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1911; attd. Colombo kach., Aug., 1912; ag. pol. mag., Gampola, Jan. 1913; office asst., Ratnapura kach., Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Galle, May, 1914; 4th asst. col. sec., Sept., 1914; on military duty as asst. censor, Nov., 1914; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Apr., 1916; 4th asst. col. sec., Aug., 1916; ag. third asst. col. sec., Oct., 1916; priv. sec. to gov' in addn. Nov., 1916; on military duty, 1918; extra asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1920; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1920; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admty. govt., 5th-23rd Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Kandy, Sept., 1922; ag. asst. gov't. agt., Kegalla, Mar., 1923; asst. gov't. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1924; ditto, Puttalam and Chilaw, Dec., 1924; ditto, Matare, Oct., 1927; chmn., munie. coun., Kandy, Nov., 1927.

WHITEHOUSE, BERTRAM REGINALD.—B. 1891; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham, and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1913; cadet, S. Stltms. civ. ser., Feb., 1915, and lent to Johore; attached to censor's office, Singapore, till Apr., 1918; granted permission to join H.M. Forces, Apr., 1918; attached to land office, Johore, Sep., 1919; ag. aspt. of Indian imgrm., Nov., 1919; ag. ofl. of land rev., Johore Bahru, June, 1920; offr., cls. IV., 9th Jan., 1922.

WHITFIELD, HUGH.—B. 1885; ed. Royal Acad. Inst., Belfast and Belfast Univ.; B.E. (2nd cls. hons.); asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Perak, Nov., 1912; asst. engrnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1914; ag. exec. engrnr., grade III, in 1915-16-18; ag. exec. engrnr., grade II, Aug., 1919-Jan., 1920; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; exec. engrnr., govt. factory, Nov., 1923-Aug., 1927; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., Seremban, May, 1928; anti-malaria engrnr., F.M.S., Dec., 1928.

WHITLEY, MICHAEL HENRY.—B. 1872; ed. at Blundell's Schol. and King's Coll., London; jun. offr., Selangor, 1896; dep. pub. pros., F.M.S., 1906; legal adviser, Johore, 1910; called to the Bar, Inner Temple, 1913; ag. legal adviser, F.M.S., 1916; puisne judge, S.S. and jud. commr., F.M.S., 1918; ag. senr. puisne judge, S.S., 1921; ag. puisne judge, Penang, 1st Jan., 1922; ag. senr. puisne judge, Penang, June, 1922; atty.-gen., S.S., 1925.

WHITTALL, LIEUT.-COL. PERCYAL FREDERICK.—B. 1877; ed. Felsted; joined army, 1896; lieut., Lincolnshire Regt., 1902; served in S. African war, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal and three clasps); lieut., G. Coast Regt., 1907; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services during outbreak of yellow fever at Secondee, 1910; asst. dist. commr., N. Territories, G. Coast, 1912; released for service in European War, 1915-1919 (D.S.O. and one bar, French Croix de Guerre, 1914-16; Star (ment. 4 times desps.); dist. commr., N. Territories, G. Coast 1918; dep. prov. commr., 1927; prov. commr., 1928.
WHITTING, CHARLES EDWARD JEWEL.—B. 1900; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, 26th July, 1922.

WHITTLE, REGINALD ALFRED, M.C.—B. 1897 ed. Manchester Grammar Schol. and Queen's Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. math. mods., 1919; B.A., 1920; 2nd lieut., Manchester Regt., 1916; served in France, 1917; invalided from Army, 1918; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 1921; asst. sec., 1922.

WHYBRO, CHARLES, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; ed. Berkhainsted Schol., London Univ., Schol. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1920-23; economic tripes, pt. I, 3rd cls., 1923; law tripes, pt. II, 3rd cls., 1923; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WHYTE-SMITH, THOMAS STODDAERT.—B. 1883; ed. Merchiston Castle Schol. and Edinburgh Univ.; writer to signet, 1906; 2nd lieut., the Royal Scots, 1915; lieut., 1916; capt., K.A.R., 1918; 2nd asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, Feb., 1924; ag. asst. land offr., Mar. to Nov., 1924 and Sept., 1926; asst. crown solr., 1926; ag. mag., Kowloon, 1929.

WICKHAM, CAPT. DENIS HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; Connaught Rangers, Sept., 1914; served B.E.F., France, 1915-18; mentd. in desps.; transf'd. to Queen's Royal Regt., 1922; served with 5th K.A.R. Kenya, 1922-25; admstive. offr., Kenya col.; seconded to Somaliland, Apr., 1929.

WICKRAMASINHA, SENA PANDUKABHAYA, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1922; attd. to Matara kach., Dec., 1922; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Apr., 1923; extra office ast., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1925; office ast., ditto, June, 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Feb., 1926; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, June, 1927; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1927; ditto, Point Pedro, Apr., 1929; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, Mar., 1930.

WICKREMESINGHE, CYRIL LEONARD.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1912; att., Badulla kach., Apr., 1912; Matara kach., Oct., 1912; office ast. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Nov., 1913; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, and Avissawella, Mar., 1921; addnl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1925; ast. govt. agt., Putalam, Oct., 1926; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1928; ag. govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1930.

WIDDUP, CHARLES PONSONBY.—Clerical ast., treasy., B. Guiana, 1902; 6th cls. clk., lands and mines dept., 1903; ag. sub.-inspr. of police, 1903; sub.-inspr. of police, 1904; qualified as musketry instr., Hythe, 1905; dist. inspr. of police, 1908; passed Hindustani exam., 1909; county inspr. of police, 1914; dep. inspr. gen., B. Guiana, 16th Dec., 1920; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and commdt., local forces, Mar. to Oct., 1924 and from Aug., 1926 to Jan., 1927.
WIGHTMAN, J. W. E.—Ast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, Apr., 1914; ag. res. comsnr., 1926.

WIGHTON, ALEXANDER RITCHIE, Mem., Transvaal Socy. of Accnts.—Joined Impl. military rly., S. Africa, June, 1902; audit inspr., ch. accnt.'s dept., June, 1903; accntn. engrn.'s dept., Feb., 1904; ch. clk., ditto, July, 1908; inspr.; dept. of audr., S. African rly. and harbs., Apr., 1910; ch. inspr., ast. controller and audr.-gen.'s dept. Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; ch. inspr. (rly. and harb.), cont. and audit office, July, 1916.

WIGLEY, WILFRID MURRAY.—Ed., Derby schl.; held various clerical appts. under govt., St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1898; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1901; practised at bar of Leeward Is., for some years; ag. admtr., St. Kitts, puisne judge, Leeward Is. and atty.-gen., on various occasions; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

WILCOCKS, CARL THEODORUS MULLER.—B. 1861; ed. Godbolt's Comm. Acad., Graaff Reiniet; represented Fauresmith included Jagersfontein and Koffyfontein in first Union parl.; M.L.A. for Winburg (O.F.S.), 1915-24; mem., rlys. and harbs. bd., 1924; pub. debt. comsnr., 1925; admin., O.F.S., Mar., 1929.

WILFORD, SIR THOMAS MASON, K.C.M.G. (1930), K.C. (1929).—B. 1870; ed. Christ Coll., Canterbury, N.Z.; M.P. for Wellington Suburbs, 1900 and for Hutt from 1903-29; cmn., Wellington harbr. bd., 1908-9 (mem. 10 years); min. of just., marine, etc., in national govt., two years; leader of opposition and liberal party, 1920-25; min. of just. and defence, 1928-29; high comsnr. for N.Z. in London, 1929.

WILKINSON, ALAN FREDERICK LAWDER—B. 1892; ed. Royal Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; war service (Navy), Aug., 1914, to June, 1919; ast dist. comsnr., G. Coast, July, 1919; dist. comsnr., 1st April, 1924.

WILKINSON, ERNEST EDWARD, M.B.E. (1922).—B. 1874; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to the bd. of agriculture, May, 1894; transfd. to C.O., June, 1896; minor staff offr., ch. clk.'s dept., 1902; clk. to finance and concessions comte., 1916; clk. for legal instruments, Sept., 1921; staff offr., 1st April, 1922; librarian, 26th Feb., 1923.
WILKINSON, RICHARD CLARY.—B. 1905; ed. Kings Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, civ. serv., Dec., 1928.
WILKINSON, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1906; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1929; att., Kandy kach., Jan., 1930.

WILLAN, HAROLD CURWEN, M.C., R.A., OXON, Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple, 1930.—B. 1896; ed. Kendal Grammar Schi. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; open exhib., Jesus Coll., Oxford; lieut., R.G.A.; M.C., 7th June, 1917; cadet F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Tapah, May, 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Jan., 1923; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Dec., 1923; ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1925 and in addn., Dec., 1926; offr. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1926; ag. ast. regis. sup. et., Ipoh, Apr., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Ipoh, Aug., 1930.
WILLAN, REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Oakham Schi. and Guy's Hospital; med. offr. to Baro-Kano rly., Nigeria, 1909-1911; joined West African med. staff, 10th Jan., 1912.

WILCOCKS, MAJOR JAMES LUGARD, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; 2nd heut., The Black Watch, 14th Feb., 1912; A.D.C. to G.O.C., N. Army, India, July, 1913; A.D.C. to G.O.C. Indian Army Corps, France, Sept., 1914; (twice ment. in despa.); brig. maj., 1916; brig. maj., Bermuda, Mar., 1919; ret., Feb., 1922; placed on R. of O.; A.D.C., Kenya, 19th Feb., 1925; ag. sec., native affrs. dept., 1st May, 1928.
WILLDON, MARGARET ISABEL, M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; matron, Basutoland leper asylum, 1913.

WILLETT, THOMAS GRAHAM.—B. 1883; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; off. ast. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Aug., 1908; pol. mag., Chilaw, Dec., 1909; addl. pol. mag., Galle, Matara, Tangalla, Hambantota and Balapitiya, July, 1910; pol. mag., Jaffna, Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Aug., 1913; ast. land settm. offr., and special offr. under waste land ordce., June, 1914; spec. comsnr. in connection with the riots, June, 1915; ast. land settm. offr., Sept., 1915; on military duty, 1918; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Feb., 1920; pol. mag., Point Pedro, May, 1922; ast. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Jan., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Dec., 1924; ag. ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, 16th to 27th, Jan., 1926; ast. settm. offr., Feb., 1929; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1930.
WILLIAMS, A. D. J. B.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1912; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dep. dir., med. services, 1926.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, O.B.E. (1918), M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1873; ed. Llandovery Coll.; ast. wks. man., Vulcan loco. works, Lanch.; inspr., N.Z. and W. Australian rlys., 1898; Crown Agent's office, 1900; ast. med..
WILLIAMS, G. A., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.C.R.F. (Lond.)—Tempy. commission, R.A.M.C., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1921.

WILLIAMS, GILBERT NEVILLE, D.S.O., V.D.—Cape civ. serv., surv. office, 1892; dept. of agr., 1893; prin. clk., 1906; ch. clk., 1909; ch. clk., Union dept. of agr., 1912; ag. under-secr., 1919; confirmed, 1920; served, Bechuanaland Rebellion, 1897 (med. and clasp); S. A. War, 1899-1900 (med. and three clasps); served with Union troops, S.W. Africa, 1914-15; A.A. and Q.M.G., 3rd divn., E. African Expedy. Force, 1917-19 (D.S.O., 3 times ment. in despatches); sec. for agr., 1st Aug., 1926; del. to Imp. Agril. Confoc., London, 1927.

WILLIAMS, JOHN BASIL.—B. 1906; ed. Marlborough, McGill Univ.; Montreal and Cambridge; colonial exhibitioner, Trinity Coll., 1924-26; 2nd cls., nat. sci. tripos, pt. I., 1927; 2nd cls. pt. II., 1928, B.A. 1927; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 17th Oct., 1929.
WILLIAMS, J. H., M.I.C.E., M.Am.Soc.C.E.—B. 1873; res. engrnr., Singapore govt. rly.; engrnr.-in-ch., Canton-Hankow rly.; res. engrnr., Benue Bridge constrn., Nigeria, 1928.

WILLIAMS, O. G. R.—B. 1886; ed. Landing Coll. and Oxford; exhibitioner, Hertford Coll., 1906; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1909; Aubrey Moore studentship, 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in sec.'s off., In. Rev., Oct., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 4th June, 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Hewins, 26th Sept., 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 14th Jan., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 8th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. asst. sec., Feb., 1926.
WILLIAMS, PATRICK EDWARD WILLOWS.—B. 1904; ed. Shrewsbury Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WILLIAMS, PHILIP STANHOPE.—B. 1889; ed. at Harrow (head of the schl. 1907-08) and at Caius Coll., Camb. (schol. and Sayer schol.), 1st cls. classical tripos, 1911; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Dec., 1913; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Feb., 1915; passed cadet, June, 1915; offr., cls. V., Jan., 1916; asst. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1920; ag. temp. as dist. offr., Batang Padang, July-Aug., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1921; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Dec., 1921; 2nd mag. Penang, Mar., 1925; ag. Br. advr., Perla, May, 1925, to May, 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore, July, 1929; offr. cls. II., 1930.

WILLIAMS, E. GREGSON.—Ed. at Rossall Schl., Caius Coll., Camb.; B.A. 3rd cls. hon., Science Tripos; Royal Schol. of Mines, A.R.S.M., M. Inst. M.M., 1st cls. diploma; post-graduate course, Mysore goldfields, India; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Moh., 1911; ag. inspr. of mines, June, 1911; ag. chief survr., Sept., 1911; contr., mines, Tanganyika Territory, 1926; omsnr., of mine, 1929.
WILLIAMS, SIDNEY BRUCE.—B. 1880; S.A. constab., 1901; Swaziland pol., 1908; clk., 1911; R.J.P., 1916; dep. asst. comsnr., 1928.
WILLIAMS, THOMAS ALFRED.—B. 1875; accontg. clk., Basutoland, 1905; accontg., 1913; finan. sec., 1916; transfd. Bech. Prot. as audr., H.C. Territories, July, 1927.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1880; ed. Carnarvon County Schl., Univ. Coll., N. Wales and Univ. of Liverpool (engnr. dept.); attd: Portsmouth dockyard, 1909; sent to Hong Kong for electrification H.M. naval yd., May 1909; ch asst. engr., Shanghai munic. elec. dept., 1911; engr.-in-ch. and man., Penang munic. electy. supply and tramways undertakings, 1920; engr. and man., Penang Hill rly., Oct., 1923; dir., elec. dept., F.M.S., Jan., 1927.

WILLIAMSON, E.—B. 1884; elk., G.P.O., Transvaal, Mar., 1903; treas., Transvaal, July, 1904; senr. elk., Jan., 1919; prin. elk., Apr., 1921; sec., cabinet comte. on expenditure, 1922-24; ch. elk., estimates, June, 1929.

WILLIMOTT, STANLEY GORDON.—B. 1899; ed. Liverpool Univ. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; war serv., 1917-19; B.Sc., 1st asst. schl. of chemistry, Liv. Univ., 1921; A.I.C., 1921; Ph.D. (Liv.), 1923; research schl., St. Catharine's Coll., Camb.; Ph.D. (Cantab.) in agric. science, 1927; research chem., Imp. Chem. Industries, 1928-29; govt. analyst, Cyprus, Nov., 1929; author of numerous papers in scientific and med. journals.

WILLINGDON, 1ST VISCOUNT (cr. 1924); 1st Baron of Rotton (cr. 1910); Freeman Freeman-Thomas, G.B.E. (1917); G.C.S.I. (1918); G.C.I.E. (1913).—B. 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Brassey when gov. of Victoria, 1895; M.P., Hastings, 1900-06; Bodmin divn. of Cornwall, 1906-10; jn. lord of treas., 1906-12; J.P.; gov. of Bombay, 1913-19; ditto, Madras, 1919-24; major, Sussex Imperial Yeomanry; lord-in-waiting to His Majesty; govr.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1926.

WILLIS, HARRY GRAHAM.—B. 1875; ed. Marlborough Coll.; apptd. to N.E. Rhodesia, Oct., 1900; asst. native comntr., Jan., 1901; div. comntr., Sept., 1903; native comntr., Nov., 1904; ret. owing to ill-health, Oct., 1906; rejoined as elk., mag.'s ct., Livingstone, N.W.R., Dec., 1907; native comntr., Oct., 1908; ag. asst. mag., N.R., Sept., 1912-Mar., 1913; asst. mag., Apr., 1913; ag. res. mag., Barots dist., Sept., 1913-Mar., 1914; ag. mag., Nov., 1923-May, 1924; mag., May, 1924; J.P.; prov. comntr., Apr., 1929.

WILLIS, MAURICE A.—B. 1900; ent. M.O.D., G.P.O., May, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. elk., Apr., 1918; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Feb., 1919; cler. off., 1st Jan., 1920; transf., P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; transf., C.O., May, 1924.

WILLIS, NORMAN EDWARD OMMANNEY.—B. 1872; ed. at Eton; entd. crown agent's office, 1st Jan., 1891; asst. cashier, 4th Jan., 1899; asst. acctnt., 1st July, 1902; acctnt. and dep. chief elk., 9th Jan., 1913; head of gen. dept. and ch. elk., 15th Nov., 1920; head, finance dept., 26th Feb., 1930.

WILLIS, STANLEY SCOTT.—B. 1889; ed. St. Lawrence Coll.; P.A.S.I., 1912; staff survr., Kenya, 1914; mil. survr., 1915-19; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; on Anglo-Belgian bly. comm., 1922-24.

WILLIS, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1897; ed. London; elk., treas. and cust. house, Turks and Caicos Is., Jan., 1925; ag. asst. treas., postmr., collr. of cust., regur. of shipping and 'en. man., savings bank, May, 1928 to Aug., 1928; elk. to comntr., Aug., 1928.

WILLMOT, ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1881; ed. Lancing Coll. and Aspatria Agric. Coll., Cumberland; ast. survr., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1907; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., May, 1908; ag. ast., D.P.W., Apr.-Nov., 1913; engr.-in-charge, Kampala Port Bell rlyw. constrn., Dec., 1913; served with 4th K.A.R. in E. African Campaign, May, 1915 to Dec., 1918 (twice ment. in desps.); ag. D.D.P.W., Oct., 1920 to Apr., 1922; D.D.P.W., June, 1923; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1923-24, 1926-27 and 1929.
WILMOT, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.D., D.P.H., L.R.C.P.—Ast. health offr., dept. of pub. health, Union of S. Africa, 1913.

WILSON, BARI MAYOR, M.B., Ch.B. (1901), D.P.H. (1911), M.D. (1911).—B. 1878; ed. Malvern Coll. (Faber Exhib.), and Univ. of Manchester; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1904; dist. med. offr., Levuka, 1907; dist. med. offr., Ba., 1915; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1918-19; served with B.E.F. in France; res. med. offr., col. hosp., Suva, M.O.H. and bacteriologist, Fiji, 1922; prin. med. offr., chun., cent. bd. of health, mem., leg. coun., Br. Hond., 1923; suptg. med. offr., chun., cent. bd. of health, chun., quarantine bd., Jamaica, 1925; nominated mem., leg. coun.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES.—B. 1889; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (miz., jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. mod., B.A.); cert B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1913; attd. Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; ast. contr. of lab., Penang, Dec., 1914; passed cadet, Jan., 1915; on mil. serv., 2nd liet., 6th K.S.L.I., July, 1915; France, Feb., 1916; offr., o.v., May, 1916; liet., July, 1916; capt., Feb., 1919; demob., Mar., 1919; ment. in desp., dep. contr., lab., Seremban, June, 1919; offr., cl. IV., Jan., 1920. ag. sec. to res., N. Sembilan, in addn., during June, 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Ipoh, Dec., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., Dec., 1920; 2nd liet. and adj., Malay Vol. Infy., Perak, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, Jul., 1922; emigr. agt. in India, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. stamp and probate offr., Singapore, May, 1924; offr., cl. III., Jan., 1925; ag. dep. treas. (B), Singapore, in addn., Mar., 1926; ag. emigr. comntr. for Malaya, Madras, Feb., 1926; accomp. contr., lab. to Delhi, Feb. to Mar., 1926 and to Simla, Aug. to Sept., 1927; state treas., Perak, Oct., 1928; chmn. sny. bd., Larut and Matang in addn., Oct., 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore., Jan., 1929; ag. dep. contr., lab., Malaya, May, 1929; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang in addn., Nov., 1929; offr., cl. II., Jan., 1930.
WILSON, C. J.—Medical offr., E.A.P., May, 1911; dep. dir., med. serv., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., med. and sny. servs., 1926; prin. med. offr., F.M.S., 1926, 1928.

WILSON, CHARLES LIVINGSTONE.—B. 1874; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; entd. civ. serv., Grenada, as 2nd elk., G.P.O., Aug., 1892; ag. ch. elk., 1893; 2nd elk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; ex. off. sec., gen. bd. of health and bd. of educn., 1897-1903; ag. ch. elk. to gov. of Windward Is., 1907-8; ch. elk., govt. office, St. Vincent, elk. of couns., man. of printing sub-dept., and sec., hurricane loan bd., 1907-11; ch. elk., col. sec.'s office, elk. of couns. and man., printing sub. dept., 1911-19; col. postmr., Grenada, 1919; a dir. of the pub. offra. guarantee fund, 1919; sec. of reception comte., visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to Grenada, Sept., 1920.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and elk., Br. Solomon Is., June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, Jan., 1915 and Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, Nov., 1916; ag. collr., cust., Ma., 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., Apr., 1919 and Dec., 1920; acctnt. and intl. elk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1920; visiting dist. offr., Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, Apr., 1923; dep. comsnr. for W. Pacific, July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, Dec., 1923; ag. gov't. sec., Mar., 1925; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1925.
WILSON, ERNEST PIERRE FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed. Ameraham Gram. Sch.; clk. acctnt-gen's. dept., Admiralty, 1914-1922; cler. offr., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1922.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Bath; apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1903; clk. ch. sec'a. off., 1904; ag. ast. sec., ch. sec'a. off., Jan. to July, 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; collr., cust., Sept., 1919; ag. ch. clk., ch. sec'a. off., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. comsnr. in 1923, 1926, 1927, 1928 and 1929; ch. collr., cust. and excise, July, 1924; reg'r., Br. ships, reg'r., Cyprus ships, inspr., fisheries; ag. reg'r., trade marks, ag. reg'r., companies, ag. reg'r., patents, July, 1924; mem. leg. coun., 1928; title altered to compt'r., cust. and excise, July, 1927; reg'r., partnerships, Apr., 1929.
WILSON, GROUSE HENRY SUMNER.—B. 1887; ed. Chigwell Schl., Essex; ast. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1921.
WILSON, G. R. C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. of London S.T.M.—Tempy. capt., R.A.M.C., May, 1915 to Feb., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; foundation scholar, 1878; sen. Bell's Univ. scholar, 1879; Chancellor's medal for English verse, 1880; 6th in 1st class classical tripos, and distinguished in exam. for Chancellor's clas, med., 1882; fellow of Trin. Coll., 1884; M.A., 1886; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; sec. to Trinidad judicial inquiry coman., 1892; sent to Malta to collect evidence for the Protestant communities in connection with the Marriages Case, 1893; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cols., June, 1895; legal ast. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal ast. on high comsnr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov'r., Apr. to Aug., 1903, Dec., 1903, to Aug., 1904, and from Aug. to Dec., 1906; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (represented O.R.C. on rly. comte.); retired; was sec. to the Royal Colonial Institute.

WILSON, Lieut.-Col. JOHN WOLSELEY, M.C., V.D.—B. 1878; man., Tobago farm, 1899; sub-inspr., Trinidad constab., 1906; inspr., 1907; senr. inspr., 1916; served with Imp. Army, 1914-19; dep. inspr. gen., constab., 1920; ag. commdt., local forces on several occasions.

WILSON, CAPT. LOGIE WIKR.—B. 1894; ed. Gigglewick Schl., St. And'ew's Coll., Dublin, Royal Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Royal Irish Rifles, 1917; ast. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 23rd July, 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dist. comsnr., Pujehun and Bonthe dista., 1921; dist. comsnr., 1st Aug., 1926.

WILSON, REGINALD CHARLES, D.Sc., F.G.S., Assoc.Inst.M.M.—B. 1888; ed. Scotch Coll. and Melbourne Univ.; capt., Royal Welsh Fusiliers and R.E., France, 1915-18; geologist, Portuguese E. Africa, 1911-14; geologist, geol. survey, Nigeria, 1919-27; dir., 1927.

WILSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, G.C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B. (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), K.B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major 1913; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in S. Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1916; Commandeur de la Couronne (Belgian), 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; French croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comte., 1911-14; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff offr., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comte., 19th Nov., 1918; gov't. of Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov't., Jamaica, 1st Sept., 1924; assumed gov't., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the colo., 5th Aug., 1926.
WILSON, WILLIAM GLASSFORD WALKER, M.I.L.—B. 1894; ed. Kilinarock Acad.; war serv., 1914-19; ast. loco. supdt., Nigerian rly., 1923; wks. man., 1927; ag. ch. mech. engr., 1929.

WILTSHIRE, ERIC.—B. 1893; articled pupil to county survr. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; ast. engr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; ag. dist. engr., in 1926, 1926 and 1927 ag. dist. engr., 1927, 1928 and 1930; ag. ast. dir. in 1927, 1928 and 1930.
WINGATE, ANDREW FREDERICK.—B. 1880; ed. Blundell's Schl.; on Indian rly., 1912-14; Guayaquil and Quito rly., 1914-20; ast. traffic supt., Tanganyika Territory cent. r.y., 1920.
WINBERG, OTHELLO, C.M.G., (1928), M.V.O. (1920).—Priv. sec. to govt. of S. Australia since May, 1915.

WINSON, VICTOR HANTON, B.Sc., A.K.C., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Tiffine Schl., Kingston-on-Thames and King's Coll., London (1st cls. hon., elect. engring.); 2nd cls. engr., G.P.O., engring dept., Oct., 1909; ast. engr., July, 1911; supdt., posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1913; war serv., Aug., 1917-Apr., 1919; ag. div. engr., Penang, Nov., 1919-Jan., 1921; ag. supdt., posts and tels., Malacca, Jan., 1921; supdt., ditto, Jan., 1922; ag. tels. engr., posts and tels., F.M.S., Feb.-Sept., 1926; senr. engr., posts and tels., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct., 1926; ag. engr.in-ch., posts and tels., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct.-Dec., 1926; senr. engr., posts and tels., Penang, July, 1928.

WINSTEADT, RICHARD OLAF, C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. at Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; ag. inspr. of scha., Perak; passed in Malay, 1904; in law, Oct., 1904; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1904; ag. ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Tapah, Oct., 1909; ag. ast. dist. offr., Matang, and insp. of fisheries, Jan., 1908; class V., Jan., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., Gopeng, Apr., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Sept., 1912; sec. to comte. for Malay studies in addition, Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Dec., 1913; federal examiner in Malay in addition, Dec., 1913; promoted to class III., ast. dir. of education, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sanitary bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addition; cls. II., Nov., 1919; ag. dir., educn., S. Stiltnsts., and F.M.S., Feb., 1920; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Stiltnsts., and F.M.S., June, 1920; ag. chmn., comte. for Malay studies, F.M.S., 1920; seconded to act as prin. of Raffles' Coll., Singapore, June, 1921; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., May, 1923; offg. as sec. to high commr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addition, Mar., 1923; dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. LA, July, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comttee, in addn., July, 1924; chmn., tech., educn. comttee, in addn., Nov., 1925; rep. F.M.S., govt. at C.O. confce., Apr., to July, 1927; chmn., comttee., med. research, S.S. and F.M.S., in addn., Aug., 1928; author of an "English-Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Sawu and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melaya.
WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd clk., post office, Dominica, 1920; ast. clk., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. clk., Sept., 1924; ag. audit clk., Jan., Apr., 1927.

WISL, KENNICK STANTON.—B. 1881; ed. Dover, Cambridge, and London; M.B., B.S., B.S.C., Lond., Univ.; M.R.C.S., England; L.R.C.P., Lond.; D.P.H.; govt. bacteriologist, Br. Guiana, 1906; govt. med. offr. of health, 1912; surg.-gen. and regt.-gen., 1914; surg.-gen., Trinidad, 4th Aug., 1919.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1906; 2nd cl. math. moda., 1906; 2nd cl. hist., 1908; 2nd cl. jurisprudence, 1909 apptd. after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Mch., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comttee., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917, to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comttee., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comttee., May, 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cl. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.
WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, M.B.E. (1918), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; 3rd cl. Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.

WITTEENOOM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resign. in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resign. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dec., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901: M.L.C.

WOODEHOUSE, LIMUT.-GEN. SIR JOSEPHINE HENNEAGE, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; commr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; commd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toekli; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cl. Medjidie; 2nd cl. Osmanlieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WOODEHOUSE, P. P. J., C.I.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first clk., regt.-gen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; census offr., 1900; ast. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjab police, 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902; census offr., 1906; pol. mag., N.T. (Islands), Sept., 1906; passed in Punjabi, 1908; mem. of bd. of examiners, 1907; ag. dep. supt. of pol. and fire brig., 1907, 1908 to 1909, 1911 to 1912; census offr., 1911; dep. supt. of police and fire brigade, 1912; aide-de-camp to govt., 1918 to 1919; ag. capt. supt. of pol., on various occasions, 1918-20.

WOODEMAN, GUY STANLEY.—B. 1896; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office ast. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; ast. commr. of excise attached to the office of the excise commr., Apr., 1913; ast. commr. of excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; ag. ast. govt. agt., Matale, Aug., 1915; attached to chief censor's office, Sept., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1926; dep. commr., excise, Nov., 1926; commr., excise, Sept., 1927.

WOHLMANN, WARD GEORGE.—B. 1872; ed. N.Z. state schls.; N.Z. permanent arty., 1894-95; N.Z. pol., 1895; sergt., 1906; senr. sergt., 1914; sub-inspr., 1918; inspr., 1921; supt., 1926; commr., 1930; was commr. of pol., Samoa, 1920-22.

WOLFE, EDWARD DUDLEY CORBACDIN, C.M.G. (1828).—Tonbridge-schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langa., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langa., tripods. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); M.A. (1925); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; sec., F.W.D. enquiry commr., 1901; regist., land ct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1902; ag. ast. regist.-gen., 1902; regist., land ct., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and ast. supt. of corps, 1903-5; seconded to Transvaal govt. serv., 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907; ag. regist.-gen., 1907; ast. regist.-gen., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907 to 1909; head of sanitary dept., 1909; postmr.-gen., 1913; ag. col. treas., 1914-16; 1st pol. mag., 1917; ag. col. treas., and custodian of enemy property, 1917 and 1918; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1918; inspr.-nen., pol., 1920; ag. col. sec., 1921; attended internat. pol. confce., New York, 1925.

WOLFE, HENRY, M.Sc. (Agric.) Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. ast., dept. of agr., S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17; dep. dir. agr. (plant industry), Kenya, 1931.

WOLFFE, PHINEAS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow Acad.; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; ag. office supt., 1917; land ast., 1919; regist., titles, doc. and coast land titles, 1920; liquidator of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.

WOLSELEY-BOURNE, JAMES FREDERICK, M.C.—B. 1878; 2nd lieut. Royal Berks. Regt.; lieut., S. African Constab.; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's and King's medals); capt., K.A.R.; served in E. Africa, 1914-1918 ("1914-15" Star, War and Victory medals); commanded a column in operations against Aulihan (Somali) tribe, Jubaland, 1917-18 (M.C., African Gen. Serv. medal); ast. commr., pol., Kenya, Jan., 1927.

WOOD, ALAN EUSTACE.—Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; scholar of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; 1st mods.; 2nd lit. hum.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrants, 1908; ag. asst. regiar.-gen., 1908 to 1909; passed cadet, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. asst. regis. gen., Apr.-May, 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., N.T., 1910 to 1911; 2nd asst. regiar.-gen., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., South dist., New Territories, 1911 to 1912; passed in Hakka, June, 1912; title of asst. regiar.-gen. altered to 2nd asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1914; ag. dist. offr., 1915; ag. chief asst. to S.C.A., 1916, and 1913 to 1919; passed in Pekingese, June, 1916; sent to Japan to study Japanese, 1916; passed in Japanese, May, 1918; ag. dist. offr., Taipoo, 1919 to 1920; ag. ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Taipoo in addn., 1922; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923 and 1924; ag. dir., educ., Dec., 1923; dir., educ., Jan., 1926; sec., Chinese affrs. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Feb., 1930.

WOOD, BERNARD READER.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Schol., Sid. Suss. Coll. Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; asst. conservator, Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conservator, 1918; conservator, of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925.

WOOD, HARRY EDWIN.—B. 1881; ed. Manchester Grammar Schol. and Owens Coll. Manchester. (B.Soc., 1902, M.Sc., 1906); asst. lect., dept. of physics, Manchester Univ., 1906; ch. asst., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1906; ch. asst., Union observatory, Johannesburg, 1912; served, German E. Africa campaign, 1916; Union astronomer, Jan., 1928.

WOOD, CAPT. JOHN CALDER, M.C., R. of O., R.E.—Qual. Land Surveyor, S. Africa—B. 1885; asst., R. Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1903; cadastral and trig. survr., F.M.S., 1911; war surv., France and England, 1915-19; offr.-in-ch., cadastral framework traverse, Gold Coast survey, 1923; dep. survr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926; surv.-gen., 1930.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1896; 1st cl. class, tripus, 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; called to the bar, 1919; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; regiar. of land ct., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. land ct., 1903-4; asst. land offr. in N. T., Jan., 1905; ag. asst. regi.-gen., 1905; mem. of squatters' bd., 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1908; dep. regiar. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1908; 2nd pol. mag., 1909; ag. dir. of educ., 1911 to 1912; ag. 1st pol. mag., on several occasions; 1st pol. mag., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920-22 and in 1925; chmn., Sui An piracy comm., 1922; puisne judge, 1925; ag. ch. just., 1927 and 1930.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queen's coll., Camb.; 1st cls. math. tripus, pt. I.; 2nd cls. math. tripus., pt. 2; B.A., 1912; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commnr., 1914-20; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. clk. of couns., in 1922, 1929-30; senr. asst. col. sec., 1930.

WOOD, CAPT. REGINALD CECIL MEWYN.—B. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; coy. commnr., 1923; asst. treas., Kenya, 1923; estab. offr., secretariat, Mar., 1928.
WOODFORD, Hon. W. W.—Elected M.H.A., Harbour Main, Newfoundland, 1889; chrmn., bd. of works, 1897; min. of public works, 1898, 1909, and 1918; M.E.C., 1918.
WOODHOUSE, CHARLES ASHBEE, B.A.—B. 1884; ed. Marlborough and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; dist. offr., 1914; rec., 1923; senr. rea., 1929.

WOODHOUSE, JOHN LESLIE.—B. 1892; ed. Merchant Taylors Schol.; asst. transport offr., with rank of lieut., Uganda Transport Corps, Nov., 1914; ment. in despatches for service in German E. Africa, Nov., 1916; asst. dist. commnr., Uganda, Dec., 1916; 2nd grade adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1920; dist. offr., June, 1926.

WOODMAN, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1873; ed. pvt. schl., Winchester Coll. and St. Mary's Hosp.; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.S.A.; attd. to R.A.M.C. at Fort Pitt Hosp., Chatham, during S. African War; London Schol. of Trop. Med. exam., 1909; med. offr. and radiologist and dist. commnr., Br. Honduras, 1910-15; med. offr., hosp., gaol, etc., Hong Kong; ag. med. offr. of health and med. offr. of health, Kowloon, 1916; seconded for serv. in war, 1918-19; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.), in charge of sanitary company, Salonica and Constantinople; in charge, Belize hosp., Jan. to June, 1928; Bahamas med. offr., Out Islands, June to Sept., 1928; ag. res. surg. and radiologist, Bahamas, Sept., 1928-Aug., 1929; ag. dir. med. services, Aug. to Nov., 1929.

WOODS, A. E., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1874; extra master's certif., 1897; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1903; lieut. 1906; lieut.-commdr., 1914; commr., 1919 (retd.); 1st and gunnery offr. H.M.S. "Naid" and "Oropesa," 1914-16; commanded H.M.S.Y. "St. George" sp. serv., Mediterranean, 1915; commanded destroyer divn., extended defence Channel patrol, 1917; ch. exam. offr., Gibraltar, 1918; mast. of "Lord Milner," Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; mast. of "Azania," Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1925.

WOODS, SIR WILFRID WENTWORTH, Kt BAOH. (1930), C.M.G. (1928)—B. 1876; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cls. final hon. schl., mod. hist., 1901; olk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Sept., 1901; asst. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; asst. col. auditor, Caylon, Feb., 1908; ag. col. auditor on several occasions; col. auditor, Aug., 1915; temp. employed at the War Office, Dec., 1917 to 1918; ag. col. treas., May to Nov., 1920; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1920 to Apr., 1921; col. treas., Feb., 1922; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Oct., 1926.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon ciy. serv., May, 1921; attld., Kandy Kach, June, 1921; ag. office astt. to govt.; agt. N.C. Prov., Sept., 1922; 4th astt. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd astt. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attld. C.O., 1925 to June, 1926; addnl. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd astt. col. sec., Oct., 1926; astt. govt. agt., N'Elia, May, 1927; astt. col. sec. and olk., leg. coun., Sept., 1928.

WORKMAN, CHARLES RUFUS MARSHALL, C.B.E. (1927).—B. 1874; ed. Christ Ch., Oxford. M.A.; called to bar, Inner Temple; pol. offr., Ocean Is., 1909; dep. commnr., Western Pacific, 1911; admtr., Nauru, 1914; ag. res. commnr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., May, 1916 to Feb., 1917; ag. res. commnr., Solomon Is., June, 1917; res. commnr., Apr., 1919; judl. commnr., Feb., 1920; col. sec., Gambia, Sept., 1921; ag. gov. on various occasions, 1921-28.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; aast. contr. of lab., Kampar, July, 1917; aast. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; aast. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; aast. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; aast. dir. of educ. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; ag. aast. dir. educ. (Chinese), S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1924; ag. prot. Chinese, Selangor and Pahang in addn., Feb., 1925 and Nov., 1926; aast. dir. educ. (Chinese), F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1927; ag. dir., educ., F.M.S. and S.S., Apr., July, 1927; ag. prot. Chinese, Selangor and Pahang in addn., Feb. to July, 1928.

WORLLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1896; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in desps.; Ireland, 1919; aast. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; aast. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. aast. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. aast. audr., Nov., 1925.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEABURN, B.A. Oxon.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon div. ser., Nov., 1910; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1910; attd. col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office aast. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1911; aast. land settm. offr., Jan., 1912; office aast. to govt. agt., Central Prov., July, 1914; aast. censor, Dec., 1914; on mily. duty, Feb., 1915; ag. aast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1919; aast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1921; ag. govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, Aug. to Oct., 1922; aast. stimlt. offr., Jan., 1924; aast. govt. agt., Batticaloa, May, 1924; ag. govt. agt., Batticaloa, May, 1928; aast. govt. agt., Matara, Jan., 1929; ag. govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Feb., 1930.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOCELYN, O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica, govt. lab. and sp. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. socy., 1906; jnr. aast. chem., Jamaica, 1902; aast. chem., 1905; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmar., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; comnrr. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food comnrr., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comitee, on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1920; dir. agr., Trinidad, 1930; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases," and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."

WRIGHT, MAJOR ANDREW BARKWORTH, M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar (1918).—B. 1893; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (schol.); served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; aast. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 3rd June, 1922; aast. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; ag. priv. sec. to high comnrr., Apr. to June and Sept. to Oct., 1924; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., June to Sept., 1924; ag. ch. aast. sec., regr. of co-op. societies and regr. of co-op. credit societies, Apr. to Oct. 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1924; ditto, Turkish, Apr., 1925; ag. ch. aast. sec. for various periods, 1926-29; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G July-Oct., 1926; ag. col. sec., in 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1930; ch. aast. sec., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1930.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR ALAN, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. St. Edmund's Schl., Canterbury; open exhibn., St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1906; lieut., 3rd Bn. Bedfordshire Regt. (S.R.), 1909; 2nd cls. hons. schl., mod. hist., Oxford, 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, Fiji, 1912; ag. sub-inspr., constab., 1912; ag. elk., ex. coun. and lst cls. elk., col. sec.'s office, 1913; passed cadet, 1914; ag. dist. comnrr., Rewa and prov. comnrr., Naitasiri, 1915; 2nd grade dist. comnrr., 1915; dist. comnrr., Nadi, 1915; dist. comnrr., Colo East, 1916; on mily. duty, 1917; 2nd lieut., 6th Bn. Rifle Brigade, 1917; on active serv., France, 1917; dist. comnrr., Macuata, 1920; ditto (1st grade), Nadroga and prov. comnrr., Nadroga and Colo West, 1922; ditto, Colo North, 1923; ag. aast. col. sec., Jan., 1927; aast. col. sec., Jan., 1928; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1929.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD.—M.A., F.I.C., F.C.S.; B. 1880; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1902; M.A., 1916; aast. analyst and science master, Trinidadd, Apr., 1908; ag. govt. analyst and prof. of chemistry, Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. comnrr., Kadavu, July-Aug., 1918; govt. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agr. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, HERBERT ARTHUR.—B. 1882; instr., govt. technical schl., Accra, G. Coast, 10th Apr., 1909; principal, ditto, 28th Sept., 1911; attached to Togoland field force, 16th Aug. to 8th Sept., 1914; in joint charge of detention camp for German prisoners of war, 8th Dec., 1914 to 13th Jan., 1915; aast. censor, 2nd Oct., 1914 to 2nd Feb., 1915, and 2nd Oct., 1915 to 25th Feb., 1916; prov. inapr. of schls., 25th July, 1917; ag. senr. inapr. of schls., 29th July to 19th Sept., 1917; aast. dir., educon., 1st April, 1926.

WRIGHT, JAMES ROGERS.—B. 1875; ed. at C.M.S Grammar Schl., Sierra Leone; 2nd cls. queen's advo. office, June, 1895; transf'd, gov't. office, July, 1895; aast. mast., circuit ct., Oct., 1903; pol. elk. and elk., ot. of request, Nov., 1908; J.P. for Freetown pol. dist., July, 1909; dep. coroner, Freetown pol. dist., Aug., 1909 and in Apr., 1928; ch. elk. master's office, Jan., 1914; comnrr. of oaths and affidavits, sup. ot., Nov., 1915; dep. mast. and registr., sup. ot. and registr., prize ot., May, 1920; comnrr., ot. of requests, Freetown pol. dist., Apr., 1924; mast. and registr., sup. ot., registr., trade marks, curator of intestate estates and administrator, civ. servants' estates, May, 1924.

WRIGHT, VICTOR WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; served with R. Naval Divn. in Gallipoli and France; wounded, 1914-16; lieu., R.E. and general staff, Macedonia, Caucasus, Trans-Caspia, Constantinople, 1916-20; mast., technical schl., educon., dept., Gold Coast, 1921; organiser, prac. educon., June, 1927.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, CAPT. RODNEY GEORGE, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Schl., stud., engnrng. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E., (S.R.), 1909; engnr. on survey and constr. of Magadi rly. and water wks., 1911-14; R.E., 1914-22; served, Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded, twice ment. in desps., M.C.; div. offr., R.E., Londonderry and Enniskillen Divs., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; sup't. pub. wks., Antigua, July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, Aug., 1922; aast., D.P.W., B. Guiana, Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W., Apr. to Sept., 1927.
WROUGHTON, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1877; med. offr., Basutoland, 1908.
WUPPERMAN, RUDOLF CARL, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1883; entered med. serv., Trinidadd, Oct., 1909; aast. res. surg., colonial hosp. Port of Spain, Oct., 1914; res. surg., col. hosp., May, 1923.

WYATT, ALBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1900; ed. Leamington Coll. and R.M.C.; Sandhurst; lieut., Hampshire Regt., 1919-26; 2nd K.A.R., 1923-26; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Biharamulo, June, 1930.

WYNNE-JONKS, EDWARD IRVINE.—B. 1896; ed. Oxford and Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1920; ag. dist. offr., South, June, 1922; attd. coll. secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; dist. offr., North, May, 1923; ditto, South, Dec., 1926; attd. coll. secretariat, July, 1928; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1928; extra asst. coll. sec., Nov., 1928; ag. 2nd asst. coll. sec. and elk. of couns., in 1929; extra asst. coll. sec. and dep. elk., couns., Dec., 1929; dist. offr., North, 1930.

YATES, GEO. W.—B. 1872; ed. London pub. and high schla.; ent. civ. serv. of Ont. as priv. sec. to various mins., from Feb., 1899 to Oct., 1911; accompanied Hon. F. Cochrane, Ont. min. of lands, forests and mines, to Ottawa on latter accepting portfolio of min. of rlys. and canals in Borden govt.; cont. priv. sec., min. of rlys. and canals until formation of union govt., 1917, when became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Robt. Borden; asst. deputy minister of railways and canals, 1920.

YATES, JOSEPH MERVYN ST. JOHN, B.A.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1903; northern circuit, 1903-15; H.M. Forces, 1915-19; reglar.-gen. Bahamas, Feb., 1920; ag. stip. magis., Mar., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1920; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar., 1921; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; seconded for serv. as ag. asst. to atty.-gen., Jamaica, Sept., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, 1923; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1923 to Jan., 1924; ag. judge, Kingston et al., Nov., 1924 to Oct., 1925; ag. puisne judge, Oct., 1925; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1926; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1928.

YOULL, JOSEPH.—Seconded from Impl. serv. to B. E. Africa and Uganda Prots. as 1st grade dist. surv., 1st Jan., 1913 to 17th Dec., 1915; seconded to G. Coast, 31st May, 1916; offr.-in-charge, post and telegraph dept., Togoland, 22nd May, 1918 to 23rd Jan., 1919, and from 12th Aug., 1919.
YOUNG, ALFRED ERNEST, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1891; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, SIR ALFRED KARNET, K.T. BACH. (1923), K.C., B.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd elk., coll. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and elk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admstr. of the govt., 1896; ag. reglar.-gen., 1896-7; dist. comnstr., Orange Walk, 1898; reglar.-gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., 17th Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; admstr. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1911, and from 16th to 27th Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914; ag. chief just., Jan., 1915 and from Mar., 1919 to May, 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Feb., 1921; chief just., Fiji, 1923; ret. June, 1929.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON; G.C.M.G. (1916); K.O.M.G. (1908); K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; late oomdt., mil. pol., Kyrena, Aug., 1878; asst. comnstr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; comnstr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admstr. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connctn. with relief work, Nov., 1902; coll. sec., S. Stilimts., June, 1906; admstr. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Stilimts., 8th Aug., 1911; assumed govt., 2nd Sept., 1911; also high comnstr. for Malay States and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo and Sarawak; ret. 25th Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.T. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1909; comnstr. of crown lands and immigrtn., 1912-14; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915-18; mem. of comittee, for stilimt. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1890; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906; asst. atty.-gen., 1913; senr. res. mag., 1917; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions; pol. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1922; puisne and circuit judge, Sierra Leone, 1930.

YOUNG, MAJOR HUBERT WINTHROP, C.M.G. (1923), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; comand., R. Garrison Arty, 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; asst. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb.-Dec., 1915 (1914-15 Star); asst. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, Dec., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir., local resources, Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in desps.); gen staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejas operations, Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., 3rd class Order of Nahla); pres., local resources bd., Damascus, Oct., 1918; seconded to F.O., Feb., 1919 to Mar., 1921; seconded to C.O., Middle East Dept. as asst. sec., Mar., 1921 to Dec., 1926; accompanied Mr. Churchill to Cairo confce., Apr., 1921; visited Palestine and Iraq on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv., Lausanne for Turkish peace treaty., Jan., 1922 and at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier dispute on various occasions during 1926 and 1928; ret. from army and apptd. col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1927; couns. to high comnstr., Iraq, Sept., 1929.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Univ.; late house surg., Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on resignation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919; asst. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, MARK AITCHISON.—B. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th asst. coll. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and elk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd asst. sec. coll., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1915-1919; 3rd asst. coll. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd asst. coll. sec., July, 1920; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. ast. coll. sec. and elk., exec. coun., Mar., 1923; coll. sec., Sierra Leone, 1928; ch. sec., Palestine, 1930.

YOUNG, MAJOR-GEN. ROBERT, C.B. (1919); C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1915).—Volunteer serv. in N.Z.; left N.Z. with main body, N.Z. Exped. Force with rank of major, 1914; temp. lieut.-col., May, 1915; O.C. (temp.) 1st batt., Auckland Regt., May-Nov., 1915; lieut.-col., Nov., 1915; in command, 1st batt., Canterbury Regt., Nov., 1915 to Aug., 1917; comdr. (temp.), 2nd N.Z. Infy. Brig., Dec., 1916 to Jan., 1917; brig.-gen. (temp.), Aug., 1917; in command, 1st N.Z. infy. brig., Dec., 1917 to Feb., 1918; in command, 2nd N.Z. infy. brig., Feb., 1918 to Apr., 1919; comdr., “A” group, N.Z. Exped. Force, Sling Camp, Apr. to July, 1919; col., N.Z. staff corps, Oct., 1919; O.C., Canterbury dist., Oct., 1919 to Jan., 1921; O.C., Southern Command, Feb., 1921 to Dec., 1925; col.-comdt., 1921; major-gen. and comdt., N.Z. mily. forces, Dec., 1925; chevalier of Legion of Honour; five times ment. in despatches.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.Ch. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Sch. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhib., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; clin. asst., children and ear depts., St. Thomas’s hosp., 1922; R.M.O., Ealing hosp., 1922-23; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923; ag. R.S.O., Zanzibar, 1927 and 1930.

YOUNG, Sir WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.’s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. asst. govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comanr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admstr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. govt., Leeward Is., 1909; admstr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. govt., Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; govt., Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; assumed govt., May, 1915; ret., 1920.

ZAMMIT, HON. PROFESSOR SIR THOMAS TOOLES, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G. (1911), M.D. (hon.), D.Litt. (Oxon.).—B.1864; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1882; grad. in medicine, 1889; govt. analyst, 1891-1920; curator, Valletta Museum, 1903; prof. of chemistry, Malta Univ., 1905-20; mem., Mediterranean fever comm., 1904-07; rector, Malta Univ., July, 1920; mem., exec. and leg. coun., 1920-21; hon. fellow, Royal Anthropological Inst., 1919; local sec. (Malta) of Soc. of Antiquaries, 1921; medallist (Mary Kingsley), Liverpool Sch. of Trop. Med.; delegate of Malta govt. at Imp. Educat. Conf., London, June to July, 1923.
ZIAI, MUSTAPHA FUAD.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray’s Inn, 1923; inspr., Cyprus mily. pol., Nov., 1906; judge, dist. ct., Apr., 1913; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.
